National Response Center report number assigned when the incident was reported to the federal hotline for oil and hazardous substance releases.
Total amount of regulatory penalties or fines assessed for the environmental incident, in USD.
Indicates whether regulatory penalties or fines were assessed against the company for the environmental incident.
Date when remediation activities were completed and site restoration was achieved.
Name of the environmental remediation contractor engaged to perform cleanup and restoration activities.
Reference identifier or document number for the approved remediation plan addressing the environmental contamination.
Date when active remediation activities commenced at the incident site.
Identifier or serial number of the specific equipment unit that was the source of the release.

Type of fuel center equipment that was the source of the environmental release. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: underground_storage_tank|dispenser|piping|vent_system|delivery_connection|spill_bucket|overfill_prevention — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date and time when the state environmental agency was notified of the environmental incident.
Indicates whether the state environmental agency was notified of the environmental release as required by state regulations.
Total cost incurred for environmental remediation, monitoring, and regulatory compliance activities related to the incident, in USD.
Timestamp when this environmental incident record was last modified in the system.
Estimated or measured volume of fuel or hazardous material released during the incident, in gallons. Critical for EPA release reporting thresholds.

business_glossary_term: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Compliance Report ID
description: Unique identifier for the EPA compliance report record.
Identifier of the fuel center location where this compliance report or incident occurred.

Environmental medium impacted by the incident: soil for subsurface contamination, groundwater for aquifer pollution, surface water for waterway impact, air for vapor emissions, or multiple media for incidents affecting more than one medium. Null for routine compliance reports.

EPA-mandated compliance category: leak detection for Underground Storage Tank (UST) monitoring systems, release reporting for fuel discharge events, spill/overfill prevention for equipment safeguards, corrosion protection for tank integrity, financial responsibility for liability coverage, or operator training for certification requirements.

Current lifecycle status of the compliance report or incident: compliant for meeting all requirements, non-compliant for violations identified, pending review for submissions under agency evaluation, corrective action required for mandated remediation, remediation in progress for active cleanup, or closed for completed incidents.

Detailed description of corrective actions or remediation measures required by the regulatory agency, including equipment repairs, system upgrades, soil excavation, groundwater treatment, or monitoring well installation. Null if no corrective action required.
Deadline by which corrective action or remediation must be completed as mandated by the regulatory agency. Null if no corrective action required.
Indicates whether corrective action or remediation is mandated by the regulatory agency. True if action is required, False if no action needed.
Timestamp when this compliance report record was first created in the system.

Estimated total cost of remediation and corrective actions in USD, including cleanup, equipment replacement, monitoring, and regulatory compliance expenses. Null if no remediation required or cost not yet estimated.
Type of fuel product involved in the incident or compliance report: regular unleaded, mid-grade, premium, diesel, E85 ethanol blend, or biodiesel.

Description of immediate containment, cleanup, and safety actions taken upon discovery of the incident, including emergency shutdowns, spill containment, and site securing. Null for routine compliance reports.

Date when the regulatory agency formally closed the incident or compliance case, indicating all corrective actions completed and remediation verified. Null for open incidents or routine compliance reports.
Date when the environmental incident or compliance violation occurred. Null for routine compliance reports.
Precise timestamp when the environmental incident or compliance violation was detected. Null for routine compliance reports.

Classification of environmental incident: fuel spill for surface discharge, tank leak for Underground Storage Tank (UST) breach, vapor release for airborne emissions, soil contamination for subsurface pollution, groundwater contamination for aquifer impact, or surface water contamination for waterway pollution. Null for routine compliance reports.
Date when the regulatory inspection or compliance audit was conducted. Null for incident reports without formal inspection.
Summary of findings from regulatory inspection or compliance audit, including violations identified, equipment deficiencies, and procedural gaps. Null for reports without formal inspection.
Insurance claim reference number for financial responsibility coverage related to the incident or remediation. Null if no insurance claim filed.
Timestamp when this compliance report record was last modified, reflecting updates to compliance status, remediation progress, or regulatory actions.
Date when the next compliance report, follow-up submission, or remediation status update is due to the regulatory agency. Null for closed incidents or one-time reports.

Name of the regulatory agency overseeing this compliance report or incident, typically EPA regional office or state environmental agency with delegated Underground Storage Tank (UST) program authority.
Date when the regulatory agency was formally notified of the incident or compliance issue, as required by EPA 24-hour release reporting requirements. Null for routine compliance reports.
Official reference number or case identifier assigned by the regulatory agency for tracking this compliance report, incident, or enforcement action.
Reference number or identifier for the formal remediation plan submitted to the regulatory agency, linking to detailed cleanup strategy and timeline. Null if no remediation plan required.

Current status of remediation activities: not started for approved plans awaiting execution, in progress for active cleanup, monitoring for post-cleanup observation, completed for finished remediation awaiting verification, or verified closed for agency-approved closure. Null if no remediation required.
Business identifier for the compliance report or incident, used for external regulatory reference and tracking.

Classification of the EPA report: compliance reporting for routine submissions, environmental incident for release events, inspection finding for regulatory audit results, corrective action for required remediation, or remediation status for ongoing cleanup tracking.
End date of the compliance reporting period covered by this report.
Start date of the compliance reporting period covered by this report.
Name of the legal entity or party responsible for the Underground Storage Tank (UST) system and compliance obligations, typically the fuel center operator or corporate owner.
Date when the compliance report or incident notification was submitted to the regulatory agency.
Physical tank number or designation at the fuel center involved in the incident or compliance report.
Identifier of the specific Underground Storage Tank system involved in the incident or compliance report, linking to tank registration and equipment records.
Quantity of fuel or petroleum product released during the incident, measured in gallons. Null for routine compliance reports or incidents where volume cannot be determined.
Unique identifier for the rack price record.
Identifier for the fuel grade (regular, mid-grade, premium, diesel) to which this rack price applies.
The approval status for this rack price record, supporting workflow controls for price changes.
The name or identifier of the manager or system user who approved this rack price for use in pricing decisions.
The timestamp when this rack price record was approved for use.
The contract or agreement number under which this rack price is provided, if applicable.
The end date of the contract term for negotiated rack pricing agreements.
The start date of the contract term for negotiated rack pricing agreements.
The timestamp when this rack price record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rack price (e.g., USD).
The method or channel by which this rack price data was received: EDI (Electronic Data Interchange), API, Manual entry, Email, or FTP.
The internal or industry-standard code identifying the petroleum distributor.
The name of the petroleum distributor or supplier providing this rack price.
The date on which this rack price becomes effective for pricing and margin calculations.
The precise timestamp when this rack price becomes effective, supporting intraday price changes.
Environmental or regulatory fees per gallon (e.g., underground storage tank fees, emissions fees) applicable to this rack price.
reference: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency), State Environmental Regulations

description: The date on which this rack price expires and is superseded by a new price, nullable for open-ended pricing.
The freight or transportation cost per gallon from the terminal to the fuel center, used to calculate delivered cost.
The applicable geographic market or region for this rack price (e.g., Gulf Coast, Midwest, West Coast).
The published market index source for the rack price (e.g., OPIS, Platts, DTN, Argus) or Internal for proprietary pricing.
reference: OPIS (Oil Price Information Service), Platts, DTN, Argus Media

description: Free-text notes or comments regarding this rack price record, capturing context, exceptions, or special conditions.
The wholesale petroleum rack price per gallon from the distributor or spot market index, serving as the cost basis for fuel margin calculation.
A multiplier or adjustment factor applied to the base rack price for volume discounts, loyalty programs, or contract terms.
The basis or source type for the rack price: terminal (local terminal rack), spot (spot market), contract (negotiated contract), or index (published index).
The business reason or trigger for this rack price change (e.g., market volatility, contract renewal, seasonal adjustment, regulatory change).
The current lifecycle status of this rack price record: active (in use), pending (scheduled future), expired (past effective period), superseded (replaced by newer price), or cancelled.
The classification of the price: wholesale (distributor rack), retail_reference (suggested retail), cost_plus (cost plus markup), or market_index (published index).
The date on which this rack price was published or announced by the distributor or index source.
The timestamp when this rack price record was received or ingested into the system from the distributor or index feed.
The applicable federal, state, and local excise tax per gallon included in or added to the rack price.
The city where the petroleum terminal is located.
The name or identifier of the petroleum terminal or distribution point where this rack price applies.
The state or province code where the petroleum terminal is located.
The total delivered cost per gallon including rack price, freight, taxes, and fees, representing the true cost basis for margin calculation.
The timestamp when this rack price record was last modified or updated.
The volume tier or bracket that qualifies for this rack price (e.g., 0-10000 gallons, 10001-50000 gallons), supporting tiered pricing structures.
System-generated unique identifier for each fuel tax record.
Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_center. Business justification: Fuel tax rules apply to specific fuel centers; linking tax to center enables compliance reporting and eliminates silo.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns fuel tax expense to the cost center responsible for the fuel operation.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax liabilities are posted to a dedicated GL account for tax reporting.
Self-referencing FK on tax (superseded_tax_id)
Specifies whether the tax applies at the point of sale, during delivery, or both.
Result of internal audit of the tax record.
Timestamp when the most recent audit was performed.
Internal code representing the tax category for reporting and accounting purposes.
Indicates whether the tax record complies with current regulatory requirements.
Timestamp when the tax record was first created in the system.
Date on which the tax rate becomes effective.
Code representing the reason for tax exemption (e.g., SNAP, EBT).
Detailed description of the exemption reason.
True if the tax does not apply to the transaction under the exemption rules.
Narrative description of rules that qualify a transaction for tax exemption.
Date on which the tax rate expires or is superseded; null if indefinite.
Day of the month by which the tax filing must be submitted.
Government‑issued identifier for the tax (e.g., state tax ID).
Standard code identifying the jurisdiction (e.g., state abbreviation, FIPS county code).
Level of government imposing the tax: federal, state, county, or city.
Human‑readable name of the jurisdiction imposing the tax.
Timestamp when the effective date or rate was last modified.
Free‑form comments or remarks about the tax record.
Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the tax rate (e.g., USD).
Exact timestamp when the tax rate became effective.
Monetary amount of tax charged per gallon of fuel, expressed in the applicable currency.
Internal finance account used for remitting the tax to the authority.
How often the tax must be remitted to the governing authority.
Originating system that supplied the tax data (e.g., SAP FI, Oracle Retail).
Current lifecycle status of the tax record.
Classification of the tax, such as excise, environmental surcharge, underground storage tank (UST) fee, or other applicable tax.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the tax record.
Unique identifier for the fleet card record. Primary key.
Identifier of the driver or employee associated with this fleet card. Used for driver-specific cards to track fuel purchases per individual. Nullable for vehicle-specific cards.
Reference to the parent fleet card account that this individual card belongs to. Links to the fleet_card_account product.
Comma-separated list of fuel grades that this fleet card is authorized to purchase (e.g., regular, mid-grade, premium, diesel). Used to enforce fuel type restrictions at the pump.
The date when the fleet card was permanently cancelled and removed from service. Nullable if the card is still active or suspended.
Free-text explanation of why the fleet card was cancelled (e.g., vehicle sold, driver terminated, account closed). Nullable if card is not cancelled.
The physical format and technology of the fleet card: magnetic stripe, chip (EMV), RFID, contactless (NFC), or hybrid (multiple technologies).
reference: ISO/IEC 7816 - Smart Card Standards, EMV Payment Standards

description: The unique RFID fleet card number embossed or encoded on the physical card. Used for transaction authorization at fuel pumps.
reference: PCI DSS - Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

description: Reason why this fleet card was issued as a replacement for a previous card. Tracks the history of card reissuance events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: lost|stolen|damaged|expired|compromised|upgrade|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Current lifecycle status of the fleet card indicating whether it is active and authorized for use, or inactive due to suspension, loss, theft, expiration, or cancellation.
Classification of the fleet card indicating its intended use: vehicle-specific, driver-specific, universal (any vehicle/driver), temporary, emergency, or contractor card.
The timestamp when this fleet card record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Maximum dollar amount that can be spent per day using this fleet card. Used for budget control and fraud prevention. Nullable if no limit is set.
Maximum number of fuel transactions allowed per day using this fleet card. Used to detect and prevent fraudulent activity. Nullable if no limit is set.
Days of the week when this fleet card is authorized for use (e.g., Monday-Friday for weekday-only vehicles). Formatted as comma-separated day names or codes. Nullable if no day restrictions apply.
The date when the fleet card expires and is no longer valid for transactions. Cards must be renewed or replaced before this date.
The date when the fleet card was issued and activated for use.
The timestamp when this fleet card record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Indicates whether transactions using this fleet card are eligible for loyalty-linked fuel discount redemptions. True if eligible, false if excluded from loyalty programs.

Comma-separated list of merchant category codes (MCC) where this fleet card is authorized or restricted. Used to limit card usage to fuel purchases only or allow additional categories like maintenance.
Indicates whether the driver must enter the vehicle odometer reading at the pump when using this fleet card. True if odometer entry is mandatory, false otherwise.
Maximum number of gallons that can be purchased in a single transaction using this fleet card. Used to prevent fraud and enforce fueling limits. Nullable if no cap is set.
Indicates whether a PIN is required for transactions using this fleet card. True if PIN authentication is enabled, false if card can be used without PIN.
reference: PCI DSS - Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

business_glossary_term: PIN (Personal Identification Number) Last Changed Date
description: The date when the PIN for this fleet card was last changed or reset. Used for security auditing and PIN rotation policy enforcement.
reference: PCI DSS - Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

description: The card number of the previous fleet card that this card replaces. Used to maintain card replacement history and link transactions across card generations. Nullable if this is the original card.
reference: PCI DSS - Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

description: The type of RFID technology used in the fleet card: passive (no battery, powered by reader), active (battery-powered), or semi-passive (battery-assisted passive).
The date when the fleet card was suspended. Nullable if the card has never been suspended or is currently active.
Free-text explanation of why the fleet card was suspended (e.g., suspected fraud, policy violation, vehicle out of service). Nullable if card is not suspended.
Time windows when this fleet card is authorized for use (e.g., 06:00-22:00 for day-shift drivers). Formatted as comma-separated time ranges. Nullable if no time restrictions apply.
Identifier of the vehicle associated with this fleet card. Used for vehicle-specific cards to track fuel consumption per vehicle. Nullable for driver-specific or universal cards.
Unique identifier for the fleet card account. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Account manager associate oversees fleet card accounts; required for account audit and billing responsibility reports.
Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_center. Business justification: Fleet card accounts are managed per fuel center; linking provides clear ownership and reporting.
Date when the fleet card account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.
Externally-known unique account number assigned to the fleet operator for billing and identification purposes.
Date when the fleet card account was first opened and activated for use.
Current lifecycle status of the fleet card account indicating whether it is operational, suspended, or closed.
Current count of active fleet cards under this account that are authorized for transactions.

Comma-separated list of fuel grades (regular, mid-grade, premium, diesel) that cards under this account are authorized to purchase. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular|mid_grade|premium|diesel|e85|biodiesel|cng — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether the fleet card account contract automatically renews upon expiration.
First line of the billing address for invoice delivery and account correspondence.
Second line of the billing address (suite, unit, building number).
City name for the billing address.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the billing address.
Day of the month (1-31) when the billing cycle closes and invoices are generated for this fleet account.
Postal or ZIP code for the billing address.
Two-letter state or province code for the billing address.
Type of fleet card program under which this account operates (proprietary store-branded, WEX, Voyager, Mastercard Fleet, Visa Fleet, Fleet One).
Date when the current fleet card contract or agreement expires and requires renewal.
Timestamp when this fleet card account record was first created in the system.
Maximum credit limit extended to the fleet account for fuel purchases and related transactions.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this account (e.g., USD, CAD).
Maximum number of fuel transactions allowed per card per day under this account.
Maximum total fuel volume (in gallons) that can be purchased per card per day under this account.
Per-gallon discount rate (in currency units) applied to fuel purchases for this fleet account.
Pricing tier or discount level assigned to the fleet account based on volume commitments or negotiated contracts.
Legal or business name of the commercial fleet operator or company holding this account.
Timestamp when this fleet card account record was last updated or modified.
Date of the most recent fuel transaction processed under this fleet account.
Indicates whether this fleet account is linked to the retailer's loyalty program for additional fuel discount redemptions.
Free-text field for internal notes, special instructions, or account-specific comments related to this fleet account.
Number of days from invoice date within which payment is due (e.g., Net 30, Net 15).
Maximum fuel volume (in gallons) allowed for a single transaction per card under this account.
Indicates whether a PIN is required for all card transactions under this account for enhanced security.
Email address of the primary contact for account communications, billing notifications, and support.
Full name of the primary contact person for this fleet account (fleet manager or account administrator).
Primary phone number for the fleet account contact person.
Indicates whether the fleet cards under this account use RFID technology for contactless pump authorization.
Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax ID for the fleet operator, used for tax reporting and compliance.
Total number of individual fleet cards issued under this account across all vehicles and drivers.
Maximum total spending allowed per card per week under this account.
Unique identifier for the loyalty fuel redemption transaction. Primary key.
Reference to the Point of Sale (POS) fuel transaction at the pump where the discount was applied.
Identifier of the loyalty program member who redeemed the fuel discount.

Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: REQUIRED: Tie fuel loyalty redemption to the overall order to reflect total loyalty activity in order‑level reports and customer satisfaction metrics.
Identifier of the external partner loyalty program (e.g., airline miles, credit card rewards) if the discount originated from a partner reward. Null if internal loyalty only.
Identifier of the promotional campaign or offer that enabled this fuel discount. Null if not part of a specific promotion.
Identifier of the specific fuel pump where the redemption was processed.
Identifier of the store location where the fuel center is located and the redemption occurred.
Channel or interface through which the loyalty discount was activated or redeemed (at-pump, mobile app, kiosk, cashier-assisted).
Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount. Typically USD for US-based grocery retail operations.
Date by which the fuel discount offer must be redeemed. Format: yyyy-MM-dd. Null if no expiration.
The discount rate applied to the fuel purchase, expressed in cents per gallon (e.g., 10 cents off per gallon).
Masked fleet card number used for the transaction (e.g., last 4 digits only). Applicable for fleet card transactions tracked via Radio Frequency Identification (RFID).
The grade or type of fuel purchased (e.g., regular, midgrade, premium, diesel) to which the discount was applied.
Total number of gallons of fuel to which the loyalty discount was applied during this transaction.
Loyalty program tier or membership level of the customer at the time of redemption (e.g., silver, gold, platinum). May influence discount eligibility or rate.
Maximum dollar amount of discount allowed per transaction based on loyalty program rules or promotional terms. Used to cap total discount.
Maximum number of gallons eligible for discount per transaction based on loyalty program rules or promotional terms.
Payment instrument used for the fuel transaction (credit card, debit card, fleet card, mobile payment, cash). Distinct from redemption source.
Loyalty member's points balance immediately after this fuel discount redemption.
Loyalty member's points balance immediately before this fuel discount redemption.
Number of loyalty program points redeemed to obtain this fuel discount. Null if discount source is not loyalty points.
Identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) terminal or pump controller that processed the redemption transaction.
Date when the fuel redemption transaction was reconciled with the loyalty program system. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Status of the redemption record in the loyalty program reconciliation process (reconciled with loyalty system, pending, discrepancy identified, adjusted).
Source or origin of the fuel discount being redeemed (e.g., loyalty points accumulated, promotional offer, partner reward program, credit card linked discount).
Current status of the fuel discount redemption transaction in its lifecycle (completed, pending, reversed, failed, expired).
Date and time when the loyalty fuel discount was redeemed at the pump. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX
Reason for reversing or voiding the fuel discount redemption, if applicable. Null if redemption was not reversed.
Total dollar value of the fuel discount redeemed in this transaction (calculated as discount rate × gallons discounted).
Receipt or confirmation number printed for the customer at the pump, linking the fuel purchase to the loyalty redemption.
Timestamp when this redemption record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX
Unique identifier for the fuel center location. Primary key for the fuel center master record. Serves as the anchor for all fuel domain operations and relationships.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating fuel center operating expenses to the cost center in budgeting and expense reporting.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.carrier. Business justification: Fuel procurement contracts assign a primary carrier per fuel center; this FK supports carrier performance and contract compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.node. Business justification: Store Operations Dashboard combines fuel and grocery metrics; linking fuel_center to its fulfillment node enables unified reporting of sales, inventory, and labor.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posts fuel center sales revenue to the appropriate GL account for financial statements.
Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Maps each fuel center to its legal entity for corporate tax filing and regulatory compliance.
Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Required for Fuel Center Manager staffing report; each center has a manager associate for labor budgeting and compliance.

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_rule. Business justification: Each fuel center is assigned a primary pricing rule that governs fuel price calculations and compliance, needed for rule‑driven price updates.
Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Zone‑based fuel pricing strategy requires each fuel center to be linked to a price zone for consistent pricing across locations.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables profit‑center revenue reporting for each fuel center in profit‑and‑loss statements.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required for the 'Fuel Center Compliance Assignment' process that maps each center to its EPA/state compliance program for reporting and audits.
Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Associates a fuel discount campaign with a particular fuel center for campaign performance analysis and localized marketing.

Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: Cluster-level fuel pricing and promotion planning report aggregates fuel_center data by store cluster, requiring direct FK to store_cluster.
Foreign key reference to the parent store location that operates this fuel center. Links fuel center operations to the primary retail store entity.

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Fuel center tracks primary fuel supplier for contract management and compliance reporting, a standard practice in grocery fuel operations.
Primary street address of the fuel center location. First line of the physical site address including street number and name.
Secondary address information for the fuel center location. Optional field for suite, building, or additional location details.
Indicates whether alternative fuels such as E85, biodiesel, or compressed natural gas are available at this fuel center. True if any alternative fuel is offered.
Physical canopy structure configuration covering the fuel dispensing area. Describes the overhead shelter arrangement for customer protection.
Indicates whether a car wash facility is available at this fuel center location. True if car wash services are offered.
City or municipality where the fuel center is located. Part of the complete physical address for the facility.
Indicates whether a convenience store or retail shop is physically attached to the fuel center. True if convenience store is present.
Three-letter ISO country code for the fuel center location. Identifies the country where the facility operates.
Timestamp when this fuel center record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Indicates whether diesel fuel is available at this fuel center. True if diesel is offered, false otherwise.
Total number of individual fuel dispensers (pumps) at the fuel center. Represents the maximum number of vehicles that can fuel simultaneously.
Current operational status of the fuel center facility. Indicates whether the location is actively serving customers or in a non-operational state.
Unique business identifier code for the fuel center. Externally-known alphanumeric code used in operational systems, reporting, and vendor communications. Format: FC followed by 6 digits.
Business name or designation of the fuel center location. Human-readable identifier used for operational reference and customer communication.
Comma-separated list of fuel grades available at this fuel center. Examples: Regular 87, Mid-Grade 89, Premium 91, Diesel, E85. Defines the product mix for pricing and inventory management.
Date of the most recent preventive maintenance performed on fuel dispensing equipment. Used for equipment lifecycle management and reliability tracking.
Date of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection conducted at the fuel center. Used for compliance tracking and maintenance scheduling.
Timestamp when this fuel center record was last updated in the system. Used for data lineage and change tracking.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the fuel center location. Used for mapping, routing, and proximity analysis. Decimal degrees format.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the fuel center location. Used for mapping, routing, and proximity analysis. Decimal degrees format.
Indicates whether the fuel center participates in the loyalty program fuel discount redemption. True if loyalty-linked fuel discounts are available at this location.
Scheduled date for the next required regulatory or safety inspection. Used for compliance planning and operational scheduling.
Date when the fuel center first opened for business operations. Used for lifecycle tracking and performance analysis.
Standard operating hours for the fuel center on weekdays (Monday through Friday). Format: HH:MM-HH:MM or 24/7 for continuous operation.
Standard operating hours for the fuel center on weekends (Saturday and Sunday). Format: HH:MM-HH:MM or 24/7 for continuous operation.
Primary contact phone number for the fuel center location. Used for operational communication and customer inquiries.

business_glossary_term: POS (Point of Sale) Integration Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the fuel center is integrated with the store POS system for payment processing and transaction reconciliation. True if POS integration is active.
ZIP or postal code for the fuel center location. Five-digit or ZIP+4 format for US locations.
Indicates whether premium grade fuel is available at this fuel center. True if premium fuel is offered, false otherwise.
Total number of pump islands at the fuel center. Each island typically contains multiple dispensers. Used for capacity planning and operational analysis.

business_glossary_term: RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) Fleet Card Enabled Flag
description: Indicates whether the fuel center supports RFID-enabled fleet card transactions for commercial customers. True if RFID fleet card processing is available.
State-issued license number authorizing the fuel center to sell petroleum products. Required for regulatory compliance and operational authorization.
Two-letter state or province code for the fuel center location. ISO 3166-2 standard subdivision code.
Total number of underground storage tanks at the fuel center. Used for inventory management, compliance tracking, and capacity planning.
Total fuel storage capacity across all underground tanks at the fuel center, measured in gallons. Represents maximum inventory holding capacity.
Indicates whether the fuel center operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. True for continuous operation, false for limited hours.
Underground Storage Tank registration number issued by state or federal authorities. Required for environmental compliance and tank monitoring.
Unique identifier for the fuel delivery record. Primary key for the fuel delivery lifecycle from order placement through receipt confirmation.
Identifier of the transportation carrier delivering the fuel. May be the same as distributor or a third-party logistics provider.
Identifier of the fuel center receiving the delivery. Links to the fuel center location where fuel is being replenished.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigns fuel delivery expense to the cost center responsible for inventory replenishment.
Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Needed for driver performance, safety incident, and EPA reporting; delivery driver is an associate employee.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Delivery costs are posted to a GL account for cost of goods sold.
Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: Fuel procurement uses purchase orders; linking deliveries to orders enables spend reporting and order‑to‑receipt reconciliation.
Identifier of the fuel distributor or supplier fulfilling the delivery. Links to the vendor providing the fuel.
Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier_site. Business justification: TRACEABILITY: Delivery manifests record the exact supplier site of origin for tax, traceability, and inventory reconciliation.
Identifier of the underground storage tank receiving the fuel delivery. Links to the specific tank within the fuel center.
Actual date when the fuel delivery occurred. Used for reconciliation and variance analysis against scheduled date.
Precise timestamp when the fuel delivery was completed. Marks the end of the physical fuel transfer and receipt confirmation.
Precise timestamp when the fuel delivery began. Marks the start of the physical fuel transfer process.
Bill of lading number for the fuel shipment. Legal document issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment.
reference: 49 CFR Part 373 - Receipts and Bills of Lading

description: Total cost of the fuel delivery in USD. Includes fuel product cost and may include transportation charges.
Unit cost per gallon of fuel delivered in USD. Used for inventory valuation and margin analysis.
Timestamp when the fuel delivery record was first created in the system. Marks the beginning of the delivery lifecycle tracking.

Current lifecycle status of the fuel delivery. Tracks the delivery from scheduling through completion or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in_transit|arrived|in_progress|completed|cancelled|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Name of the fuel distributor or supplier company. Business name of the vendor fulfilling the delivery.
Indicates whether the driver signature was captured electronically or on paper at delivery completion. Used for proof of delivery validation.
Indicates whether EPA emissions and environmental compliance was verified for the delivery. Tracks adherence to underground storage tank regulations.
Estimated volume of fuel ordered in gallons. Represents the planned quantity at the time of order placement.
Separate freight or transportation charge for the delivery in USD. May be itemized separately from fuel product cost.

Grade or type of fuel being delivered. Indicates octane rating for gasoline or fuel type classification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular_87|midgrade_89|premium_91|premium_93|diesel|e85|e15 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ASTM D4814 - Standard Specification for Automotive Spark-Ignition Engine Fuel

description: Gross volume of fuel delivered in gallons before temperature correction. Raw measurement from the delivery truck meter.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11.1 - Volume Correction Factors

description: Vendor invoice number for the fuel delivery. Links the delivery to accounts payable for payment processing.
Timestamp when the fuel delivery record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any delivery attribute.
Net volume of fuel delivered in gallons after temperature correction to 60°F. Temperature-adjusted volume used for inventory and billing.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11.1 - Volume Correction Factors

description: Free-text notes or comments about the delivery. Captures exceptions, issues, or special circumstances during delivery execution.
Confirmation reference number from the fuel order placement system. Links the delivery to the original replenishment order.
Fuel tank level in gallons after the delivery was completed. Final measurement from Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system used for reconciliation and shrink analysis.
Fuel tank level in gallons before the delivery began. Baseline measurement from Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system used for reconciliation.
Indicates whether the delivered fuel passed quality testing at receipt. Used for quality assurance and compliance tracking.
reference: ASTM D4814 - Standard Specification for Automotive Spark-Ignition Engine Fuel

description: Method that triggered the fuel replenishment order. Indicates whether order was automated via Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system, Computer-Assisted Ordering (CAO), or manually placed.
Planned date for the fuel delivery. Used for supply planning and fuel availability management.
End of the scheduled delivery time window. Defines the latest expected time for delivery completion.
Beginning of the scheduled delivery time window. Precise timestamp for when the delivery is expected to begin.
Temperature of the fuel at the time of delivery in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for temperature correction calculations to determine net gallons.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11.1 - Volume Correction Factors

description: External delivery ticket number issued by the carrier or distributor. Serves as the business identifier for the delivery transaction.
Identification number of the delivery truck or tanker. Vehicle identifier for tracking and compliance purposes.
Difference between estimated gallons ordered and net gallons delivered. Used for supply planning accuracy analysis and shrink detection.
Percentage variance between estimated and actual delivery quantity. Calculated as (variance_gallons / estimated_gallons_ordered) * 100.
Unique identifier for the fuel delivery schedule record. Primary key.
Identifier of the transportation carrier responsible for delivering the fuel shipment.
Identifier of the fuel center location receiving the delivery.
Identifier of the parent store location associated with the fuel center.
Identifier of the fuel distributor or vendor assigned to fulfill this delivery.
Identifier of the specific underground storage tank at the fuel center that will receive this delivery.
The actual date and time when the fuel delivery was completed, if applicable.
The actual volume of fuel in gallons that was delivered, recorded upon completion.
The bill of lading number issued by the carrier for this fuel shipment, serving as a receipt and contract.
Explanation or reason code if the delivery schedule was cancelled (e.g., supplier issue, weather, tank maintenance).
External confirmation number or reference code provided by the distributor or carrier for this scheduled delivery.
Name of the on-site contact person at the fuel center for coordinating the delivery.
Phone number of the on-site contact person for delivery coordination.
Username or identifier of the system user or automated process that created this delivery schedule record.
The date and time when this delivery schedule record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the delivery cost (e.g., USD).
The current fuel inventory level in gallons in the target tank at the time the delivery was scheduled.
Special instructions or notes for the carrier regarding site access, safety protocols, or delivery procedures.
Name of the driver who delivered the fuel shipment.
Indicates whether this delivery meets EPA environmental and emissions compliance requirements (True/False).
The estimated total cost in USD for this fuel delivery, including product and transportation charges.
The estimated volume of fuel in gallons ordered for this delivery.
The grade or type of fuel scheduled for delivery (e.g., Regular, Premium, Diesel).
Username or identifier of the system user or automated process that last modified this delivery schedule record.
The date and time when this delivery schedule record was last updated or modified.
Business identifier for the fuel order associated with this delivery schedule.
Current lifecycle status of the delivery schedule (e.g., Scheduled, Confirmed, In Transit, Delivered, Cancelled, Delayed).
The urgency or priority level assigned to this delivery to prevent out-of-stock (OOS) conditions.
Certification or quality assurance reference number confirming the fuel meets EPA and industry standards.
The inventory level in gallons at which a reorder is automatically triggered to prevent stockouts.
The method that triggered the reorder: ATG (Automatic Tank Gauge) automated, CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) automated, manual, or emergency.
The original scheduled delivery date if this delivery was rescheduled from a prior date.
The planned date on which the fuel delivery is scheduled to occur.
The end of the scheduled delivery time window, including date and time.
The beginning of the scheduled delivery time window, including date and time.
The fuel temperature in degrees Fahrenheit recorded at the time of delivery, used for volume correction calculations.
The contracted or estimated price per gallon in USD for the fuel being delivered.
The difference in gallons between the estimated and actual delivered quantities (actual minus estimated).
Identifier or license plate number of the delivery vehicle (tanker truck) used for this shipment.
Primary key for the FuelPricing association
Foreign key linking to fuel_center
Foreign key linking to fuel_grade
Wholesale cost per gallon for the center
Date and time when this pricing becomes effective
Date and time when this pricing expires
Margin earned per gallon (price minus cost)
Retail price per gallon at the pump
Unique identifier for each fuel dispensing transaction event. Primary key for the fuel transaction record.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocates transaction revenue to the cost center for expense tracking and budgeting.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each fuel sale must be posted to a GL account for revenue recognition.
FK to fuel.fuel_grade

Foreign key linking to customer.household. Business justification: Needed for household‑level fuel rewards program: aggregate fuel spend per household to determine eligibility, tier status, and regulatory reporting of SNAP/EBT usage.
Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: REQUIRED: Link fuel sale to stored payment method for refunds, tokenization, and loyalty reward calculations.

Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: REQUIRED: Integrated POS transaction linking fuel sale to grocery order for combined loyalty points, sales reporting, and financial reconciliation.
Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: REQUIRED: Reconcile each fuel sale with its POS payment record for daily financial reporting and settlement.
Driver identifier captured during fleet card transaction. Used for driver-level fuel consumption reporting and expense allocation.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Links each fuel sale to a profit center for profitability analysis.

Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_offer. Business justification: Required for tracking which promo offer discounts a fuel transaction used, enabling redemption reporting and financial reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to customer.shopper. Business justification: Required for loyalty and CLTV reporting: each fuel purchase must be linked to the shopper who made it to calculate rewards, churn risk, and compliance with fuel discount regulations.
Identifier of the store location where the fuel center is located. Links to the store master data.
Payment authorization code received from payment processor. Used for payment reconciliation and dispute resolution.
Indicates whether a carwash service was bundled with this fuel purchase. Used for cross-sell analytics and bundled promotion tracking.
System timestamp when this transaction record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction. Typically USD for US operations.
Indicates whether this transaction requires inclusion in EPA emissions compliance reporting. Used for regulatory reporting and environmental compliance tracking.
Federal excise tax amount collected on this fuel transaction. Required for IRS Form 720 quarterly federal excise tax reporting.
Fleet card number used for commercial vehicle fuel purchases. Supports RFID fleet card transaction processing and fleet account reconciliation.
Discount amount in cents per gallon applied through loyalty program redemption. Used for loyalty-linked fuel discount reconciliation and program cost tracking.
Volume of fuel dispensed in gallons. Primary measure for volume sales reporting, inventory depletion, and tank level management.
reference: NIST Handbook 44 - Specifications, Tolerances, and Other Technical Requirements for Weighing and Measuring Devices

description: Total sale amount before any discounts or loyalty redemptions are applied. Calculated as gallons dispensed multiplied by unit price.
Local or municipal excise tax amount collected on this fuel transaction where applicable.
Customer loyalty card number used for this transaction. Links to customer loyalty profile for discount redemption and points accrual tracking.
System timestamp when this transaction record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Final amount charged to the customer after all discounts are applied. This is the revenue recognized for the transaction.
Payment instrument used for the transaction. Distinguishes between credit card, debit card, fleet card, Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT), cash, and mobile payment methods.
Reference number linking this fuel transaction to the corresponding POS system transaction record for payment reconciliation and receipt generation.
Indicates whether this was a prepay transaction where payment was authorized before fuel dispensing. Used for fraud prevention and payment processing workflow.
Promotional campaign code applied to this transaction. Used for tracking effectiveness of fuel pricing promotions and temporary price reductions.
Physical pump identifier at the fuel center where fuel was dispensed. Used for equipment tracking and maintenance scheduling.
Unique receipt number printed for the customer. Used for customer service inquiries and transaction lookup.
State excise tax amount collected on this fuel transaction. Required for state-level fuel tax reporting and remittance.
Total tax amount included in the transaction. Includes federal, state, and local fuel excise taxes for regulatory reporting and remittance.
Payment terminal identifier where the transaction was processed. Used for terminal reconciliation and equipment tracking.
Total dollar amount of discount applied to this transaction. Calculated as fuel discount cents per gallon multiplied by gallons dispensed.
Current status of the fuel transaction in its lifecycle. Tracks whether transaction was successfully completed, voided, refunded, or failed.
Date and time when the fuel dispensing transaction occurred at the pump. Primary business event timestamp for volume sales reporting and reconciliation.
Price per gallon at the time of transaction. Reflects current fuel grade pricing including any temporary price reductions or promotional pricing.
Vehicle identification number for fleet card transactions. Standard 17-character VIN used for fleet vehicle tracking and reporting.
reference: ISO 3779 - Road vehicles - Vehicle identification number (VIN)

description: Odometer reading captured at time of fleet card transaction. Used for fleet management reporting and mileage tracking.
Unique identifier for the fuel grade. Primary key for the fuel grade reference catalog.
Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: QUALITY & Sourcing: Each fuel grade is sourced from a specific supplier; linking enables quality certification and rebate calculations.

Measure of how heavy or light a petroleum liquid is compared to water. Higher API gravity indicates lighter, less dense fuel. Typical range: 30-45 for gasoline, 30-42 for diesel. Used for inventory volume-to-weight conversions and quality control.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 9.1 (Hydrometer Test Method for Density)

description: Percentage of biodiesel (fatty acid methyl esters) blended into diesel fuel. Common blends include B5 (5%), B10 (10%), B20 (20%). Null for gasoline and non-diesel products.

Lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions intensity measured in grams of CO2 equivalent per megajoule of energy. Used for Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) compliance in California and other jurisdictions. Lower scores indicate cleaner fuels.
reference: California LCFS Regulation (17 CCR § 95480-95503); EPA Greenhouse Gas Reporting

description: Measure of the combustion quality of diesel fuel during compression ignition. Higher cetane numbers indicate shorter ignition delay and better combustion. Typical range: 40-55. Null for gasoline and non-diesel products.
reference: ASTM D613 (Cetane Number of Diesel Fuel Oil)

description: Hexadecimal color code used for visual identification of the fuel grade on pump displays, signage, and digital interfaces. Standardized colors help customers quickly identify fuel types (e.g., green for diesel, yellow for mid-grade).
Timestamp when this fuel grade record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Integer value controlling the display order of fuel grades on pump interfaces, POS screens, and customer-facing applications. Lower values appear first. Typically ordered by octane rating or customer preference (e.g., 1=Regular, 2=Mid-Grade, 3=Premium).
Date when this fuel grade was discontinued or became unavailable for sale. Null for currently active grades. Used for historical analysis and regulatory reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Date when this fuel grade became available for sale across fuel centers. Used for historical tracking and seasonal blend transitions. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

business_glossary_term: Energy Content (BTU - British Thermal Unit per Gallon)
description: Energy content of the fuel measured in British Thermal Units per gallon. Gasoline typically 115,000-125,000 BTU/gal; diesel typically 130,000-138,000 BTU/gal; E85 approximately 81,000 BTU/gal. Used for energy equivalency calculations and fleet efficiency analysis.
reference: EPA Fuel Economy Guide; DOE Alternative Fuels Data Center

description: EPA regulatory classification of the fuel product for emissions compliance and reporting. Categories include conventional gasoline, reformulated gasoline (RFG), ULSD, renewable diesel, and alternative fuels. Used for environmental compliance tracking.
reference: EPA Fuel Quality Standards (40 CFR Part 80)

description: Percentage of ethanol blended into the gasoline product. Standard unleaded typically contains up to 10% (E10); E85 contains 51-83% ethanol. Null for diesel and non-ethanol products.

Lowest temperature at which vapors of the fuel will ignite when exposed to an ignition source. Critical safety parameter for storage and handling. Gasoline typically -45°F; diesel typically 125-180°F.
reference: ASTM D93 (Flash Point by Pensky-Martens Closed Cup Tester)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this fuel grade is available for purchase using RFID-enabled fleet cards and commercial fuel cards. True if accepted for fleet transactions; False if restricted to retail consumer purchases only.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the fuel grade within the system (e.g., REG87, MID89, PREM93, DSL, E85, DEF). Used for operational lookup and integration with POS and pricing systems.

Detailed textual description of the fuel grade including key characteristics, intended use, performance benefits, and any special handling or regulatory notes. Used for customer communication, training materials, and internal documentation.
Full business name of the fuel grade as displayed to customers and used in reporting (e.g., Regular Unleaded, Mid-Grade, Premium, Diesel, E85 Flex Fuel, Diesel Exhaust Fluid).

Current lifecycle status of the fuel grade. Active grades are available for sale; inactive grades are temporarily unavailable; discontinued grades are permanently retired; seasonal grades are available only during specific periods (e.g., winter blends).

High-level classification of the fuel product type. Gasoline includes unleaded grades; diesel includes standard and bio-diesel blends; alternative fuel includes E85, CNG, electric; additive includes DEF and other supplemental products.
14-digit Global Trade Item Number used for supply chain and EDI transactions. Provides globally unique identification for fuel products across trading partners and distribution channels.

Department of Transportation hazardous materials classification code for the fuel grade. Used for transportation, storage, and emergency response planning. Example: UN1203 for gasoline, UN1202 for diesel fuel.
reference: DOT Hazardous Materials Regulations (49 CFR Parts 171-180)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether purchases of this fuel grade qualify for loyalty program fuel discount redemptions. Typically True for standard gasoline and diesel grades; may be False for premium or specialty fuels depending on program rules.

The octane number representing the fuel's resistance to knocking or pinging during combustion. Typical values: 87 (Regular), 89 (Mid-Grade), 91-93 (Premium). Null for non-gasoline products such as diesel or DEF.
reference: ASTM D2699 and D2700 (Research and Motor Octane Number)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the fuel grade is reformulated gasoline designed to reduce smog-forming and toxic air pollutants. RFG is required in certain metropolitan areas with severe ozone pollution. True if RFG; False otherwise.
reference: EPA Reformulated Gasoline Program (40 CFR Part 80 Subpart D)

description: 38-character unique identifier assigned to batches of renewable fuel under the EPA Renewable Fuel Standard. Used to track and trade renewable fuel credits for compliance with federal mandates. Null for non-renewable fuels.
reference: EPA Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS) Program (40 CFR Part 80 Subpart M)

description: Sulfur content measured in parts per million. EPA Ultra-Low Sulfur Diesel (ULSD) standard requires maximum 15 ppm. Gasoline typically contains less than 10 ppm under Tier 3 standards. Critical for emissions compliance.
reference: EPA Tier 3 Gasoline Sulfur Standards; EPA ULSD Requirements (40 CFR 80)

description: Product code or SKU used by the primary fuel supplier or refiner to identify this grade in purchase orders, invoices, and EDI transactions. Facilitates supply chain integration and vendor reconciliation.

Code identifying the applicable federal and state excise tax category for the fuel grade. Different grades and blends may have different tax rates (e.g., gasoline vs diesel vs alternative fuels). Used for tax calculation and regulatory reporting.
reference: IRS Publication 510 (Excise Taxes); State Fuel Tax Regulations

description: 12-digit Universal Product Code used for point-of-sale scanning and inventory tracking. Aligns with GS1 standards for retail fuel products sold in packaged form or tracked via loyalty systems.
Timestamp when this fuel grade record was last modified. Updated whenever any attribute changes. Used for change tracking and data synchronization. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Boolean flag indicating whether the fuel grade is formulated as a winter blend with higher Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP) to improve cold-weather starting and performance. True if winter blend; False if summer blend or year-round formulation.
reference: EPA Volatility Standards (40 CFR Part 80 Subpart C)

description: Unique identifier for the fuel inventory reconciliation record. Primary key for this entity.
Identifier of the user who approved or closed the reconciliation record. Links to workforce associate master data for audit trail.
Identifier of the fiscal period to which this reconciliation belongs. Links to fiscal calendar for financial reporting and period-over-period analysis.
Identifier of the store location where the fuel center and tank are located.
Identifier of the fuel storage tank being reconciled. Links to the fuel tank master data.
Date and time when the reconciliation was approved or closed. Part of audit trail for compliance and internal controls.
Physical inventory volume in gallons measured by the Automatic Tank Gauge (ATG) system at the end of the reconciliation period. Represents actual measured inventory in the tank.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 (ATG requirements for leak detection)

description: Identifier of the ATG system or device that provided the physical volume reading. Used for equipment tracking and calibration audit trails.
Average fuel temperature in the tank during the reconciliation period measured in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for temperature compensation calculations and thermal expansion adjustments.
Calculated book inventory volume in gallons at the end of the reconciliation period. Formula: opening_book_volume + total_deliveries - total_dispensed. Represents theoretical ending inventory.

Financial impact of the inventory variance calculated by multiplying variance volume by the weighted average cost per gallon for the period. Used for shrink cost reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) impact analysis.
Date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost of variance amount. Typically USD for US operations.
Number of fuel delivery transactions received during the reconciliation period. Used to validate delivery volume totals and identify delivery frequency patterns.
Code representing the fuel grade or product type stored in the tank (e.g., Regular 87, Premium 93, Diesel). Standardized fuel product classification.
reference: ASTM D4814 (gasoline grades), ASTM D975 (diesel fuel grades)

description: Free-text notes documenting investigation findings, root cause analysis, corrective actions taken, and resolution details for variances requiring investigation.
Boolean indicator whether the variance requires formal investigation by operations or compliance teams. True triggers investigation workflow and root cause analysis.
Date and time when this reconciliation record was last updated. Part of audit trail for change tracking and compliance.

Boolean indicator whether this reconciliation meets EPA Underground Storage Tank (UST) leak detection compliance requirements. True indicates compliance, False indicates potential leak or non-compliance requiring immediate action.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 Subpart D (leak detection requirements)

description: Book inventory volume in gallons at the start of the reconciliation period. Represents the theoretical starting inventory based on prior period closing balance.
Method used to perform the inventory reconciliation. Manual indicates human-performed reconciliation, automated indicates system-generated reconciliation, hybrid indicates combination of both.
End date of the inventory reconciliation period. Defines the close of the measurement window for this reconciliation cycle.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 (UST leak detection requirements)

description: Start date of the inventory reconciliation period. Defines the beginning of the measurement window for this reconciliation cycle.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 (UST leak detection requirements)

description: Current lifecycle status of the inventory reconciliation record. Tracks approval workflow and investigation state.
Date and time when the physical inventory reconciliation was performed and recorded. Primary business event timestamp for this reconciliation.
Classification of the inventory variance into business-defined shrink categories based on variance magnitude and direction. Used for shrink management reporting and escalation workflows.

Boolean indicator whether temperature compensation was applied to volume measurements to adjust for thermal expansion/contraction of fuel. True indicates volumes are temperature-corrected to standard conditions.
Total volume of fuel delivered to the tank during the reconciliation period in gallons. Sum of all delivery receipts for this tank and fuel grade.
Total volume of fuel dispensed from the tank during the reconciliation period in gallons. Sum of all Point of Sale (POS) transactions for this tank and fuel grade.
Number of Point of Sale (POS) fuel dispensing transactions during the reconciliation period. Used to validate dispensed volume totals and assess transaction activity.

Inventory variance expressed as a percentage of closing book volume. Formula: (variance_volume / closing_book_volume) * 100. Used to assess materiality of variance and trigger investigation thresholds.
Boolean indicator whether the variance exceeds the acceptable threshold defined by business policy or EPA leak detection requirements. True indicates variance requires investigation.
Inventory variance in gallons between book and physical inventory. Formula: atg_physical_volume - closing_book_volume. Positive values indicate overage (gain), negative values indicate shrink (loss).
Water level detected at the bottom of the tank measured in inches. Water accumulation can indicate tank integrity issues or contamination. Part of EPA compliance monitoring.
Unique identifier for the fuel price record. Primary key for the fuel_price product.
Identifier of the manager who approved this price change. Required for manual overrides and discretionary pricing decisions. Supports audit trail and accountability for pricing decisions.
Identifier of the fuel center location where this price applies. Links to the fuel center master data.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price changes affect GL entries for cost of goods sold and margin calculations.
FK to fuel.fuel_grade
Foreign key linking to pricing.price_rule. Business justification: Fuel price records must reference the specific pricing rule used to generate the price for audit and optimization reporting.
FK to pricing.price_zone
Identifier of the promotional campaign associated with this price record. Links to promotion master data for campaign tracking and ROI analysis. Null for non-promotional pricing.

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: PRICING NEGOTIATION: Fuel price records must reference the supplying vendor for price negotiations, audit trails, and regulatory price filing.
Net change in street price per gallon (current price minus prior price). Positive values indicate price increases, negative values indicate decreases.

Business reason for the price change event. Competitive response to nearby stations, rack price index threshold breach, promotional campaign, manual manager override, scheduled adjustment, or cost recovery action.

System or process that initiated the price change. Automated competitive pricing feed, rack index threshold alert, manual manager decision, centralized pricing system recommendation, or promotional calendar event.
Reference price from competitive intelligence feed representing nearby competitor pricing. Used to justify competitive response price changes and maintain market positioning.
Wholesale cost per gallon paid to the supplier or based on rack pricing. Used for margin calculation and profitability analysis. Business-confidential pricing data.
Date and time when this price record was first created in the system. Distinct from effective timestamp; represents data capture time for audit and reconciliation purposes.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. USD for United States Dollar. Supports potential future international expansion.
Date and time when this fuel price becomes active and is displayed on signage and charged at the pump. Supports intraday price changes in response to competitive moves or rack price fluctuations.
Date and time when this fuel price is superseded by a new price record. Null for the current active price. Enables complete price history reconstruction and temporal queries.
Date and time when this price record was last updated. Tracks corrections, adjustments, or status changes after initial creation. Supports data quality audit and change tracking.

Indicates whether this fuel grade and price point is eligible for loyalty program fuel discount redemptions. True allows loyalty members to apply cents-per-gallon discounts earned through grocery purchases.
Gross profit margin per gallon calculated as street price minus cost per gallon. Key metric for fuel center profitability and pricing strategy effectiveness.

business_glossary_term: Maximum Loyalty Discount Cents Per Gallon
description: Maximum cents-per-gallon discount that can be applied through loyalty program redemption for this fuel grade. Caps the discount to protect margin floors and prevent negative pricing.
Free-text notes or comments explaining the business context for this price change. Used by pricing managers to document competitive intelligence, market conditions, or special circumstances.
Status of price synchronization to the fuel center POS system and pump controllers. Confirmed indicates successful deployment, failed indicates synchronization error requiring intervention.
Date and time when the price change was successfully synchronized to the POS system and pump controllers. Critical for reconciling price effective time with actual system deployment.
Previous street price per gallon before this price change event. Used for price change magnitude analysis and competitive response tracking.
Indicates whether this price is part of a temporary promotional campaign. True for limited-time offers, grand opening specials, or competitive market entry pricing.
Wholesale terminal rack price index at the time of this price record. Used as a benchmark for cost-based pricing strategies and margin protection rules.

Indicates whether this price change requires regulatory notification or public posting under state fuel pricing transparency laws. True triggers compliance workflow for jurisdictions with price-posting requirements.
Date and time when the price change was reported to regulatory authorities or posted to public transparency portals. Required for compliance audit trail in regulated jurisdictions.
Name of the source system or pricing platform that generated this price record. Examples include Oracle Retail Price Management, proprietary pricing engine, or manual entry system identifier.
Customer-facing retail price per gallon displayed on the fuel center sign and charged at the pump. This is the base price before any loyalty discounts or promotions.
Sequential version number for price changes within the same fuel center and fuel grade. Increments with each price change event to support complete audit trail and change frequency analysis.
Unique identifier for the fuel price change event. Primary key.
Identifier of the manager or authorized personnel who approved this price change. Required for audit trail and compliance with pricing authorization policies.
Identifier of the fuel center location where the price change occurred.
Identifier of the fuel grade (regular, mid-grade, premium, diesel) affected by this price change.
Identifier of the promotional campaign associated with this price change, if the change was triggered by a marketing promotion or loyalty fuel discount program.

The business reason or trigger that initiated this price change event. Competitive response indicates reaction to competitor pricing, rack price movement indicates wholesale cost changes, promotional indicates marketing campaign, manual override indicates manager discretion, scheduled adjustment indicates planned periodic changes, cost recovery indicates margin protection.
The name of the competitor whose pricing was referenced for this price change, if applicable.
The competitor fuel price that was used as a reference or benchmark for this price change decision, if applicable. Used for competitive positioning analysis.
The date and time when this price change event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record. Typically USD for US-based grocery retail fuel operations.
The exact date and time when the new fuel price became effective at the pump. This is the principal business event timestamp for the price change.
Business-facing unique identifier or sequence number for the price change event, used for audit and reference purposes.
The date and time when this price change event record was last modified or updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Boolean flag indicating whether this price change includes or is related to a loyalty program fuel discount redemption structure.
The calculated gross margin per gallon (new_price minus rack_price minus taxes and fees). Critical metric for profitability analysis and pricing strategy.
The new fuel price per gallon that became effective with this price change event.

Confirmation status indicating whether the price change has been successfully synchronized to the fuel center POS system and pump controllers. Critical for ensuring price accuracy at transaction time.
The date and time when the price change was successfully synchronized to the POS system. Used to measure synchronization lag and ensure pricing integrity.
The absolute difference between the new price and prior price (new_price minus prior_price). Positive values indicate price increase, negative values indicate price decrease.
Free-text notes or comments from the approving manager or pricing analyst explaining the rationale, context, or special circumstances for this price change.
The percentage change in price calculated as ((new_price - prior_price) / prior_price) * 100. Used for margin and competitive analysis.
The date and time when the fuel center price sign was updated to display the new price.
Boolean flag indicating whether the physical or digital price sign at the fuel center has been updated to reflect the new price. Required for customer transparency and regulatory compliance.
The fuel price per gallon that was in effect immediately before this price change event.
The wholesale terminal or rack price for this fuel grade at the time of the price change. Used for margin analysis and cost-based pricing decisions.
Boolean flag indicating whether this price change was reviewed for compliance with state and federal fuel pricing regulations, including price gouging laws and EPA requirements.
reference: EPA Clean Air Act, State Fuel Pricing Regulations

description: Unique identifier for the fuel dispenser pump. Primary key for the pump master record.
Reference to the fuel center location where this pump is installed.
Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: ASSET MANAGEMENT: Pump equipment warranty and maintenance contracts require linking each pump to its equipment supplier.
Indicates whether the pump meets ADA accessibility requirements for customers with disabilities, including reach range, controls placement, and signage.
reference: ADA Title III Public Accommodations Standards

description: Certified measurement accuracy of the fuel dispenser meter as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.9995 represents 99.95% accuracy). Must meet EPA and state weights and measures standards.
reference: EPA Fuel Dispenser Accuracy Standards; State Weights and Measures Regulations

description: Timestamp when this pump master record was first created in the system.
Date when the fuel dispenser was permanently removed from service and decommissioned. Null for active pumps.
Business reason for decommissioning the fuel dispenser, such as equipment failure, technology upgrade, fuel center closure, or regulatory non-compliance.
Type of emergency fuel shutoff mechanism installed on the pump. Manual requires physical button activation. Automatic triggers on impact or fire detection. Both provides redundant safety systems.
reference: OSHA Workplace Safety Standards; NFPA 30A Fuel Dispensing Standards

description: EPA-issued certification number for the fuel dispenser equipment, verifying compliance with federal emissions and vapor recovery standards.
reference: EPA Clean Air Act; EPA Vapor Recovery Standards

description: Indicates whether the pump supports RFID fleet card transactions for commercial fleet customers.
Number of different fuel grades (octane levels or fuel types) that this pump can dispense. Common values are 3 (regular, mid-grade, premium) or 4 (including diesel).
Date when the fuel dispenser was installed and commissioned at the fuel center location.
Date when the fuel dispenser meter was last calibrated and certified by a licensed technician. Required for EPA compliance and state weights and measures regulations.
reference: EPA Fuel Dispenser Accuracy Standards; State Weights and Measures Regulations

description: Date when the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance was performed on the fuel dispenser.
Indicates whether the pump supports loyalty program integration for fuel discount redemptions and rewards point accrual at the pump.
Name of the company that manufactured the fuel dispenser equipment.
Manufacturer model number or designation for the fuel dispenser unit.
Scheduled date for the next required calibration and certification of the fuel dispenser meter. Typically required annually or per state regulation.
reference: EPA Fuel Dispenser Accuracy Standards; State Weights and Measures Regulations

description: Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance service on the fuel dispenser.
Number of fuel nozzles (hoses) available on this dispenser unit. Typically ranges from 1 to 8 nozzles per pump.

Current operational state of the fuel dispenser. Active indicates the pump is available for customer use. Maintenance indicates temporary unavailability for servicing. Out of service indicates equipment failure. Decommissioned indicates permanent removal from service.

Type of payment technology supported by the pump terminal. RFID supports Radio Frequency Identification fleet cards. EMV supports chip-based credit/debit cards. NFC supports contactless mobile payments. Magnetic stripe supports legacy swipe cards. Hybrid supports multiple technologies.
Indicates whether the pump is integrated with the store POS system for real-time transaction processing and inventory management.
Indicates whether the pump is configured to require prepayment before fuel dispensing, typically enabled during high-risk hours to prevent drive-offs.
Business identifier for the pump, typically displayed on the dispenser unit for customer reference. Unique within a fuel center.
Indicates whether the pump can be remotely disabled from the fuel center control console or store POS system in emergency situations.
Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific dispenser unit. Used for warranty tracking and equipment lifecycle management.
State weights and measures certification number for the fuel dispenser meter accuracy, issued by the state regulatory authority.
Timestamp when this pump master record was last modified in the system.

Type of EPA-mandated vapor recovery system installed on the pump. Stage 1 captures vapors during fuel delivery to underground tanks. Stage 2 captures vapors during customer fueling. Required for EPA emissions compliance.
reference: EPA Clean Air Act; EPA Vapor Recovery Standards

description: Date when the manufacturer warranty coverage for the fuel dispenser equipment expires.
