Unique identifier for the pump maintenance record. Primary key for the pump maintenance transaction.
Identifier of the technician or associate who performed the maintenance work. May be internal staff or vendor technician.
Identifier of the fuel center location where the pump is installed.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links pump maintenance expenses to the cost center that owns the pump.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maintenance costs are recorded in a GL account for expense tracking.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Maintenance work orders are driven by specific compliance obligations (e.g., calibration, leak detection) tracked in compliance_obligation.
Identifier of the fuel dispenser pump that received maintenance. Links to the pump master resource.
Identifier of the external vendor or service provider who performed the maintenance, if applicable. Null for internal maintenance.
Timestamp when the maintenance work was completed and the pump was ready for return to service.
Timestamp when the maintenance work actually began. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
Certificate number issued by the calibration authority or weights and measures inspector, if calibration was performed.
Indicates whether pump calibration was performed during this maintenance activity. Critical for regulatory compliance tracking.
Timestamp when this maintenance record was first created in the system.
Total duration in minutes that the pump was out of service due to this maintenance activity. Critical for availability tracking and revenue impact analysis.
Classification of the failure or issue type (e.g., mechanical failure, electrical fault, calibration drift, leak detection). Used for root cause analysis and equipment reliability tracking.
Indicates whether the pump passed inspection during this maintenance activity. False indicates failure requiring follow-up.
Detailed description of the problem, defect, or reason for maintenance. For preventive maintenance, describes the scheduled service performed.
Total labor cost for the maintenance activity, in USD. Includes internal labor or vendor service fees.
Timestamp when this maintenance record was last updated in the system.
Current lifecycle status of the maintenance work order.

Classification of the maintenance activity: preventive (scheduled routine), corrective (repair), calibration (accuracy adjustment), inspection (regulatory check), emergency (unplanned critical), or compliance (regulatory requirement).
reference: EPA Underground Storage Tank Regulations, State Weights and Measures

description: Date when the next preventive maintenance or inspection is scheduled for this pump, based on maintenance intervals.
Additional notes, observations, or comments from the technician regarding the maintenance activity.
Total cost of parts and materials used in the maintenance activity, in USD.
Comma-separated list or description of parts and components replaced during the maintenance activity. Used for inventory tracking and warranty management.
Priority classification of the maintenance work order. Critical indicates immediate safety or compliance issue.
Detailed description of the corrective actions taken, repairs completed, and final resolution of the maintenance activity.
Timestamp when the pump was officially returned to operational service and made available for customer transactions.
Identified root cause of the issue requiring maintenance. Used for preventive maintenance planning and equipment lifecycle management.
Date when the maintenance activity was originally scheduled to occur.
Total cost of the maintenance activity including parts, labor, and any additional fees, in USD.
Indicates whether this maintenance activity is covered under manufacturer or vendor warranty.
Warranty claim reference number if this maintenance is being claimed under warranty.
Externally-known work order number assigned to this maintenance activity. Used for tracking and vendor billing.
Unique identifier for the underground storage tank (UST). Primary key.
Reference to the fuel center location where this tank is installed.
Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: COMPLIANCE: Tank ownership/manufacturer tracking is needed for EPA compliance, inspections, and service agreements.
Maximum storage capacity of the tank measured in gallons. Critical for inventory management and refill scheduling.
Date of the most recent cathodic protection system test. Required every three years for tanks with cathodic protection per EPA regulations.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 - Cathodic Protection Testing

description: Method of corrosion protection applied to the tank (e.g., cathodic protection, protective coating, inherently non-corrosive material). EPA requires corrosion protection for steel tanks.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 Subpart B - Corrosion Protection

description: Timestamp when this tank record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Current fuel volume in the tank measured in gallons. Updated by automatic tank gauging systems. Used for inventory management and reorder triggers.
Date when the tank was permanently removed from service and decommissioned per EPA closure requirements. Null for active tanks.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 Subpart G - Out-of-Service UST Systems and Closure

description: Method used to decommission the tank (e.g., removed from ground, filled in place with inert material). EPA requires proper closure procedures.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 Subpart G - Closure Requirements

business_glossary_term: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Underground Storage Tank (UST) Registration Number
description: Official EPA registration number for this underground storage tank, required for federal compliance tracking and reporting.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 - Underground Storage Tanks

description: Type of fuel grade stored in this tank (e.g., regular unleaded, premium, diesel). Determines pricing and customer selection at pump.

business_glossary_term: High Level Alarm Threshold in Gallons
description: Fuel volume level that triggers a high-level alarm during delivery to prevent overfill. Typically set at 90-95% of capacity.
Date when the tank was originally installed at the fuel center. Used to track tank age and plan replacement schedules per EPA guidelines.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 - UST Installation Requirements

description: Date when the tank was last cleaned and inspected internally. Periodic cleaning removes sediment and water accumulation that can cause fuel quality issues.
Date of the most recent fuel delivery to this tank. Used for inventory reconciliation and delivery scheduling.
Volume of fuel delivered during the most recent delivery. Used for inventory reconciliation and variance analysis.
Date of the most recent regulatory or internal inspection of the tank and associated equipment.
Timestamp when this tank record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Date of the most recent precision tightness test performed on the tank to verify structural integrity and absence of leaks. EPA requires periodic testing.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 - Tightness Testing Requirements

description: EPA-approved method used to detect leaks from this tank (e.g., automatic tank gauging, vapor monitoring, interstitial monitoring). Required for EPA compliance.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 Subpart D - Release Detection

description: Type of protective lining installed inside the tank to prevent corrosion and leakage (e.g., epoxy, polyurethane). Critical for EPA compliance and leak prevention.

business_glossary_term: Low Level Alarm Threshold in Gallons
description: Fuel volume level that triggers a low-level alarm. Set above reorder threshold to provide early warning of potential stockout.
Name of the company that manufactured the tank. Used for warranty tracking and technical support.
Primary construction material of the tank shell (e.g., steel, fiberglass, composite). Impacts corrosion risk and maintenance requirements.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 - UST Technical Standards

description: Manufacturer model number or designation for this tank type. Used for parts ordering and technical specifications.
Scheduled date for the next tank cleaning. Typically performed every 3-5 years depending on fuel quality and contamination risk.
Scheduled date for the next required inspection. Critical for maintaining EPA compliance and avoiding penalties.
Scheduled date for the next required tightness test. Used for compliance tracking and maintenance scheduling.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 280 - Tightness Testing Requirements

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, maintenance history, or special handling instructions for this tank.

Current operational state of the tank. Active tanks are in use; inactive tanks are installed but not currently dispensing; out-of-service tanks require repair; decommissioned tanks have been permanently removed from service.
Type of overfill prevention equipment installed on the tank (e.g., automatic shutoff, high-level alarm). EPA requires overfill prevention for all USTs.
Minimum fuel volume that triggers a reorder or delivery request. Set based on daily sales velocity and delivery lead time to prevent stockouts.
Indicates whether the tank has secondary containment (double-walled construction or external containment system). Reduces environmental risk and may lower insurance premiums.
Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer. Used for warranty claims and equipment tracking.
Type of spill prevention equipment installed at the fill pipe (e.g., catchment basin, spill bucket). EPA requires spill prevention for all USTs.
Business identifier for the tank, typically a sequential number assigned at the fuel center (e.g., Tank 1, Tank 2). Used for operational reference and signage.
Indicates whether the tank is equipped with temperature monitoring sensors. Temperature data is used for volume compensation and leak detection.
Date when the manufacturer warranty expires. Used for maintenance planning and cost forecasting.
Indicates whether the tank is equipped with water detection sensors at the bottom. Water accumulation can cause fuel quality issues and microbial growth.
Unique identifier for the tank level reading record. Primary key for this event log entity.
Identifier of the inventory reconciliation period to which this reading belongs. Links to daily or shift-level reconciliation cycles.
Identifier of the store location where the fuel center and tank are located.
Identifier of the fuel storage tank from which this reading was captured. Links to the fuel tank master resource.

Alarm or alert status reported by the ATG system at the time of reading. Indicates operational issues requiring attention. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: normal|low_level|high_level|water_detected|leak_suspected|sensor_fault|overfill_warning — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: EPA UST Regulations 40 CFR Part 280 - Release Detection

description: Identifier of the ATG device or sensor that captured this reading. Used for equipment maintenance tracking and data quality auditing.
Timestamp when this reading record was first inserted into the data system. Distinct from reading_timestamp which is the business event time.
Quality assessment flag indicating the reliability of this reading. Suspect or invalid readings are excluded from compliance reporting and inventory calculations.
Indicates whether a fuel delivery was in progress at the time of this reading. True if delivery active; False otherwise. Used to exclude readings during delivery from leak detection calculations.

Grade or type of fuel stored in the tank at the time of reading. Octane rating for gasoline or fuel type designation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regular_87|midgrade_89|premium_91|premium_93|diesel|e85|e15 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ASTM D4814 - Standard Specification for Automotive Spark-Ignition Engine Fuel

description: Height of fuel in the tank measured in inches from the tank bottom. Raw measurement used in conjunction with tank strapping tables to calculate volume.
Temperature of the fuel in the tank measured in degrees Fahrenheit. Used for temperature correction calculations to derive net volume.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11 - Temperature and Pressure Volume Correction

description: Total volume of fuel in the tank measured in gallons, not adjusted for temperature. Raw measurement from the ATG probe.
Result of the automatic leak detection test performed by the ATG system. EPA requires continuous leak detection monitoring for underground storage tanks.
reference: EPA UST Regulations 40 CFR Part 280 Subpart D - Release Detection

description: Temperature-corrected volume of fuel in the tank measured in gallons. Adjusted to standard temperature (60°F) for accurate inventory accounting and reconciliation.
reference: API MPMS Chapter 11 - Temperature and Pressure Volume Correction

description: Free-text notes or comments recorded by operators or technicians regarding this reading. May include context for alarms, manual interventions, or unusual conditions.
Sequential number of this reading within the day or shift for the specific tank. Used to order readings chronologically and detect missing observations.
Source or method by which the tank level reading was obtained. ATG automated readings are continuous; manual dipstick readings are performed during inspections or system failures.
Date and time when the tank level reading was captured by the ATG system or manual measurement. Primary business event timestamp for this observation.
Available capacity remaining in the tank measured in gallons. Calculated as tank capacity minus current gross volume. Used to determine reorder timing and delivery scheduling.

Calculated variance between expected inventory level and actual measured level in gallons. Used for inventory reconciliation and shrink analysis. Positive variance indicates gain; negative indicates loss.
Height of water detected at the bottom of the tank measured in inches. Water accumulation indicates contamination risk and must be monitored for EPA compliance and product quality.
reference: EPA UST Regulations 40 CFR Part 280 - Release Detection

description: Primary key for carrier

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Carriers must meet compliance program requirements (e.g., hazardous‑material handling); linking records which program a carrier is certified under for compliance monitoring.
Indicates whether the carrier provides API connectivity for real-time rate quotes, shipment booking, and tracking.
Mean number of days from shipment pickup to delivery across all service levels for this carrier.
Legal business name of the carrier or delivery service provider.
Current operational status of the carrier relationship in the fulfillment network.

Classification of carrier based on service model: parcel (small package), LTL (Less Than Truckload), FTL (Full Truckload), last-mile (final delivery leg), gig-economy (on-demand platform), or courier (express delivery).
Indicates whether the carrier is certified to handle temperature-controlled shipments for perishable goods and pharmacy products, maintaining cold chain integrity per HACCP standards.
Date when the carrier service contract becomes active and rates/terms take effect.
Date when the carrier service contract expires and requires renewal or renegotiation.
Reference identifier for the master service agreement or transportation contract governing the carrier relationship.
Date and time when the carrier record was first created in the system.
Historical performance metric representing the percentage of shipments resulting in damage claims or loss incidents.
Unique identifier assigned by the U.S. Department of Transportation to carriers operating commercial motor vehicles, required for regulatory compliance and safety monitoring.
Indicates whether the carrier supports EDI integration for automated shipment notifications, tracking updates, and invoicing.
Indicates whether the carrier applies variable fuel surcharges to shipment costs based on market fuel prices.
Comma-separated list of geographic zones or regions where this carrier operates (e.g., Northeast, West Coast, National, International).
Indicates whether the carrier is certified to transport hazardous materials in compliance with DOT and EPA regulations.
First line of the carrier's corporate headquarters street address.
Second line of the carrier's corporate headquarters street address (suite, floor, building).
City where the carrier's corporate headquarters is located.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the carrier's headquarters location.
Postal or ZIP code of the carrier's corporate headquarters location.
State or province where the carrier's corporate headquarters is located.
Maximum cargo insurance coverage amount (in USD) provided by the carrier per shipment.
Date and time when the carrier record was most recently updated.
Date of the most recent carrier performance evaluation and scorecard review.
Operating authority number issued by the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration (FMCSA) for interstate commerce carriers.
Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or carrier-specific requirements.
Historical performance metric representing the percentage of shipments delivered within the committed service level agreement (SLA) window.
Negotiated payment terms for carrier invoices (e.g., Net 30, Net 45, prepaid, collect).
Internal classification indicating carrier preference level for routing decisions: tier_1 (primary preferred), tier_2 (secondary), tier_3 (tertiary), backup (emergency use only).
Email address of the primary carrier contact for operational communications and issue resolution.
Full name of the primary account representative or contact person at the carrier organization.
Phone number of the primary carrier contact for urgent operational matters.
Four-letter unique identifier assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA) for transportation companies.
Comma-separated list of delivery service levels this carrier provides (e.g., same-day, next-day, two-day, standard, economy).
URL template for customer shipment tracking, with placeholder for tracking number substitution (e.g., https://carrier.com/track?id={TRACKING_NUMBER}).
Unique identifier for the delivery route. Primary key.
Identifier of the driver assigned to execute this delivery route.
Identifier of the carrier responsible for executing this route, applicable for third-party Last Mile Delivery (LMD) providers.
Identifier of the originating facility (Distribution Center, Micro-Fulfillment Center, or store) from which this route departs.
Identifier of the vehicle assigned to this delivery route.
Identifier of the warehouse wave planning batch that generated the orders for this route.
reference: Manhattan Associates Warehouse Management System (WMS) - Wave Planning

description: Actual distance traveled for the route measured in miles, captured from vehicle telematics or driver reporting.
Actual total duration taken to complete the route in minutes, measured from start to end time.
Actual time the driver completed the route and returned to the facility.
Actual time the driver departed the facility to begin the route.
Number of stops successfully completed on this route.
Timestamp when this delivery route record was first created in the system.
Time when the route was dispatched and assigned to the driver for execution.
Estimated total duration for completing the route in minutes, calculated during route optimization.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Duration

description: Number of stops that failed delivery on this route.
Fuel cost incurred for this route based on distance traveled and fuel consumption rate.
Timestamp when this delivery route record was last modified.
Free-text notes or special instructions for the driver or dispatcher regarding this route.
Percentage of stops on this route that met their scheduled delivery window, calculated as (on-time stops / total stops) * 100.
System or method used to optimize and plan this route (JDA Transportation Management System, manual planning, or other optimization engine).
Scheduled end time for the route completion and return to facility.
Scheduled start time for the route departure from the originating facility.
Total cost incurred for executing this route, including labor, fuel, vehicle, and carrier charges.
The scheduled date for this delivery route execution.
Business identifier for the delivery route, externally known and used for operational tracking and communication.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Identifier

description: Current lifecycle status of the delivery route indicating its execution state.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Status

description: Classification of the route based on delivery purpose and destination type.
Delivery service level commitment for this route, determining Service Level Agreement (SLA) targets.
Indicates whether the route met all Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments for on-time delivery across all stops.
Temperature control requirement for this route to maintain cold chain integrity for perishable goods.
Total planned distance for the route measured in miles.
Total number of customer orders included in this route across all stops.
Total number of delivery stops planned on this route.
Total volume of all shipments on this route measured in cubic feet.
Total weight of all shipments on this route measured in pounds.
Unique identifier for the physical slot or bin location within a warehouse, distribution center, micro-fulfillment center, or store backroom. Primary key.
Identifier of the facility (DC, MFC, or store) where this slot location resides. Links to the facility master data.

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Slot locations map to physical storage locations for put‑away and picking, required by the 'Warehouse Slotting' operational report.

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether the slot is currently active and available for warehouse operations. Inactive slots are excluded from putaway and picking logic, typically due to decommissioning, reconfiguration, or permanent removal.
Aisle identifier within the warehouse zone. Used for navigation and pick path optimization.
Barcode identifier printed on the slot label for scanning during warehouse operations. Used by handheld devices for location verification during putaway, picking, and cycle counting.
Bay or section number within the aisle. Represents a vertical or horizontal segment of shelving or racking.
Individual bin or slot identifier within the level. Represents the smallest addressable storage unit.
Timestamp when the slot location record was first created in the WMS. Used for audit trail and tracking warehouse layout changes over time.
Date when the slot location was deactivated and removed from active warehouse operations. Nullable for active slots. Used for historical analysis of warehouse layout evolution and capacity planning.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Location Lifecycle Management

description: Reason code explaining why the slot was deactivated. Used for root cause analysis of capacity changes and warehouse optimization initiatives.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Location Lifecycle Management

description: Front-to-back depth dimension of the slot in inches. Used for slotting optimization to ensure product dimensions fit within slot constraints.

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether the slot is certified and equipped for storing hazardous materials. Slots must meet EPA and OSHA requirements for ventilation, containment, and segregation before hazmat products can be assigned.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 262 - Hazardous Waste Storage; OSHA 1910.120 - Hazardous Waste Operations

description: Vertical height dimension of the slot in inches. Used for slotting optimization to ensure product dimensions fit within slot constraints.
Date when the slot location was last physically counted during cycle count operations. Used to schedule recurring cycle counts and maintain inventory accuracy compliance.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Cycle Count Management

description: Timestamp when the slot location record was last updated. Captures changes to slot attributes, status, or configuration. Critical for change tracking and SOX compliance in financial reporting environments.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls Over Financial Reporting

description: Timestamp when inventory was last picked from this slot location. Used for slot velocity analysis and slotting optimization to position fast-moving SKUs in ergonomic, accessible locations.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Pick Transaction Log

description: Timestamp when inventory was last placed into this slot location. Used for slot utilization analytics and identifying slow-moving or stagnant inventory locations.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Putaway Transaction Log

description: Vertical level or shelf position within the bay (e.g., ground, 1, 2, 3, mezzanine). Impacts picking ergonomics and slotting strategy.

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether the slot is authorized for storing DEA-controlled substances (Schedule II-V medications). Requires enhanced security controls including locked cages, access logging, and surveillance per DEA regulations.
reference: DEA 21 CFR Part 1301 - Controlled Substance Security Requirements

description: Numeric sequence number used to optimize pick path routing. Lower numbers are visited first during wave-based picking operations to minimize travel time and improve productivity.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Wave Planning and Pick Path Optimization

description: Reference identifier linking this physical slot to a planogram definition. Used in store backrooms and retail display areas to ensure compliance with visual merchandising plans and category management strategies.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Planogram Management

description: Inventory quantity threshold that triggers automatic replenishment from reserve or bulk storage to the pick face. Used to maintain optimal pick face inventory levels and prevent stockouts during high-volume picking.
Unique identifier of the RFID tag affixed to the slot location for automated inventory tracking and real-time location verification. Enables hands-free scanning during putaway and picking operations.
reference: GS1 EPC (Electronic Product Code) Standard for RFID

description: Human-readable unique code for the slot location, typically formatted as zone-aisle-bay-level-bin (e.g., A-12-05-03-B). Used by warehouse associates for picking and putaway operations.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Location Identifier Standard

description: Current operational status of the slot location. Empty indicates available for putaway, occupied indicates inventory present, blocked indicates temporarily unavailable, reserved indicates allocated for specific operation, maintenance indicates under repair or inspection.

Functional classification of the slot indicating its intended use in warehouse operations. Bulk slots hold large quantities for replenishment, pick face slots are optimized for order picking, reserve slots hold overflow inventory, and endcap slots are promotional display locations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bulk|pick_face|reserve|endcap|pallet|case|each — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the slot based on pick frequency and throughput velocity. Fast-moving slots are positioned in golden zones (waist-to-shoulder height, near shipping) while slow-moving slots are placed in less accessible areas. Recalculated periodically based on historical pick activity.
reference: Manhattan Associates WMS Slotting Optimization - ABC Velocity Analysis

description: Maximum allowable temperature for the slot location in Fahrenheit. Critical for cold chain compliance and perishable inventory management. Monitored continuously in refrigerated and frozen zones.
reference: FDA Food Code - Temperature Control for Safety (TCS) Foods

description: Minimum allowable temperature for the slot location in Fahrenheit. Critical for cold chain compliance and perishable inventory management. Monitored continuously in refrigerated and frozen zones.
reference: FDA Food Code - Temperature Control for Safety (TCS) Foods

description: Total volumetric capacity of the slot in cubic feet, calculated as height × width × depth. Used for space utilization analytics and slotting optimization.
Maximum weight capacity of the slot in pounds. Critical for safety compliance and preventing structural damage to racking systems. Enforced during putaway operations.
reference: OSHA 1910.176 - Material Handling and Storage Safety Standards

description: Horizontal width dimension of the slot in inches. Used for slotting optimization to ensure product dimensions fit within slot constraints.

Warehouse zone identifier indicating the environmental or functional area (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen, pharmacy, hazmat, returns, cross-dock). Critical for temperature-controlled cold chain compliance and product segregation.
Classification of the environmental or functional zone type. Determines storage requirements and handling protocols for products assigned to this slot.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA) - Temperature Control Requirements

Unique identifier for the fulfillment order. This is the primary key for the fulfillment domain representation of an order released for physical execution.

Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to deliver this fulfillment order for home delivery or ship-to-store scenarios. Maps to the carrier master data in the transportation management system.
Identifier of the specific facility (distribution center, micro-fulfillment center, or store) responsible for executing this fulfillment order. Maps to the facility master data.
Reference to the commercial order in the order domain that originated this fulfillment order. Links the fulfillment execution back to the customer-facing order.

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.prescription. Business justification: Required for Prescription Order Fulfillment process: each fulfillment order must reference the originating prescription for compliance and patient tracking.
Identifier of the warehouse associate who performed the picking operations for this fulfillment order. Used for productivity tracking and quality accountability.
Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Order‑level promotion attribution is required for margin analysis and rebate calculations.

Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Required for Store Pickup Fulfillment Order Assignment report, linking each fulfillment order to the store that fulfills the in‑store pickup, enabling inventory allocation and SLA tracking.
Identifier of the pick wave to which this fulfillment order has been assigned. Wave planning groups orders for efficient batch picking and resource optimization in the warehouse.

Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains alcoholic beverages requiring age verification at delivery and compliance with state alcohol shipping regulations. True if alcohol is present, false otherwise.

The reason why this fulfillment order was cancelled. Customer request for customer-initiated cancellation, inventory unavailable for stockout after release, address issue for undeliverable address, payment failure for payment authorization problems, fraud detected for suspected fraudulent orders, system error for technical failures.
The date and time when this fulfillment order was cancelled. Applicable when the order is cancelled after release to fulfillment but before completion.

The transportation service level selected for this fulfillment order delivery. Same-day for ultra-fast delivery, next-day for overnight service, two-day for expedited service, standard for 3-5 day ground, economy for 5-7 day low-cost delivery.

Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains temperature-sensitive perishable items requiring cold chain integrity throughout pick, pack, and delivery. True if cold chain handling is required, false otherwise.
The date and time when this fulfillment order record was first created in the fulfillment system. Represents the initial receipt of the order from the order management system.
The date and time when this fulfillment order was successfully delivered to the customer or confirmed as picked up by the customer. Marks the completion of the end-to-end fulfillment process.

The date and time when this fulfillment order was dispatched from the fulfillment node and handed over to the carrier or made available for customer pickup. Marks the completion of warehouse operations.

Code identifying the type of fulfillment exception or issue encountered during order execution. Examples include damaged inventory, address validation failure, delivery attempt failure, or customer unavailable. Used for exception management and root cause analysis.
Free-text notes describing the fulfillment exception or issue in detail. Provides context for exception resolution and customer service follow-up.

The physical fulfillment channel through which this order is being executed. DC (Distribution Center) for traditional warehouse fulfillment, MFC (Micro-Fulfillment Center) for automated urban fulfillment, store backroom for in-store pickup or local delivery, DSD (Direct Store Delivery) for vendor-managed delivery, cross-dock for transfer without storage, or vendor direct for drop-ship scenarios.
Human-readable business identifier for the fulfillment order, used for tracking and operational reference across warehouse management systems and transportation systems.

Current lifecycle status of the fulfillment order in the warehouse management system workflow. Received indicates order accepted by WMS, waving indicates wave planning in progress, picking indicates active pick task execution, packing indicates items being packed, staged indicates ready for dispatch, dispatched indicates handed to carrier, in-transit indicates en route to customer, delivered indicates successful delivery, completed indicates fulfillment closed, cancelled indicates order cancelled, exception indicates fulfillment issue requiring intervention. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|waving|picking|packing|staged|dispatched|in_transit|delivered|completed|cancelled|exception — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The method by which the order will be delivered to the customer. Home delivery for last-mile delivery to customer address, curbside pickup for click-and-collect at store parking, in-store pickup for customer collection inside store, ship-to-store for transfer to another location, or locker pickup for automated pickup point.

Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, packaging, labeling, and transportation compliance. True if hazmat items are present, false otherwise.
The date and time when the order management system released this order to the fulfillment domain for physical execution. Marks the handoff from order orchestration to warehouse operations.
The date and time when packing operations were completed for this fulfillment order. Marks the order as ready for staging and dispatch.
The number of physical packages or parcels into which this fulfillment order was packed for shipment. Used for shipping cost calculation and carrier manifest generation.
The date and time when all picking tasks for this fulfillment order were completed. Used for pick cycle time calculation and throughput analysis.
The date and time when picking operations began for this fulfillment order in the warehouse. Used for cycle time analysis and picker productivity measurement.

Indicates whether this fulfillment order contains prescription medications requiring pharmacy fulfillment, HIPAA-compliant handling, and controlled substance tracking. True if prescriptions are included, false otherwise.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule, DEA Controlled Substances Act

description: The fulfillment priority level assigned to this order based on SLA requirements, customer tier, order value, or business rules. Urgent for same-day or critical orders, high for premium customers or tight SLAs, standard for regular fulfillment, low for backorders or flexible delivery windows.

The number of line items in this fulfillment order where the picked quantity was less than the ordered quantity due to inventory discrepancies or stockouts. Used for fill rate calculation and inventory accuracy tracking.

The target date and time by which this fulfillment order must be completed to meet the customer promise and service level agreement. Used for SLA compliance tracking and priority sequencing in wave planning.
Customer-provided or system-generated special handling instructions for this fulfillment order. Examples include delivery instructions, gift wrapping requests, or handling precautions.

The number of line items in this fulfillment order where a substitute product was picked due to out-of-stock conditions on the originally ordered item. Used for substitution rate analytics and customer experience tracking.

The temperature control requirement for this fulfillment order. Ambient for shelf-stable products, chilled for refrigerated items (0-4°C), frozen for frozen products (-18°C or below), multi-temp for orders containing items across multiple temperature zones requiring specialized handling.
The total number of distinct line items (SKUs) in this fulfillment order. Used for complexity assessment, wave planning, and picker productivity analysis.
The total number of individual units (eaches) across all line items in this fulfillment order. Represents the total pick quantity for the order.
The total cubic volume of all items in this fulfillment order measured in cubic meters. Used for capacity planning, vehicle loading optimization, and dimensional weight calculations.
The total weight of all items in this fulfillment order measured in kilograms. Used for carrier selection, freight cost calculation, and load planning.
The carrier-provided tracking number for this fulfillment order shipment. Used for customer shipment visibility and delivery status tracking.
The date and time when this fulfillment order record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.
Unique identifier for the fulfillment order line. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_item. Business justification: ASSORTMENT VERIFICATION: Order‑line fulfillment is validated against the specific assortment item (SKU, store cluster, planogram) to ensure correct placement and inventory allocation.

Name of the regulatory or governing body involved in the event (e.g., FDA, USDA, DEA, State Board of Pharmacy, OSHA, EPA, Fire Marshal, Health Department). Applicable for inspection and compliance events.
Detailed notes describing how the event was resolved, actions taken, and final outcome. Captures the response and remediation activities performed.

Number of violations or deficiencies identified during a regulatory inspection event. Zero indicates no violations found.

Unique identifier for the physical retail location. Primary key for the store_location entity. This is the single source of truth (SSOT) for store identity across the enterprise and the anchor entity for all store-domain relationships.
Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Cash handling and bank reconciliation require each store to reference its dedicated bank account.

Foreign key linking to store.banner. Business justification: Store locations have a textual banner_name; adding store_location.banner_id FK creates a proper reference to the banner master table, enables consistent reporting, and removes the redundant banner_nam
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Store‑level expense reporting requires linking each store to its primary cost center for P&L and budget variance analysis.

Foreign key linking to supply.dc_facility. Business justification: REQUIRED: Store replenishment planning needs the primary DC assignment to calculate inbound logistics; experts expect a dc_facility_id on each store for routing and capacity planning.
Foreign key linking to store.district. Business justification: Each store belongs to a district; adding district_id creates the hierarchical link without redundancy.
Foreign key linking to store.store_format. Business justification: Store format is a master reference; replace string format_type with FK to store_format for consistency and normalization.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_gateway. Business justification: Required for POS settlement reporting: each store is assigned a payment gateway that processes its transactions, needed for daily settlement batch generation and compliance audits.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Daily sales posting uses a default GL account per store; linking enables automated journal entry generation.
Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Tax and regulatory filings require each store to be linked to its legal entity for jurisdictional compliance.

Foreign key linking to pricing.price_zone. Business justification: Pricing zone assignment per store drives zone‑specific price strategies; required for Price Zone Performance Report and zone‑based price optimization.

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability dashboards aggregate sales by profit center; each store must be assigned to a profit center for store‑wide profit reporting.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Required for store‑level loyalty program settings used in earn‑rate calculations, store‑specific promotions, and compliance reporting; a store manager configures the program per store.
Foreign key linking to store.region. Business justification: Allows direct regional performance reporting and inventory allocation per store without traversing district hierarchy.

Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: REQUIRED: Assortment planning assigns each store to a store cluster for category mix, planogram templates, and performance reporting; linking store_location to store_cluster enables cluster‑based asso

Indicates whether this store is authorized to accept WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits. Impacts product assortment, POS configuration, and regulatory compliance.

Primary email address for store-level communication. Used for customer inquiries, vendor coordination, and internal communications. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.

Total building square footage including sales floor, backroom, offices, restrooms, and mechanical spaces. Used for facility management, lease agreements, and property tax calculations.

Geographic longitude coordinate of the store location. Used for mapping, proximity analysis, delivery routing, and trade area modeling.

Unique identifier for the allocation detail line item. Primary key for store-level allocation records.
Reference to the parent allocation plan that this detail line belongs to. Links to the header-level allocation strategy.
User who approved this allocation detail for release. Captures accountability for allocation decisions.
Distribution center or warehouse from which this allocation will be fulfilled. Identifies the origin facility for the shipment.
Reference to the inbound shipment that fulfilled this allocation detail. Links allocation planning to physical shipment execution.
Product being allocated to the store. References the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) in the allocation.
Destination store receiving the allocated inventory. Identifies the specific retail location for this allocation line.
Actual date the allocation was received at the destination store. Used to measure on-time delivery performance.
Actual date the allocation was shipped from the distribution center. Used to measure on-time shipment performance.
Calculated quantity allocated to this store based on allocation rules and store tier. Represents the system-recommended allocation amount.
Algorithm used to calculate the allocated quantity for this store. Defines the business rule applied during allocation planning.
Current lifecycle status of this allocation detail line. Tracks progression from planning through fulfillment.
Date and time when this allocation detail was approved for fulfillment. Marks the transition from planning to execution.
Date and time when this allocation detail record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
Percentage of the final allocated quantity that was successfully received at the store. Calculated as (received_quantity / final_allocated_quantity) * 100. Key metric for allocation effectiveness.
Final quantity to be shipped to the store. Equals override_quantity if present, otherwise equals allocated_quantity. Used for fulfillment execution.
Status of physical fulfillment for this allocation line. Indicates whether the allocated quantity has been picked, shipped, and received at the store.
Date and time when this allocation detail record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.
Sequential line number within the allocation plan. Orders allocation detail records within the parent plan.
Maximum quantity that can be allocated to this store based on shelf capacity, backroom space, or inventory policy. Ceiling constraint for allocation.
Minimum quantity that must be allocated to this store to meet presentation standards or planogram requirements. Floor constraint for allocation.
Manual override quantity entered by planner or merchandiser. Replaces the system-calculated allocated quantity when business judgment requires adjustment.
Reason code explaining why the allocation was manually overridden. Captures business justification for deviating from system recommendation.
Free-text explanation for the allocation override. Provides additional context beyond the reason code.
Number of units per case or pack. Used to convert case quantities to eaches for inventory tracking.
Expected date for the allocation to arrive at the destination store. Used for store labor planning and receiving dock scheduling.
Target date for shipping this allocation from the distribution center. Used for transportation planning and store receiving scheduling.
Fulfillment priority for this allocation detail. CRITICAL=immediate need (OOS recovery), HIGH=promotional support, NORMAL=standard replenishment, LOW=backfill.
Actual quantity received and confirmed at the destination store. May differ from shipped quantity due to damage or discrepancies.
Actual quantity shipped from the distribution center to the store. May differ from final allocated quantity due to inventory availability.
Performance tier classification of the destination store. Used to prioritize allocation quantities based on store sales volume and strategic importance (A=highest, F=lowest).
Unit of measure for allocated quantities. EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, LB=Pound, KG=Kilogram.
Difference between final allocated quantity and received quantity. Positive values indicate over-shipment, negative values indicate under-shipment.

Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center for routing and logistics optimization.
Date when the distribution center facility first became operational and began receiving and shipping inventory.

Indicates whether the facility is certified for handling and storing USDA organic products with required segregation and handling protocols.

Total cubic feet of refrigerated storage capacity for temperature-controlled perishable inventory (typically 32-40°F).
Indicates whether this facility is equipped with RFID infrastructure for automated inventory tracking and shipment verification.

State or province code for the distribution center location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Number of retail store locations actively served by this distribution center for replenishment.

Total building square footage of the distribution center facility including all storage, operational, and administrative areas.
Indicates whether the distribution center operates 24 hours per day, 7 days per week for continuous receiving and shipping.
Identifier for the WMS system instance deployed at this distribution center. References Manhattan Associates WMS or other warehouse management platform.

Actual arrival date at the destination location when the shipment was received. Null until delivery occurs.
Actual departure date from the origin location when the shipment left the facility. Null until shipment departs.

description: Indicator whether the transfer shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and carrier certification per DOT regulations.

Indicator whether product damage was reported during receiving inspection. Triggers claims process and carrier performance review.

Total transportation cost for the transfer shipment in US dollars. Includes carrier charges, fuel surcharges, and accessorial fees. Used for supply chain cost allocation.
System timestamp when the transfer record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Free-form text field for additional transfer instructions, special handling requirements, or operational comments. Used for communication between origin, carrier, and destination.

Classification of the origin facility. DC is distribution center, store is retail location, MFC is micro-fulfillment center, cross-dock is flow-through facility.

Total volume of the transfer shipment in cubic feet. Used for trailer utilization optimization and space planning.
Total number of pallets included in the transfer shipment. Used for capacity planning and receiving resource allocation.

Total weight of the transfer shipment in pounds. Used for freight costing, carrier capacity planning, and compliance with weight restrictions.

Externally-known business identifier for the transfer transaction, used for tracking and reference across systems and with carriers.

description: Business justification for the transfer. Planned indicates scheduled but not started, picking indicates warehouse fulfillment in progress, loaded indicates staged on carrier, in-transit indicates shipment en route, delivered indicates arrival at destination, confirmed indicates receiving complete, cancelled indicates transaction voided. description: Primary key for demand_forecast
Reference to the demand planner responsible for reviewing and approving this forecast. Links to the workforce or employee master data.
Reference to the product or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being forecasted. Links to the item master data.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standards

description: Reference to the promotional event or campaign driving the promotional lift in this forecast period. Links to the promotions master data.
Reference to the store or DC (Distribution Center) for which the forecast is generated. Supports both store-level and DC-level forecasting.
The baseline demand forecast in units, excluding promotional lift. Represents normal demand patterns based on historical trends and seasonality.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Baseline Forecast

description: A score between 0 and 1 indicating the quality of input data used for forecast generation. Factors include completeness of historical sales, POS (Point of Sale) data accuracy, and inventory data reliability.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Data Quality Assessment

description: The coefficient of variation for historical demand, measuring demand volatility. Higher values indicate more variable demand patterns requiring higher safety stock.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Demand Variability Analysis

description: The date and time when the forecast was approved by the demand planner and released for use in replenishment planning.
The historical forecast bias as a percentage, indicating systematic over-forecasting (positive) or under-forecasting (negative). Used for forecast accuracy monitoring.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Forecast Accuracy Metrics

description: A confidence score between 0 and 1 indicating the reliability of the forecast. Higher scores indicate greater confidence based on historical accuracy and data quality.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Forecast Accuracy Metrics

description: The date and time when the forecast was initially generated by the Blue Yonder Demand Planning system.
The number of weeks into the future this forecast covers from the period start date. Typical horizons range from 4 to 52 weeks.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Planning Horizon

description: The lower bound of the forecast confidence interval in units, representing the pessimistic scenario for demand.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Confidence Intervals

description: The method used to generate the forecast. Statistical methods include time series models, machine learning uses advanced algorithms, manual override indicates planner adjustments, hybrid combines multiple methods, and consensus represents collaborative planning.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Forecasting Methods

description: The specific forecasting model or algorithm used to generate the forecast (e.g., ARIMA, exponential smoothing, neural network, ensemble model).
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Model Configuration

description: The end date of the forecast period. Typically represents the end of a week for weekly forecasts.
The start date of the forecast period. Typically represents the beginning of a week for weekly forecasts.

The current lifecycle status of the forecast. Draft forecasts are under review, approved forecasts are validated by planners, published forecasts are released to replenishment systems, superseded forecasts are replaced by newer versions, and rejected forecasts are not used.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Forecast Workflow

description: The upper bound of the forecast confidence interval in units, representing the optimistic scenario for demand.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Confidence Intervals

description: Version number of the forecast for the same item-location-period combination. Increments with each revision to support forecast history and audit trails.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Version Control

description: The date and time when the forecast was last modified, either by system recalculation or manual planner override.
The Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) for this item-location combination, measuring historical forecast accuracy. Lower values indicate better accuracy.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Forecast Accuracy Metrics

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this forecast is for a new product introduction with limited or no historical sales data. True indicates a new product.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - New Item Forecasting

description: Free-text notes entered by the planner explaining the rationale for manual forecast overrides or adjustments.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Override Management

description: The reason code when a planner manually overrides the system-generated forecast. Captures business context for forecast adjustments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotion|stockout|weather|seasonality|new_product|discontinuation|market_trend|competitive_action|supply_constraint|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Override Management

description: The incremental demand units expected from promotional activities such as BOGO (Buy One Get One), TPR (Temporary Price Reduction), or ad circular features.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Promotional Lift

description: The recommended safety stock level in units to buffer against forecast variability and supply chain uncertainty. Used in replenishment calculations.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Safety Stock Calculation

description: The seasonal index applied to the forecast for this period, representing the expected demand variation due to seasonality. A value of 1.0 represents average demand, above 1.0 indicates peak season, below 1.0 indicates off-season.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Seasonality Modeling

description: The total demand forecast in units, combining baseline forecast and promotional lift. This is the primary forecast value used for replenishment planning.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Total Forecast

description: The trend factor applied to the forecast, representing the expected growth or decline in demand over time. Positive values indicate growth, negative values indicate decline.
reference: Blue Yonder Demand Planning - Trend Analysis

Timestamp when the DSD delivery was fully reconciled between received quantities, invoice amounts, and POS integration. Marks completion of the DSD workflow.
Vendor-assigned route identifier for the delivery driver's scheduled stops. Used for delivery sequencing and driver performance tracking.
Planned date for the DSD delivery to arrive at the store location. Used for store labor planning and receiving dock scheduling.
Planned timestamp for the DSD delivery arrival, including time of day. Enables precise receiving dock coordination and labor allocation.
Total tax amount included in the DSD delivery invoice. Used for sales tax reconciliation and financial reporting.

description: Actual temperature reading (in Fahrenheit) recorded at the time of DSD delivery receipt. Used for cold chain compliance verification and quality control.

Indicates whether this receipt is part of a cross-dock operation where goods are transferred directly from inbound to outbound without storage (True) or follows standard receiving and putaway (False).

Indicates whether this receipt is a direct store delivery from a vendor bypassing the distribution center (True) or a standard DC receipt (False).

The outcome of the quality inspection: PASSED (all items accepted), FAILED (all items rejected), PARTIAL (some accepted, some rejected), PENDING (inspection not yet complete), NOT_REQUIRED (no inspection needed).
The date and time when this goods receipt record was last modified or updated in the system.

Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt: DRAFT (not yet started), IN_PROGRESS (receiving in process), COMPLETED (physically received but not posted), POSTED (posted to inventory), CANCELLED (receipt cancelled).
The precise date and time when the goods receipt transaction was initiated or completed.

Classification of the receipt transaction: PO (purchase order receipt), DSD (direct store delivery), RETURN (vendor return), TRANSFER (inter-location transfer), CROSS_DOCK (cross-dock operation), SAMPLE (vendor sample).
Indicates whether RFID tags were scanned during the receipt process for automated item tracking (True) or manual receipt was performed (False).

The security seal number affixed to the trailer or container, verified at receipt to ensure shipment integrity.

The measured temperature (in Celsius) of the shipment at the time of receipt, critical for cold chain compliance and perishable goods.

description: Classifies the purpose of the physical or mailing address captured in this record (e.g., home, billing, delivery, pickup, mailing). Null when the record represents a non-address contact type such as email or phone.
reference: USPS Publication 28 — Postal Addressing Standards; SAP Retail SD — Partner Function Address

description: Current lifecycle status of the contact record. 'active' indicates the contact is valid and in use; 'bounced' indicates email hard-bounce or phone disconnection; 'suppressed' indicates the contact is on a suppression list for compliance reasons; 'pending_verification' indicates awaiting confirmation.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Contact Point Status

description: Classifies the nature of the contact channel captured in this record (e.g., email, mobile phone, home phone, work phone, fax). Drives channel-specific communication routing and CAN-SPAM/TCPA compliance enforcement.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act); CAN-SPAM Act; Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Contact Point Type

description: The actual contact detail for non-address contact types: email address, mobile phone number, home phone number, work phone number, or fax number. Null when the record represents a physical address. Contains PII and must be handled per GDPR/CCPA requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA

description: County or parish name associated with the address. Used for local tax jurisdiction mapping, regulatory reporting, and geographic segmentation analytics.
reference: USPS Publication 28 — Postal Addressing Standards; FIPS 6-4 — County Codes

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this contact record was first created in the Grocery customer data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR/CCPA data subject request processing.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) — Accountability; SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls; ISO 8601

description: Internal delivery zone identifier assigned to this address based on geographic proximity to fulfillment centers, micro-fulfillment centers (MFC), or store locations. description: Most recent date on which this phone contact record was checked against the FTC National Do Not Call (DNC) Registry. Required for TCPA compliance; phone numbers must be scrubbed against the DNC registry at least every 31 days before telemarketing calls.
reference: FTC National Do Not Call Registry; TCPA 47 CFR Part 64.1200

description: Indicates whether all outbound communications to this contact record are suppressed regardless of opt-in status. Set when an entity is on a national Do Not Call (DNC) registry, has filed a legal complaint, or has requested complete communication cessation. Overrides all opt-in flags.
reference: FTC National Do Not Call Registry; TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act; GDPR Article 21 — Right to Object

description: Date after which this contact record is no longer active or valid. Null for currently active records. Supports bi-temporal modeling, address history retention, and regulatory data retention requirements.
reference: SAP Retail SD — Validity Period; GDPR Article 5(1)(e) — Storage Limitation; ISO 8601

description: Date from which this contact record is considered active and valid for use in communications and fulfillment operations. Supports bi-temporal modeling and historical contact data management.
reference: SAP Retail SD — Validity Period; ISO 8601 — Date Representation

description: Identifier of the parent entity (shopper, wholesale account, or pharmacy patient) to whom this contact record belongs. Supports multiple contact records per entity.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Contact Object; SAP Retail SD — Customer Master

description: Discriminator indicating the type of entity this contact record belongs to, enabling the contact_info product to serve B2C shoppers, B2B wholesale accounts, and pharmacy patients (non-PHI) within the customer domain.

description: Indicates whether this address record is the default address for the entity. Used by the Order Management System (OMS) and omnichannel fulfillment to pre-populate delivery and billing addresses at checkout.

description: Indicates whether this contact record is the primary contact of its type for the entity. Only one record per entity per contact_type should have is_primary = true. Used to determine the default channel for outbound communications.

description: Indicates whether the contact value (email or phone) or address has been verified through an active confirmation process (e.g., email click-through, SMS OTP, USPS validation). Drives communication eligibility and fraud risk scoring.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA; GDPR Article 7 — Consent Verification

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) derived from USPS address normalization and geocoding. Supports last-mile delivery (LMD) route optimization, store assignment, and geographic segmentation.

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (decimal degrees, WGS84) derived from USPS address normalization and geocoding. Supports last-mile delivery (LMD) route optimization, store assignment, and geographic segmentation.

description: Indicates whether the entity has provided explicit opt-in consent to receive marketing email communications on this email contact record. Required for CAN-SPAM and GDPR compliance. Null or false for non-email contact types.

description: Indicates whether the entity has opted in to receive push notifications through the Grocery mobile application or digital commerce platform. Supports omnichannel personalization and promotional targeting via Salesforce Commerce Cloud.
reference: GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798.120; Apple APNS / Google FCM Consent Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the entity has provided explicit opt-in consent to receive SMS/text message communications on this mobile phone contact record. Required for TCPA compliance. Null or false for non-mobile-phone contact types.

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this contact record originated (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud for digital registrations, NCR POS for in-store capture, SAP Retail for wholesale accounts, McKesson for pharmacy patients). Supports data lineage and master data management reconciliation.
reference: SAP Retail SD; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; NCR POS; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when this contact record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) — Accountability; SOX Section 404; ISO 8601

description: Calendar date on which the contact value or address was last successfully verified. Used to assess verification currency and trigger re-verification workflows when verification has lapsed.

description: The date and time when this identity resolution event record was first written to the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the system audit creation timestamp, distinct from resolution_timestamp which captures the business event time.

business_glossary_term: Salesforce CRM Contact ID - Side A

business_glossary_term: Salesforce CRM Contact ID - Side B
description: The Salesforce CRM contact identifier for the Side B record. Preserved for cross-system reconciliation and to support downstream CRM merge/deduplication operations in Salesforce.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; Internal MDM Governance Standard

description: Categorical data quality assessment assigned to this resolution event. CLEAN = no data quality concerns; SUSPECT_DUPLICATE = additional duplicates may exist; CONFLICTING_SIGNALS = identity signals contradict each other; INCOMPLETE_PROFILE = one or both records lack sufficient attributes for high-confidence matching; SYNTHETIC = test or synthetic record detected.
reference: Internal Data Quality Framework; ISO/IEC 25012 Data Quality

description: Indicates whether the email addresses on Side A and Side B records matched (exact or normalized) during the resolution evaluation. Supports granular analysis of which identity signals contributed to the match decision.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Lawful Processing Basis
description: The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 under which the identity resolution processing activity is conducted. Required for compliance with privacy regulations applicable to Grocery's customer data processing. NOT_APPLICABLE for non-EU shoppers.

description: Indicates whether the loyalty program identifiers (Point of Sale (POS) loyalty IDs) on Side A and Side B records matched during the resolution evaluation. Loyalty ID is a high-confidence deterministic signal in grocery retail identity resolution.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; NCR POS Loyalty Integration

description: Numeric probability score between 0.0000 and 1.0000 representing the algorithm's confidence that Side A and Side B records belong to the same individual. Scores above the configured threshold trigger automatic merge; scores in the review band are flagged for manual review. Critical for data quality KPI reporting.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; ISO/IEC 27001 Data Quality

description: The version identifier of the matching rule set or ML model that was applied during this resolution event (e.g., 'RULE_SET_v2.3', 'ML_MODEL_v1.0.5'). Enables retrospective analysis of resolution quality across rule engine versions and supports model governance.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; ISO/IEC 27001 Data Quality

description: The primary data attribute(s) that triggered the match between Side A and Side B records. Indicates which identity signal (e.g., email, phone, loyalty ID, name+address combination) drove the resolution decision. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EMAIL|PHONE|LOYALTY_ID|NAME_ADDRESS|EMAIL_PHONE|NAME_DOB|DEVICE_ID|PAYMENT_TOKEN — promote to reference product]

The configured confidence score threshold that was in effect at the time of this resolution event. Records the threshold value used to classify the match as auto-merge, review, or reject, enabling retrospective audit of threshold policy changes.

Numeric similarity score (0.0000–1.0000) measuring the degree of name match between Side A and Side B records, computed using fuzzy string matching algorithms (e.g., Jaro-Winkler, Levenshtein). Used in probabilistic resolution methods.
Indicates whether the phone numbers on Side A and Side B records matched (exact or normalized) during the resolution evaluation. Supports granular signal-level analysis of match quality.

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Loyalty ID - Side A
description: The NCR Point of Sale (POS) loyalty program identifier associated with the Side A record. A high-confidence deterministic identity signal in grocery retail. Preserved for audit trail and cross-system identity traceability.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Internal MDM Governance Standard

business_glossary_term: Point of Sale (POS) Loyalty ID - Side B
description: The NCR Point of Sale (POS) loyalty program identifier associated with the Side B record. Paired with pos_loyalty_id_a to evaluate loyalty ID match signal during deterministic resolution.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Internal MDM Governance Standard

description: Identifier of the batch deduplication run or scheduled job that generated this resolution event. Groups all resolution events produced in the same processing cycle for operational monitoring, reprocessing, and data quality KPI reporting.

The processing channel through which the resolution event was initiated. BATCH = overnight bulk deduplication run; REAL_TIME = triggered at point of customer interaction; MANUAL_UI = data steward action via MDM console; API = triggered by external system call; SCHEDULED = periodic reconciliation job.

The date after which a REJECTED or FLAGGED_FOR_REVIEW resolution decision should be re-evaluated. Supports periodic re-review of borderline cases as new identity signals become available. Null for permanent decisions (MERGED, LINKED).

The algorithmic or procedural approach used to determine that two records represent the same shopper. DETERMINISTIC = exact match on email, phone, or loyalty ID; PROBABILISTIC = fuzzy match on name and address; MANUAL_MERGE = data steward decision; RULE_BASED = configurable business rules; ML_MODEL = machine learning classifier.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; ISO/IEC 27001 Data Quality

description: Current workflow state of the identity resolution event. MERGED = records combined into a single golden record; LINKED = records associated but kept separate; FLAGGED_FOR_REVIEW = queued for manual data steward review; REJECTED = determined to be distinct individuals; PENDING = awaiting processing or review.

The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the identity resolution event was executed. Represents the principal business event time for this record. Used for MDM audit trail, data quality KPI trending, and regulatory compliance reporting.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: The category of action taken during this resolution event. MERGE = two records collapsed into one golden record; LINK = records associated without merging; UNMERGE = previously merged records separated; UNLINK = previously linked records disassociated; RECLASSIFY = resolution status changed without structural change.

The username, service account name, or system process identifier that executed the resolution action. For manual merges, this is the data steward's user ID. For automated resolutions, this is the system or batch process name (e.g., 'MDM_AUTO_MERGE_JOB'). Supports accountability and audit trail requirements.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: The outcome decision made by the data steward during manual review of a flagged resolution event. APPROVED = steward confirmed the match and authorized the action; REJECTED = steward determined records are distinct; ESCALATED = referred to senior steward or governance committee; DEFERRED = decision postponed pending additional information.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: Free-text notes entered by the data steward during manual review, documenting the rationale for their decision. Supports audit trail completeness and institutional knowledge for recurring edge cases in identity resolution.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: Identifier of the data steward review queue or work item to which this resolution event was assigned when flagged for manual review. Null for automated resolutions that did not require human intervention. Supports workflow management and SLA tracking for MDM operations.
The date and time when the data steward completed their manual review and recorded a review_decision. Null for automated resolutions. Used to calculate review SLA compliance and MDM operational KPIs.

The username of the data steward or MDM analyst who reviewed and adjudicated this resolution event when it was flagged for manual review. Null for fully automated resolutions. Distinct from resolved_by_user which captures the executor.
reference: Internal MDM Governance Standard; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: Internal shopper identifier for the first (source) record participating in the identity resolution event. Represents one of the two candidate duplicate records being evaluated or merged.
Internal shopper identifier for the second (candidate duplicate) record participating in the identity resolution event. Paired with shopper_id_a to form the resolution pair.

Operational system of record that originated the Side A shopper record. Identifies which platform (e.g., NCR Point of Sale (POS), Salesforce CRM, eCommerce, McKesson Pharmacy, mobile app, loyalty system) contributed the first identity.

description: Operational system of record that originated the Side B shopper record. Identifies which platform contributed the second (candidate duplicate) identity for comparison against Side A.

Unique surrogate identifier for each shopper preference record in the Grocery customer preference data product. Serves as the primary key for all preference-related joins and lineage tracking.
Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: PROMOTION: Department‑level preference allows personalized promotions and shelf‑stock decisions per department.

description: Specifies a product certification standard that the shopper prefers when purchasing. Used to filter product catalog results in digital commerce and to drive assortment recommendations. description: A numeric score between 0.0000 and 1.0000 representing the system's confidence in the accuracy of an inferred preference. Used by the personalization engine to weight recommendations.

description: Indicates whether the shopper has provided explicit consent for Grocery to collect, store, and use their preference data for personalization and targeted marketing purposes. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance. When FALSE, preference data must not be used for marketing purposes.

description: The date and time when the shopper provided or last renewed consent for preference data collection and use. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails.
reference: GDPR Article 7 (Conditions for Consent); CCPA Section 1798.100

description: The version identifier of the privacy policy or consent notice under which the shopper's preference consent was collected (e.g., 'v2.1'). Enables compliance teams to identify shoppers who consented under older policy versions and trigger re-consent workflows when policies are updated.

description: The date and time when this preference record was first created in the system. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and GDPR data subject access request (DSAR) responses.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Storage Limitation); Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Classifies the nature of a dietary restriction preference. 'allergy' indicates a medically significant food allergy. 'intolerance' indicates a food sensitivity. 'medical' indicates a clinically prescribed dietary requirement. 'religious' indicates a faith-based dietary rule (e.g., kosher, halal). 'ethical' indicates a values-based choice (e.g., vegan). 'lifestyle' indicates a personal wellness choice (e.g., keto). Null for non-dietary preference categories.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA); USDA Organic Certification Standards

business_glossary_term: Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) Eligible Filter Flag
description: Indicates whether the shopper has enabled the EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) eligible product filter in their digital commerce experience.
