description: Name of the regulatory body relevant to this interaction when regulatory_flag = True (e.g., 'FDA', 'DEA', 'State Board of Pharmacy', 'OSHA', 'FTC'). Supports compliance reporting and audit trail requirements.
reference: FDA; DEA; HIPAA; OSHA; FTC; State Boards of Pharmacy

description: Indicates whether this interaction involves a regulatory or compliance matter requiring escalation or formal reporting (e.g., FDA food safety complaint, DEA pharmacy concern, HIPAA-related inquiry, OSHA workplace incident report). True triggers compliance workflow.
reference: FDA (Food and Drug Administration); DEA (Drug Enforcement Administration); HIPAA; OSHA; FTC

description: Current resolution state of the interaction. Tracks whether the shopper's issue or inquiry was resolved, escalated, or left pending. Critical for customer service SLA monitoring and CRM workflow management.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Case Status; ITIL Service Management Standard

description: Post-interaction satisfaction rating provided by the shopper, typically on a 1–5 scale (CSAT). Distinct from NPS score; captures immediate interaction-level satisfaction rather than overall brand loyalty. Null for interactions where no rating was solicited.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — CSAT Survey; CRM Industry Standard (ICMI)

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this interaction record originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, NCR POS, McKesson Pharmacy, eCommerce platform). Supports data lineage and multi-source reconciliation in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Architecture

description: Secondary classification providing more granular detail within the interaction_category (e.g., within 'Delivery': 'Late Delivery', 'Missing Item', 'Damaged Item'). Enables drill-down root cause analysis.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Case Sub-Category; CRM Standard

description: Brief subject or topic label for the interaction (e.g., 'Missing item in delivery', 'Pharmacy prescription inquiry', 'Loyalty points dispute'). Used for categorization, routing, and reporting in CRM and contact center systems.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Case Subject; CRM Standard

description: Status of the shopper's response to a survey interaction: complete (all questions answered), partial (some questions answered), opted_out (shopper declined), or no_response (survey sent but not answered). Applicable to NPS and other survey interaction types.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Survey Response Status; CRM Standard

description: Timestamp when this interaction record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Audit Trail Requirements; Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Time in seconds the shopper waited before being connected to an agent or receiving a response. Used for service level agreement (SLA) monitoring and customer experience benchmarking.
reference: Contact Center Industry Standard (ICMI); Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacy patient profile record in the Grocery lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running enrollment/registration relationship between a shopper and Grocery pharmacy services.

Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Pharmacy counseling assigns a pharmacist associate to each patient; required for compliance reporting and medication counseling audit.
Identifier of the Grocery store location where the patient prefers to fill prescriptions. Used for routing Rx notifications, staffing, and inventory planning at the pharmacy counter.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Store Master

description: Reference to the shopper master record in the customer domain. Links the pharmacy patient profile to the unified shopper identity. Every pharmacy patient must have a corresponding shopper record.

Date on which the patient most recently disclosed or updated their self-reported allergy information with pharmacy staff. Nullable when allergy_flag is False. Used to prompt periodic allergy review during counseling.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR Part 164); State Boards of Pharmacy — Allergy Review Standards

description: Non-clinical, self-reported indicator that the patient has disclosed one or more drug or food allergies to pharmacy staff. This is a flag only — clinical allergy details reside in McKesson Pharmacy Systems and are excluded from this non-PHI profile per HIPAA compliance requirements.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR Part 164) — PHI Exclusion; McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Allergy Profile

description: Date on which the patient provided or last updated consent for the auto-refill program. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance. Nullable when auto_refill_opt_in is False.
reference: State Boards of Pharmacy — Informed Consent Requirements

description: Indicates whether the patient has opted into the automatic prescription refill program. When True, eligible maintenance medications are automatically queued for refill before the supply runs out. Drives CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) logic for pharmacy inventory.

Patient's preferred method for receiving pharmacist counseling when picking up a new or changed prescription. 'waived' indicates the patient has formally declined counseling. Required by OBRA-90 regulations for Medicaid patients; captured for all patients as best practice.
reference: OBRA-90 (Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1990) — Pharmacist Counseling Requirements; State Boards of Pharmacy

description: Date on which the patient most recently waived pharmacist counseling. Nullable when counseling_preference is not 'waived'. Supports compliance audit trail for OBRA-90 and State Board of Pharmacy inspections.
reference: OBRA-90; State Boards of Pharmacy — Counseling Waiver Documentation

description: Timestamp when the pharmacy patient profile record was first created in the Grocery lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record retention scheduling.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Internal Controls; HIPAA Privacy Rule — Record Retention

description: Primary street address line for prescription home delivery. Captured separately from the shopper's primary address to support patients who require delivery to a different location (e.g., workplace, caregiver address). Used by LMD (Last Mile Delivery) routing.
reference: JDA Transportation Management — Address Validation; HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR Part 164) — Minimum Necessary Standard

description: Secondary address line for prescription home delivery (apartment, suite, unit number). Nullable. Complements delivery_address_line1 for complete delivery address specification.
City component of the prescription home delivery address. Used for LMD (Last Mile Delivery) zone assignment and carrier routing.
ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the prescription home delivery address. Used for delivery zone mapping, carrier selection, and geographic analytics.
reference: USPS — ZIP Code Standard; JDA Transportation Management

description: Two-letter US state code for the prescription home delivery address. Used for carrier zone assignment and state-level pharmacy licensing compliance verification.
reference: USPS — State Abbreviation Standard; State Boards of Pharmacy — Interstate Delivery Regulations

description: Date on which the pharmacy patient profile was deactivated or the patient relationship ended. Nullable for currently active patients. Used for record retention scheduling and compliance reporting.
reference: State Boards of Pharmacy — Patient Record Retention Requirements

description: Date on which the shopper was first registered as a pharmacy patient at Grocery. Marks the effective start of the pharmacy patient relationship. Used for tenure analysis and loyalty program integration.
reference: State Boards of Pharmacy — Patient Registration Requirements

business_glossary_term: Patient Guardian or Authorized Representative Name
description: Full name of the legally authorized representative or guardian for patients who cannot consent on their own behalf (e.g., pediatric patients, patients with power of attorney arrangements). Non-PHI reference field. Nullable for adult patients without a designated representative.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR § 164.502(g)) — Personal Representatives; State Boards of Pharmacy

description: Relationship of the authorized representative or guardian to the pharmacy patient. Used to validate authorization for prescription pickup and counseling consent. Nullable when no guardian is on file.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR § 164.502(g)); State Boards of Pharmacy — Authorized Representative Policies

business_glossary_term: HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices Acknowledgment Channel
description: Method by which the patient acknowledged the HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices. Supports audit trail for HIPAA compliance. 'verbal_noted' is used when the patient verbally acknowledged but declined to sign, per HIPAA good-faith effort documentation requirements.

business_glossary_term: HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices Acknowledgment Date
description: Date on which the patient acknowledged receipt of the HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices. Nullable when hipaa_notice_acknowledged is False. Required for HIPAA compliance audit trail.

business_glossary_term: HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices Acknowledgment Flag
description: Indicates whether the patient has acknowledged receipt of the pharmacy's HIPAA Notice of Privacy Practices (NPP). Required by HIPAA Privacy Rule for all new pharmacy patients. Pharmacies must make a good-faith effort to obtain written acknowledgment.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule (45 CFR § 164.520) — Notice of Privacy Practices

business_glossary_term: Pharmacy Benefit Insurance Bank Identification Number (BIN)
description: Six-digit Bank Identification Number (BIN) used to route pharmacy claims to the correct Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) during adjudication. Non-PHI operational identifier. Standard NCPDP field used in electronic claims processing.
reference: NCPDP (National Council for Prescription Drug Programs) — Telecommunication Standard; HIPAA Transaction Standards

description: Insurance group identifier associated with the patient's pharmacy benefit plan. Used during claims adjudication to identify the employer group or plan sponsor. Non-PHI operational field. Standard NCPDP field.
reference: NCPDP Telecommunication Standard — Group ID Field

description: Processor Control Number (PCN) used in conjunction with the BIN to route pharmacy claims to the correct adjudication processor within a PBM network. Non-PHI operational field. Standard NCPDP field.

Name of the patient's primary pharmacy benefit insurance plan (e.g., 'BlueCross BlueShield Rx', 'Aetna Pharmacy'). Non-PHI operational field used for routing adjudication and patient communication. Detailed insurance/PBM data resides in McKesson.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Insurance Plan Master

description: Patient's preferred language for pharmacy communications, prescription labels, and counseling materials. Stored as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'zh-TW'). Used to route multilingual pharmacy staff and generate translated prescription materials per FDA labeling guidance.
reference: FDA — Prescription Drug Labeling; Title VI of the Civil Rights Act — Language Access Requirements

description: Date of the patient's most recent prescription fill at any Grocery pharmacy location. Non-PHI operational field used for patient activity monitoring, inactive patient identification, and MedSync scheduling. Sourced from McKesson Pharmacy Systems.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Prescription Fill History

business_glossary_term: Pharmacy Loyalty Prescription (Rx) Points Eligibility Flag
description: Indicates whether the patient is eligible to earn loyalty program points for qualifying prescription fills. Integrates pharmacy activity with the Grocery loyalty program managed via NCR POS Systems and CRM. Certain prescription categories (e.g., controlled substances) may be ineligible per program rules.
reference: NCR POS Systems — Loyalty Integration; Grocery Loyalty Program Terms

description: External patient identifier assigned by McKesson Pharmacy Systems (the operational system of record for prescription management). Used to cross-reference non-PHI profile data in the lakehouse with clinical records in McKesson. Required for operational reconciliation.

Indicates whether the patient is enrolled in the Medication Synchronization (MedSync) program, which aligns all of the patient's maintenance prescriptions to a single monthly pickup date. Improves adherence and reduces pharmacy counter traffic variability.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — MedSync Program; NCPA Medication Synchronization Guidelines

description: Date on which the patient was enrolled in the MedSync program. Used for program tenure tracking and cohort analysis. Nullable when medsync_enrolled is False.

The designated monthly calendar date on which all synchronized prescriptions for this patient are scheduled for pickup or delivery. Nullable when medsync_enrolled is False. Used for pharmacy workload planning and inventory pre-positioning.

Indicates whether the patient is eligible for the 340B Drug Pricing Program, which provides discounted outpatient drugs to qualifying patients of covered entities. Non-PHI eligibility flag used for claims routing and pricing.
reference: HRSA (Health Resources and Services Administration) — 340B Drug Pricing Program; 42 U.S.C. § 256b

description: Current lifecycle status of the pharmacy patient profile. 'active' indicates the patient is actively using Grocery pharmacy services. 'inactive' indicates no recent activity. 'on_hold' indicates a temporary suspension of services. 'transferred' indicates the patient has moved to another pharmacy. 'deceased' is set upon notification of patient death for record retention compliance.
reference: State Boards of Pharmacy — Patient Record Retention Requirements

description: Classification of the pharmacy patient by service type. 'retail' is the standard in-store patient. 'mail_order' indicates the patient primarily uses mail-order fulfillment. 'specialty' indicates enrollment in specialty pharmacy programs for high-cost or complex medications. 'long_term_care' indicates a patient in a care facility. 'pediatric' indicates a minor patient with a guardian on file.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Patient Classification; NCPDP — Patient Type Codes

description: Self-reported name of the patient's primary care physician (PCP). Used for prescription verification and physician outreach by pharmacy staff. This is a non-PHI reference field — clinical prescriber details reside in McKesson. Stored as a free-text name, not a structured provider record.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Prescriber Reference; DEA — Prescriber Verification

description: Contact phone number for the patient's primary care physician office. Used by pharmacy staff to verify prescriptions or coordinate care. Non-PHI operational reference field. Stored in E.164 international format.

Patient's preferred communication channel for prescription-related notifications, including ready-for-pickup alerts, refill reminders, and drug interaction notices. Drives outbound messaging routing in McKesson and NCR POS Systems.
reference: McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Patient Communication Preferences

description: Patient's preferred method for collecting ready prescriptions. Supports omnichannel fulfillment including in-store counter, curbside pickup (Click-and-Collect), drive-through pharmacy window, and home delivery. Drives OMS (Order Management System) routing for pharmacy fulfillment.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Order Management; McKesson Pharmacy Systems — Fulfillment Preferences

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Benefit Eligibility Flag
description: Indicates whether the patient has been identified as eligible for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits, which may affect OTC product eligibility at the pharmacy counter. Non-PHI flag aligned with the shopper master SNAP eligibility field.
reference: USDA — SNAP Program Regulations (7 CFR Part 278)

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the pharmacy patient profile record in the Grocery lakehouse Silver layer. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) — Internal Controls; HIPAA Privacy Rule — Record Retention

business_glossary_term: Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) Benefit Eligibility Flag
description: Indicates whether the patient has been identified as eligible for WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits, which may affect eligible OTC and formula purchases at the pharmacy counter. Non-PHI flag.
reference: USDA — WIC Program Regulations (7 CFR Part 246)

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each status change event record in the shopper and wholesale account lifecycle audit trail. Primary key of this immutable history table.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: The Salesforce CRM case or service ticket identifier associated with this status change when the change was triggered by a customer service interaction. Supports end-to-end traceability from customer complaint to account action for NPS and escalation analytics.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — CRM Case Management

description: The identifier of the human user (associate, customer service representative, or administrator) who initiated the status change. Null when the change was triggered by an automated system process. Used for accountability and audit trail in compliance investigations.
reference: SOX Section 404; OSHA — Workplace Safety Records; PCI DSS Requirement 10

description: Reference to the shopper or wholesale account record whose status changed. Links this history event to the customer master record in the customer domain.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Customer Account Object

description: When reversal_flag is True, references the status_history_id of the original status change event that this record reverses or corrects. Null for non-reversal records. Maintains the immutable append-only audit trail while enabling error correction traceability.

Indicates whether this status change required managerial or compliance approval before being applied. Supports segregation-of-duties controls for high-risk transitions such as fraud suspensions and regulatory holds.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls; PCI DSS Requirement 7 — Access Control

description: The date and time at which the status change was approved by the designated approver. Null when approval_required is False or when the change is pending approval. Used for SLA tracking on approval workflows.

The interaction channel through which the status change was initiated. Supports omnichannel analytics and helps identify whether closures or suspensions originate from self-service digital channels, call center interactions, or automated back-office processes.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Omnichannel Order Management

description: Standardized code classifying the business reason that triggered the status change. Used for compliance reporting, fraud investigation workflows, and customer service escalation routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary_closure|fraud_suspension|inactivity|reactivation|merge|duplicate_detected|deceased|regulatory_hold — promote to reference product]
reference: FTC — Consumer Protection; HIPAA Privacy Rule (for pharmacy patient records)

description: Free-text narrative providing additional context for the status change beyond the standardized reason code. Supports customer service escalation notes, fraud investigation documentation, and compliance audit trails.

The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the status change event occurred in the operational system. This is the authoritative business event time, distinct from the record audit timestamp. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5 — Data Accuracy

description: The name of the operational system or automated process that triggered the status change when no human user is responsible. Examples include the fraud detection engine, batch inactivity sweep, CRM automation, or account merge process. Null when changed_by_user_id is populated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|ncr_pos|sap_retail|oracle_rms|mckesson|manual|batch_inactivity|fraud_engine|merge_process — promote to reference product]
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 10 — Audit Logging; SOX Section 404

description: The date and time at which this status history record was first written to the Databricks Silver layer. Distinct from change_timestamp (the business event time). Used for data pipeline audit, SLA monitoring, and Silver layer lineage tracking. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail; PCI DSS Requirement 10 — Log Management

description: The legal basis under which this status change event and associated personal data is processed and retained. Required for GDPR compliance documentation. Particularly relevant for closure and erasure events where the legal basis for continued data retention must be established.
reference: GDPR Article 6 — Lawfulness of Processing; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: The calendar date on which the new status becomes operationally effective. May differ from change_timestamp when status changes are scheduled in advance (e.g., a reactivation scheduled for a future date or a closure effective at end of billing period).
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Price Management Effective Dating

description: Discriminator identifying the type of customer entity whose status changed. Distinguishes between B2C shoppers, B2B wholesale accounts, pharmacy patients (non-PHI), and government program participants such as SNAP and WIC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shopper|wholesale_account|pharmacy_patient|snap_participant|wic_participant — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Customer Classification

description: Reference number linking this status change to an active fraud investigation case when the change_reason_code is fraud_suspension or regulatory_hold. Enables cross-referencing with the fraud management system for investigation workflows and regulatory reporting.
reference: FTC — Consumer Protection; PCI DSS Requirement 12 — Incident Response

description: Indicates whether the status change was voluntarily initiated by the customer (True) or was applied by the business or an automated system without customer request (False). Critical for distinguishing voluntary closures from fraud suspensions or inactivity-driven deactivations in compliance and NPS analysis.
reference: FTC — Consumer Protection; GDPR Article 7 — Consent

description: The account lifecycle status applied as a result of this change event. Captures the to-state in the status transition pair. Together with previous_status, defines the complete transition vector for lifecycle analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|suspended|pending|closed|merged|fraud_hold — promote to reference product]
reference: FTC — Advertising and Pricing Practices; SOX Section 404

description: Supplementary free-text notes entered by the associate or system at the time of the status change. May include customer service context, escalation details, or operational observations not captured by the structured reason code. Not intended for PHI or PCI data.

Indicates whether a customer-facing notification (email, SMS, or push) was dispatched to inform the shopper of the status change. Supports compliance with consumer notification requirements and customer service follow-up tracking.
reference: FTC — Consumer Notification Requirements; GDPR Article 34 — Communication of Data Breach

description: The date and time at which the customer notification was dispatched following the status change. Null when notification_sent is False. Used for SLA compliance tracking on customer communication obligations.
reference: FTC — Consumer Notification Requirements; GDPR Article 34

description: Indicates whether the status change affects a shopper who is also registered as a pharmacy patient (non-PHI flag only). When True, triggers a notification to the McKesson pharmacy system to review active prescription refill schedules and patient communication preferences.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule — Minimum Necessary Standard; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: The Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) loyalty tier held by the shopper immediately before the status change. Captured at the time of the event to preserve the historical tier context for loyalty program compliance, reactivation offers, and NPS analysis.

The marketing or behavioral shopper segment assigned to the account immediately before the status change. Preserved as a point-in-time snapshot to support segmentation drift analysis, reactivation campaign targeting, and churn modeling.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — Personalization and Segmentation

description: The account lifecycle status immediately before this change event was applied. Captures the from-state in the status transition pair for audit and investigation purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|suspended|pending|closed|merged|fraud_hold — promote to reference product]
reference: FTC — Advertising and Pricing Practices; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether the account is eligible for reactivation outreach following a closure or suspension event. Set to False for fraud suspensions, deceased records, or regulatory holds. Used by CRM and marketing automation to filter win-back campaign audiences.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud — CRM Campaign Management; FTC — Advertising Practices

description: The date after which the account is no longer eligible for reactivation, typically driven by data retention policies or regulatory requirements. Null for accounts with no reactivation expiry. Supports GDPR right-to-erasure scheduling and loyalty win-back campaign timing.
reference: GDPR Article 17 — Right to Erasure; SOX Section 404

description: The date after which this status history record may be purged or anonymized in accordance with the applicable data retention policy. Driven by regulatory requirements (GDPR, CCPA, SOX) and the nature of the status change event. Null for records with indefinite retention requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 5(1)(e) — Storage Limitation; SOX Section 802 — Record Retention

description: Indicates whether this record represents a reversal or correction of a previously applied status change. When True, the reversed_event_id field identifies the original event being corrected. Supports immutable audit trail integrity while allowing error correction.
reference: SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls; SAP Retail — Reversal Document Management

description: Indicates whether the status change affects the shopper's Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) / Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) eligibility or enrollment. Triggers downstream notification workflows to program administrators when True.
reference: USDA — SNAP Program Regulations; FNS (Food and Nutrition Service) Compliance

description: The native event or transaction identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce case ID, SAP change document number). Enables exact-match reconciliation between the Silver layer audit trail and the source system's own change log.
reference: SAP Retail — Change Document Management; Salesforce CRM — Audit Trail

description: The operational system of record that originated this status change event. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver layer and upstream operational systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|ncr_pos|sap_retail|oracle_rms|mckesson|workday_hcm|manual_entry — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP Retail — SD Sales and Distribution; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Indicates whether the status change affects the shopper's Women, Infants, and Children (WIC) program enrollment or benefit access. Triggers downstream notification workflows to WIC program administrators when True.
reference: USDA — WIC Program Regulations; FNS Compliance

description: Unique identifier for the loyalty engagement event record. Primary key.
Reference to the loyalty membership that generated this engagement event.
Reference to the customer who performed the engagement activity.
Reference to the marketing or engagement campaign that triggered or is associated with this event. Null if the engagement was organic and not campaign-driven.

Reference to the store location where the engagement occurred. Applicable for in-store events such as visit check-ins, pharmacy consultations, and click-and-collect pickups. Null for digital-only engagements.
Reference to the specific survey instrument completed in a survey response event. Null for non-survey events.
The version of the mobile app or web application used during the engagement event. Null for non-digital events.

The channel or interface through which the engagement activity was performed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mobile_app|website|in_store|email|sms|push_notification|social_media|call_center|kiosk — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Timestamp when this engagement event record was first created in the system in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

The store department or section visited during a store visit check-in event (e.g., Produce, Pharmacy, Deli, Bakery). Supports department-level dwell time and traffic analytics. Null for non-visit events.

The technical method used to detect and capture the engagement event (e.g., loyalty card scan at POS, app-based check-in, beacon proximity detection, Wi-Fi connection, manual entry by associate). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: card_scan|app_checkin|beacon|wifi|manual_entry|qr_code|nfc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The type of device used by the customer to perform the engagement activity. Applicable for digital engagements.

Duration in minutes that the customer spent in the store or department during a visit check-in event. Calculated from entry to exit detection. Supports dwell time analytics and customer journey insights. Null for non-visit events.

Duration in seconds of the engagement activity from start to completion. Applicable for activities with measurable duration such as survey completion, app session, or content viewing. Null for instantaneous events.
Status of the engagement event indicating whether the activity was fully completed, partially completed, abandoned, or failed due to technical issues.
Calendar date of the engagement event in format yyyy-MM-dd. Used for daily aggregation and reporting.
Date and time when the engagement activity occurred in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Classification of the non-purchase loyalty engagement activity. Covers digital interactions, in-store visits, surveys, referrals, social shares, pharmacy check-ins, click-and-collect first use, store visit check-ins, and campaign-triggered engagements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: app_download|profile_completion|survey_response|referral|social_share|pharmacy_consultation_checkin|click_and_collect_first_use|store_visit_checkin|campaign_engagement|email_open|email_click|push_notification_open|sms_response|gamification_achievement|tier_upgrade_celebration|birthday_engagement|product_review|recipe_view|digital_coupon_clip|wishlist_add|app_login — promote to reference product]
The type or name of the gamification achievement unlocked by the customer (e.g., First Purchase Badge, 10 Visits Milestone, Category Explorer). Null for non-gamification events.
The gamification level or rank achieved by the customer at the time of this engagement event. Null if gamification is not applicable.
Latitude coordinate of the customer location at the time of engagement, captured via GPS or beacon. Used for location-based analytics. Null if location data not available.
Longitude coordinate of the customer location at the time of engagement, captured via GPS or beacon. Used for location-based analytics. Null if location data not available.
The IP address of the device from which the digital engagement originated. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. Null for in-store events.
Timestamp when this engagement event record was last updated in the system in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Classification of the NPS score into standard categories: Promoter (9-10), Passive (7-8), or Detractor (0-6). Derived from nps_score. Null for non-survey events.

The numeric NPS score provided by the customer in a survey response event, typically on a scale of 0-10. Null for non-survey events. Supports NPS contribution tracking and customer sentiment analysis.
Free-text customer feedback or comment provided along with the NPS score in a survey response. Null for non-survey events. Used for qualitative sentiment analysis and voice-of-customer insights.
The operating system of the device used for the engagement activity. Applicable for digital engagements.
Number of loyalty points awarded to the member for completing this engagement activity. Zero if no points were awarded for this event type.
The unique referral code entered or used by the customer during a referral engagement event. Null for non-referral events.
Unique identifier for the customer session during which the engagement occurred. Used to group multiple engagement events within a single customer journey or session.
Identifier of the specific content item (recipe, product, promotion) that was shared in a social share event. Null for non-social-share events.
The type of content shared by the customer in a social share event. Null for non-social-share events.

The social media platform where a social share engagement occurred. Null for non-social-share events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: facebook|instagram|twitter|pinterest|tiktok|linkedin|youtube — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The operational system or platform that captured and recorded this engagement event (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, NCR POS, Mobile App Backend, CRM).
Unique identifier for the gamification challenge. Primary key for the gamification challenge entity.
Indicates whether eligible members are automatically enrolled in this challenge (true) or must opt-in manually (false).
URL to the badge or achievement icon awarded to members upon challenge completion, displayed in their profile.
Comma-separated list of product category codes that qualify for this challenge. Used for category exploration and assortment-driven challenges. Empty means all categories qualify.
Unique business identifier code for the challenge, used for external references and system integration.
Display name of the gamification challenge shown to loyalty members in the app and communications.

Current lifecycle status of the challenge: draft (being designed), scheduled (future launch), active (currently available), paused (temporarily suspended), completed (ended successfully), expired (ended by time), or cancelled (terminated early). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|scheduled|active|paused|completed|expired|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the challenge mechanics: streak (consecutive actions), milestone (cumulative target), category exploration (shop new categories), spend threshold (reach spending level), visit frequency (number of visits), or product trial (try specific products).

Comma-separated list of shopping channels where purchases count toward challenge completion: in_store, online, mobile_app, pickup (click-and-collect), or delivery. Empty means all channels qualify. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_store|online|mobile_app|pickup|delivery|pharmacy|fuel_center — promote to reference product]
Username or identifier of the loyalty program administrator who created this challenge.
Date and time when this challenge record was first created in the system.
Subjective difficulty rating of the challenge based on effort required: easy (minimal effort), moderate (regular engagement), hard (significant effort), or expert (exceptional commitment).
Numeric sort order for displaying this challenge in lists and carousels, with lower numbers appearing first.
Number of days the challenge is active and available for member participation, calculated from start to end date.
Comma-separated list of customer segment codes that are eligible to participate in this challenge. Empty means all segments are eligible.
Comma-separated list of loyalty tier codes that are eligible to participate in this challenge. Empty means all tiers are eligible.
Date when the challenge expires and members can no longer work toward or complete it.

Detailed description of the challenge explaining the rules, objectives, and how members can complete it.
URL to the visual image or icon representing this challenge in the mobile app and web portal.
Username or identifier of the loyalty program administrator who last updated this challenge.

Maximum number of times a single member can complete and earn rewards from this challenge. Null means unlimited completions.

Cumulative number of loyalty points that have been issued to members who completed this challenge, tracked for budget management.
Specific actions or behaviors the member must perform to complete the challenge, such as purchase frequency, category shopping, or spending targets.
Detailed description of the reward the member will receive, including any terms, conditions, or redemption instructions.
Number of loyalty points awarded to the member upon successful completion of the challenge.

Type of reward granted upon challenge completion: points (loyalty points), discount (percentage or dollar off), free item (specific product), fuel discount (cents per gallon), tier boost (accelerated tier progress), or badge (achievement recognition).
Brief summary of the challenge for display in list views and notifications, typically 1-2 sentences.
Date when the challenge becomes available to eligible members and they can begin working toward completion.
Comma-separated list of store IDs where this challenge is available. Used for location-specific or pilot challenges. Empty means all stores.
Numeric target the member must achieve to complete the challenge, such as number of visits, items purchased, or transactions completed.

Unit of measurement for the target quantity: visits (store visits), transactions (purchases), items (products bought), categories (product categories shopped), days (consecutive days), or dollars (spending amount).
Total loyalty points budget allocated for this challenge across all member completions. Used for financial planning and ROI tracking.
Total number of times this challenge has been completed by all members, used for engagement analytics and program effectiveness measurement.
Number of distinct members who have started or completed this challenge, used for reach and engagement analysis.

Unique identifier for the member challenge participation record. Primary key.
Reference to the gamification challenge definition that this member is participating in.

Reference to the points transaction or reward issuance record when the challenge reward was granted. Null if reward not yet issued.

Date when the member successfully completed the challenge requirements. Null if not yet completed.
Percentage of challenge completion calculated as (progress_value / target_value) * 100. Used for progress tracking and near-completion nudges.
Timestamp when this member challenge participation record was first created in the system.
Date when the member was disqualified from the challenge. Null if not disqualified.
Reason why the member was disqualified from the challenge, if applicable (e.g., fraud detection, terms violation, account suspension).
Channel through which the member enrolled in the challenge.
Date when the member enrolled in or was auto-enrolled into this challenge.
Date when this challenge expires for the member. After this date, no further progress can be made.
Date of the first transaction that qualified toward this challenge.
Timestamp when this member challenge participation record was last modified.
Date when the most recent notification was sent to the member regarding this challenge.
Timestamp when the progress_value was last updated. Used for real-time progress tracking.
Date of the most recent transaction that qualified toward this challenge.
Current status of the member's participation in this challenge.

Type of the last notification sent to the member about this challenge.

Unit of measure for the progress value (e.g., transactions, dollars, visits, SKUs purchased).

Number of qualifying transactions that have contributed to this challenge progress.
Indicates whether the completion reward has been issued to the member. True if reward issued, False otherwise.
Number of loyalty points awarded upon challenge completion.

Typical time respondents spend completing the survey, measured in minutes.

Unique identifier for the household member association record. Primary key.
Reference to the household unit to which this member belongs.
Reference to the individual loyalty membership record associated with this household member.
Reference to the store location this household member most frequently shops at. Used for localized offers and inventory planning.
Total dollar amount spent by this household member in the current calendar year. Used for member value segmentation and household contribution analysis.
Total number of shopping visits or transactions by this household member in the current calendar year. Used for engagement and frequency analysis.
Date when this household member association was approved. Null if approval is pending or not required.

Approval status for this household member association. Pending members await primary member approval, rejected members were denied, revoked members had approval withdrawn, and approved members are active.

Level of household benefits this member is eligible to receive. Full tier receives all household benefits, partial receives select benefits, restricted has limited access, and none receives no shared benefits.
Physical or virtual loyalty card number assigned to this household member for POS (Point of Sale) identification. May be shared across household or unique per member depending on program rules.

Indicates whether this member inherits household communication preferences, has custom individual preferences, or suppresses all household communications. Enables personalized communication strategies within households.
Timestamp when this household member record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Indicates whether this household member has linked their individual digital wallet to the household loyalty account for seamless point accrual and redemption at POS (Point of Sale).
Indicates whether this household member has opted in to receive email communications about household loyalty benefits and offers. Only applies when communication_preference_override is custom.
Indicates whether this member can access and redeem household fuel rewards at fuel centers. Enables household-level fuel discount pooling and redemption tracking.

Current status of this household member association. Active members participate in household benefits, inactive members have left, suspended members are temporarily blocked, and pending members await approval.
Date when this member accepted the household invitation. Null if invitation is pending or was declined.
Unique code used to invite this member to join the household. Tracks invitation source and enables secure household joining workflows.
Date when the household invitation was sent to this member. Used to track invitation response time and follow-up campaigns.
Date when this member was added to the household. Used to track household growth and member tenure.
Timestamp when this household member record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.
Date of the most recent purchase or loyalty activity by this household member. Used to track member engagement and identify inactive members.
Date when this member left or was removed from the household. Null if the member is currently active in the household.
Total loyalty points this member has contributed to the household pool since joining. Used for household contribution analytics and member value assessment.
Total loyalty points this member has redeemed from the household pool since joining. Used to track member redemption behavior and benefit utilization.

Role of this member within the household loyalty structure. Primary members have full administrative rights, secondary members have shared benefits, dependents have limited access, and authorized users can transact on behalf of the household.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Free-text notes about this household member association. May include special circumstances, restrictions, or customer service history relevant to household management.

Override flag for household-level offer eligibility. Inherit uses household rules, eligible forces inclusion in household offers, ineligible excludes this member from household offers. Used for compliance or member preference scenarios.
Indicates whether this member can access household pharmacy loyalty benefits such as prescription discounts or health rewards. Subject to HIPAA privacy rules and state pharmacy regulations.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule 45 CFR 164.502, State Boards of Pharmacy

description: Indicates whether this member participates in household-level points pooling. When true, points earned by this member contribute to the shared household balance.
Percentage of points earned by this member that are contributed to the household pool. Range 0.00 to 100.00. Enables partial pooling scenarios where members retain some individual points.

Type of relationship this member has to the primary household member. Used for household composition analytics and targeted family offers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|partner|child|parent|sibling|roommate|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether this household member has opted in to receive SMS text messages about household loyalty benefits and offers. Only applies when communication_preference_override is custom.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act), GDPR Article 7

description: Level of authority this member has to redeem household points or use household benefits. Full authority allows unrestricted redemption, limited requires approval thresholds, view-only can see but not transact, and none has no access.
Maximum dollar value or points this member can redeem per transaction or per period without additional approval. Null indicates no limit. Used for dependent and secondary member governance.
Primary key for membership

Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: ENROLLMENT_TRACKING: Loyalty enrollment reports require the associate who performed the member sign‑up to attribute enrollment volume and compliance; the associate_id FK enables this.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Loyalty program cost allocation uses a cost center to budget and report program expenses per member.
Reference to the household group this membership belongs to, if the member is part of a household loyalty account where multiple members share points and benefits. Null for individual memberships.
The store location the member shops at most frequently, determined by transaction history. Used for localized offers and store-specific promotions.
The store location where the customer enrolled, if enrollment occurred in-store. Null for digital enrollments. Used for store-level enrollment performance tracking.
Foreign key linking to loyalty.program_config. Business justification: Membership participates in a specific loyalty program configuration; link to program_config for governance.
The membership ID of the existing member who referred this customer, if enrollment came through the member referral program. Null for non-referred enrollments. Used for referral reward attribution.
Reference to the customer who owns this loyalty membership. Links to the customer SSOT in the customer domain.
Foreign key linking to loyalty.tier. Business justification: Membership belongs to a tier; replace string tier with foreign key for referential integrity.
Total purchase amount by the member in the current calendar or program year. Used for tier qualification thresholds and high-value member identification. Resets annually.
Total number of shopping trips or transactions by the member in the current calendar or program year. Used for frequency-based tier qualification and engagement scoring. Resets annually.

Segmentation of the member based on their predicted or actual customer lifetime value. High value members receive premium treatment and exclusive offers. At-risk members are targeted for retention campaigns.
The timestamp when this membership record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Indicates whether the membership card has been added to a digital wallet (Apple Wallet, Google Pay) for contactless loyalty identification at POS.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive physical mail communications including ad circulars and exclusive member offers.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive promotional emails and loyalty program communications via email.

The channel through which the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Used for channel attribution analysis and enrollment campaign effectiveness measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_store|online|mobile_app|pharmacy|fuel_center|call_center|partner — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The date the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Used to calculate membership tenure and anniversary-based rewards.
The current level or rank the member has achieved in the loyalty program gamification system through completing challenges, missions, or milestones. Higher levels unlock exclusive badges and rewards.
The most recent date the member earned or redeemed points through any transaction. Used for churn prediction and reactivation targeting.
The timestamp when this membership record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.
The most recent date the member made a purchase that earned loyalty points. Used for recency-based segmentation and lapsed member identification.
Cumulative total of all loyalty points earned since enrollment, including redeemed and expired points. Used for tier qualification and lifetime value analysis.
Cumulative total of all loyalty points redeemed since enrollment. Used for redemption rate analysis and program liability calculation.
The externally-known loyalty card number or membership identifier presented at POS and used for points accrual and redemption. This is the business identifier for the membership.

Current lifecycle status of the membership. Active memberships can earn and redeem points. Inactive memberships have no recent activity. Suspended memberships are temporarily blocked due to policy violations. Closed memberships are permanently terminated. Pending activation memberships are enrolled but not yet verified.
The date of the next scheduled points expiration event. Null if no points are scheduled to expire. Used for proactive member communication.

The most recent Net Promoter Score provided by the member through loyalty surveys. Scale of 0-10 where 9-10 are promoters, 7-8 are passives, 0-6 are detractors. Used for satisfaction tracking and churn prediction.
The date the member last completed an NPS survey. Used to determine survey recency and eligibility for follow-up surveys.
Current available loyalty points balance that can be redeemed for rewards, discounts, or merchandise. Updated in real-time with each transaction and redemption.
Number of points scheduled to expire within the next 30 days. Used for expiration reminder campaigns and breakage forecasting.
Indicates whether this member is the primary account holder for a household loyalty membership. Primary members have administrative privileges to add or remove household members.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive push notifications through the mobile app for personalized offers and program updates.

The source or campaign that led to the member enrollment. Examples: member referral program, social media campaign, in-store promotion, partner co-marketing. Used for acquisition cost analysis and campaign ROI measurement.
Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive promotional text messages and loyalty alerts via SMS.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act), GDPR Article 7

description: The date the current status became effective. Used for status history tracking and reactivation eligibility determination.
Detailed reason or code explaining the current membership status. Examples: voluntary closure, fraud suspension, inactivity timeout, duplicate account merge, deceased member.
The date the customer achieved their current tier level. Used for tier anniversary calculations and tier retention analysis.
The date the current tier expires if tier qualification thresholds are not maintained. Null for lifetime tiers. Used for tier retention campaigns and downgrade warnings.

Default points earned per dollar spent across all categories before any multipliers or bonuses are applied.

Rule defining how multiple earn bonuses (category, channel, tier) are combined when a transaction qualifies for more than one.

Additional points bonus rate applied to last mile delivery orders.

Number of days after expiry date during which points can still be redeemed before permanent forfeiture.

Date and time when this program configuration record was most recently updated.

Maximum number of points that can be redeemed in a single transaction to prevent excessive value extraction.

Indicates whether this configuration supports members participating in multiple loyalty programs simultaneously (e.g., grocery + pharmacy + fuel).
Additional points bonus rate applied to purchases made through digital commerce channels (web, mobile app).
Indicates whether AI-driven personalized reward offers and recommendations are enabled for members in this program.
Additional points bonus rate applied to click-and-collect or curbside pickup orders.
Strategy used to determine when loyalty points expire (rolling window from last activity, fixed annual date, tier-dependent rules, or never).
Points multiplier applied to purchases of store-owned brand products to incentivize private label sales.

Current lifecycle status of the loyalty program configuration.
Classification of the loyalty program by its primary business purpose and reward structure.
Minimum number of points a member must accumulate before they are eligible to redeem for rewards.
Conversion rate defining how many points equal one dollar of redemption value (e.g., 100 points = $1.00).
Number of points awarded to the new member who joins the program through a referral link or code.

Expected return on investment percentage target for this loyalty program configuration used for financial planning and performance evaluation.
Web address where the complete legal terms and conditions for this loyalty program version are published.
Cadence at which member tier status is recalculated based on points earned or spend thresholds.
Metric used to determine member tier qualification (current points balance, points earned in period, spend in period, or visit frequency).
Semantic version identifier for this program configuration (e.g., 1.0.0, 2.1.3) to support A/B testing and phased rollouts.
Number of points automatically awarded to new members upon successful enrollment in the loyalty program.
Unique identifier for the loyalty program tier. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to product.product_fuel_grade. Business justification: Business process: Tier discounts are defined per fuel grade; linking tier to product_fuel_grade enables accurate pricing and reporting of fuel discount per gallon.
Annual membership fee in USD required to maintain this tier. Zero for free tiers; positive amount for premium tiers. Used in billing and revenue recognition.
Comprehensive text description of all benefits and privileges associated with this tier. Used in customer communications, marketing materials, and mobile app displays.
Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to members of this tier on their birthday. Used for gamification and customer engagement.
Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this tier (e.g., USD, CAD). Supports multi-currency loyalty programs.
Numeric order in which this tier should be displayed in user interfaces and reports. Used for consistent presentation across channels.
Business rule description explaining conditions under which customers are downgraded from this tier to a lower tier. Includes minimum points requirements and grace periods.
Date when this tier configuration is no longer active for new assignments. Null for currently active tiers. Supports tier retirement and program evolution.
Date when this tier configuration becomes active and available for customer assignment. Supports time-based tier launches and program changes.
Number of days over which points are accumulated and evaluated for tier qualification (e.g., 365 for annual evaluation, 90 for quarterly). Defines the rolling window for tier assignment calculations.
Indicates whether members of this tier receive exclusive promotional offers and early access to sales. True if exclusive offers are a tier benefit; false otherwise.
Indicates whether members of this tier are eligible for free delivery on online orders. True if free delivery is a tier benefit; false otherwise.
Fuel discount in cents per gallon offered to members of this tier at participating fuel centers. Integrates with fuel operations pricing systems.
Number of days customers retain this tier status after falling below the minimum points threshold before being downgraded. Provides buffer period for customers to re-qualify.
URL path to the tier icon image used in digital channels (mobile app, website, email). Supports visual tier recognition in customer-facing applications.
Timestamp when this tier record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
Hexadecimal color code used to represent this tier in marketing materials, mobile apps, and customer communications (e.g., #FFD700 for Gold). Ensures consistent brand presentation.
Maximum number of loyalty points for this tier. Customers earning more than this threshold during the evaluation period qualify for the next higher tier. Null for the highest tier.
Minimum number of loyalty points required to qualify for this tier. Customers must earn at least this many points during the evaluation period to achieve or maintain this tier.
Username or identifier of the user who last modified this tier record. Supports audit trail and accountability for tier configuration changes.
Multiplier applied to base points earned by members of this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base tier, 1.5 for Silver, 2.0 for Gold). Used to calculate accelerated points accrual for higher tiers.
Indicates whether members of this tier receive priority customer service (e.g., dedicated phone line, faster response times). True if priority service is a tier benefit; false otherwise.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., MEMBER, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM). Used as business identifier in external systems and customer communications.
Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Member, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Used in customer-facing applications and marketing materials.

Numeric rank order of the tier within the loyalty program hierarchy. Lower numbers indicate higher status (e.g., 1=Platinum, 2=Gold, 3=Silver, 4=Member). Used for tier comparison and upgrade/downgrade logic.

Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for customer assignment; inactive tiers are no longer offered but may have existing members; deprecated tiers are being phased out; pending tiers are scheduled for future launch.
Business rule description explaining how customers qualify for upgrade to this tier from a lower tier. Includes points thresholds, evaluation periods, and any additional criteria.
Version number of this tier configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change history and rollback capabilities.
Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to customers when they first achieve this tier. Used to incentivize tier upgrades.
Unique identifier for each tier change event in the loyalty program. Primary key for the tier history transactional record.
System user identifier of the customer service representative or administrator who performed the manual tier adjustment. Null for automated tier changes.
Reference to the loyalty program member who experienced this tier change event.
Reference to the omnichannel order that triggered this tier change event, if applicable. Null for in-store or system-initiated tier changes.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Tier changes are often triggered by cumulative spend on payment transactions; linking supports tier‑evaluation reports and automated tier upgrades.
Reference to the specific Point of Sale (POS) transaction that triggered this tier change event, if applicable. Null for system-initiated or manual tier changes.
The date when the new tier status became active and the member began receiving benefits associated with the new tier level.
Precise timestamp when the tier change was applied in the loyalty system, used for real-time benefit activation and audit purposes.
The end date of the qualification period used to evaluate tier eligibility for this change event.
The start date of the qualification period used to evaluate tier eligibility for this change event (e.g., program year start, rolling 12-month window start).
The date when the new tier status is scheduled to expire, typically aligned with program year-end or qualification period end. Null for indefinite tier assignments.
Boolean flag indicating whether this tier change was manually adjusted by customer service or loyalty program administrators rather than automatically triggered by system rules.
Detailed explanation for manual tier adjustments, including customer service case reference, goodwill gesture context, or system error correction details. Null for automated tier changes.
The loyalty tier code the member transitioned to as a result of this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).
Human-readable name of the new loyalty tier for reporting and customer communication.
The communication channel used to notify the member of the tier change: email, SMS (text message), app_push (mobile app notification), in_store (receipt or kiosk), or mail (postal).
Boolean flag indicating whether a tier change notification (email, SMS, app push) was successfully sent to the member.
Timestamp when the tier change notification was sent to the member through Customer Relationship Management (CRM) or loyalty communication channels.
The total loyalty points balance the member held at the exact moment of the tier change event, used for tier progression analytics and member lifecycle tracking.
The loyalty tier code the member held immediately before this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).
Human-readable name of the previous loyalty tier for reporting and customer communication.
The loyalty program year during which this tier change occurred, used for annual tier reset and year-over-year progression analytics.
The cumulative spend amount that qualified the member for this tier change, measured over the qualification period. Null for non-spend-based tier changes.
The number of qualifying transactions completed during the evaluation period that contributed to this tier change. Null for non-transaction-based tier changes.
Timestamp when this tier history record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer, used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Timestamp when this tier history record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer, used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
The operational system that originated this tier change record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, NCR POS Systems, custom loyalty platform).
The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Boolean flag indicating whether all tier-specific benefits (discounts, free delivery, exclusive offers) have been successfully activated in the members profile.
Business-facing unique identifier for this tier change event, used for customer service inquiries and audit trails.

Standardized code indicating why the tier change occurred (e.g., EARNED_UPGRADE for qualifying spend threshold, ANNUAL_RESET for program year rollover, MANUAL_ADJ for customer service adjustment, DEMOTION for inactivity, PROMO_BOOST for promotional tier grant).
Detailed explanation of the business reason for the tier change, including any special circumstances or promotional context.
Current lifecycle status of this tier change record: active (currently in effect), expired (tier period ended), reversed (change was rolled back), or pending (scheduled but not yet effective).

Classification of the tier movement direction: upgrade (tier improvement), downgrade (tier reduction), lateral (same tier level but different program), initial_enrollment (first tier assignment), or reset (annual program reset).
Bonus loyalty points awarded to the member as a welcome gift upon achieving the new tier level. Zero or null if no bonus was granted.
