Batch identifier grouping multiple partner earn transactions for consolidated settlement processing.
Date when financial settlement was completed between the loyalty program and partner.
Financial settlement status between the loyalty program and the partner for this earn transaction.
Unique identifier for the loyalty points account. Primary key for the points account ledger.
Reference to the loyalty membership that owns this points account. Each membership may have multiple accounts (points, fuel rewards).
Externally visible unique account number displayed to members on statements, mobile app, and at POS. Human-readable identifier for customer service interactions.

Current lifecycle status of the points account. Active accounts allow earn and redemption; suspended accounts block transactions pending review; frozen accounts prevent redemptions but allow earning; closed accounts are permanently inactive.

Type of loyalty currency tracked in this account. Points accounts track standard loyalty points; fuel reward accounts track cents-per-gallon discounts; bonus and promotional accounts track time-limited currencies.
Date when the points account was activated and became eligible for earning and redemption. May differ from creation date if account required verification or approval.

Current balance of loyalty currency available for immediate redemption. Excludes pending and expired amounts. This is the authoritative SSOT for real-time balance inquiries at POS, digital channels, and fuel pumps.
Date when the points account was permanently closed. Null for active accounts. Closed accounts cannot be reopened; members must create new accounts.
Reason code for account closure. Used for churn analysis, compliance reporting, and member service quality tracking. Null for active accounts.
Timestamp when the points account record was first created in the system. Used for account age analysis, audit trails, and data lineage tracking.
Code representing the loyalty currency unit. PTS=points, FPG=fuel points per gallon, CPG=cents per gallon, USD=cashback dollars. Aligns with program currency definitions.
Current earning rate multiplier applied to this account. Base rate is 1.0; tier upgrades or promotions may increase to 1.5x, 2.0x, etc. Applied to all qualifying purchases.
End date of the current earning period window. Points earned during this period may have specific expiration rules or tier qualification impact.
Start date of the current earning period window. Used for time-bound promotions, seasonal campaigns, and rolling expiration calculations.

Code identifying the expiration policy applied to this account. References program rules for time-based expiration, rolling windows, or activity-based extension. Used for automated expiration processing.
Account identifier from the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation, data integration, and troubleshooting. May differ from the lakehouse primary key.
Timestamp when the fraud hold was placed on the account. Used for hold duration tracking and compliance with fraud investigation timelines. Null if no hold is active.
Indicates whether the account is under fraud review hold. When true, all earn and redemption transactions are blocked pending investigation. Set by fraud detection systems.
Indicates whether loyalty currency from this account can be combined with other payment methods or loyalty currencies in a single transaction. Used for POS redemption logic.
Indicates whether loyalty currency in this account can be transferred to another member or household account. Typically false for standard points, may be true for family pooling programs.
Date of the most recent account activity of any type (earn, redemption, adjustment, inquiry). Used for dormancy rules, account closure policies, and member lifecycle management.
Date of the most recent loyalty currency earning transaction. Used for inactivity tracking, dormancy detection, and member re-engagement campaigns.
Date of the most recent loyalty currency redemption transaction. Used for engagement analysis, redemption velocity tracking, and member behavior segmentation.
Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in this points account record. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and data freshness monitoring.
Total cumulative amount of loyalty currency adjusted due to corrections, reversals, goodwill credits, or fraud chargebacks. Can be positive or negative. Used for audit and reconciliation.
Total cumulative amount of loyalty currency earned since account inception. Never decreases. Used for tier qualification, member recognition, and lifetime value analytics.
Total cumulative amount of loyalty currency that has expired due to inactivity or time-based expiration policies. Used for breakage analysis and program liability management.
Total cumulative amount of loyalty currency redeemed since account inception. Never decreases. Used for program ROI analysis and member engagement metrics.
Maximum amount of loyalty currency that can be redeemed within a 24-hour period. Used for fraud prevention and program liability management.

Maximum amount of loyalty currency that can be redeemed in a single transaction. For fuel rewards, this typically represents maximum gallons eligible for discount (e.g., 20 gallons). Enforced at POS and fuel pump.
Amount of loyalty currency scheduled to expire on the next expiration date. Used for proactive member engagement campaigns to encourage redemption before expiration.
Date when the next batch of loyalty currency is scheduled to expire. Used for member communication, expiration alerts, and breakage forecasting. Null if no expiration policy applies.
Balance of loyalty currency earned but not yet confirmed or posted to available balance. Typically represents transactions awaiting settlement, return windows, or fraud review periods.
Operational system of record that created and maintains this points account. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and system migration tracking.
Total loyalty currency earned in the current calendar year. Resets to zero on January 1. Used for annual tier qualification and member performance tracking.
Total loyalty currency redeemed in the current calendar year. Resets to zero on January 1. Used for redemption velocity analysis and program engagement metrics.
Unique identifier for the loyalty points transaction. Primary key for the points transaction ledger.

Identifier of the system user or customer service representative who processed this transaction. Populated for manual adjustments, customer service interventions, and call center transactions. Null for automated system transactions. Used for SOX compliance audit trail.

Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_item. Business justification: Points earned per transaction are often SKU‑specific; linking to assortment_item supports points‑earned‑by‑SKU reporting for loyalty program analytics.
Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Required for points‑fraud audit reports linking specific points transactions to audit findings.

Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_center. Business justification: Required for loyalty points transaction fuel discount reporting per fuel center, enabling the Loyalty Discount Utilization Report used by program managers.

Foreign key linking to product.product_fuel_grade. Business justification: Business process: Fuel purchase points and discount amounts depend on the fuel grade bought; linking transaction to product_fuel_grade allows correct points accrual and audit.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Accounting process posts each points transaction to a GL account for expense/revenue recognition; required for financial statements.
Reference to the loyalty membership account that owns this transaction.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Required for Points‑Earned per Transaction report, linking each points transaction to its originating payment transaction for audit and financial reconciliation.
Foreign key linking to pharmacy.prescription. Business justification: Needed to attribute earned points to the specific prescription fill for audit, tier‑based discounts, and points‑earned reporting.

Reference to the originating order or purchase transaction that triggered this points movement. Populated for earn and redeem transactions tied to a specific order. Null for non-purchase transactions (expire, adjust, bonus).

Reference to the promotional campaign or offer that triggered this points movement. Used for bonus points, promotional earn multipliers, and targeted reward offers. Null for standard earn/redeem transactions.

Reference to another points transaction that this transaction is related to. Used for reversal transactions (points to the original transaction being reversed), transfer transactions (links sender and receiver transactions), and adjustment transactions (links to the transaction being corrected). Null for standalone transactions.
Reference to the store location where the transaction occurred. Populated for POS, pharmacy, and fuel center transactions. Null for eCommerce and partner transactions.

Reference to the external partner or coalition program involved in this transaction. Used for co-branded loyalty programs, airline miles transfers, and third-party reward integrations. Null for internal Grocery loyalty transactions.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.wave. Business justification: Needed for Operational Efficiency Analysis tying points earned to the specific picking wave that fulfilled the order, used in performance dashboards.

Quantity of loyalty currency moved in this transaction. Positive for accruals (earn, bonus, transfer-in), negative for deductions (redeem, expire, adjust-down, transfer-out). For points currency this is the point count; for fuel currency this is cents-per-gallon discount value.

Identifier of the batch processing job that created this transaction record. Used for bulk operations such as nightly expiration processing, monthly tier bonus awards, and partner point transfers. Null for real-time individual transactions. Supports operational troubleshooting and reprocessing scenarios.

Indicates whether this transaction is eligible for breakage revenue recognition under ASC 606. True for earned points that may expire unused, allowing the company to recognize a portion of deferred revenue as breakage. False for redeemed, expired, or adjusted points. Used in financial reporting and actuarial breakage analysis.

Timestamp when this transaction record was first created in the loyalty system. Distinct from transaction_timestamp which represents the business event time. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and troubleshooting data latency issues. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Type of loyalty currency involved in this transaction. Points are the primary loyalty currency; fuel cents-per-gallon represents fuel discount rewards earned through the loyalty program.

Date when the loyalty currency from this transaction will expire if not used. Populated for earn and bonus transactions based on program expiration policy (e.g., points expire 12 months from earn date). Used to calculate points-expiring-soon alerts and ASC 606 breakage analysis. Null for redeem, expire, and adjust transactions. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

Total dollar value of fuel discount applied at the pump, calculated as gallons dispensed multiplied by cents-per-gallon reward rate. Used for financial reconciliation and revenue recognition. Populated only for fuel redemptions. Null for all other transaction types.

Current state of the reward fulfillment for redeem transactions. Pending indicates redemption request received but not yet processed, fulfilled indicates reward successfully delivered, failed indicates technical or business rule failure, reversed indicates redemption was cancelled and points restored, cancelled indicates customer or system cancelled before fulfillment. Null for non-redemption transactions.

Number of gallons of fuel dispensed for fuel redemption transactions. Used to calculate the total dollar discount applied (gallons × cents-per-gallon). Populated only for fuel center redemptions. Null for all other transaction types.

Timestamp when this transaction record was last updated. Used to track changes to fulfillment status, corrections to transaction details, and audit trail for SOX compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Standardized code explaining why this transaction occurred. Used primarily for adjust, expire, reversal, and bonus transactions. Examples: ADJ-001 (customer service goodwill), EXP-POLICY (standard expiration), REV-RETURN (order return), BONUS-TIER (tier anniversary bonus). Supports audit trail and customer service inquiry.

Free-text explanation of the transaction reason, providing additional context beyond the reason code. Used for customer service notes, adjustment justifications, and audit documentation. Particularly important for manual adjustments and reversals.
Receipt or transaction number from the Point of Sale (POS) system or eCommerce order confirmation. Used for customer service lookup and reconciliation with financial systems.

How the redemption was triggered. Automatic redemptions apply rewards without customer action (e.g., auto-apply fuel discount), manual redemptions require cashier or customer service intervention, customer-initiated redemptions are explicitly requested by the member (e.g., selecting reward in mobile app), system-initiated redemptions are triggered by business rules (e.g., expiring points converted to charity donation). Populated only for redeem transactions.

Specific method of reward redemption for redeem transactions. Points-for-discount applies points as basket discount at checkout, free item claims a specific product reward, fuel cents-per-gallon applies fuel discount at pump, pharmacy copay reduction lowers prescription cost, charity donation converts points to charitable contribution, partner transfer moves points to external program. Null for non-redemption transactions.

Indicates whether this transaction is a reversal of a previous transaction. True for reversal transactions that cancel or correct prior points movements (e.g., order return, redemption cancellation). False for all original transactions. Used to filter reversals from financial reporting and customer lifetime value calculations.

Cumulative balance of loyalty currency in the membership account after this transaction is applied. Provides point-in-time snapshot for audit trail and customer service inquiry. Calculated as prior balance plus/minus transaction amount.

Channel or system where the transaction originated. POS (Point of Sale) for in-store purchases, eCommerce for online orders, pharmacy for prescription fulfillment, fuel center for fuel pump transactions, mobile app for app-based activities, call center for customer service adjustments, partner API for third-party integrations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pos|ecommerce|pharmacy|fuel_center|mobile_app|call_center|partner_api — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Name of the operational system that originated this transaction record. Examples: NCR-POS-LOYALTY, SFCC-ECOMMERCE, MCKESSON-PHARMACY, FUEL-CENTER-SYSTEM, CRM-MANUAL-ADJUST. Used for data lineage, troubleshooting, and cross-system reconciliation.

Unique identifier of this transaction in the originating source system. Used for end-to-end traceability, reconciliation with source systems, and troubleshooting data quality issues. Enables lookup of original transaction details in NCR POS, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, or other source systems.

Dollar value of tax impact associated with this loyalty transaction. Used for financial accounting when loyalty redemptions affect taxable sales (e.g., fuel discount reduces taxable fuel revenue). Supports ASC 606 revenue recognition and tax compliance reporting. Null for transactions with no tax impact.
reference: ASC 606 Revenue Recognition, IRS Tax Compliance

description: Business-facing unique transaction number for customer service reference and audit trail. Externally visible identifier for this points movement.
Date and time when the loyalty currency movement occurred in the source system. This is the business event time, not the ETL load time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Type of loyalty currency movement: earn (points accrual from purchase or activity), redeem (points used for reward), expire (points removed due to expiration policy), adjust (manual correction by customer service), transfer (points moved between accounts), bonus (promotional or tier bonus points), reversal (cancellation of prior transaction). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: earn|redeem|expire|adjust|transfer|bonus|reversal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Unique identifier for the loyalty reward redemption transaction. Primary key.
Employee identifier of the cashier who processed the in-store redemption. Null for online/self-service redemptions.
Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_item. Business justification: Redemption Impact Report requires tying each redemption to the exact assortment_item for margin and inventory analysis.
Reference to the fuel center location where fuel discount was redeemed. Null for non-fuel redemptions.
Reference to the loyalty membership that performed this redemption.
Reference to the omnichannel order (delivery or pickup) where the redemption was applied. Null for in-store POS transactions.
External transaction identifier from the Point of Sale (POS) or Order Management System (OMS) where the redemption occurred. Links to NCR POS or Salesforce Commerce Cloud transaction.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Reference to the pharmacy prescription where copay reduction was applied. Null for non-pharmacy redemptions. Contains protected health information.
Reference to the marketing campaign that generated the redeemed offer. Null for standard points redemptions.
Reference to the specific personalized reward offer or promotion that was redeemed. Null if redemption was a standard points-for-discount conversion.
Reference to the store location where the redemption occurred. Null for online-only redemptions.
Total transaction amount after the loyalty redemption discount was applied.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Total transaction amount before the loyalty redemption discount was applied. Provides context for redemption impact.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud

description: Channel or interface where the redemption occurred: in-store POS, online web, mobile app, fuel center, pharmacy, or curbside pickup.
Timestamp when this redemption record was first created in the loyalty system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the redemption value amount.
Type of device used to process the redemption: POS terminal, web browser, mobile app, in-store kiosk, or fuel pump interface.
Expiration date of the redeemed offer or reward. Null for non-expiring points redemptions. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Explanation of why the redemption failed or was reversed. Null for successful redemptions.
Cents-per-gallon discount applied to fuel purchase. Null for non-fuel redemptions.
Number of gallons of fuel purchased when fuel discount was applied. Null for non-fuel redemptions.

Current status of the redemption fulfillment: completed (successfully applied), pending (awaiting confirmation), failed (could not be applied), reversed (refunded/cancelled), or expired (offer expired before use).
Indicates whether this redemption was part of a gamification event or challenge (True) or a standard redemption (False).
Timestamp when this redemption record was last updated (e.g., status change, reversal). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether this redemption event qualifies the member for a Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey (True/False).
Member's loyalty points balance immediately after this redemption was processed.
Member's loyalty points balance immediately before this redemption was processed.
Number of loyalty points deducted from the member's balance for this redemption. Zero if redemption was a non-points reward (e.g., free item offer).

How the redemption was initiated: member-initiated (customer selected), auto-applied (system automatically applied best offer), cashier-applied (store associate applied), or system-triggered (rule-based application).
Date and time when the loyalty reward was redeemed at checkout. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Category of loyalty reward redeemed: points-for-discount (points converted to dollar discount), free item (product reward), fuel cents-per-gallon (fuel center discount), pharmacy copay reduction (prescription discount), percentage off, or dollar off.
Point of Sale (POS) register or terminal number where the redemption was processed. Null for online redemptions.
Business reason for reversing the redemption (e.g., transaction void, return, system error). Null if not reversed.
Date and time when the redemption was reversed (points restored, discount removed). Null if not reversed.
Stock Keeping Unit identifier of the product associated with the redemption (for free item or product-specific discount redemptions). Null for non-product redemptions.
Loyalty tier code of the member at the time of redemption. Captured for historical analysis of tier-based redemption behavior.
Monetary value of the redemption in local currency (dollar discount applied, fuel savings, copay reduction, or retail value of free item).
Unique identifier for the member offer assignment record. Primary key for this association entity linking a loyalty member to a specific reward offer.

Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_item. Business justification: Targeted offer assignment process uses member_offer to apply offers to specific SKUs; linking to assortment_item enables SKU‑level offer performance tracking.
Reference to the loyalty membership record. Identifies which member this offer is assigned to.
Reference to the Point of Sale (POS) transaction where the offer was redeemed. Links the offer redemption to the specific purchase event.
Reference to the marketing campaign that generated this offer assignment. Null if the offer was not part of a campaign. Enables campaign-level performance tracking.
Reference to the reward offer definition. Identifies which offer has been assigned to this member.
Store location where the offer was redeemed. Enables store-level redemption analytics and location-based offer performance tracking.
Channel through which the member activated the offer. Tracks which touchpoint the member used to clip or engage with the offer.
Date when the member activated or clipped the offer. Null if the offer has not been activated yet. Represents member engagement with the offer.
Precise timestamp when the member activated the offer. Used for detailed engagement tracking and time-to-activation analytics.
Channel through which the offer was assigned to the member. Indicates the touchpoint where the offer was delivered or made available.
Date when the offer was assigned to the member. Represents when the offer became available for this member to view or activate.

Source or method by which the offer was assigned. Distinguishes between targeted offers pushed by the system, offers selected by the member, tier-based automatic benefits, and other assignment mechanisms.
Precise timestamp when the offer was assigned to the member. Used for detailed tracking and sequencing of offer assignments.
Indicates whether this offer is automatically applied at Point of Sale (POS) without requiring member activation. True for automatic offers, false for clip-to-card offers.
Timestamp when this member offer assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Dollar value of the discount applied when the offer was redeemed. Null if not redeemed or if the offer is points-based rather than discount-based. Represents actual savings to the member.
Date when this member offer assignment expires. After this date, the member can no longer redeem the offer. May differ from the base offer expiration if member-specific extensions apply.
Timestamp when the member first viewed this offer. Null if the member has not viewed the offer. Used to calculate time-to-activation and engagement velocity.
Timestamp when this member offer assignment record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to any field in this record.
Timestamp when the member most recently viewed this offer. Tracks ongoing member interest and re-engagement patterns.
Current lifecycle status of the member offer assignment. Tracks progression from loaded to card, through activation, redemption, or expiration.
Channel used to notify the member about this offer. Null or none if no notification was sent. Enables channel effectiveness comparison.
Indicates whether a notification was sent to the member about this offer assignment. True if notification was sent, false otherwise.
Timestamp when the notification about this offer was sent to the member. Null if no notification was sent. Supports notification effectiveness analysis.

Algorithmic score representing how well this offer matches the member's predicted preferences and purchase behavior. Higher scores indicate better personalization. Used for offer targeting optimization.
Number of loyalty points awarded to the member upon redemption of this offer. Null if the offer has not been redeemed or if the offer does not award points.
Priority ranking of this offer relative to other offers assigned to the same member. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for offer display ordering and conflict resolution.
Date when the member redeemed the offer through a qualifying purchase. Null if the offer has not been redeemed yet.
Precise timestamp when the offer was redeemed. Matches the transaction timestamp for the qualifying purchase.
Date when the offer was revoked or removed from the member's account. Null if the offer has not been revoked. Used for fraud prevention or policy violations.
Explanation for why the offer was revoked from the member. Null if not revoked. Supports compliance and customer service inquiries.
Indicates whether this offer can be combined with other offers in the same transaction. True if stackable, false if mutually exclusive.
Customer segment or cohort used to target this offer to the member. Identifies the segmentation strategy that resulted in this assignment.
Number of times the member viewed this offer in digital channels. Used to measure offer visibility and member interest before activation.
Primary key for the offer_category association
Foreign key linking to category
Foreign key linking to reward_offer
Store where this reward offer is applicable for the linked category

Comma-separated list of product category codes eligible for this offer. Null if offer applies to specific SKUs or all products. [Moved from reward_offer: List of categories an offer applies to belongs to the relationship, not to the offer itself]
Comma-separated list of store IDs where offer is valid. Null if valid at all locations. [Moved from reward_offer: List of stores an offer applies to belongs to the relationship]

business_glossary_term: Offer Tender Eligibility - Offer Tender Eligibility Id
description: Primary key for the offer_tender_eligibility association

business_glossary_term: Offer Tender Eligibility - Reward Offer Id
description: Foreign key linking to reward_offer

business_glossary_term: Offer Tender Eligibility - Tender Type Id
description: Foreign key linking to tender_type
Discount amount or percentage specific to the offer‑tender combination
Indicates whether the reward_offer is applicable to the tender_type
Unique identifier for the loyalty reward offer. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Offer redemption costs are charged against a predefined budget; required for offer ROI and budget variance reporting.

Foreign key linking to product.product_fuel_grade. Business justification: Business process: Fuel‑related reward offers apply to specific fuel grades; the FK identifies the grade for eligibility checks and reporting.
Identifier linking this offer to a broader marketing campaign or promotional initiative. Null if standalone offer.
Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Supplier‑sponsored reward offers need a supplier reference to manage budgets, compliance, and performance metrics per supplier.
Indicates whether offer is automatically applied at checkout when conditions are met (True) or requires manual activation (False).
Finance cost center code responsible for funding this reward offer for budget tracking and ROI analysis.
Timestamp when the reward offer record was first created in the system.
Running total of redemptions that have occurred for this offer across all members.
Type of discount applied when offer is redeemed. BOGO = Buy One Get One. Null if not a discount offer.
Numeric value of the discount (percentage or dollar amount depending on discount_type). Null if not applicable.
Comma-separated list of purchase channels where offer can be redeemed (e.g., in-store, online, mobile app, pickup, delivery).
Comma-separated list of specific SKU codes eligible for this offer. Null if offer applies to categories or all products.
Comma-separated list of loyalty membership tiers eligible for this offer (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum). Null if available to all tiers.
Date when the reward offer expires and is no longer available for activation or redemption.
Projected financial cost to the business for each redemption of this offer including discounts, points liability, and operational costs.
Comma-separated list of SKU codes explicitly excluded from this offer even if they fall within eligible categories.
Discount amount in cents per gallon applied at fuel centers when offer is redeemed. Null if not a fuel reward offer.
Name of digital badge or achievement unlocked when offer is completed. Null if not part of gamification program.
Timestamp when the reward offer record was most recently updated.
Maximum number of times a single member can redeem this offer during its validity period. Null for unlimited.
Maximum total number of redemptions allowed across all members for this offer. Null for unlimited.
Defines whether the offer is for earning points, redeeming points, or a combination of both.
Minimum number of qualifying items that must be purchased to activate the offer. Null if no quantity requirement.
Minimum purchase amount required to qualify for the reward offer. Null if no minimum required.
Unique alphanumeric code identifying the reward offer for tracking and redemption purposes.
Detailed description of the reward offer including terms, conditions, and member benefits.
Marketing name of the reward offer displayed to loyalty members.
Current lifecycle status of the reward offer indicating availability to members.
Category of reward offer defining the benefit mechanism provided to the member.
Indicates whether this offer was generated through personalized recommendation engine (True) or is a program-wide offer (False).
Description of pharmacy-specific reward benefit (e.g., discount on prescriptions, health screening credit). Null if not a pharmacy offer.
Fixed number of bonus points awarded when offer conditions are met. Null if offer uses multiplier instead.
Multiplier applied to base points earning rate for qualifying purchases (e.g., 2.0 for double points, 3.0 for triple points). Null if not applicable.
Final date by which activated offers must be redeemed. May extend beyond offer end_date.
Date when members can begin redeeming this offer. May differ from activation date for earn-then-redeem mechanics.
Indicates whether this offer can be combined with other offers or promotions (True) or is mutually exclusive (False).
Date when the reward offer becomes active and available to qualifying members.
Number of sweepstakes entries awarded when offer conditions are met. Null if not a sweepstakes offer.
Target return on investment percentage for this offer based on incremental revenue and customer lifetime value impact.
Business rules defining which member segments are eligible for this offer (e.g., tier, spend level, purchase behavior, demographics).
Full legal terms and conditions governing the reward offer including restrictions, exclusions, and member obligations.

FK to product.brand
FK to store.department
Indicates whether this item requires age verification at checkout (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, certain medications).

description: Average customer rating for this item on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00, calculated from customer reviews.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Reviews and Ratings

description: Hierarchical category path for navigation and filtering (e.g., Grocery > Dairy > Milk > Whole Milk).
Timestamp when this digital catalog item record was first created in the system.
Current availability status of the item in the digital catalog. Controls whether the item can be added to cart.
The date this item was or will be removed from the digital catalog. Null if still active.
The price displayed to customers on the digital storefront. May differ from store-specific pricing.
Detailed product description for the digital catalog, including features, benefits, ingredients, and usage instructions.
The customer-facing title displayed on the digital storefront. Optimized for online presentation and may differ from physical product name.
The date this item was first made available in the digital catalog.
Indicates whether this item can be purchased using Electronic Benefits Transfer payment methods.
Global Trade Item Number assigned by GS1 for worldwide product identification. May be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits.
Alternative text for the hero image, used for accessibility (screen readers) and SEO purposes.
Primary product image URL displayed prominently on product detail pages and search results.
Timestamp when this digital catalog item record was last updated.
Multiplier applied to base loyalty points earned when purchasing this item. Default is 1.0.
reference: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Loyalty Program Rules

description: Minimum age in years required to purchase this item. Null if no age restriction applies.
reference: FDA and State Alcohol Beverage Control Regulations

description: Indicates whether this item is available exclusively through digital channels and not sold in physical stores.
Indicates whether this item carries USDA Organic certification.
The size or quantity of the package as displayed to customers (e.g., 12 oz, 1 gallon, 6-pack).
Indicates whether this item is perishable and requires temperature-controlled handling and expedited delivery.
reference: FDA Food Code and HACCP Guidelines

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the digital display price.
Indicates whether this item is a store-owned brand (private label) product rather than a national Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) brand.
Indicates whether this item can be included in promotional campaigns and discounts.
Comma-separated list of keywords and phrases used to improve internal site search relevance and product discoverability.
HTML meta description tag content that appears in search engine results. Typically 150-160 characters.
HTML meta title tag content optimized for search engine results pages. Typically 50-60 characters.
The physical product SKU that this digital catalog item represents. Links the digital presentation layer to the physical product domain.

The source system that provided this digital catalog item data (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail).
Indicates whether this item can be substituted with an alternative product during order fulfillment if out of stock.
Required storage and transportation temperature zone for this item. Critical for cold chain logistics.

description: Smaller thumbnail image URL used in product listings, carousels, and grid views.
Total number of customer reviews submitted for this item.

description: The unit in which this item is sold (e.g., each, pound, ounce, gallon, liter).
12-digit Universal Product Code for the item, used for barcode scanning and product identification.

description: The planned date and time when the driver is scheduled to complete this delivery route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Planning

description: The planned date and time when the driver is scheduled to begin executing this delivery route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Planning

description: Free-text notes or special instructions for the driver regarding this delivery route, such as traffic alerts or access restrictions.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Communication

description: Business identifier for the delivery route, externally known and used for operational tracking and driver assignment.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Management

description: The name or version of the route optimization algorithm used by JDA TMS to generate this route plan.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Optimization Engine

description: Version number tracking route resequencing events, incremented each time the stop sequence is modified during execution.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Resequencing

description: Current lifecycle status of the delivery route indicating its operational state.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Status Workflow

description: Classification of the delivery route based on the type of fulfillment service being provided.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Classification

description: The delivery service level commitment associated with this route, defining customer expectations for delivery speed.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Service Level Management

description: The temperature control requirement for this delivery route, critical for cold chain compliance in grocery delivery.
The actual duration in minutes taken to complete this delivery route from start to finish.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Execution Tracking

description: The actual distance in miles traveled during the execution of this delivery route, captured from vehicle telematics or GPS.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Execution Tracking

description: The actual number of delivery stops successfully completed on this route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Execution Tracking

description: The number of delivery stops that failed or could not be completed on this route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Execution Tracking

description: The total number of customer orders included in this delivery route across all stops.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Planning

description: The total number of packages or totes loaded onto the vehicle for this delivery route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Load Planning

description: The total planned duration in minutes for completing this delivery route, including drive time and stop dwell time.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Optimization

description: The total distance in miles planned for this delivery route based on route optimization algorithms.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Optimization

description: The total number of delivery stops planned for this route at the time of route creation.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Route Optimization

description: The total volume in cubic feet of all packages and orders on this delivery route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Load Planning

description: The total weight in pounds of all packages and orders on this delivery route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Load Planning

description: Identifier of the vehicle assigned to this delivery route.
reference: JDA Transportation Management System - Fleet Management

description: Unique identifier for the payment gateway configuration record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: PCI‑DSS certification: gateways must be associated with a compliance program to track certification status and audit schedules.
Name of the acquiring bank or financial institution that sponsors the merchant account and settles funds (e.g., JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo Merchant Services).
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 12.8 - Service Provider Management

description: Version of the gateway API currently in use (e.g., v2.1, 2023-10). Critical for integration compatibility and upgrade planning.
Indicates whether the gateway contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term. True = auto-renews, False = requires manual renewal.
Rolling average response time in milliseconds for authorization requests to this gateway over the last 24 hours. Performance metric for gateway selection and monitoring.
Time of day (HH:MM format, 24-hour) when batch settlement is submitted to this gateway. Determines when daily transaction batches are closed and funds are transferred.
Date when the gateway provider's PCI DSS certification or compliance attestation expires. Requires renewal to maintain operational status.
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 12.9 - Annual Compliance Validation

description: Fee charged by the processor per chargeback dispute in settlement currency. Confidential pricing information.
Date when the contract with the gateway provider expires or is scheduled for renewal. Null for open-ended contracts.
Date when the contract with the gateway provider became effective. Marks the beginning of the service agreement.
Timestamp when this gateway configuration record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.
The HTTPS endpoint URL for authorization and settlement API calls. Confidential operational configuration.
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 2.2 - Secure System Configuration

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the gateway configuration (e.g., FIRSTDATA_POS, STRIPE_ECOM, WORLDPAY_FUEL). Used for routing logic and system integration references.
Human-readable name of the payment gateway configuration (e.g., First Data POS Gateway, Stripe eCommerce Gateway).
Current operational status of the gateway configuration. Active gateways are available for transaction routing; inactive/suspended gateways are excluded from routing logic.
Classification of the gateway by channel or use case. Determines which business systems route transactions through this gateway.
Boolean flag indicating whether this gateway is currently active and available for transaction routing. True = active, False = inactive.
Timestamp of the most recent successful health check or connectivity test to the gateway endpoint. Used for monitoring and alerting.
Timestamp when this gateway configuration record was last updated. Audit trail for record lifecycle.
The unique merchant identifier assigned by the processor or acquirer. Used in authorization and settlement messages. Confidential business identifier.
reference: ISO 8583 Field 42 - Card Acceptor Identification Code

description: Fixed monthly fee charged by the gateway provider for access and maintenance in settlement currency. Confidential pricing information.
Free-text field for operational notes, configuration details, or special instructions related to this gateway configuration.

business_glossary_term: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) Compliance Level
description: The PCI DSS compliance level of the gateway provider based on annual transaction volume. Level 1 is highest (6M+ transactions/year), Level 4 is lowest (under 1M transactions/year). Determines audit and validation requirements.

Numeric priority rank for multi-gateway routing logic. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. When multiple gateways support the same tender type, the system routes to the highest priority (lowest rank number) available gateway.
Name of the underlying payment processor or acquirer (e.g., First Data, Worldpay, Stripe, Chase Paymentech, Elavon). The entity that processes authorization and settlement requests.
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 12.8 - Service Provider Management

description: Defines the retry behavior when a gateway request fails or times out. Options: none (no retry), once (single retry), twice (two retries), exponential_backoff (progressive delay retries).
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this gateway settles funds (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN). Determines currency conversion and reconciliation logic.
Number of business days between batch settlement submission and funds availability in the merchant bank account. Typical values: 1-3 days.
Email address for the primary technical support contact at the gateway provider. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.
Name of the primary technical support contact at the gateway provider for incident escalation and operational issues.
Phone number for the primary technical support contact at the gateway provider. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.

Comma-separated list of tender types this gateway can process (e.g., credit_card, debit_card, ebt, snap, wic, gift_card, mobile_wallet, contactless, fuel_rewards). Drives tender routing logic at Point of Sale (POS) and digital checkout.
Indicates whether this gateway supports 3D Secure authentication protocol for card-not-present transactions (e.g., eCommerce). True = 3DS supported, False = not supported.
Indicates whether this gateway supports recurring billing or subscription payment processing. True = recurring billing supported, False = not supported.
Indicates whether this gateway supports payment tokenization for secure storage and reuse of payment credentials. True = tokenization supported, False = not supported.
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 3.2 - Tokenization as Alternative to Storage

description: Prefix used to construct terminal identifiers for Point of Sale (POS) devices routing through this gateway. Enables terminal-level transaction tracking and reconciliation.
reference: ISO 8583 Field 41 - Card Acceptor Terminal Identification

description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a response from the gateway before timing out the authorization request. Critical for customer experience at Point of Sale (POS).
Fixed per-transaction fee charged by the processor in settlement currency (e.g., 0.1000 = $0.10 per transaction). Confidential pricing information.
Percentage-based transaction fee charged by the processor per authorization (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50%). Confidential pricing information used for cost allocation and profitability analysis.
Gateway uptime percentage over the last 30 days (e.g., 99.95). Service level agreement (SLA) performance metric.
Unique identifier for the gift card record. Primary key.
Identifier of the employee who processed the gift card issuance or activation. Used for audit trail and fraud investigation.
Identifier of the customer who purchased the gift card. Nullable for anonymous purchases or third-party bulk orders.
Identifier of the original gift card that this card replaces, in cases of lost, stolen, or damaged cards. Nullable for original issuances.
Identifier of the store location where the gift card was issued or purchased. Nullable for online or bulk purchases.
Current activation lifecycle status of the gift card: pending (issued but not yet activated), activated (ready for use), suspended (temporarily blocked), or closed (permanently deactivated).
The date and time when the gift card was activated and became available for redemption. Nullable for cards that have not yet been activated.
Barcode or UPC (Universal Product Code) printed on physical gift cards for POS scanning. Nullable for digital cards.
Batch or lot number for physical gift cards, used for inventory tracking and recall management. Nullable for digital cards.
The date and time when the gift card record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the gift card monetary value (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
The current available balance on the gift card after all redemptions, reloads, and adjustments. Updated in real-time with each transaction.
The date on which the gift card balance was escheated to state authorities due to dormancy. Nullable for non-escheated cards.

Status of the gift card balance with respect to state escheatment (unclaimed property) laws. Not_applicable for active cards, pending for dormant cards approaching escheatment, escheated for balances transferred to state authorities.
The date on which the gift card expires and can no longer be used for purchases. Nullable for non-expiring cards. Subject to state and federal gift card regulations.
reference: Credit CARD Act of 2009, State Gift Card Laws

description: Indicates whether the gift card has been flagged for suspected fraudulent activity. True if fraud is suspected, False otherwise.
Description of the reason the gift card was flagged for fraud (e.g., stolen card, unauthorized use, suspicious transaction pattern). Nullable if no fraud flag is set.
Type of gift card instrument: physical (plastic card issued in-store or mailed), digital (mobile wallet or app-based), or e-gift (email delivery).
The original monetary value loaded onto the gift card at the time of issuance or first activation. Immutable after creation.
Indicates whether the gift card is currently active and available for use. False if the card has been deactivated, suspended, or closed.
Indicates whether the gift card has passed its expiration date and is no longer valid for redemption. True if expired, False if still valid.
Indicates whether the gift card supports reloading of additional funds after initial issuance. True for reloadable cards, False for single-load cards.
The date the gift card was issued or created in the system. Distinct from activation date.
The date and time when the gift card record was last updated. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.
The date and time of the most recent transaction (redemption, reload, or balance inquiry) on this gift card. Used for dormancy tracking and fraud detection.
Tokenized gift card number for PCI DSS compliance. The actual card number is stored securely in the payment gateway; this token is used for transaction processing and balance inquiries.
Custom message from the purchaser to the recipient, displayed on e-gift cards or included with physical card delivery. Limited to 250 characters.
One-way cryptographic hash of the gift card PIN for secure validation. The actual PIN is never stored in plaintext.
Indicates whether a PIN is required for gift card transactions. True for cards requiring PIN entry at POS or online checkout, False for swipe-only cards.
Name of the gift card program or campaign (e.g., Grocery Gift Card, Holiday Gift Card, Employee Appreciation Card). Used for marketing segmentation and program performance tracking.

The channel through which the gift card was originally purchased: POS (in-store point of sale), ecommerce (online storefront), mobile_app (mobile application), customer_service (phone or chat), bulk_order (corporate or wholesale), or third_party (external retailer or marketplace).
Email address of the gift card recipient for e-gift delivery and balance notifications. Required for digital and e-gift card types.
Phone number of the gift card recipient for SMS delivery and balance inquiry notifications. Optional field for digital card delivery.
Unique identifier for the stored payment method. Primary key for the payment_method entity.

Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: In‑store card enrollment requires the associate who captured the payment method for audit, fraud prevention, and compliance reporting.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_gateway. Business justification: A payment_method is processed through a specific payment gateway; linking via payment_gateway_id enforces consistency and removes the free‑text gateway column.
Reference to the shopper who owns this stored payment credential. Links to the customer domain shopper entity.
Reference to the tender type category (credit card, debit card, EBT, gift card, mobile wallet) that this payment method belongs to.
The most recent AVS result code returned during payment method verification. Used to assess fraud risk and address match quality.
The primary street address line associated with the payment method for billing and Address Verification Service (AVS) checks.
The secondary street address line (apartment, suite, unit) associated with the payment method for billing purposes. Optional field.
The city associated with the billing address for this payment method. Used for AVS verification.
The three-letter ISO country code for the billing address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Used for international payment processing and compliance.
The state or province code associated with the billing address. Used for AVS verification and tax calculation.
The postal code associated with the billing address. Critical for Address Verification Service (AVS) fraud prevention checks.
The first six digits of the payment card number, identifying the issuing bank and card type. Used for routing and fraud detection. Not considered PII per PCI DSS.
The payment card network brand for credit and debit cards (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, JCB, Diners Club). Null for non-card payment methods.

The functional type of payment card: Credit (revolving credit), Debit (linked to bank account), Prepaid (preloaded funds), Charge (full payment required), Unknown (type not determined). Null for non-card payment methods.
The name embossed on the payment card as provided by the cardholder. Used for verification and display purposes.
The channel through which this payment method was originally registered: POS (Point of Sale), eCommerce, Mobile App, Call Center, or Kiosk. Distinct from tender_type.
The date and time when this payment method record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field.
The result of the most recent CVV verification check: Verified (match), Failed (no match), Not Checked (skipped), Not Applicable (non-card payment method).
The date and time when this payment method was soft-deleted by the customer or system. Null for active records. Supports GDPR right to erasure compliance.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) Card Number Token
description: Tokenized EBT card number for SNAP or WIC benefit programs. Complies with federal privacy requirements for benefit program data.

The government assistance program type for EBT cards: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women Infants and Children), TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families). Null for non-EBT payment methods.
The month (1-12) when the payment card expires. Used to validate card validity at transaction time. Null for non-expiring payment methods.
The four-digit year when the payment card expires. Used to validate card validity at transaction time. Null for non-expiring payment methods.
Risk score (0-100) assigned by fraud detection systems based on payment method characteristics, usage patterns, and verification results. Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk.
The current available balance on the gift card in USD. Updated after each transaction. Null for non-gift-card payment methods.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is the shoppers default payment method for transactions. Only one payment method per shopper should be marked as default.
Boolean flag indicating whether this payment method has passed its expiration date and is no longer valid for transactions. Calculated based on expiration_month and expiration_year.
Boolean flag indicating whether this payment method has been successfully verified through AVS, CVV check, or micro-deposit validation.
The name of the financial institution that issued the payment card. Derived from the Bank Identification Number (BIN) during tokenization.
The last four digits of the payment account number, used for customer identification and display purposes without exposing the full PAN.
The date and time when this payment method was last successfully used for a transaction. Used to identify dormant payment methods for cleanup.

Current lifecycle status of the payment method: Active (available for use), Inactive (customer disabled), Suspended (fraud hold), Expired (past expiration date), Pending Verification (awaiting validation), Blocked (permanently disabled).
Optional customer-provided friendly name for this payment method (e.g., My Visa Card, Work Amex) to help identify it in the wallet UI.
PCI DSS compliant tokenized representation of the payment account number (PAN). This token is used in place of the actual card number for secure storage and processing.
The third-party tokenization service provider that generated the payment_method_token (e.g., Fiserv, Worldpay, Stripe, internal vault). Critical for token lifecycle management.
The date and time when this payment method record was last modified. Updated on any field change for audit trail purposes.
The unique identifier in the external payment vault or tokenization system. Used to retrieve or update the tokenized credential from the vault provider.
The date when this payment method was last successfully verified. Used to determine if re-verification is required per security policies.
The digital wallet provider if this payment method is a mobile or digital wallet credential (Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, PayPal, Venmo). Null or None for traditional payment methods.
The tokenized credential provided by the digital wallet provider for secure transaction processing. Distinct from payment_method_token.
Unique identifier for the payment plan record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Financing plans must be approved by a store associate; the approving employee is recorded for regulatory audit and risk management.
Reference to the payment method used for automatic installment payments.
Reference to the customer or wholesale account associated with this payment plan.
Store location where the payment plan was originated, if applicable.
Status of the payment plan application approval process.
Name or identifier of the person or system that approved the payment plan.
Date and time when the payment plan was approved.
Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this plan.
Reason for payment plan cancellation, if applicable.
Date and time when the payment plan was cancelled.
Date and time when the payment plan was fully paid and completed.
Date and time when this payment plan record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this plan.
Number of days the payment plan is overdue, calculated from the most recent missed payment date.
Date and time when the payment plan was marked as defaulted due to non-payment.
Classification of payment plan delinquency based on days past due. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current|30_days|60_days|90_days|120_plus_days|default — promote to reference product]
Total fees charged for the payment plan (origination, service, administrative).
Scheduled date for the final installment payment.
Scheduled date for the first installment payment.
Number of days after a missed payment before late fees are applied or the plan is marked delinquent.
Total interest charged over the life of the payment plan.
Annual percentage rate (APR) applied to the payment plan, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0599 for 5.99%).
Date and time when this payment plan record was last updated.
Standard late fee charged per missed or overdue installment payment.
Date when the payment plan is scheduled to be fully paid and closed.
Amount due for the next scheduled installment payment.
Date when the next installment payment is due.
Free-text notes or comments related to the payment plan for internal reference.
Total number of scheduled installment payments for this plan.
Channel through which the payment plan was initiated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pos|ecommerce|mobile_app|pharmacy|customer_service|wholesale_portal — promote to reference product]
Date when the payment plan was established and became effective.
Remaining unpaid balance on the payment plan.
Frequency at which installment payments are scheduled.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the payment plan, used for customer communication and account management.
Current lifecycle status of the payment plan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|completed|defaulted|cancelled|suspended|pending — promote to reference product]
Classification of the payment plan arrangement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: layaway|pharmacy_payment_assistance|b2b_net_terms|installment_purchase|deferred_payment|other — promote to reference product]
Base amount financed before interest and fees.
Name of the payment assistance or financing program (e.g., Pharmacy Patient Assistance, Wholesale Net-30 Terms).
Organization or entity sponsoring the payment plan program (e.g., pharmaceutical manufacturer, third-party lender).
Cumulative amount paid to date across all installments.
Total amount financed under the payment plan, including principal and any fees.
Unique identifier for the tender type. Primary key for the tender type reference master.
Indicates whether this tender type is accepted for online orders through Salesforce Commerce Cloud. True for credit/debit cards and digital wallets; false for cash and check.
Indicates whether this tender type is accepted at fuel center pay-at-pump terminals. True for credit/debit cards and fleet cards; false for government benefits and gift cards.
reference: Internal Payment Standards, Fuel Center Operations Standards

description: Indicates whether this tender type is accepted for orders placed through the Grocery mobile application. True for digital payment methods; false for cash and check.
Indicates whether this tender type is accepted for pharmacy prescription transactions. True for most tender types; may have restrictions for certain government benefit programs.
reference: HIPAA, State Pharmacy Regulations, Internal Payment Standards

business_glossary_term: Accepted at POS (Point of Sale) Flag
description: Indicates whether this tender type is accepted at in-store NCR POS terminals. True for most tender types; false for online-only payment methods.
Indicates whether this tender type is currently active and available for use across all channels. False indicates the tender type has been discontinued or temporarily suspended.
Indicates whether transactions using this tender type are subject to chargeback disputes initiated by cardholders. True for credit/debit cards; false for cash, check, and gift cards.
reference: PCI DSS, Payment Network Chargeback Rules

description: Timestamp when this tender type record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for this tender type. Typically USD for US operations. Supports multi-currency scenarios for international expansion.
Numeric sequence controlling the display order of tender types in POS and eCommerce checkout interfaces. Lower numbers appear first. Used to prioritize preferred payment methods.
Date when this tender type was or will be discontinued. Null for currently active tender types with no planned end date. Supports sunset of deprecated payment methods.
Date when this tender type became or will become available for use. Supports future-dated activation of new payment methods.

Code identifying product eligibility restrictions for this tender type. Used for government benefit programs (SNAP/WIC) that restrict purchases to approved product categories. Null for unrestricted tender types.
reference: USDA SNAP Regulations, State WIC Program Standards

description: Indicates whether this tender type supports contactless payment via NFC (Near Field Communication) or RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology. True for Apple Pay, Google Pay, and contactless cards.
Timestamp when this tender type record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Updated whenever any attribute value changes.
Maximum transaction amount allowed for this tender type per transaction. Null if no maximum applies. Used for gift cards, fuel rewards, and certain government benefit programs.
reference: Internal Payment Standards, USDA SNAP Regulations

description: Minimum transaction amount required to use this tender type. Null if no minimum applies. Used for certain credit card merchant agreements or promotional tender types.
reference: Payment Network Standards, Internal Payment Standards

description: Payment processing network or gateway used to authorize and settle transactions for this tender type. Examples: Visa, Mastercard, Discover, American Express, NYCE, STAR, Pulse, FIS, First Data, EBT Network.
Indicates whether refunds can be issued back to this tender type. True for most tender types; false for certain government benefit programs with regulatory restrictions.
reference: Internal Payment Standards, USDA SNAP Regulations

description: Code identifying the government regulatory program associated with this tender type. Examples: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women Infants and Children), EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer). Null for non-government tender types.
reference: USDA SNAP Regulations, State WIC Program Standards

description: Indicates whether this tender type requires PIN entry for authorization. True for debit cards, EBT, and some gift cards. False for credit cards and contactless wallets.

Indicates whether this tender type requires customer signature for authorization above certain transaction thresholds. True for credit cards above signature threshold; false for PIN-based and contactless payments.

Indicates whether transactions using this tender type are included in end-of-day settlement batch processing. True for electronic payment methods; false for cash and check which are reconciled separately.
Transaction amount threshold above which signature is required for this tender type. Null if signature is never required or always required regardless of amount.
reference: Payment Network Standards, Internal Payment Standards

description: Indicates whether this tender type can be used for partial payment of a transaction total, allowing split tender scenarios. True for most tender types; false for some government benefit programs with restrictions.

High-level classification grouping similar tender types for reporting and analytics. Categories: card (credit/debit), electronic_wallet (mobile wallets), government_benefit (EBT/SNAP/WIC), gift_card, fuel_reward, cash, check. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: card|electronic_wallet|government_benefit|gift_card|fuel_reward|cash|check — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tender type across all systems. Used by NCR POS and Salesforce Commerce Cloud for payment routing. Examples: CC, DC, EBT, SNAP, WIC, GPAY, APAY, GC, FUEL, CHK, CASH.
Detailed description of the tender type including usage guidelines, restrictions, and business rules. Provides context for associates and system integrators.
Full business name of the tender type displayed to customers and associates. Examples: Credit Card, Debit Card, EBT/SNAP, WIC, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Gift Card, Fuel Rewards, Check, Cash.
Indicates whether this tender type supports payment tokenization for secure storage and recurring transactions. True for credit/debit cards and digital wallets; false for cash and check.
Unique identifier for the payment chargeback record. Primary key.
Reference to the employee or user responsible for managing and resolving this chargeback case.
Reference to the original payment transaction that is being disputed.
Reference to the order associated with the disputed payment transaction.
Reference to the store location where the disputed transaction occurred.
Foreign key linking to supply.vendor_chargeback. Business justification: Chargeback alignment: link payment‑side chargeback records to supplier‑side chargebacks for unified dispute tracking.
Reference number assigned by the acquiring bank or payment processor for tracking the chargeback.
Last four digits of the payment card number for identification purposes, compliant with PCI DSS masking requirements.
Type of payment card involved in the disputed transaction.
Internal case tracking number assigned by Grocery for chargeback management and resolution workflow.

Current lifecycle status of the chargeback case through the dispute resolution process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|under_review|representment_submitted|won|lost|withdrawn|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Timestamp when this chargeback record was first created in the Grocery system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the dispute amount and fees.
Indicates whether Grocery attempted to contact the cardholder to resolve the dispute before representment.
The monetary amount being disputed by the cardholder, typically matching the original transaction amount.
High-level classification of the chargeback type for reporting and analysis purposes.
Indicates whether supporting evidence has been submitted to contest the chargeback.
Administrative fee charged by the payment processor or card network for processing the chargeback.
Indicates whether the chargeback is classified as a fraud-related dispute.
Unique identifier for the issuing bank, typically the Bank Identification Number (BIN) or Issuer Identification Number (IIN).
Name of the financial institution that issued the payment card and initiated the chargeback.
Timestamp when this chargeback record was last updated, tracking the most recent change to case status or details.
Total financial impact to Grocery including dispute amount, fees, and any recovery, calculated as (dispute_amount + chargeback_fee - recovery_amount).
Free-text notes and comments from chargeback analysts regarding case investigation, evidence, and resolution strategy.
Authorization code from the original payment transaction, used to match and validate the chargeback claim.
Date when the original payment transaction that is being disputed occurred.
Standardized code provided by the card network indicating the reason for the chargeback (e.g., fraud, authorization issue, processing error, cardholder dispute).
reference: Visa Chargeback Reason Codes, Mastercard Chargeback Reason Codes

description: Detailed explanation of the chargeback reason provided by the cardholder or issuing bank.
Date when Grocery was notified of the chargeback by the payment processor or acquiring bank.
Precise timestamp when the chargeback notification was received in Grocery systems.
Amount recovered by Grocery if the chargeback was won or partially reversed.
Unique reference number assigned by the card issuer or payment network to identify this chargeback case.
Number of times Grocery has submitted representment evidence for this chargeback case.
Final deadline for submitting representment evidence as specified by the card network rules.
Date when Grocery submitted representment evidence to contest the chargeback.
Date when the chargeback case was resolved with a final decision (won, lost, or withdrawn).
Final outcome of the chargeback dispute indicating whether Grocery successfully defended the transaction.
Precise timestamp when the chargeback case reached final resolution.
Deadline by which Grocery must submit a response or representment to contest the chargeback.
Unique identifier for the fraud case record. Primary key.
Identifier of the loss prevention or fraud analyst assigned to investigate this case.
Identifier of the associated chargeback record if a chargeback was filed. Null if no chargeback filed.
Identifier of the payment method (card, account) involved in the fraud case.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Obligation tracking: each fraud case fulfills a specific compliance obligation to investigate and remediate fraud, supporting obligation‑based reporting.
Identifier of the payment transaction that triggered the fraud alert. May be null if case originated from pattern analysis.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Required for Fraud Case Compliance Program Reporting: links each fraud case to the compliance program governing anti‑fraud policies, enabling program‑level metrics.
Identifier of the customer or shopper account associated with the suspected fraud activity.
Identifier of the store location where the fraudulent transaction occurred. Null for ecommerce or mobile transactions.
Indicates whether the customer account or payment method was blocked as a result of this fraud case.
Severity level assigned to the fraud alert based on risk scoring and detection confidence.
Type of real-time fraud detection alert that triggered the case investigation.
Date and time when the fraud case was closed or resolved. Null if case is still open.
Business-facing unique case number assigned to the fraud investigation for external reference and tracking.
Date and time when the fraud case was first opened and investigation initiated.
Current lifecycle status of the fraud case investigation.
Sales channel through which the fraudulent transaction was attempted or completed.
Indicates whether a chargeback was filed with the card issuer or payment processor related to this fraud case.
Total dollar amount of confirmed fraudulent transactions after investigation completion. Null if investigation is ongoing.
System timestamp when this fraud case record was first created in the database.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fraud amounts.
System or channel that originated the fraud detection alert or case.
Unique identifier of the device used for the transaction, generated by device fingerprinting technology.
Final determination or outcome classification of the fraud investigation.
Detailed investigator notes documenting findings, evidence reviewed, and rationale for the case disposition.
Numerical risk score (0-100) assigned by the fraud detection system at the time of alert generation.
Classification of the fraud incident based on the method or pattern detected.
Latitude coordinate of the transaction location based on IP geolocation or device GPS.
Longitude coordinate of the transaction location based on IP geolocation or device GPS.
Priority level assigned to the case based on financial impact, risk, and resource availability.
Internet Protocol address from which the suspicious transaction originated. Applicable to digital channels.
System timestamp when this fraud case record was last updated.
Name of the law enforcement agency to which the case was referred. Null if not referred.
External case number assigned by law enforcement agency. Null if not referred or not yet assigned.
Indicates whether the case was referred to law enforcement authorities for criminal investigation.
Net financial loss to the business after recovery efforts, calculated as confirmed fraud amount minus recovery amount.
Indicates whether this fraud case constitutes a PCI DSS security incident requiring formal reporting to payment brands and acquirers.
Dollar amount recovered from the fraudulent transaction through chargebacks, insurance claims, or other recovery mechanisms.
Number of other fraud cases linked to this case through common patterns, accounts, or devices.
Total dollar amount of suspected fraudulent transactions associated with this case at the time of case opening.
