Date selected by the shopper for order fulfillment (delivery or pickup). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud Order Management System (OMS)

description: End time of the selected fulfillment time window (e.g., 12:00 PM for a 10:00-12:00 delivery slot). Format: HH:mm.
Start time of the selected fulfillment time window (e.g., 10:00 AM for a 10:00-12:00 delivery slot). Format: HH:mm.
Identifier of the source system that created or owns this cart record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Mobile App, Kiosk).
Free-text field for shopper to provide special instructions for fulfillment (e.g., gate code, ripe bananas, contactless delivery).

Shopper preference for item substitutions when items are out of stock (OOS). Allow_all permits any substitute, allow_similar permits category-matched substitutes, contact_me requires shopper approval, no_substitutions rejects all substitutes.
Total number of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) in the cart. Does not account for quantity per SKU, only unique items.
Total quantity of all items in the cart, accounting for quantities of each SKU. Supports fractional quantities for weight-based items (e.g., produce, deli).
Unique identifier for each line item within a digital shopping cart. Primary key for the cart_item product.
Reference to the parent shopping cart that contains this item. Links to the cart header entity.
Reference to the promotion applied to this cart item. Links to the promotion master for details on discount rules, funding source, and promotion terms.
Unique session identifier for the browsing session during which this item was added. Links cart activity to broader session behavior for journey analytics.
Timestamp when this item was added to the cart. Critical for cart abandonment analysis, session analytics, and time-in-cart metrics.
Indicates whether this item requires age verification at checkout. Includes alcohol, tobacco, and certain over-the-counter medications.
Indicates whether this item is part of a buy-one-get-one promotion. Supports promotional compliance and basket analysis.
Product category code within the department hierarchy. Supports assortment planning and category management analytics.
Digital or manufacturer coupon code applied to this specific item. Captured for redemption tracking and vendor chargeback processing.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this cart item. Typically USD for US operations.
Retail department code for merchandising classification. Used for category performance analysis and store layout optimization.
Type of device used to add this item to cart. Enables device-specific user experience optimization and responsive design analytics.
Estimated weight of this cart item in pounds. Used for delivery route planning, vehicle capacity planning, and shipping cost estimation for variable-weight items.
Total price for this cart line calculated as quantity requested multiplied by unit price, before any item-level promotions or discounts are applied.
Global trade item number assigned by GS1 for worldwide product identification. May be 8, 12, 13, or 14 digits depending on product type.
Total discount amount applied to this specific cart item from promotions, coupons, or temporary price reductions. Reduces the extended price to arrive at net item amount.
Current status of this item within the cart lifecycle. Tracks whether item is active in cart, removed by customer, saved for later purchase, unavailable, or substituted during fulfillment.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cart item. Tracks quantity changes, substitution preference updates, or special instruction edits.
Sequential line number indicating the order in which items were added to the cart. Used for display ordering and cart recovery workflows.
Indicates whether this item qualifies for loyalty points accrual. Some categories like alcohol, tobacco, and pharmacy may be excluded from loyalty programs.
Net amount for this cart line after item-level discounts are applied. Calculated as extended price minus item discount amount.
Indicates whether this item is certified organic. Supports customer preference tracking and organic category performance analysis.
Indicates whether this item is perishable and requires temperature-controlled handling. Drives fulfillment workflow and cold chain logistics.
Price look-up code used primarily for fresh produce and bulk items sold by weight. Typically 4 or 5 digit codes assigned by the International Federation for Produce Standards.
Specific SKU the customer has designated as an acceptable substitute if the original item is unavailable. Only populated when substitution preference is specific_only.
Indicates whether this item is a store-owned private label brand. Used for private label penetration analysis and margin reporting.
Human-readable product name displayed to the customer in the digital cart. Captured at time of cart add to preserve the product description as shown to the shopper.
Quantity of the item requested by the customer. May be whole units for packaged goods or fractional for weighted items like produce or deli products.
Source that led to this item being added to cart. Tracks effectiveness of recommendation engines, search, and merchandising strategies.
Timestamp when this item was removed from the cart by the customer. Used for cart abandonment analysis and product affinity modeling.
Internal stock keeping unit code identifying the specific product variant added to cart. Primary product identifier for inventory and merchandising systems.

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this item is eligible for purchase using SNAP Electronic Benefits Transfer. Required for SNAP transaction processing and compliance reporting.
Digital channel through which this item was added to the cart. Supports omnichannel behavior analysis and channel-specific merchandising strategies.
Free-text customer instructions for this specific item. Common for produce ripeness preferences, deli slicing thickness, bakery customization, or other item-specific requests.
Customer preference for item substitution if the requested product is out of stock during fulfillment. Drives picker behavior and customer communication workflows.
Indicates whether this item is currently on temporary price reduction. Used for promotional analytics and margin analysis.

Unit of measure for the quantity requested. Indicates whether item is sold by each, weight, volume, or other standard retail units. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|lb|oz|kg|g|gal|qt|pt|fl_oz|l|ml|dozen|case — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Price per unit of measure at the time the item was added to cart. Captured to preserve pricing for cart recovery and price change analysis.
12-digit universal product code barcode identifier. Standard retail product identifier used for scanning and product lookup.

business_glossary_term: Women Infants and Children (WIC) Program Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this item is approved for purchase under the WIC program. Required for WIC voucher processing and state compliance reporting.
Primary key for catalog
Reference to the primary category this item belongs to in the digital catalog hierarchy. Used for navigation and merchandising.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Category Management

description: Reference to the digital storefront channel this catalog serves (e.g., web, mobile app, kiosk). Each catalog is tied to a specific storefront and may differ by channel.
Indicates whether the product requires age verification for purchase (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, certain medications). Triggers age verification workflow at checkout.
reference: State and Federal Age Restriction Laws

description: Comma-separated list of allergen warnings (e.g., Contains: Milk, Eggs, Peanuts). Critical for customer safety and regulatory compliance.
The date when this item will no longer be available for online purchase. Used for limited-time offers and seasonal discontinuation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Lifecycle

description: The date when this item becomes available for online purchase. Used for scheduled product launches and seasonal items.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Lifecycle

description: The brand name of the product. Used for filtering, faceted search, and brand-specific merchandising.
The primary display name of the product as shown on the digital storefront. Optimized for customer-facing presentation and search.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud - Product Catalog Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the item in the digital catalog. Indicates whether the item is available for online shopping, temporarily unavailable, or removed from the digital assortment.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Hierarchical category path for navigation (e.g., Grocery > Dairy > Milk > Organic). Defines where the product appears in the digital storefront taxonomy.
Indicates whether this item is excluded from certain channels (e.g., kiosk catalog may exclude alcohol). True means channel-specific restrictions apply.
The timestamp when this catalog record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the display price (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).
The price displayed to customers on the digital storefront. May differ from the base price due to promotions, channel-specific pricing, or personalized offers.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Price Management

description: Comma-separated list of channel codes where this item is not available (e.g., KIOSK, MOBILE_APP). Used to enforce channel-specific assortment rules.
Indicates whether this product is featured in promotional placements, homepage carousels, or special merchandising zones.
Indicates whether the product is certified or labeled gluten-free. Used for dietary filtering and allergen management.
URL of the primary product image displayed prominently on the product detail page and in featured placements.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud - Digital Asset Management

description: The timestamp when this catalog record was last updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization with source systems.
Detailed rich product description displayed on the product detail page. May include HTML formatting, nutritional information, usage instructions, and ingredient lists.
Numeric rank used to control the display order of products within a category. Lower numbers appear first. Used for strategic product placement.
The minimum age required to purchase this product (e.g., 18, 21). Null if no age restriction applies.
reference: State and Federal Age Restriction Laws

description: Indicates whether this product is marked as a new arrival. Used for New Items merchandising and filtering.
Marketing callouts highlighting nutritional benefits (e.g., High Protein, Low Sodium, No Added Sugar). Used for merchandising and filtering.
Indicates whether the item is currently available for purchase through the digital channel. True means the item can be added to cart and ordered online.
Indicates whether the product is certified organic. Important for customer filtering and regulatory compliance.
The size or quantity of the product package as displayed to customers (e.g., 12 oz, 1 gallon, 6-pack). Used for customer decision-making.
Indicates whether the product is perishable and requires temperature-controlled handling. Affects fulfillment method and delivery logistics.
Indicates whether this is a store-owned brand (private label) product. True for private label, false for national brands.
Numeric score (0.00-999.99) used to boost or demote this item in search results. Higher scores increase prominence in search rankings.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud - Search Ranking Algorithm

description: Comma-separated list of keywords and synonyms to improve product discoverability in site search. Includes common misspellings and alternate terms.
Brief product description used in search results, category listings, and mobile views. Typically 100-200 characters.

The unique product identifier (Stock Keeping Unit) representing the item in the digital catalog. This is the commerce-layer view of what is shoppable online, distinct from the physical product master.

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the product is eligible for purchase using SNAP benefits (formerly food stamps). Required for government assistance program compliance.
The source system that provided this catalog data (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.
The required storage and handling temperature zone for the product. Critical for cold chain management and fulfillment operations.
URL of the thumbnail image used in search results, category grids, and cart views.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud - Digital Asset Management

description: The unit of measure for the product as sold online (e.g., each, pound, gallon). Important for pricing and quantity selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|lb|oz|kg|g|gal|qt|pt|fl_oz|l|ml|dozen|case — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GS1 General Specifications - Unit of Measure

business_glossary_term: Women Infants and Children (WIC) Program Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the product is eligible for purchase using WIC benefits. Required for government assistance program compliance.
Primary key for channel_config
Reference to the digital catalog assigned to this channel. Determines which products are available for browsing and purchase.
Identifier of the price book used for this channel. Determines pricing rules and promotional pricing logic.
Reference to the digital storefront that this channel configuration applies to. Links to the storefront master data.
Comma-separated list of payment tender types accepted on this channel (e.g., credit_card, debit_card, gift_card, paypal, apple_pay, google_pay, ebt, snap, wic).
Indicates whether age verification is required for purchases on this channel (e.g., for alcohol, tobacco, pharmacy items).

business_glossary_term: Application Programming Interface (API) Endpoint Uniform Resource Locator (URL)
description: Base API endpoint URL for this channel. Used by mobile apps and integrations to connect to backend services.
Unique business identifier code for the digital channel (e.g., WEB_US, IOS_APP, ANDROID_APP, KIOSK).
Current operational status of the digital channel configuration.
Human-readable name of the digital channel (e.g., Web Storefront, iOS Mobile App, Android Mobile App, In-Store Kiosk).
Classification of the digital channel platform type.
Customer support email address for inquiries related to this channel.
Customer support phone number for inquiries related to this channel.
Timestamp when this channel configuration record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for pricing and transactions on this channel (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
Indicates whether this channel accepts Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) payments for government assistance programs.
JSON or comma-separated list of feature flags and their enabled/disabled state for this channel (e.g., enable_voice_search=true, enable_ar_preview=false). Drives runtime behavior.
Comma-separated list of fulfillment methods available on this channel (e.g., delivery, pickup, curbside, in_store, ship_to_home). Drives OMS routing logic.
Indicates whether customers can complete purchases without creating an account (guest checkout) on this channel.
Timestamp when this channel configuration record was last updated.
Date when the digital channel was first made available to customers.
Locale identifier for language and regional formatting (e.g., en_US, es_MX, fr_CA). Drives localization behavior.
Identifier of the loyalty program integrated with this channel. Null if no loyalty integration.
Indicates whether the loyalty program is integrated and active on this channel, allowing customers to earn and redeem points.
Maximum number of distinct line items (SKUs) allowed in a single cart on this channel. Enforces cart size limits.
Minimum age in years required to use this channel or purchase age-restricted items. Typically 18 or 21 depending on jurisdiction and product category.
Minimum order subtotal required for checkout on this channel. Used to enforce order minimums for delivery or pickup.
Indicates whether personalized product recommendations and content are enabled on this channel.
Operating system or platform on which the channel operates (iOS, Android, Web Desktop, Web Mobile, Windows, Linux).
Indicates whether the promotion engine is active on this channel, allowing application of discounts, coupons, and promotional offers.
Indicates whether product search functionality is enabled on this channel.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Delivery Hours
description: Target time in hours for order delivery from order placement to customer receipt for this channel. Used for service level commitments.

business_glossary_term: Service Level Agreement (SLA) Target Order Processing Minutes
description: Target time in minutes for order processing from submission to fulfillment assignment for this channel. Used for performance monitoring.

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this channel accepts SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) payments.
Indicates whether product substitutions are allowed by default on this channel when items are out of stock during fulfillment.
Planned or actual date when the digital channel will be or was decommissioned. Null for active channels with no planned sunset.
Tax jurisdiction code for calculating sales tax on transactions in this channel. Used for tax compliance.
IANA timezone identifier for the channel (e.g., America/New_York, America/Los_Angeles). Used for scheduling and time display.

business_glossary_term: Women Infants and Children (WIC) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this channel accepts WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program vouchers or EBT.
Unique identifier for the checkout session. Primary key.
Reference to the shopping cart that was submitted for checkout.
Reference to the order header created from this checkout session upon completion. Links to the OMS (Order Management System) for order routing and fulfillment.
Identifier of the customer who initiated the checkout session.
Store location selected for pickup or fulfillment, if applicable.
Timestamp when the checkout session was abandoned by the customer without completing the purchase.
The checkout funnel step at which the customer abandoned the session, if applicable. Used for conversion funnel analysis.
Web browser or mobile app version used during the checkout session.
Digital channel through which the checkout session was initiated (web storefront, mobile app, in-store kiosk, call center).
Timestamp when the checkout session was successfully completed and order was placed.
Comma-separated list of coupon codes validated and applied during checkout.
Timestamp when this checkout session record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this checkout session.
Delivery or service fee charged for order fulfillment, if applicable.
Type of device used by the customer to complete the checkout session.
Total discount amount applied during checkout from promotions, coupons, and loyalty rewards.

business_glossary_term: EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) Flag
description: Indicates whether EBT SNAP benefits were selected as a payment tender during checkout.
Timestamp when the checkout session expired due to inactivity timeout.
The fulfillment method selected by the customer during checkout (home delivery, in-store pickup, curbside pickup).
Timestamp when the customer began the checkout process by submitting their cart. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.
Timestamp when this checkout session record was last updated. Audit trail for record modification.
Loyalty card number entered or scanned during checkout for rewards and personalization.
Number of loyalty program points earned from this purchase, calculated during checkout.
Number of loyalty program points redeemed by the customer during this checkout session.
External reference identifier assigned by the Order Management System upon successful checkout completion and order routing.
Authorization code returned by the payment processor upon successful payment validation.

Primary payment method selected by the customer during checkout. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ebt|gift_card|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
External reference identifier from the payment gateway or processor for this checkout transaction.
Comma-separated list of promotional codes applied during checkout for discounts or offers.
End time of the delivery or pickup time slot selected by the customer.
Start time of the delivery or pickup time slot selected by the customer.
Total duration of the checkout session in seconds, from initiation to completion or abandonment.
Business-friendly unique identifier for the checkout session, used for customer service and tracking.
Current lifecycle status of the checkout session. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or abandonment.

business_glossary_term: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) Eligible Amount
description: Total amount of items in the cart that are eligible for SNAP benefits payment.
Name of the source system or platform that captured this checkout session (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, custom eCommerce platform).
Free-text special instructions or notes provided by the customer for order fulfillment (e.g., delivery instructions, item preferences).
Indicates whether the customer completed the delivery/pickup address step in the checkout funnel.
Indicates whether the customer completed the fulfillment method and time slot selection step.
Indicates whether the customer completed the payment information entry step.
Indicates whether the customer completed the order review and confirmation step.
Customer preference for item substitutions if ordered items are out of stock during fulfillment.
Total amount of all items in the cart before discounts, taxes, and fees are applied.
Total sales tax calculated and applied to the order during checkout.
Optional tip amount entered by the customer for delivery or pickup service.
Final total amount after all discounts, taxes, fees, and tips are applied. Net amount the customer will pay.
Total number of distinct line items in the cart at checkout initiation.
Total quantity of all units across all items in the cart at checkout.
Indicates whether WIC benefits were selected as a payment tender during checkout.
System-generated unique identifier for each GS1 data synchronization record.
Self-referencing FK on gs1_sync (prior_gs1_sync_id)
Identifier of the product item being synchronized.
Identifier of the external trading partner (vendor or supplier) involved in the synchronization.
Identifier for a batch of synchronization messages sent together.
Indicates whether the synchronized item meets applicable regulatory requirements.
Identifier used to correlate related synchronization messages across systems.
Timestamp when the synchronization record was first created in the lakehouse.
Serialization format of the synchronization payload.
Date when the synchronized data ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended records).
Date when the synchronized data becomes effective for downstream systems.
Standardized code representing any error encountered during synchronization.
Human‑readable description of the error associated with the synchronization attempt.
Three‑letter ISO country code representing the jurisdiction of the regulatory requirement.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the synchronization record.
Globally unique identifier for the synchronization message.
Category of data contained in the payload (e.g., product_data, pricing_data).
Version of the data payload format used in the synchronization message.
Lifecycle status of the synchronization record within the data pool.
Governing body responsible for the compliance aspect (e.g., FDA, USDA, DEA).
Number of retry attempts made after a failed synchronization.
Name of the internal system that originated the synchronization message (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Retail).
Type of synchronization operation performed on the item data.
Current processing status of the synchronization record.
Exact date and time when the synchronization event occurred.
Name of the external system or data pool that receives the synchronization message.
Primary key for the line_item association
Foreign key linking to the payment transaction containing the line
Foreign key linking to the product_item being sold
Discount applied to this line item
Number of units of the product sold in this line
Tax amount calculated for this line item
Final total price for this line after discounts and taxes
Price per unit of the product at the time of sale
Primary key for payment_event
Reference to the original authorization transaction for capture, void, or refund events. Links related payment events in the transaction chain.
Reference to the digital commerce order that initiated this payment event. Links payment event to the order header in the digital shopping flow.
Reference to the customer who initiated this payment. Links to the customer master for analytics and personalization.
Reference to the digital storefront where this payment event occurred. Links to the commerce platform configuration.
Unique identifier for the customer's digital session during which this payment event occurred. Used for conversion funnel analysis.
Approval code returned by the payment processor or issuing bank when the transaction is authorized. Used for settlement reconciliation.
Response code from the Address Verification System indicating whether the billing address provided matches the address on file with the card issuer.
Card network or brand for card-based payments (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover). Null for non-card tender types.
Timestamp when this payment event record was first created in the commerce system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).
Response code indicating whether the CVV/CVC security code provided matches the card issuer's records. Used for fraud prevention.
Code indicating the reason the transaction was declined by the processor or issuer (insufficient funds, invalid card, fraud suspicion, etc.).
Unique identifier for the device used to initiate the payment. Used for fraud detection and device fingerprinting.
Remaining balance on the EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) card after this transaction. Null for non-EBT transactions.
Indicates whether this transaction was flagged as potentially fraudulent by automated fraud detection systems.
Risk score assigned by fraud detection system indicating the likelihood this transaction is fraudulent. Higher scores indicate higher risk.
Remaining balance on the gift card after this transaction. Null for non-gift-card tender types.
Internet Protocol address of the device that initiated the payment transaction. Used for fraud detection and geolocation.
Timestamp when this payment event record was last updated. Tracks changes to transaction status or settlement information.
Number of loyalty program points redeemed as part of this payment transaction. Null if no points were used.
Identifier for the merchant account used to process this transaction. Multiple accounts may be used for different brands or regions.
Digital channel or interface through which the payment was initiated (web storefront, mobile app, mobile web, in-store kiosk, call center).
Name or identifier of the payment gateway or processor that handled this transaction (e.g., Stripe, Adyen, Cybersource).
Reference to the tokenized payment instrument in the payment domain. The payment domain is the authoritative SSOT for tokenization and PCI compliance.
Response code returned by the payment gateway or processor indicating the outcome of the transaction attempt (approved, declined, error codes).
Human-readable message from the payment processor describing the transaction outcome or reason for decline.
Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment processor or gateway. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.
Sequential number indicating which retry attempt this is for the same payment. First attempt is 1, subsequent retries increment.
Date when the transaction was settled and funds were transferred. Null for unsettled transactions.
Status of funds settlement with the payment processor. Indicates whether the transaction has been finalized and funds transferred.
Portion of the transaction amount that qualifies for SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) benefits. Null for non-SNAP transactions.

Payment instrument type used for this transaction. Includes credit card, debit card, EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer), SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program), WIC (Women Infants and Children Program), loyalty points, or gift card.
Indicates whether 3D Secure authentication (Verified by Visa, Mastercard SecureCode) was used for this transaction. Enhances security and shifts liability.
Version of 3D Secure protocol used (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 2.1). Null if 3D Secure was not used.
Monetary value of this payment event. For authorizations and captures, this is the gross amount; for refunds, this is the amount being returned.
Current status of the payment transaction event in its lifecycle. Tracks progression from initiation through settlement or failure.
Precise date and time when the payment event was initiated in the digital commerce flow. Primary business event timestamp for this transaction.
Type of payment event: authorization (hold funds), capture (settle funds), void (cancel authorization), refund (return funds), partial_refund (return portion), or reversal (undo transaction).
Portion of the transaction amount that qualifies for WIC (Women Infants and Children Program) benefits. Null for non-WIC transactions.
Unique identifier for the product recommendation record. Primary key.
Identifier of the product category to which the recommended product belongs.
Identifier of the merchandising or promotional campaign associated with this recommendation, if applicable.
Identifier of the order in which the recommended product was purchased, if conversion occurred.
Identifier of the shopper to whom the recommendation was presented.
Identifier of the digital storefront where the recommendation was displayed.
Unique identifier for the digital browsing session during which the recommendation was presented.
Identifier of the A/B test variant or experiment group to which this recommendation belongs, enabling testing of recommendation strategies.
Indicates whether the shopper added the recommended product to their cart (True if added, False otherwise).
The date and time when the recommended product was added to the cart, if applicable.
The name or identifier of the personalization algorithm or strategy used to generate the recommendation.
The brand name of the recommended product.
The digital channel through which the recommendation was delivered to the shopper.
Indicates whether the shopper clicked on the recommended product (True if clicked, False otherwise).
The date and time when the shopper clicked on the recommendation, if applicable.
The page or context within the digital storefront where the recommendation was displayed.
Indicates whether this recommendation was part of a control group in an A/B test (True for control, False for treatment).
Indicates whether the shopper purchased the recommended product (True if purchased, False otherwise).
The date and time when the recommended product was purchased, if applicable.
The date and time when this recommendation record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the display price.
The type of device used by the shopper when the recommendation was presented.
The ordinal position where the recommendation was displayed on the page (1 for first position, 2 for second, etc.).
The price of the recommended product as displayed to the shopper at the time of the recommendation.
The date and time when the recommendation is no longer considered valid or relevant for tracking purposes.
The latitude coordinate of the shoppers location at the time of the recommendation, if available.
The longitude coordinate of the shoppers location at the time of the recommendation, if available.
The exact date and time when the recommendation was displayed to the shopper.
The IP address of the shopper at the time of the recommendation impression, used for fraud detection and analytics.
The date and time when this recommendation record was last updated.
Version identifier of the machine learning model or recommendation engine that generated this recommendation.
Indicates whether a promotional discount was applied to the recommended product at the time of display.
Business-friendly reference number for the recommendation event, used for tracking and reporting.
The origin or system that generated the recommendation, distinguishing between automated and manual recommendations.
Current lifecycle status of the recommendation indicating shopper interaction level.
The category or methodology of the recommendation strategy employed.
The display name of the recommended product as shown to the shopper.
The SKU of the product that was recommended to the shopper.
Confidence or relevance score assigned by the recommendation engine, indicating predicted likelihood of shopper interest.
The number of seconds the shopper spent on the page where the recommendation was displayed.
The browser or application user agent string captured at the time of the recommendation impression.
Unique identifier for each product search query event executed by a shopper on a digital storefront.
Reference to the authenticated shopper who executed the search. Null for guest users.
Reference to the physical store selected by the shopper for fulfillment context, if applicable to the search session.
Reference to the digital storefront where the search was executed.
Unique identifier for the shopper session during which the search was performed, enabling session-level analytics and journey tracking.
The A/B test variant or experiment group assigned to this search event, enabling controlled testing of search algorithms and merchandising strategies.
Indicator of whether the shopper added any product to their cart as a result of this search, measuring search conversion effectiveness.
The autocomplete suggestion selected by the shopper, if applicable.
Indicator of whether the shopper selected a suggestion from the autocomplete dropdown rather than typing the full query.
The position (rank) of the clicked product in the search results list, used to measure relevance quality.
The SKU of the first product clicked by the shopper from the search results, if any.
The timestamp when this search query record was created in the system.
The type of device used by the shopper to perform the search.
The brand filter applied by the shopper to narrow search results, if any.
The product category filter applied by the shopper to narrow search results, if any.
Dietary or nutritional filters applied by the shopper (e.g., organic, gluten-free, vegan, low-sodium) to narrow search results.
The maximum price threshold applied by the shopper as a filter, if any.
The minimum price threshold applied by the shopper as a filter, if any.
The IP address of the device from which the search was executed, used for fraud detection and geographic analytics.
The locale or language setting of the shopper at the time of search (e.g., en-US, es-MX), affecting search relevance and merchandising.
The search query after normalization processing (lowercased, stemmed, stop-words removed) used for relevance matching and analytics.
Indicator of whether personalized ranking or filtering was applied to the search results based on shopper profile or behavior.
Indicator of whether the shopper refined or modified the search query after viewing initial results, signaling potential relevance issues.
The raw search text entered by the shopper, capturing the exact keywords or phrases used to find products.
The subsequent search query entered by the shopper if they refined their original search.
Indicator of whether the shopper clicked on any product from the search results, measuring search effectiveness.
The total number of product results returned by the search engine for this query.
The digital channel through which the search was executed (e.g., web browser, mobile app, mobile web, in-store kiosk).
The version of the search engine or algorithm used to process this query, enabling A/B testing and performance tracking.
The method used by the shopper to initiate the search (e.g., text input, voice search, barcode scan, image search).
The time in milliseconds taken by the search engine to return results, used for performance monitoring.
The precise date and time when the shopper executed the search query.

The sort order selected by the shopper to organize search results (e.g., relevance, price ascending, price descending, newest arrivals). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: relevance|price_low_to_high|price_high_to_low|newest|best_selling|customer_rating|alphabetical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The source system or platform that captured this search event (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, custom eCommerce platform).
The user agent string of the browser or application used to perform the search, providing device and browser details.
Indicator of whether the search returned no results, critical for identifying search relevance gaps and merchandising opportunities.

description: Boolean indicator of whether the substitute item must have the same allergen profile as the primary item. Critical for food safety and customer health. True enforces allergen matching; False allows different allergen profiles.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA)

description: Current approval status of this substitution relationship in the governance workflow. Draft indicates initial creation; pending_review awaits category manager approval; approved is active; rejected was denied; suspended is temporarily inactive; expired has passed its effective end date.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Date and time when this substitution relationship was approved by the authorized user.

Boolean indicator of whether this substitution can be applied automatically by the Order Management System (OMS) or Computer Assisted Ordering (CAO) system without manual intervention. True enables automatic substitution; False requires manual review.

Indicates the brand matching requirement for substitution: exact (same brand only), private_label (store-owned brand preferred), national_brand (Consumer Packaged Goods brand preferred), or any (brand agnostic).
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Boolean indicator of whether the substitute item must belong to the same product category as the primary item. True enforces category matching; False allows cross-category substitution.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Category Management

description: Indicates which fulfillment channels this substitution rule applies to: all channels, in-store only, online only, pickup (click-and-collect), delivery (last mile delivery), or curbside pickup.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud - Omnichannel Order Fulfillment

description: Date and time when this substitution relationship record was first created in the system.
Percentage of times customers accepted this substitution when offered (accepted substitutions divided by total offers). Used to measure customer satisfaction and optimize substitution rules.

Boolean indicator of whether explicit customer approval is required before making this substitution. True means the customer must approve the substitute before fulfillment; False means the substitution can be made automatically.
reference: FTC Fair Packaging and Labeling Act

description: Boolean indicator of whether the substitute item must match dietary restrictions of the primary item (e.g., organic, gluten-free, kosher, halal, vegan). True enforces dietary attribute matching; False allows different dietary profiles.
The date when this substitution relationship expires and should no longer be used. Null indicates an open-ended relationship with no expiration.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: The date when this substitution relationship becomes active and can be used for order fulfillment and replenishment decisions.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Date and time when this substitution was most recently applied in an order fulfillment or replenishment transaction.
Date and time when this substitution relationship record was last updated or modified.
Free-text field for category managers to document special instructions, business rationale, customer communication guidelines, or other relevant information about this substitution relationship.

Boolean indicator specific to pharmacy items indicating whether the substitute drug is therapeutically equivalent to the primary drug per FDA Orange Book standards. True means the substitute has the same active ingredient, strength, dosage form, and route of administration.
reference: FDA Orange Book - Approved Drug Products with Therapeutic Equivalence Evaluations

description: Boolean indicator for pharmacy items indicating whether the substitute must have the same prescription requirement status as the primary item (both Rx or both OTC). True enforces matching; False allows substitution between prescription and over-the-counter items.

Maximum acceptable percentage difference in price between the primary item and substitute item. Positive values allow higher-priced substitutes; negative values allow lower-priced substitutes. For example, 15.00 means the substitute can cost up to 15% more.
Numeric ranking indicating the order of preference when multiple substitutes exist for the same primary item. Lower numbers indicate higher priority (1 = first choice, 2 = second choice, etc.).

Acceptable percentage variance in product size or quantity between the primary item and substitute item. For example, 10.00 means the substitute can be up to 10% larger or smaller than the original item.

Identifies the originating system or process that created this substitution rule: merchandising system, eCommerce platform, Computer Assisted Ordering (CAO), manual entry by category manager, or category management tool.

Classification of the substitution relationship indicating whether the substitute is an equivalent product, an upgrade (higher quality or price), a downgrade (lower quality or price), similar product, alternative brand, or generic equivalent.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Cumulative count of how many times this substitution has been applied in actual order fulfillment or replenishment transactions. Used for effectiveness tracking and optimization.
