Indicates whether surge pricing was applied to this slot reservation due to high demand or holiday periods.
Unique surrogate key for each order origination channel. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity is a reference lookup for channel classification.
Self-referencing FK on order_channel (parent_order_channel_id)

List of payment methods accepted for this channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|gift_card|mobile_wallet|cash|check|ebt|snap|wic|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay — promote to reference product]
Short alphanumeric code used internally to identify the channel (e.g., POS, WEB, MOB).
Human‑readable name of the order channel (e.g., In‑Store POS, Web Store, Mobile App).
Broad classification of the channel based on interaction mode.
Timestamp when the channel record was first created in the system.
ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code used for monetary limits on the channel.
Date when the channel was retired or disabled (null if still active).
Date when the channel became operational.
Indicates whether the channel is currently enabled for order capture.
Indicates whether the channel is operated by an external marketplace partner.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the channel record.
Maximum monetary value allowed for a single order on this channel.
Minimum monetary value required for an order to be accepted via this channel.
Detailed description of the channel's purpose, capabilities, and typical use cases.
True if the channel must verify customer age for restricted items.
True if the channel can process pharmacy prescription orders.
Identifier of the routing rule set that governs order flow for this channel.
Name of the operational system of record that defines this channel (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail).
Comma‑separated list of fulfillment methods the channel can route to.
Unique identifier for the order discount record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Discount expense must be posted to a specific GL account for accurate discount expense reporting.
Reference to the order header to which this discount is applied. Links to the parent order transaction.
The unique transaction identifier from the source system (POS transaction number, eCommerce order number). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit.
Reference to the store associate or cashier who processed this discount at the point of sale. Nullable for automated or online discounts.
Reference to the specific order line item to which this discount is applied. Nullable for header-level discounts that apply to the entire order rather than a specific line.
Reference to the promotion campaign or offer that generated this discount. Links to the promotion master data.
Reference to the physical store location where this discount was applied, if applicable. Nullable for online-only transactions.
Reference to the vendor or supplier funding this discount, if applicable. Nullable for store-funded or loyalty-funded discounts.
The date and time when this discount was applied to the order during the transaction or order processing workflow.
Indicates whether this discount required manager or supervisor approval before being applied. True if approval was required, false otherwise.
The date and time when this discount was approved by a manager or supervisor. Nullable if no approval was required.

The sales channel through which this discount was applied. In-store for POS (Point of Sale) transactions, online for eCommerce, mobile app for app-based orders, curbside pickup for click-and-collect, delivery for last-mile delivery orders, pharmacy for prescription fills.
The specific coupon or promo code entered or scanned by the customer to activate this discount. Nullable if discount was automatically applied.
The date and time when this discount record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
The absolute monetary value of the discount applied to the order or line item, in the transaction currency. Represents the reduction in price.
The percentage rate of discount applied, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 15.00 for 15% off). Nullable if discount is a fixed amount rather than percentage-based.
Internal code indicating the business reason or authorization basis for the discount. Used for audit and compliance tracking.

The level at which this discount is applied. Order header applies to entire order, order line applies to specific line item, category applies to product category, SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) applies to specific product, basket applies to qualifying basket combinations.

Current lifecycle status of the discount. Applied indicates successfully applied, reversed indicates discount was removed or refunded, pending indicates awaiting validation, expired indicates promotion period ended, invalid indicates discount failed validation rules.

Classification of the discount mechanism applied. TPR (Temporary Price Reduction), BOGO (Buy One Get One), digital coupon, loyalty reward, ad circular promotion, markdown clearance, employee discount, EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) discount, vendor-funded promotion, or store-funded promotion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tpr|bogo|digital_coupon|loyalty_reward|ad_circular|markdown|employee_discount|ebt_discount|vendor_funded|store_funded — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The final price after this discount was applied. Represents the net amount charged to the customer for this discount application.
Entity responsible for funding or absorbing the cost of this discount. Vendor-funded promotions are reimbursed by suppliers, store-funded are absorbed by the retailer.
Number of loyalty program points redeemed by the customer to obtain this discount. Nullable if discount is not loyalty-based.
The maximum discount amount permitted by the promotion rules or system configuration. Used to enforce discount caps and prevent over-discounting.
The minimum purchase amount required to qualify for this discount. Nullable if no minimum threshold applies.
The date and time when this discount record was last modified or updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The original price of the item or order total before this discount was applied. Used for calculating discount impact and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.
Identifier of the point-of-sale terminal or register where this discount was processed. Nullable for digital commerce transactions.
The order in which this discount was applied when multiple promotions are stacked on the same order or line. Lower numbers indicate earlier application in the discount calculation sequence.
The effective end date of the promotion period after which this discount is no longer valid.
The effective start date of the promotion period during which this discount is valid.
The minimum quantity of items required to qualify for this discount (e.g., buy 3 get 1 free). Nullable if discount is not quantity-based.
Explanation or code indicating why this discount was reversed or removed from the order. Nullable if discount was not reversed.
The date and time when this discount was reversed or removed. Nullable if discount was not reversed.

business_glossary_term: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this discount can be combined with SNAP benefits. True if eligible for SNAP transactions, false otherwise.
Identifier of the operational system that originated this discount record (e.g., NCR POS, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Oracle Retail). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Indicates whether this discount is applied before tax calculation (pre-tax), after tax calculation (post-tax), or if the discounted amount is tax-exempt.

business_glossary_term: WIC (Women Infants and Children) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this discount can be combined with WIC benefits. True if eligible for WIC transactions, false otherwise.

Unique surrogate identifier for every customer purchase record across all channels (POS, online, click-and-collect, curbside pickup, last-mile delivery, pharmacy Rx fills). Primary key of the order_header entity and the single canonical identity for all customer transactions in the enterprise.

Reference to the workforce associate (cashier) who processed the POS transaction. Applicable only for in_store_pos channel type. Used for cashier performance reporting, void/return authorization auditing, and labor analytics. Null for self-checkout, online, and OMS orders.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Kronos Workforce Central; Internal HR Policy

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost center assignment enables internal cost allocation and budgeting reports per order, a standard practice in grocery chains.
Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Links each order to its fiscal period, enabling period‑based financial statements and variance analysis.

Reference to the retail fiscal calendar week in which this order was placed. Enables alignment of sales reporting to the 4-5-4 retail fiscal calendar used for comp sales (comparable store sales) and same-store sales analysis. Derived from order_date at ingestion.
reference: SAP Retail FI; NRF 4-5-4 Retail Fiscal Calendar; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Revenue GL account needed for GAAP sales reporting; each order must post revenue to a specific GL account, obvious to finance analysts.

Reference to the customer household entity for household-level purchase analytics, loyalty program household linking, and targeted promotions. Enables household basket analysis and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) calculations at the household level. Null for anonymous transactions.
reference: CRM; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; Customer Loyalty Program

description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Identifies the corporate legal entity owning the store for consolidation and regulatory reporting.

Foreign key linking to loyalty.membership. Business justification: REQUIRED: Loyalty reporting needs to tie each order to the shopper's membership to calculate points earned, tier changes, and generate the 'Points Earned per Order' report.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: Order‑level reference to the tokenized payment method allows reporting of payment method usage per order and supports refunds to the original method.

Foreign key linking to order.order_channel. Business justification: Orders belong to a channel; replace the free‑text channel_type with a foreign key to order_channel for referential integrity and channel‑level analytics.

Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Business process: Org‑unit performance reporting requires linking each order to the responsible org unit for labor and cost allocation.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center linkage supports profitability analysis by store/region; required for P&L statements per profit center.
Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Required for Marketing ROI report linking each order to its originating campaign; replaces denormalized promo_code.

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.rx_patient. Business justification: Orders containing prescriptions must be associated with the patient to satisfy HIPAA, claim reconciliation, and loyalty program integration.
Reference to the customer/shopper who placed or completed this order. Nullable for anonymous in-store POS transactions where no loyalty card was scanned and no account was identified.

Foreign key linking to assortment.store_cluster. Business justification: Required for cluster‑level sales performance reporting; each order must be attributed to its store cluster for weekly KPI dashboards.
Reference to the physical store or fulfillment location where the order originated or was fulfilled. Links to the store master for store-level reporting, comp sales, and same-store sales analysis.

Indicates whether the order contains age-restricted items (alcohol, tobacco, lottery tickets, certain medications) requiring cashier or delivery driver age verification at point of sale or delivery. Triggers age-check workflow in NCR POS or last-mile delivery confirmation.
reference: FDA Tobacco Regulations; State Alcohol Beverage Control Laws; NCR POS Systems

description: Total fee charged for single-use plastic or paper bags per applicable state and local bag fee ordinances. Required for regulatory compliance reporting in jurisdictions with mandatory bag fee laws (e.g., California, Illinois). Zero in jurisdictions without bag fee requirements.
reference: State and Local Bag Fee Regulations; EPA Environmental Compliance; SAP Retail FI

description: Total number of distinct line items (SKUs) in the order basket. Used for basket size analytics, express lane eligibility (e.g., 15 items or fewer), and customer segmentation. Distinct from total unit quantity which counts individual units across all line items.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; Oracle Retail Merchandising

description: Reason code explaining why the order was cancelled. Populated only when order_status = 'cancelled'. Used for cancellation root-cause analysis, OOS (Out of Stock) reporting, and fraud detection. Values: customer_request, out_of_stock, payment_failure, fraud_hold, store_closure, system_error.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud OMS; NCR POS Systems; Oracle Retail Replenishment

description: Amount of cash change to be returned to the customer when cash tender exceeds the order total. Applicable only for in-store POS and self-checkout cash transactions. Null for non-cash or digital orders. Sourced from NCR POS cash management module.

Timestamp when this order_header record was first written to the Silver layer data lakehouse. Distinct from order_timestamp (business event time) — this is the data capture audit timestamp used for data lineage, pipeline monitoring, and late-arriving data detection.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this order. Defaults to USD for domestic US grocery operations. Required for multi-currency support in international or border-region store operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP Retail FI

description: Fee charged to the customer for last-mile delivery service. Applicable for online_delivery channel orders. May be zero for fee-waived orders (e.g., loyalty member benefit, promotional free delivery). Null for in-store POS, click-and-collect, and curbside pickup orders.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; JDA Transportation Management; LMD Operations

description: Total monetary value of all discounts applied to the order, including promotional discounts (TPR — Temporary Price Reduction), loyalty discounts, coupon redemptions, and BOGO (Buy One Get One) savings. Stored as a positive value representing the reduction from subtotal.
reference: SAP Retail SD; Oracle Retail Price Management; FTC Advertising Practices

description: Indicates whether any government assistance program tender (EBT — Electronic Benefits Transfer, SNAP — Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program, or WIC — Women Infants and Children) was used in this transaction. Required for USDA SNAP/WIC program compliance reporting and retailer authorization audits.
reference: USDA SNAP Program Requirements; USDA WIC Program Requirements; FNS Retailer Compliance

description: Scheduled or actual date on which the order is to be fulfilled — delivered to the customer, made available for pickup, or dispensed (for pharmacy Rx). Used for delivery slot management, SLA tracking, and fulfillment capacity planning. Null for immediate in-store POS transactions.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud OMS; JDA Transportation Management; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: Precise timestamp when the order was actually fulfilled — delivered, picked up by the customer, or dispensed. Used for on-time delivery rate (OTD) measurement, last-mile delivery (LMD) SLA compliance, and customer experience analytics. Null until fulfillment is confirmed.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud OMS; JDA Transportation Management; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: Indicates whether a customer loyalty card or loyalty account was identified and scanned/linked at the time of the transaction. True enables loyalty points accrual and personalized offer redemption. False indicates an anonymous transaction with no loyalty attribution.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud Loyalty Integration; CRM

description: Number of loyalty reward points earned by the customer on this transaction based on the applicable loyalty program earning rules. Zero for anonymous transactions (loyalty_card_scanned = false) or transactions where no points were awarded (e.g., fuel-only purchases).
reference: NCR POS Systems Loyalty Integration; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; CRM Loyalty Program Rules

description: External order reference number assigned by the OMS (Order Management System — Salesforce Commerce Cloud) for omnichannel orders. Enables cross-system order tracing between the Silver layer canonical model and the OMS source system. Null for pure in-store POS transactions not routed through OMS.

Calendar date on which the order was placed or the POS transaction was initiated. Used for daily sales reporting, comp sales (comparable store sales), same-store sales analysis, and fiscal period alignment.
reference: SAP Retail FI; NCR POS Systems; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Externally visible, human-readable order reference number presented to the customer on receipts, confirmation emails, and order tracking pages. Generated by the source system (NCR POS transaction number, Salesforce Commerce Cloud order number, or McKesson Rx order number). Unique within the source system.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: Current lifecycle state of the order. Tracks the order from initial placement through fulfillment to completion or cancellation. POS transactions typically transition directly to a terminal state (completed or voided). OMS-originated orders traverse the full lifecycle. Values: placed, confirmed, in_fulfillment, ready, dispatched, delivered, cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: placed|confirmed|in_fulfillment|ready|dispatched|delivered|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud OMS; NCR POS Systems; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: Precise date and time at which the order was placed or the POS transaction was opened. The principal real-world business event timestamp. Used for intraday sales analytics, peak-hour staffing analysis, and order SLA measurement. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; SAP Retail SD

description: Primary payment instrument used to tender the order. For split-tender transactions, reflects the dominant or first payment method. Full tender breakdown is captured in the order_payment child entity. Values include EBT/SNAP for government assistance program transactions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|cash|ebt_snap|ebt_cash|gift_card|loyalty_points|mobile_pay|check — promote to reference product]
reference: NCR POS Systems; PCI DSS; USDA SNAP Program Requirements

description: Indicates whether this order contains one or more pharmacy prescription (Rx) line items. When true, the order is subject to HIPAA privacy requirements, DEA controlled substance regulations, and State Board of Pharmacy dispensing rules. Drives downstream routing to McKesson Pharmacy Systems.
reference: HIPAA Privacy Rule; DEA Controlled Substances Act; State Boards of Pharmacy; McKesson Pharmacy Systems

description: POS register or checkout lane identifier within the store where the transaction was processed. Applicable only for in_store_pos and self_checkout channel types. Used for lane-level performance reporting, cashier assignment, and cash reconciliation. Null for OMS-originated orders.

Indicates whether the transaction was processed through a self-checkout (SCO) lane where the customer scanned and bagged their own items without cashier assistance. Used for self-checkout adoption analytics, shrink attribution, and labor efficiency reporting.

Identifies the operational system of record that originated this order record. Drives data lineage, reconciliation, and system-specific business rule application. Values: ncr_pos (in-store and self-checkout POS transactions), salesforce_commerce_cloud (online, delivery, click-and-collect, curbside orders), mckesson_pharmacy (pharmacy Rx fill orders).
reference: NCR POS Systems; Salesforce Commerce Cloud; McKesson Pharmacy Systems; SOX Data Lineage

description: Free-text field capturing customer-provided delivery, pickup, or preparation instructions (e.g., 'Leave at front door', 'No substitutions', 'Call upon arrival'). Applicable for online delivery, click-and-collect, and curbside pickup orders. Null for in-store POS transactions.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud OMS; Last-Mile Delivery Operations

description: Customer preference indicating whether out-of-stock (OOS) items in the order may be substituted with comparable alternatives by the picker or fulfillment associate. Applicable for online delivery, click-and-collect, and curbside pickup orders. Null for in-store POS transactions.
reference: Salesforce Commerce Cloud OMS; Oracle Retail Replenishment; OOS Management

description: Sum of all line item extended prices before tax, discounts, and fees are applied. Represents the gross merchandise value (GMV) of the basket prior to adjustments. Used in COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) calculations.
reference: SAP Retail FI; SOX Financial Reporting

description: Total sales tax charged on the order, calculated based on applicable state and local tax rates for taxable items in the basket. Excludes tax-exempt items (e.g., qualifying grocery staples, SNAP/EBT-eligible items). Required for tax remittance reporting and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP Retail FI; SOX Financial Reporting; State and Local Tax Regulations

description: Human-readable summary of payment tender types used in this transaction (e.g., 'Credit $45.20, Loyalty Points $5.00'). Provides a quick overview of how the order was paid without exposing sensitive payment details. Sourced from NCR POS tender records or Salesforce Commerce Cloud payment summary.

Final net amount due from the customer after applying subtotal, discounts, fees, and tax. Equals subtotal_amount minus discount_amount plus tax_amount plus any applicable fees (delivery, service). This is the amount tendered by the customer. Core metric for revenue reporting and EBITDA calculations.
reference: SAP Retail FI; SOX Financial Reporting; PCI DSS

description: Total number of individual units (items) purchased across all line items in the order. Differs from basket_item_count which counts distinct SKUs. Used for average units per transaction (UPT) analytics and shrink analysis.
reference: NCR POS Systems; Oracle Retail Merchandising

description: Classification of the POS or OMS transaction by its business purpose. Primarily relevant for in-store POS and self-checkout channels. Values: sale (standard purchase), return (merchandise returned for refund), void (transaction cancelled before tender completion), no_sale (cash drawer opened without a sale), exchange (item swap without net monetary change).
reference: NCR POS Systems; SAP Retail SD

description: Timestamp when this order_header record was last modified in the Silver layer. Tracks order status changes, payment updates, and post-transaction adjustments. Used for incremental data processing and change data capture (CDC) pipelines.
reference: SOX Internal Controls; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit

description: System-generated unique identifier for the order hold record.
Identifier of the associate responsible for handling the hold.
Identifier of the order to which this hold is applied.
Identifier of the user who performed the fraud review.
Identifier of the order line that is subject to the hold.
Self-referencing FK on order_hold (superseded_order_hold_id)
Indicates if the hold is due to an age‑restricted item.
True when age verification has been successfully completed.
Timestamp when age verification was completed.
Monetary amount placed on hold (e.g., pending payment amount).
Timestamp when the hold was initially placed on the order.
Channel through which the order was placed that generated the hold.
Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.
Three‑letter ISO currency code for the hold amount.
Timestamp when the hold is scheduled to expire if not released.
Code indicating why the hold expired without release.
Free‑text description of the expiry reason.
Current status of the manual fraud review process.
Timestamp when the fraud review decision was recorded.
Risk score assigned by the fraud engine for this hold.
Timestamp when inventory confirmation was performed.
True if inventory availability has been confirmed for the held items.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the hold record.
Free‑form notes entered by staff regarding the hold.
Channel used to notify the customer about the hold.
True if a notification about the hold was sent to the customer.
External reference number for the hold, used in communications and reporting.
Timestamp when payment verification was completed.
True when payment verification (e.g., card auth) has succeeded.
Business priority assigned to the hold for processing urgency.
Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold.
Free‑text description of why the hold was applied.
Quantity of the related SKU involved in the hold.
SKU of the product that triggered the hold, if applicable.
Timestamp when the hold was manually or automatically released.
Indicates whether the hold met its SLA target.
Target time in minutes for the hold to be resolved.
Difference in minutes between actual resolution time and SLA target (positive = over, negative = under).
Originating system that created the hold (e.g., SAP Retail, Oracle Retail).
Current lifecycle state of the hold.
Category of the hold reason, such as fraud review, payment verification, age‑restricted item validation, inventory confirmation, or other.
Unique identifier for the payment tender record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Tracks which bank account received the payment for reconciliation and cash‑management reporting.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_method. Business justification: Needed for fraud analysis: associate each order payment with the stored tokenized payment method used, enabling compliance and recurring‑payment tracking.
Foreign key reference to the parent order header. Links this payment tender to the customer order it settles.

Foreign key linking to payment.payment_transaction. Business justification: Required for audit: link each order payment record to its underlying payment transaction for settlement reconciliation and financial reporting.
Authorization code returned by the payment processor or card issuer confirming approval of the transaction. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.
Date and time when the payment authorization was received from the payment processor. Represents the real-world event time of payment approval.

Response code returned by the Address Verification Service (AVS) indicating the match result between billing address provided and address on file with card issuer. Used for fraud detection and risk management.
Last four digits of the bank account number from the check. Applicable only when tender_type is check. Stored for reference while maintaining compliance with financial data protection standards.
Nine-digit American Bankers Association (ABA) routing number from the check. Applicable only when tender_type is check. Used for electronic check verification and processing.
reference: ABA Routing Number Standards; Internal Finance Policy

description: Postal code associated with the billing address for the payment instrument. Used for Address Verification Service (AVS) fraud checks. Applicable to card-based tenders.

Last four digits of the payment card number. Used for customer reference and dispute resolution while maintaining Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliance by not storing full card number.
Card network brand for credit and debit card tenders. Applicable only when tender_type is credit_card or debit_card.
Name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment card. Used for fraud detection and dispute resolution. Applicable to credit and debit card tenders.
Amount of change returned to the customer when payment_amount exceeds the order balance. Applicable primarily to cash tenders. Null for non-cash tenders or when exact payment is tendered.
Check number from the paper check tendered. Applicable only when tender_type is check. Used for reconciliation and fraud detection.
Date and time when this payment tender record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount. Supports multi-currency transactions in international operations.
Response code indicating whether the Card Verification Value (CVV) provided matched the value on file with the card issuer. Used for fraud detection. Applicable to card-not-present transactions.

Code returned by the payment processor indicating why the payment was declined. Examples include insufficient_funds, invalid_card, expired_card, fraud_suspected. Applicable when settlement_status is declined.

Remaining balance on the Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) card after this transaction. Applicable only when tender_type is ebt_snap. Supports customer service inquiries and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) regulatory reporting requirements.
Monetary value of fuel reward points applied as discount. Applicable only when tender_type is fuel_reward.
Number of fuel reward loyalty points redeemed as payment toward this order. Applicable only when tender_type is fuel_reward. Supports fuel center operations and loyalty program reporting.
Remaining balance on the gift card after this transaction. Applicable only when tender_type is gift_card. Supports customer service inquiries.
Masked or tokenized gift card number. Applicable only when tender_type is gift_card. Stored in compliance with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) tokenization requirements.
reference: PCI DSS; Internal Gift Card Policy

description: Type of mobile wallet used for payment. Applicable only when tender_type is mobile_wallet.

Monetary amount applied from this payment tender toward the order total. For split-tender transactions, this represents the portion paid by this specific instrument. Expressed in the transaction currency.

Channel or interface through which the payment was captured. Point of Sale (POS) for in-store register transactions; ecommerce for web storefront; mobile_app for native app checkout; kiosk for self-service terminals; phone for call center orders; curbside_terminal for pickup payment devices.
Name or identifier of the payment gateway or processor that handled this transaction. Examples include First Data, Worldpay, Stripe, or internal processor identifiers.
Unique transaction identifier assigned by the external payment processor or gateway. Used for reconciliation, dispute resolution, and cross-system traceability.
Sequential number of this payment tender within the order. Supports split-tender transactions where multiple payment instruments are applied to a single order.

Code indicating the reason for refund when settlement_status is refunded. Examples include customer_request, damaged_goods, pricing_error, order_cancellation. Used for refund analytics and fraud detection.
Date and time when the refund was processed. Applicable only when settlement_status is refunded.

Current state of the payment in the settlement lifecycle. Authorized indicates approval but funds not yet captured; captured indicates funds reserved; settled indicates funds transferred to merchant account; voided indicates authorization cancelled before capture; refunded indicates funds returned to customer; declined indicates payment rejected; pending indicates awaiting processor response. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authorized|captured|settled|voided|refunded|declined|pending — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Payment Processor Standards; Internal Finance Policy

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Eligible Amount
description: Portion of the order total that qualifies for Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) payment. Applicable when tender_type is ebt_snap. Required for SNAP regulatory reporting and compliance audits.
reference: SNAP Retailer Regulations; 7 CFR 274; USDA FNS Guidelines

description: Type of payment instrument used. Distinguishes between credit card, debit card, Electronic Benefits Transfer (EBT) for Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), Women Infants and Children (WIC) voucher, mobile wallet (Apple Pay, Google Pay), gift card, fuel reward points, cash, or check. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|ebt_snap|wic|mobile_wallet|gift_card|fuel_reward|cash|check — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: PCI DSS; SNAP Retailer Regulations; WIC Program Guidelines

description: Gratuity amount added by the customer for delivery or service. Applicable primarily to delivery and curbside pickup orders. Null when no tip is provided.

Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) compliant token representing the payment instrument. Used for recurring payments and stored payment methods without retaining sensitive card data.
reference: PCI DSS Requirement 3.2; Payment Tokenization Standards

description: Date and time when this payment tender record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

business_glossary_term: Women Infants and Children (WIC) Eligible Amount
description: Portion of the order total that qualifies for Women Infants and Children (WIC) program payment. Applicable when tender_type is wic. Required for WIC program reporting.
reference: WIC Program Guidelines; State WIC Agency Regulations

business_glossary_term: Women Infants and Children (WIC) Voucher Number
description: Unique voucher or authorization number for Women Infants and Children (WIC) program payment. Applicable only when tender_type is wic. Required for WIC program reconciliation and state reporting.
reference: WIC Program Guidelines; State WIC Agency Regulations

description: Unique identifier for the refund transaction record. Primary key for the order_refund product.
Foreign key reference to the workforce associate who authorized and approved the refund transaction. Required for audit trail and accountability.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Refunds are expense entries that require a GL account for proper accounting and audit trails.
Foreign key reference to the original order header for which this refund was issued. Links the refund back to the parent order transaction.
External payment processor transaction identifier for the refund payment. Used for payment reconciliation and dispute resolution.
External payment processor transaction identifier from the original order. Used to link refund back to original payment for reconciliation and chargeback prevention.
Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Financial reconciliation of refunds by campaign for GMROI calculations; promotion_code_reversed is a denormalized code.
Foreign key reference to the customer who requested the refund. May differ from the original order customer in cases of gift returns.
Foreign key reference to the store location where the refund was processed. Applicable for in-store and curbside refunds.
Date and time when this refund record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund transaction (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).
Optional post-refund satisfaction rating provided by customer, typically on a scale of 1-5. Used to measure refund experience quality. Null if not collected.
Free-text explanation for why the refund request was denied. Null if refund was approved. Used for customer communication and policy compliance.
Authorization level required to approve this refund if it was a policy exception. Null if no exception was needed.
Numerical fraud risk score assigned by fraud detection system, typically 0-100 where higher values indicate greater fraud likelihood. Null if fraud scoring not applied.
Action taken with the returned inventory. Restock returns item to sellable inventory, damage_out records shrink, donate sends to charity, dispose discards, vendor_return sends back to supplier.
Boolean indicator whether this refund was flagged as potentially fraudulent by fraud detection systems or manual review. Used for loss prevention analysis.

The name of the substitute product provided to the customer. Used for customer communication and order accuracy tracking.
