Standardized code indicating why a substitution was made, such as out-of-stock, product discontinued, or quality concerns.
Indicates whether this line represents the original item ordered, a substituted item, an out-of-stock item, or a substitution that the customer declined.
The total sales tax amount calculated and applied to this line item based on the product tax code and delivery location tax jurisdiction.
The required storage and handling temperature zone for this product: ambient (room temperature), refrigerated (cold chain), or frozen. Critical for cold chain compliance and food safety.
Boolean indicator of whether this line item was sold at a temporary promotional price reduction at the time of order placement.

The unit in which the product quantity is measured, such as each, pound, kilogram, gallon, or liter. Critical for accurate pricing and inventory management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|lb|oz|kg|g|gal|qt|pt|l|ml|dozen|case — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The price per single unit of the product at the time of order placement, before any line-level discounts or promotions are applied.
12-digit Universal Product Code barcode identifier used for point-of-sale scanning and product identification across retail systems.
The actual weight of the item picked and fulfilled, particularly important for variable-weight items like produce, meat, and deli products sold by weight.
The unit of measure for the actual weight field, such as pounds, ounces, kilograms, or grams.

business_glossary_term: Women Infants and Children (WIC) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this product is eligible for purchase using WIC program benefits. Required for WIC program compliance and reporting.
Unique identifier for the pharmacy prescription order record. Primary key representing the purchase-event view of an Rx fill within the order domain.
Reference to the licensed pharmacist who verified and dispensed the prescription.
Reference to the parent order header for combined grocery and pharmacy orders. Links the Rx order to the overall customer order transaction.

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: Regulatory reporting requires each Rx order to be linked to the specific pharmacy location that dispensed it for state compliance and inventory tracking.
Reference to the pharmacy patient record. Links the Rx order to the patient receiving the medication.
Reference identifier for the insurance claim submitted for this prescription fill. Links to third-party payer adjudication.
Indicates whether the medication is a controlled substance requiring DEA reporting and special handling.
Patient copayment amount in USD for this prescription fill after insurance adjudication.
Date and time this Rx order record was first created in the system.
Number of days the dispensed medication is intended to last based on prescribed dosing instructions.
DEA classification schedule for controlled substances (II through V). Non-controlled medications are marked as Non_Controlled.
Method by which the prescription was delivered to the patient: in-store pickup, curbside pickup, home delivery, or mail order.
Date and time the prescription was picked up or delivered to the patient.
Professional fee charged by the pharmacy for dispensing services in USD.

Physical form of the medication dispensed (tablet, capsule, liquid, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|liquid|injection|cream|ointment|inhaler|patch|suppository|suspension|solution — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Brand or generic name of the medication dispensed to the patient.
Eleven-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product dispensed. FDA-assigned unique identifier for drugs.
Dosage strength of the medication (e.g., 500mg, 10mcg). Includes unit of measure.
Sequential number indicating which fill this is for the prescription (1 for initial fill, 2+ for refills).

Current status of the prescription fill in the pharmacy workflow: received, in clinical verification, ready for patient pickup, dispensed to patient, on hold, cancelled, or returned. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|in_verification|ready_for_pickup|dispensed|on_hold|cancelled|returned — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date and time the prescription was filled and verified by the pharmacist.
Classification of the prescription fill event: new prescription, refill of existing prescription, transfer from another pharmacy, transfer to another pharmacy, or partial fill.
Six-digit Bank Identification Number identifying the patients insurance processor for electronic claims routing.
Group identifier for the patients insurance plan, typically employer or organization-specific.
Amount paid by insurance in USD for this prescription fill after adjudication.
Processor Control Number used by insurance plans to route claims to specific benefit plans.
Source system identifier from McKesson Pharmacy Systems for this prescription order record.
NDC code of the originally prescribed drug if a generic substitution was made.
Full name of the healthcare provider who prescribed the medication.
Ten-digit National Provider Identifier for the prescribing healthcare provider. Required for insurance claims and regulatory compliance.
Date after which the prescription is no longer valid for filling or refilling.
Unique prescription identifier assigned by the pharmacy system. Used for prescription tracking, refill management, and regulatory reporting.
Date and time the prescription was received by the pharmacy (electronically, by phone, or in person).
Date the prescription was originally written by the prescriber.
Number of units of medication dispensed to the patient (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters of liquid).
Number of authorized refills remaining on the prescription after this fill.
Prescription directions for use (Sig) provided to the patient. Instructions on how to take the medication.
Indicates whether generic substitution was permitted by the prescriber for this prescription.
Indicates whether a generic substitution was actually made when filling this prescription.
Total price charged for the prescription fill in USD before insurance coverage.
Date and time this Rx order record was last modified in the system.
Unique identifier for the allergen declaration record. Primary key for the allergen declaration entity.

business_glossary_term: Allergen Declaration Entered By Associate Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Allergen declarations are entered by a quality associate; essential for allergen compliance reporting and consumer safety alerts.
Reference to the product item (SKU) to which this allergen declaration applies. Links to the product master catalog.
Reference to the supplier or vendor who provided the allergen declaration information or ingredient specification.

Type of allergen declared per FDA FALCPA major food allergen categories: milk, eggs, fish, crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame (added 2023). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: milk|eggs|fish|shellfish|tree_nuts|peanuts|wheat|soybeans|sesame — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Name of third-party certification organization that validated the allergen-free claim (e.g., GFCO for gluten-free, FARE for allergen-free certification).
Expiration date of the allergen certification, after which re-certification or re-validation is required.
Unique certification or registration number issued by the certification body for allergen-free or allergen-controlled product claims.
Current regulatory compliance status of the allergen declaration against FDA FALCPA requirements and labeling standards.
Timestamp when this allergen declaration record was first created in the system.
Assessment of allergen cross-contact risk during manufacturing, processing, or handling based on facility design, cleaning procedures, and production scheduling.
Source of the allergen declaration information: supplier specification sheet, laboratory testing, ingredient analysis, manufacturer label review, or third-party certification body.
Laboratory test detection limit for allergen presence expressed in parts per million (ppm), used for quantitative allergen testing and threshold determination.
Boolean indicator whether this allergen declaration should be displayed on digital commerce channels (website, mobile app) for customer dietary filtering.
Date when this allergen declaration becomes effective and applicable to product labeling and customer communication.
Date when this allergen declaration expires or is superseded by a new declaration, typically due to formulation changes or supplier changes.
GS1 Global Trade Item Number for the product. Standard barcode identifier used for supply chain and point-of-sale scanning.
Specific ingredient or component that is the source of the allergen (e.g., 'whey protein concentrate' for milk allergen, 'soy lecithin' for soybean allergen).
Exact allergen statement text that appears on the product label or packaging, formatted per FDA requirements (e.g., 'Contains: Milk, Eggs, Wheat').
User ID or name of the person who last modified this allergen declaration record.
Timestamp when this allergen declaration record was last modified or updated.
Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the allergen declaration, including formulation changes, supplier communications, or regulatory guidance.
Optional precautionary allergen statement used when cross-contact risk exists (e.g., 'May contain traces of peanuts', 'Processed in a facility that also processes tree nuts').
Indicates the level of allergen presence: contains (ingredient), may_contain (cross-contact risk), free_from (certified absent), processed_in_facility (shared equipment warning).
Boolean indicator whether this allergen declaration applies to a private-label (store-owned brand) product requiring internal quality control oversight.
Date when a recall was initiated due to allergen mislabeling or undeclared allergen, if applicable.
Boolean indicator whether this allergen declaration is associated with a product recall due to undeclared allergen or labeling error.
Specific laboratory test method used to detect or quantify allergen presence (e.g., ELISA, PCR, mass spectrometry), if laboratory testing was performed.
Result of laboratory allergen testing: detected (allergen present), not_detected (allergen absent), below_threshold (trace amounts below action level), above_threshold (exceeds acceptable limit).
12-digit Universal Product Code used for retail scanning and inventory management in North America.
Date when the allergen declaration was last verified or validated by quality assurance or regulatory compliance team.
Method used to verify the allergen declaration: document review, laboratory analysis (ELISA/PCR), supplier audit, ingredient traceability review, or visual inspection.
User ID or name of the person who created this allergen declaration record, typically a quality assurance specialist or regulatory compliance analyst.
Unique identifier for the brand. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE.

Reference to the vendor designated as the category captain for the category where this brand competes. The category captain provides market insights, planogram recommendations, and assortment guidance. Used for vendor collaboration and category management.

Reference to the co-manufacturer or contract manufacturer who produces private-label products under this brand on behalf of Grocery. Null for non-private-label brands. Used for private-label sourcing and quality management.
Reference to the primary vendor or supplier who provides products under this brand. For CPG brands, this is the manufacturer. For private-label brands, this is the co-manufacturer or contract packer.
Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Brands are owned by legal entities; linking enables brand‑level profit and expense reporting.
Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Brand management is overseen by a specific associate; required for brand profitability reporting and accountability.

A comma-separated list of major food allergens that are present in products under this brand or manufactured in facilities that process these allergens. Used for FDA allergen labeling compliance and customer safety. Examples: milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame.
reference: FDA Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act (FALCPA)

description: Internal short code or abbreviation used for the brand in merchandising systems, planograms, and reporting. Serves as a business identifier for operational use.
A detailed narrative description of the brand, its history, values, product range, and unique selling propositions. Used for eCommerce content, marketing materials, and internal training.
The official marketing name of the brand as it appears on product packaging and promotional materials. This is the primary human-readable identifier for the brand.

Current lifecycle state of the brand. Active brands are currently sold and replenished. Inactive brands are temporarily not available. Discontinued brands are permanently removed from assortment. Pending launch brands are approved but not yet in stores. Phasing out brands are being replaced. Seasonal brands are active only during specific periods.

Quality and pricing tier of the brand within its category. Premium brands command higher price points and emphasize quality. Standard brands are mainstream offerings. Value and economy brands compete on price. Organic brands meet USDA organic certification. Specialty brands target niche segments.

Classification of the brand by market scope and ownership model. National brands are widely distributed CPG manufacturer brands. Regional brands have limited geographic distribution. Private-label and store brands are owned by Grocery. Generic brands are unbranded commodity products. Exclusive brands are third-party brands sold only at Grocery.

The primary country where the brand is headquartered or where the majority of its products are manufactured. Represented as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Used for country-of-origin labeling compliance and sourcing analytics.
The date and time when this brand record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
The date when the brand was permanently discontinued and removed from the assortment. Null for active brands. Used for historical analysis and SKU rationalization reporting.

Indicates whether this brand is sold exclusively at Grocery stores and not available through competing retailers. True for private-label brands and exclusive third-party brands. False for national and regional brands available at multiple retailers. Used for competitive differentiation analysis.

Indicates whether the brand is Fair Trade Certified by Fair Trade USA or another recognized fair trade certification body. True for certified brands. False otherwise. Used for ethical sourcing reporting and sustainability merchandising.

Indicates whether the brand is certified gluten-free by a recognized certification body such as GFCO (Gluten-Free Certification Organization) and meets FDA gluten-free labeling standards. True for certified brands. False otherwise. Used for allergen management and dietary preference merchandising.

The GS1-assigned company prefix that appears in the GTIN and UPC barcodes for all products under this brand. Used to identify the brand owner in global supply chain systems and for barcode validation.

Indicates whether the brand is certified halal by a recognized halal certification body and meets Islamic dietary law requirements. True for certified brands. False otherwise. Used for religious dietary compliance and specialty merchandising.

Indicates whether the brand is certified kosher by a recognized kosher certification agency and authorized to display a kosher symbol. True for certified brands. False otherwise. Used for religious dietary compliance and specialty merchandising.
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this brand record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this brand record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
The date when the brand was first introduced to the market or first carried by Grocery stores. Used for brand lifecycle analysis and new item introduction tracking.

Reference identifier or URI to the digital asset management system location where the official brand logo and visual identity assets are stored. Used for planogram design, ad circular production, and eCommerce merchandising.

Indicates whether the brand is verified by the Non-GMO Project and authorized to display the Non-GMO Project Verified seal. True for verified brands. False otherwise. Used for product labeling and consumer transparency.

Indicates whether the brand holds USDA organic certification and is authorized to use the USDA Organic seal on product packaging. True for certified organic brands. False otherwise. Used for regulatory compliance and organic category reporting.

The legal entity that owns the intellectual property rights to the brand. For CPG brands, this is the manufacturer or parent company. For private-label brands, this is Grocery or its subsidiary. For licensed brands, this is the licensor.

Reference identifier to the master packaging specification document or system record that defines standard packaging formats, materials, labeling requirements, and sustainability attributes for products under this brand. Used for private-label development and vendor compliance.
reference: Packaging Standards, FDA Food Labeling Requirements

description: The ultimate parent corporation that owns the brand owner entity. Used to group brands by corporate family for vendor relationship management and category analysis. May be the same as brand owner for independent brands.

A concise statement describing the brands value proposition, target customer segment, and competitive differentiation. Used for merchandising strategy, marketing alignment, and private-label brand development. Example: Premium organic dairy products for health-conscious families.

For private-label brands only, the current stage in the brand development lifecycle. Concept stage is initial ideation. Sourcing stage is vendor selection. Formulation stage is product development. Testing stage is quality validation. Approved stage is ready for launch. Launched stage is in-market. Mature stage is established. Null for non-private-label brands. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|sourcing|formulation|testing|approved|launched|mature — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The quality assurance and certification level for the brand. Tier 1 represents highest quality standards with rigorous testing. Tier 2 is standard quality. Tier 3 is basic quality. Certified organic meets USDA organic standards. Non-GMO meets Non-GMO Project verification. Fair Trade meets Fair Trade USA certification.
reference: USDA Organic Regulations, Non-GMO Project Standard, Fair Trade USA Standards

description: The name of any sustainability or environmental certification held by the brand, such as Rainforest Alliance, Marine Stewardship Council, Forest Stewardship Council, or B Corporation. Null if no sustainability certification. Used for ESG reporting and sustainable product merchandising. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rainforest_alliance|marine_stewardship_council|forest_stewardship_council|b_corporation|carbon_neutral|regenerative_organic|utz_certified — promote to reference product]

The primary product category or department where this brand is positioned. Used for category management, assortment planning, and category captain assignments. Examples include Dairy, Bakery, Frozen Foods, Health and Beauty, Pharmacy.
The official website URL for the brand where consumers can find product information, recipes, and brand story. Used for digital commerce integration and customer engagement.
Unique identifier for the pharmacy drug item record. Primary key for the drug item master.
Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Drug items extend core product items; FK creates proper inheritance.
Primary active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) responsible for the therapeutic effect. May include multiple ingredients separated by semicolons for combination drugs.
Proprietary brand name of the drug product as marketed by the manufacturer (e.g., Lipitor, Advil).
Indicates whether the drug is classified as a controlled substance requiring special handling, documentation, and security per DEA regulations.
Timestamp when the drug item record was first created in the pharmacy system.
DEA controlled substance classification (I through V) indicating abuse potential and regulatory restrictions. Non-scheduled indicates the drug is not a controlled substance.
Date the drug was discontinued by the manufacturer or removed from the pharmacy formulary. Null for active drugs.

Physical form in which the drug is manufactured and dispensed (e.g., tablet, capsule, liquid, injection). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|liquid|injection|cream|ointment|gel|patch|inhaler|suppository|powder|suspension|solution|spray|drops|lozenge — 16 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification code or group identifier used by pharmacy systems to screen for potential drug-drug interactions, contraindications, and adverse reactions.

Current lifecycle status of the drug item in the pharmacy inventory system. Active items are available for dispensing, discontinued items are no longer stocked, recalled items have FDA safety alerts, back-ordered items are temporarily unavailable, restricted items require special authorization.

Method used to track and manage drug expiration dates in inventory. Lot-based tracks by manufacturing lot, unit-based tracks individual units, FIFO (First In First Out) dispenses oldest stock first, FEFO (First Expired First Out) dispenses nearest expiration first.
Date the drug received FDA approval for marketing and distribution in the United States.
Insurance formulary classification indicating coverage tier and restrictions (preferred, non-preferred, excluded, or requiring prior authorization).
Non-proprietary generic name of the drug using the United States Adopted Name (USAN) or International Nonproprietary Name (INN) convention (e.g., atorvastatin, ibuprofen).
GS1 Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the drug product for supply chain and inventory management. 14-digit format.
Indicates whether the drug is classified as hazardous per NIOSH guidelines, requiring special handling, personal protective equipment, and disposal procedures.
Timestamp when the drug item record was last modified in the pharmacy system. Used for change tracking and data synchronization with McKesson Pharmacy Systems.
FDA-assigned 5-digit labeler code identifying the manufacturer or distributor. First segment of the NDC.
Name of the pharmaceutical company that manufactures or markets the drug product.
McKesson proprietary drug identifier used for ordering, inventory management, and integration with McKesson Pharmacy Systems drug database.
FDA Orange Book code indicating generic availability. Y = generic available, N = no generic available, O = original brand with generic equivalents.
FDA-assigned National Drug Code uniquely identifying the drug product, labeler, and package size. Standard 11-digit format with hyphens (5-4-2, 5-3-2, or 4-4-2 configuration).
Quantity and unit of measure for the drug package (e.g., 30 tablets, 100 mL, 1 vial). Represents the dispensing unit size.
Indicates whether the drug requires a valid prescription (Rx) or is available over-the-counter (OTC). True for prescription drugs, False for OTC drugs.
Indicates whether the drug must be stored under refrigerated conditions (2-8°C) to maintain stability and efficacy.
Indicates whether the drug has been repackaged from bulk containers into smaller dispensing units by a repackager rather than the original manufacturer.

Method by which the drug is administered to the patient (e.g., oral, topical, intravenous, intramuscular). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|topical|intravenous|intramuscular|subcutaneous|inhalation|rectal|ophthalmic|otic|nasal|transdermal|sublingual|buccal — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Required storage temperature condition to maintain drug stability and efficacy. Room temperature (15-25°C), refrigerated (2-8°C), frozen (<0°C), or controlled room temperature (20-25°C).
Concentration or potency of the active ingredient, including unit of measure (e.g., 10 mg, 500 mg/5 mL, 0.5%).
Pharmacological or therapeutic category indicating the drug's primary clinical use (e.g., antihypertensive, antibiotic, analgesic, antidiabetic).

Standard unit of measure for inventory tracking and dispensing (e.g., each, tablet, capsule, mL, gram). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|tablet|capsule|ml|gram|vial|bottle|box|tube|patch|inhaler — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
12-digit Universal Product Code barcode identifier used for point-of-sale scanning and inventory tracking of OTC pharmacy items.
Unique identifier for the fuel grade master record. Primary key for the fuel grade entity.

Foreign key linking to fuel.fuel_grade. Business justification: Required for fuel pricing and EPA compliance reports that join product fuel grade definitions with master fuel grade data; grocery analysts expect this direct reference.
Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Fuel procurement contracts require linking each fuel grade to its supplying vendor for pricing, compliance, and inventory management.
Indicates whether the fuel grade is currently available for sale at fuel centers. False for discontinued or seasonally unavailable grades.
Standard price increment above the base fuel grade (typically Regular Unleaded) in dollars per gallon. Used for automated pricing calculations and competitive positioning.
Percentage of biodiesel content in diesel fuel blends (e.g., 5.00 for B5, 20.00 for B20). Zero for non-diesel fuels. Required for EPA renewable fuel standard reporting.
Lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions in grams of CO2 equivalent per megajoule of energy (gCO2e/MJ). Used for Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) compliance in California and other jurisdictions.
Measure of diesel fuel ignition quality and combustion speed. Higher cetane numbers indicate better ignition properties. Typically 40-55 for diesel fuels. Null for gasoline products.
Lowest temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at which diesel fuel will flow through a standardized filtration device. Critical for winter operability. Applicable to diesel grades only.
reference: ASTM D6371 Cold Filter Plugging Point Standards

description: Date and time when the fuel grade master record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Date when the fuel grade was discontinued or when the specification version was superseded. Null for currently active grades. Used for historical analysis and regulatory reporting.
Date when the fuel grade became available for sale or when the current specification version became effective. Used for historical tracking and seasonal blend transitions.

business_glossary_term: Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Fuel Type Code
description: EPA-assigned code for fuel type classification used in emissions compliance reporting and fuel economy labeling. Maps to EPA fuel type taxonomy.
reference: EPA Fuel Economy Guide and 40 CFR Part 600

description: Percentage of ethanol content in the fuel blend (e.g., 10.00 for E10, 85.00 for E85). Zero for pure gasoline or diesel. Required for EPA renewable fuel standard reporting.
Minimum temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at which the fuel emits sufficient vapor to ignite. Critical for fire safety protocols and storage requirements.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the fuel grade (e.g., REG, MID, PREM, DSL, E85). Used for system integration and pump configuration.
reference: GS1 Global Trade Item Number (GTIN) Standards

description: Full descriptive name of the fuel grade (e.g., Regular Unleaded, Mid-Grade, Premium, Diesel, E85 Flex Fuel). Used for customer-facing displays and reporting.
High-level classification of the fuel product type. Aligns with EPA fuel type taxonomy for emissions reporting and regulatory compliance.
14-digit GS1 Global Trade Item Number uniquely identifying the fuel grade product for supply chain and inventory management. Used for cross-enterprise product identification.
Department of Transportation (DOT) hazardous materials classification for the fuel grade. Required for transportation, storage, and safety compliance.
Date and time when the fuel grade master record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Octane number indicating the fuel's resistance to knocking or pinging during combustion. Typically 87 for Regular, 89 for Mid-Grade, 91-93 for Premium. Null for diesel and non-gasoline fuels.
Numeric ranking of fuel grade pricing from lowest to highest (e.g., 1 for Regular, 2 for Mid-Grade, 3 for Premium). Used for pricing strategy and margin management.

Standard color coding for fuel grade selection buttons on dispensers (e.g., green for Regular, yellow for Mid-Grade, red for Premium, black for Diesel). Supports consistent customer experience across fuel centers.
reference: Industry Standard Fuel Dispenser Color Coding

description: Customer-facing label text displayed on fuel pump dispensers and signage. Includes grade name, octane rating, and ethanol content per FTC labeling requirements.
reference: FTC Fuel Rating Rule 16 CFR Part 306

description: Flag indicating whether the fuel meets EPA reformulated gasoline (RFG) requirements for ozone non-attainment areas. True for RFG-compliant fuels sold in designated metropolitan areas.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 80 Subpart D Reformulated Gasoline

description: Flag indicating whether the fuel grade qualifies as a renewable fuel under EPA Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS). True for ethanol blends, biodiesel, and other renewable fuels.
reference: EPA Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS) 40 CFR Part 80

description: Ratio of fuel density to water density at standard temperature. Used for volume-to-weight conversions in inventory reconciliation and delivery verification.
reference: ASTM D4052 Density and Relative Density Standards

description: Sulfur concentration in parts per million. EPA regulations mandate ultra-low sulfur diesel (ULSD) at 15 ppm maximum. Required for emissions compliance reporting.
Flag indicating whether the fuel grade is formulated for warm weather with lower Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP) to reduce evaporative emissions. Seasonal blend used from June through September.
reference: EPA 40 CFR Part 80 Subpart C Volatility Standards

description: Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials transportation (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline, UN1202 for diesel). Required for shipping documentation and emergency response.
reference: UN Recommendations on the Transport of Dangerous Goods

description: 12-digit Universal Product Code for the fuel grade. Used for Point of Sale (POS) transaction processing and inventory tracking at fuel centers.
Supplier or terminal product code used for wholesale fuel procurement and Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) transactions. Links to vendor catalog and supply chain systems.
reference: EDI 810 Invoice and 850 Purchase Order Standards

description: Flag indicating whether the fuel grade is formulated for cold weather performance with higher Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP). Seasonal blend used from November through March in most regions.
reference: ASTM D4814 Gasoline Seasonal Volatility Standards

description: Primary key for item_attribute

business_glossary_term: Item Attribute Maintained By Associate Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Attribute maintenance is performed by a data steward associate; needed for data quality metrics and compliance checks.
Reference to the parent item (SKU, UPC, PLU, or drug item) to which this attribute applies. Links to the item master catalog.
Indicates whether this attribute record is currently active and should be used for business operations. Supports soft delete and historical tracking.
Indicates whether the item has age restrictions for purchase (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, certain medications). Triggers POS age verification prompts.
reference: State and Federal Age Restriction Laws

description: The data type of the attribute value to enable proper parsing and validation. Supports string, numeric, boolean, date, and timestamp types.

Categorical grouping of the attribute for organizational and filtering purposes. Includes regulatory compliance, certifications, physical characteristics, marketing claims, sustainability, nutritional facts, allergen information, and safety warnings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory|certification|physical|marketing|sustainability|nutritional|allergen|safety — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The name or key of the attribute being stored (e.g., organic_certified, gluten_free_certified, country_of_origin, bioengineered_disclosure). Enables flexible schema extension without DDL changes.
External reference identifier or document number from the source system or certifying body that provided this attribute value. Supports audit trail and data lineage.

The value of the attribute stored as a string. May represent text, numeric, boolean, or date values depending on attribute_data_type. Supports regulatory flags, certifications, and compliance indicators.
Indicates whether the item contains bioengineered (genetically modified) ingredients and requires disclosure under the National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard. Mandatory for FDA compliance.
reference: FDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard

description: The unique certification or registration number issued by the certifying body. Used for audit trail and regulatory verification. Null for non-certified attributes.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Guide; USDA Organic Regulations

description: The name of the organization or regulatory agency that issued the certification or compliance designation (e.g., USDA, FDA, Non-GMO Project, Fair Trade USA, Orthodox Union for Kosher). Null for non-certified attributes.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Guide; USDA Organic Regulations

description: Current compliance status of the attribute with respect to regulatory or certification requirements. Tracks whether the item meets the declared standard.
reference: FDA Food Safety Modernization Act (FSMA); USDA Regulations

description: The DEA controlled substance schedule classification (Schedule I through V) for pharmacy items. Null for non-controlled substances. Required for pharmacy compliance and inventory controls.
The country where the item was produced, manufactured, or grown. Required for COOL (Country of Origin Labeling) compliance for certain food categories.
reference: USDA Country of Origin Labeling (COOL) Rule

description: The date and time when this attribute record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
Indicates whether the item is delivered directly to stores by the vendor rather than through the distribution center. Affects replenishment planning and receiving processes.
Indicates whether the item is eligible for purchase using EBT cards. Supports SNAP and other government assistance programs.
reference: USDA Food and Nutrition Service EBT Regulations

description: The date from which this attribute value becomes valid and applicable. Supports time-variant attribute tracking for regulatory compliance and product lifecycle management.
The date on which this attribute value expires or is no longer valid. Null for attributes with indefinite validity. Critical for certification renewals and regulatory compliance tracking.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Guide; USDA Organic Regulations

description: Indicates whether the item is certified fair trade by Fair Trade USA or equivalent certifying body. Supports sustainability and ethical sourcing claims.
Indicates whether the item is certified gluten-free by a recognized certifying body (e.g., GFCO, NSF). Required for gluten-free labeling claims under FDA regulations.
Indicates whether the item is certified halal by a recognized Islamic certifying body. Supports dietary preference filtering and marketing.

business_glossary_term: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) Hazard Classification
description: The HACCP hazard classification for the item (e.g., biological, chemical, physical). Used for food safety risk assessment and supply chain controls.
reference: FDA HACCP Principles and Application Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the item is certified kosher by a recognized rabbinical authority (e.g., OU, OK, Star-K). Supports dietary preference filtering and marketing.
The date and time when this attribute record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail.
The date when this attribute value was last verified or validated against the source system or certifying body. Used for data quality monitoring and compliance audits.
The minimum age required to purchase the item, if age restricted. Null for non-age-restricted items. Used for POS validation.
reference: State and Federal Age Restriction Laws

description: Indicates whether the item has been verified as non-GMO by the Non-GMO Project or equivalent certifying body. Supports marketing claims and consumer transparency.
Indicates whether the item is certified organic by the USDA or an accredited certifying agent. Required for organic labeling and marketing claims.
Indicates whether the item must be merchandised according to a specific planogram layout. Used for space allocation and compliance monitoring.
Indicates whether the item requires a valid prescription for purchase. Applicable to pharmacy drug items and certain medical devices.
reference: DEA Controlled Substances Act; State Pharmacy Regulations

description: Indicates whether the item is a store-owned private label brand product. Used for category management, margin analysis, and promotional planning.
The number of days the item remains safe and suitable for consumption or use under proper storage conditions. Used for inventory rotation (FIFO) and markdown planning.
reference: FDA Food Code; Internal Inventory Management Standards

business_glossary_term: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the item is eligible for purchase using SNAP benefits (formerly food stamps). Required for POS system configuration and regulatory reporting.
reference: USDA Food and Nutrition Service SNAP Regulations

description: The operational system or external data source from which this attribute was ingested (e.g., GS1 Data Sync, vendor EDI feed, certifying body API, Oracle Retail Item Management). Supports data lineage and troubleshooting.
The required temperature control zone for storage and transportation of the item. Critical for cold chain compliance and perishable inventory management.
The unit of measure for numeric attribute values (e.g., grams, ounces, milliliters, percent, degrees). Null for non-numeric attributes.

business_glossary_term: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Grade
description: The USDA quality grade assigned to the item (e.g., Prime, Choice, Select for beef; Grade A for eggs and dairy). Applicable to meat, poultry, dairy, and produce items.
reference: USDA Agricultural Marketing Service Grading Standards

business_glossary_term: Women Infants and Children (WIC) Program Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether the item is eligible for purchase using WIC benefits. Required for POS system configuration and state WIC program compliance.
reference: USDA Food and Nutrition Service WIC Regulations

description: Unique identifier for the item cost record. Primary key for the item cost entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for cost accounting reports that allocate each SKU's cost to a specific cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maps each item cost to the appropriate GL expense account for automatic posting in the general ledger.
Reference to the user or employee who approved this cost record. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance for cost change authorization.
Reference to the promotional event or campaign during which this promotional cost applies. Used to measure promotional lift and incremental margin impact.
Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this cost record applies. Links to the product master catalog.
Reference to the vendor or supplier providing this item at the specified cost. Each SKU-vendor combination may have distinct cost structures.
The date and time when this cost record was approved. Part of the audit trail for cost change management and financial controls.
The fundamental unit cost from the vendor before any allowances, discounts, or additional charges. This is the invoice line item cost per unit as stated by the supplier.

Post-invoice allowance claimed back from the vendor after the sale or promotion period. Requires submission of proof-of-performance documentation. Expressed in the same currency and unit basis as base cost.
External reference number or identifier of the vendor contract or purchase agreement under which this cost is defined. Links cost to legal or commercial terms.
Workflow approval status for this cost record. Indicates whether the cost has been reviewed and approved by procurement or finance before being applied to transactions.
The unit of measure on which the base unit cost is calculated. Indicates whether cost is per individual item (each), per case, per pallet, per unit weight, or per unit volume.
Free-text field for additional context, explanations, or special conditions related to this cost record. May include reasons for manual overrides, contract clauses, or vendor negotiation details.

Indicates the origin or method by which this cost was captured. EDI 832 is electronic price catalog from vendor; vendor contract is negotiated agreement; manual override is user-entered adjustment; system calculated is derived by ERP; purchase order is actual PO line cost.

Current lifecycle status of the cost record. Active indicates the cost is in use; pending indicates awaiting approval or effective date; expired indicates past effective end date; superseded indicates replaced by newer cost; suspended indicates temporarily inactive.

Classification of the cost record indicating the costing methodology applied. Standard cost is the baseline expected cost; actual cost reflects real invoice amounts; contract cost is negotiated rate; promotional cost applies during vendor promotions; landed cost includes all acquisition expenses; average cost is weighted average over time.

The difference between standard cost and actual cost for this SKU-vendor combination. Positive variance indicates actual cost exceeded standard; negative indicates savings. Used for procurement performance analysis.
The date and time when this cost record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the cost is denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).

The final, fully-loaded cost per unit after applying all allowances, discounts, freight, handling, and amortized fees. This is the true cost used for gross margin and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) calculations.
The date on which this cost record expires or is superseded by a new cost record. Null indicates the cost is currently active with no defined end date.
reference: SAP MM Material Master Effective Dating

description: The date from which this cost record becomes active and applicable for costing transactions. Supports time-based cost history and contract period tracking.
reference: SAP MM Material Master Effective Dating

description: Transportation cost allocated to this SKU for moving goods from vendor to distribution center or store. May be actual freight invoice or allocated based on weight/volume. Expressed per unit basis.
Warehouse or distribution center handling cost allocated to this SKU, including receiving, putaway, storage, and picking activities. Expressed per unit basis.
Boolean flag indicating whether this vendor is the primary or preferred source for this SKU. True if primary; false if secondary or backup vendor.
The date and time when this cost record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.
The most recent date on which this SKU was purchased from this vendor at this cost. Supports cost aging analysis and vendor performance tracking.
The number of days between placing a purchase order and receiving the goods from this vendor for this SKU. Used for replenishment planning and inventory optimization.
The minimum quantity that must be ordered from the vendor to qualify for this cost. Expressed in the same unit of measure as cost basis.
Immediate discount or allowance deducted directly from the invoice at the time of purchase. Reduces the base unit cost before payment. Expressed in the same currency and unit basis as base cost.
Reference to the specific purchase order from which this actual cost was derived. Applicable when cost type is actual and sourced from a PO receipt.
Allowance applied under scan-based trading arrangements where the retailer pays the vendor only when the item is sold (scanned at POS). Reflects the discount or rebate negotiated under SBT terms.
Amortized portion of the one-time slotting fee paid to secure shelf space for this SKU, allocated per unit over the expected sales volume or contract period. Contributes to total landed cost.
Unique identifier for each node in the product classification hierarchy. Primary key.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

business_glossary_term: Item Hierarchy Owner Associate Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Hierarchy ownership determines planogram and space allocation; associate owner is tracked for labor planning and audit.
Reference to the parent node in the hierarchy tree. Null for top-level department nodes. Enables recursive hierarchy traversal and roll-up calculations.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Reference to the lead vendor responsible for category management, assortment recommendations, and planogram design for this hierarchy node. Typically assigned at category or subcategory level.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Minimum age in years required to purchase items in this hierarchy node. Applies to alcohol, tobacco, and other age-restricted products. Enforced at POS with ID verification.
reference: State Alcohol and Tobacco Control Regulations

description: Average retail price per unit for items in this hierarchy node. Used in pricing strategy, competitive analysis, and ASP (Average Selling Price) reporting.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Price Management

description: Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are typically delivered via DSD (Direct Store Delivery) rather than through DC (Distribution Center). Affects receiving processes and vendor management.
Date when this hierarchy node was discontinued or retired. Null for active nodes. Used for historical reporting and category lifecycle analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Date when this hierarchy node became active and available for use in assortment planning, reporting, and SKU assignment. Supports temporal hierarchy analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are suitable for endcap (end-of-aisle display) placement. Used in planogram design and promotional space allocation.

business_glossary_term: FDA (Food and Drug Administration) Regulated Flag
description: Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are subject to FDA (Food and Drug Administration) regulation for food safety, labeling, or nutritional information disclosure.

business_glossary_term: Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) Target Percentage
description: Target GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) percentage for this hierarchy node. Used in merchandise planning and performance evaluation. Calculated as (Gross Margin / Average Inventory Cost) * 100.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Numeric level in the hierarchy tree. Typically: 1=Department, 2=Category, 3=Subcategory, 4=Segment, 5=Subsegment. Supports drill-down navigation and roll-up aggregation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Business name for the hierarchy level. Standard retail taxonomy labels used in category management and merchandise planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Timestamp when this hierarchy node record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Indicates whether this node is a leaf (has no children). True for lowest-level nodes that directly contain SKUs (Stock Keeping Units). Used to optimize query performance.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Minimum number of product facings (product fronts visible on shelf) required for items in this hierarchy node. Used in planogram compliance and shelf stocking guidelines.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: User ID or name of the person who last modified this hierarchy node. Used for audit trail and accountability in category management changes.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Business identifier code for the hierarchy node. Used in planograms, assortment planning, and financial reporting. Examples: DEPT01, CAT-DAIRY, SUBCAT-MILK.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Distance from root node (0 for root). Used for hierarchy traversal algorithms and display indentation in user interfaces.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Detailed description of the hierarchy node including scope, product types included, and merchandising guidelines.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Human-readable name of the hierarchy node. Examples: Fresh Produce, Dairy Products, Organic Milk, Private Label Snacks.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Full path from root to current node using node codes separated by forward slashes. Example: DEPT01/CAT-DAIRY/SUBCAT-MILK. Supports fast ancestor queries and breadcrumb navigation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Current lifecycle status of the hierarchy node. Active nodes are used in assortment planning and reporting. Inactive nodes are retained for historical analysis.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node require USDA organic certification. Drives supplier qualification, labeling requirements, and premium pricing strategies.
Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are perishable (fresh produce, dairy, meat, bakery). Drives cold chain requirements, FIFO (First In First Out) handling, and shrink management.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Indicates whether this hierarchy node contains primarily private-label (store-owned brand) products. Used for brand mix analysis and margin optimization.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Indicates whether this hierarchy node is frequently featured in ad circulars, BOGO (Buy One Get One) promotions, and TPR (Temporary Price Reduction) campaigns. Used in promotional planning.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Price Management

description: Standard replenishment cycle in days for items in this hierarchy node. Used in CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) and DC (Distribution Center) planning.
Primary seasonality pattern for demand in this hierarchy node. Used in seasonal assortment planning, inventory forecasting, and promotional calendar development.

Expected shrink (inventory loss from theft, spoilage, or damage) rate percentage for this hierarchy node. Used in inventory valuation, loss prevention planning, and COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) calculation.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Inventory Control

business_glossary_term: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are eligible for purchase using SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) or EBT (Electronic Benefits Transfer) benefits. Required for POS (Point of Sale) transaction processing.
Target percentage of total store shelf space allocated to this hierarchy node. Used in planogram design and space productivity analysis. Sum of all categories should equal 100% at store level.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Item Management

description: Target gross margin percentage for this hierarchy node. Used in pricing decisions, vendor negotiations, and financial planning. Calculated as ((Retail Price - Cost) / Retail Price) * 100.
reference: Oracle Retail Merchandising System - Price Management

description: Required temperature zone for storing and displaying items in this hierarchy node. Drives cold chain logistics, WMS (Warehouse Management System) slotting, and HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) compliance.
reference: FDA Food Code - Temperature Control Requirements

business_glossary_term: WIC (Women Infants and Children) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether items in this hierarchy node are eligible for purchase using WIC (Women, Infants, and Children) program benefits. Required for POS transaction processing and state reporting.
Unique identifier for the item lifecycle event record. Primary key.
Reference to the product category to which the item belonged at the time of this lifecycle event. Supports category management analysis.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lifecycle events generate costs that need allocation to the responsible cost center for budgeting and analysis.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Financial impact of lifecycle events (e.g., recalls) must be posted to a GL account for accurate financial statements.
Reference to the user who initiated or approved the lifecycle event. Null if the event was triggered by an automated system process.
Reference to the item (SKU) in the product catalog that this lifecycle event pertains to.
Reference to the vendor or supplier associated with the item at the time of this lifecycle event. Relevant for supplier-driven discontinuations, recalls, or quality issues.
Number of distribution centers impacted by this lifecycle event. Supports supply chain impact analysis.
Number of stores impacted by this lifecycle event. Relevant for recalls, discontinuations, and new item introductions to track distribution scope.
Indicates whether this lifecycle event required formal approval from management or a governance committee before execution.
Date and time when the lifecycle event was formally approved. Null if no approval was required or approval is pending.
Indicates whether this lifecycle event was triggered to maintain regulatory compliance (True) or for other business reasons (False).
Date and time when this lifecycle event record was first created in the data lakehouse. System audit field.
The date on which the lifecycle event becomes effective for business operations. May differ from event_timestamp for planned future events.
Estimated financial impact of the lifecycle event in USD, including inventory write-offs, recall costs, or lost sales. Used for financial planning and reporting.
Estimated quantity of inventory units affected by this lifecycle event across all locations at the time of the event. Used for recall scope and financial impact assessment.
Date and time when the lifecycle event occurred in the real world. This is the business event time, not the system record creation time.

Type of lifecycle event. Indicates the nature of the state change for the item (e.g., new item introduction, discontinuation, reactivation, reformulation, recall initiation, recall closure, regulatory hold, organic certification granted, organic certification revoked).
reference: FDA 21 CFR Part 7 (Recalls), USDA Organic Certification Standards

description: The lifecycle status of the item after this event was applied. Represents the target state of the transition.
Additional free-text notes or comments providing context, instructions, or follow-up actions related to the lifecycle event.
Indicates whether customer or stakeholder notifications were sent for this lifecycle event (e.g., recall notices, discontinuation alerts to vendors).
Date and time when notifications were sent to customers, vendors, or regulatory agencies regarding this lifecycle event.
The lifecycle status of the item immediately before this event occurred. Supports audit trail and state transition analysis.

Standardized code indicating the business reason for the lifecycle event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: low_sales|supplier_discontinuation|regulatory_violation|quality_issue|seasonal_rotation|product_refresh|contamination|labeling_error|allergen_mislabel|expired_certification|voluntary_recall|mandatory_recall|market_expansion|assortment_optimization — promote to reference product]
Free-text explanation providing additional context and details about why the lifecycle event was triggered. Complements the reason code with narrative detail.

FDA recall classification indicating the severity of the health hazard. Class I: dangerous or defective products that could cause serious health problems or death. Class II: products that might cause temporary health problem or slight threat of serious nature. Class III: products unlikely to cause adverse health reaction but violate FDA labeling or manufacturing regulations. Populated only for recall events.
Official recall identification number assigned by the FDA, USDA, or internal recall management system. Populated only for recall-related events.
The governing body or regulatory agency associated with this lifecycle event (e.g., FDA for food recalls, DEA for controlled substance holds, USDA for organic certification changes).
Name of the operational system of record from which this lifecycle event was captured (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System, SAP MM, McKesson Pharmacy Systems).
Unique identifier of the lifecycle event in the source operational system. Enables traceability back to the originating system.
URL or file path to supporting documentation for the lifecycle event (e.g., FDA recall notice, supplier discontinuation letter, reformulation specification sheet).

Name or identifier of the system that triggered the lifecycle event if it was automated (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System, SAP MM, Blue Yonder Demand Planning). Null if manually triggered by a user.
Date and time when this lifecycle event record was last modified in the data lakehouse. System audit field.
Unique identifier for the item packaging specification record. Primary key.
Reference to the parent product item for which this packaging specification applies. Links to the authoritative product catalog.
Reference to the supplier or manufacturer who provides this packaged item. Used for vendor performance tracking and supply chain management.
The three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product was manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations and trade compliance.
The date and time when this packaging specification record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The refundable deposit amount charged for returnable containers such as bottles and cans, measured in USD. Applicable in states with bottle bill legislation. Null for non-deposit items.
The date when this packaging specification is no longer valid for use. Null indicates currently active with no planned end date. Supports time-variant packaging changes and historical tracking.
The date when this packaging specification becomes active and valid for use in the supply chain. Supports time-variant packaging changes and historical tracking.
Total weight of the packaging level including product and all packaging materials, measured in pounds. Used for freight calculations and warehouse capacity planning.

The GS1 Global Trade Item Number assigned to this specific packaging level. GTIN-8, GTIN-12 (UPC), GTIN-13 (EAN), or GTIN-14 (case/pallet) format. Used for scanning, EDI transactions, and supply chain traceability.
Indicates whether the packaging contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and transportation compliance per DOT regulations.
The vertical dimension of the packaging unit measured in inches. Used for warehouse slotting, transportation planning, and planogram design.
The number of layers stacked vertically on a pallet in the Ti-Hi pallet configuration. Used for warehouse receiving, DC slotting, and transportation planning. Null for non-pallet packaging levels.
The date and time when this packaging specification record was most recently updated in the data platform. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The longest dimension of the packaging unit measured in inches. Used for warehouse slotting, transportation planning, and planogram design.
The maximum number of units that can be safely stacked vertically. Used for warehouse safety compliance and space planning. Null if not stackable.
Weight of the product contents only, excluding packaging materials, measured in pounds. Used for nutritional labeling and FDA compliance.

The hierarchical level of packaging in the distribution chain. Each represents the consumer unit, inner pack is a sub-case grouping, case is the standard shipping unit, pallet is the warehouse unit, display shipper is a retail-ready merchandising unit, and master case is an outer shipping container.

The primary material composition of the packaging at this level. Used for sustainability reporting, recycling programs, and environmental compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrugated_cardboard|plastic|glass|metal|wood|composite|flexible_film — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Current lifecycle status of this packaging specification. Active indicates current use in supply chain, inactive indicates temporarily out of use, discontinued indicates permanently retired, pending approval indicates awaiting vendor or regulatory approval, seasonal indicates used only during specific periods.
Indicates whether the packaging material is recyclable through standard municipal recycling programs. Supports sustainability reporting and consumer labeling requirements.
The name of the operational system of record from which this packaging specification was sourced (e.g., SAP MM, Oracle Retail, Manhattan WMS). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.
The unique identifier for this packaging record in the source operational system. Used for data reconciliation and traceability back to the system of record.
Indicates whether this packaging level can be safely stacked on top of identical units during storage and transportation. Used for warehouse space optimization and load planning.
The Harmonized Tariff Schedule classification code for this packaged item. Used for customs clearance, duty calculation, and international trade compliance.
Indicates whether this packaging level requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation (cold chain). Applicable to perishables, frozen foods, and pharmacy items.
The number of cases per layer on a pallet in the Ti-Hi pallet configuration. Used for warehouse receiving, DC slotting, and transportation planning. Null for non-pallet packaging levels.
The number of base units (each) contained within this packaging level. For example, a case may contain 24 eaches, a pallet may contain 60 cases.
The 12-digit UPC barcode for this packaging level, primarily used at point-of-sale in North America. Subset of GTIN-12.
The suppliers internal item identifier for this packaging configuration. Used for purchase orders, EDI transactions, and cross-reference with vendor catalogs.
The total cubic volume of the packaging unit measured in cubic feet. Calculated from length, width, and height. Used for warehouse capacity planning and transportation optimization.
The width dimension of the packaging unit measured in inches. Used for warehouse slotting, transportation planning, and planogram design.
Unique identifier for the item regulatory flag record. Primary key.
Reference to the product item to which this regulatory flag applies.
Free-text notes capturing audit trail information, compliance review findings, or historical changes to the regulatory flag status.
reference: SOX Internal Controls, FDA Compliance Policy Guides

description: Unique certification or approval number issued by the certifying body for traceability and audit purposes.
Name of the organization or government agency that issued the certification or approval (e.g., USDA, FDA, State Board of Pharmacy, third-party certifier).
reference: FDA, USDA, DEA, State Boards of Pharmacy

description: Current status of the regulatory compliance flag indicating whether the certification or approval is active, expired, suspended, pending, revoked, or under review.
reference: FDA Compliance Policy Guides, USDA Organic Regulations

description: Timestamp when this regulatory flag record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Indicates whether this regulatory flag should be displayed in digital commerce channels (website, mobile app) for customer filtering and product discovery. True if displayable, False otherwise.
reference: FTC Advertising Guidelines, FDA Food Labeling Requirements

description: Standardized text or statement required for regulatory disclosure on labels, receipts, or digital channels (e.g., 'Bioengineered Food', 'USDA Organic').
reference: FDA Food Labeling Guide, FDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard

description: Date when the regulatory flag or certification becomes effective and can be used for compliance purposes at POS and in digital channels.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Requirements, USDA Organic Certification

description: Date when the regulatory flag or certification expires and is no longer valid for compliance purposes. Nullable for flags without expiration.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Requirements, USDA Organic Certification

description: Type of regulatory compliance flag or certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: snap_eligible|wic_approved|ebt_eligible|organic_certified|non_gmo|gluten_free_certified|kosher|halal|fair_trade|usda_grade|fda_bioengineered_disclosure|allergen_free — promote to reference product]
reference: FDA Food Labeling Guide, USDA Organic Regulations, State Boards of Pharmacy

description: Indicates whether this regulatory flag requires mandatory disclosure on product labels or packaging (e.g., FDA bioengineered food disclosure). True if disclosure required, False otherwise.
reference: FDA Food Labeling Guide, FDA Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard

description: Timestamp when this regulatory flag record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Scheduled date for the next compliance review or recertification audit to ensure ongoing regulatory adherence.
reference: FDA Compliance Policy Guides, USDA Organic Regulations

description: Indicates whether this regulatory flag enables special transaction eligibility at POS systems (e.g., SNAP/EBT/WIC payment acceptance). True if eligible, False otherwise.
reference: SNAP Retailer Regulations, WIC Program Guidelines, PCI DSS

description: Geographic or administrative jurisdiction where this regulatory flag applies (e.g., USA, California, European Union). Uses 3-letter country codes or state abbreviations.
reference: FDA, USDA, State Boards of Pharmacy, EPA

description: Name of the operational system of record that originated this regulatory flag data (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System, SAP Retail MM).
Unique identifier for this regulatory flag record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.
URL or file path to supporting documentation, certificates, or audit reports that validate the regulatory flag or certification.
Date when the regulatory flag or certification was last verified or audited by the certifying body or internal compliance team.
reference: FDA Compliance Policy Guides, USDA Organic Regulations

description: Unique identifier for the item-vendor association record.

business_glossary_term: Item Vendor Procurement Associate Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Procurement ownership of vendor contracts is assigned to an associate; needed for spend analysis and supplier performance dashboards.
Reference to the item (SKU) in this vendor relationship.
Reference to the vendor or supplier providing this item.
Reference to the master supply agreement or contract governing this item-vendor relationship. Links to vendor contract management system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the vendor cost (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where this item is manufactured or grown by this vendor. Required for customs, labeling, and country-of-origin marketing.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes, FDA Food Labeling

description: Timestamp when this item-vendor relationship record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether this vendor delivers directly to stores, bypassing the distribution center. Common for bread, dairy, snacks, and beverages.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Transaction Set Supported
description: Comma-separated list of EDI transaction sets supported by this vendor for this item (e.g., 850 Purchase Order, 856 Advance Ship Notice, 810 Invoice). Blank if vendor does not support EDI.
Date when this item-vendor relationship expires or is terminated. Null indicates an open-ended relationship.
Date when this item-vendor relationship becomes active and can be used for purchase order creation.
Current lifecycle status of this item-vendor sourcing relationship. Blocked status prevents new purchase orders; discontinued indicates vendor no longer supplies this item.
Date when the vendor cost was last updated in the system. Used for cost change tracking and margin analysis.
Timestamp when this item-vendor relationship record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.
Date of the most recent purchase order placed with this vendor for this item. Used for vendor activity analysis and relationship management.
Date of the most recent goods receipt from this vendor for this item at any distribution center or store. Used for vendor performance tracking.
The smallest quantity that can be ordered from this vendor for this item, expressed in the vendor's unit of measure.
The quantity increment in which this item must be ordered (e.g., case pack size, pallet quantity). Orders must be in multiples of this value.
Indicates whether this vendor is the primary or preferred source for this item. Used in automated replenishment and purchase order generation.
Standard promotional discount percentage offered by this vendor for this item during promotional periods. Used in temporary price reduction (TPR) planning.
Indicates whether a return merchandise authorization (RMA) is required from this vendor before returning defective or unsold items.
One-time or recurring fee paid by the vendor for shelf space allocation for this item. Common in new item introductions and category resets.
The current negotiated cost per vendor unit of measure. Used for purchase order pricing and gross margin calculations. Subject to contract terms and promotional allowances.
The vendor's internal catalog or part number for this item, used in purchase orders and EDI transactions.
Number of days from purchase order submission to expected delivery at the distribution center or store. Critical for replenishment planning and safety stock calculations.
Number of consumer units (eaches) contained in the vendor's shipping unit (case or pallet). Used for unit conversion between vendor ordering and store selling units.
Quality certifications held by the vendor for this item (e.g., USDA Organic, Non-GMO Project, Fair Trade, HACCP, SQF). Pipe-separated list if multiple certifications apply.
Performance rating for this vendor on this item, based on fill rate, on-time delivery, and quality metrics. Used in vendor selection and category captain decisions.

The unit of measure used by the vendor for ordering and invoicing this item. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|case|pallet|pound|kilogram|liter|gallon|dozen|box — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The UPC or GTIN assigned by the vendor for this item, may differ from retailer's UPC in case of private label or vendor-specific packaging.
Unique identifier for the nutritional information record. Primary key.

business_glossary_term: Nutritional Info Entered By Associate Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Nutrition label data entry is done by a compliance associate; required for FDA reporting and label accuracy audits.
Reference to the product (SKU) this nutritional information applies to. Links to the authoritative product catalog.
Added sugars content per serving, measured in grams. Mandatory disclosure per updated FDA Nutrition Facts label requirements (2016 rule).

Full allergen declaration statement as it appears on the product label, including 'Contains' and 'May Contain' statements. Used for customer-facing allergen disclosure on eCommerce and in-store digital displays.
Indicates whether the product contains eggs or egg-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
Indicates whether the product contains fish or fish-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
Indicates whether the product contains milk or milk-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
reference: FDA FALCPA (Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act)

description: Indicates whether the product contains peanuts or peanut-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
Indicates whether the product contains sesame or sesame-derived ingredients. Added as the 9th major allergen per FASTER Act (2021).
reference: FDA FASTER Act (Food Allergy Safety, Treatment, Education, and Research Act)

description: Indicates whether the product contains crustacean shellfish or shellfish-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
Indicates whether the product contains soybeans or soy-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
Indicates whether the product contains tree nuts (almonds, walnuts, cashews, etc.) or tree nut-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
Date when allergen information was last verified by the vendor or certifying body. Critical for recall management and compliance auditing.
Indicates whether the product contains wheat or wheat-derived ingredients. Part of FALCPA major food allergen declaration.
Indicates whether the product contains bioengineered (genetically modified) ingredients, as required by USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard.
reference: USDA National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard

description: Calcium content per serving, measured in milligrams. Mandatory micronutrient disclosure per FDA regulations.
Total calories per serving, expressed in kilocalories (kcal). Mandatory field for FDA Nutrition Facts label.
Cholesterol content per serving, measured in milligrams. Required nutrient on FDA Nutrition Facts label.
Source of the nutritional information data (vendor-supplied via GS1 Sync, laboratory analysis, USDA nutrient database, manual entry). Used for data quality assessment and audit trails.

The date when this PLU code and its associated pricing became effective. Used for price change tracking and historical analysis.

Indicates whether the item carries Fair Trade certification. True if certified, False otherwise. Used for marketing, customer transparency, and ethical sourcing reporting.

description: The gross margin percentage for this item, calculated as (price - cost) / price * 100. Used for profitability analysis and category management. Business-confidential financial metric.

Human-readable description of the produce or bulk item (e.g., 'Fuji Apples', 'Organic Bananas', 'Bulk Almonds').
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date and time when this PLU code record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
Indicates whether the item is sourced from local farms or suppliers within a defined geographic radius. True if locally sourced, False otherwise. Used for marketing and sustainability reporting.
Free-text or structured field containing key nutritional facts (calories, fat, carbohydrates, protein, vitamins per serving). Used for customer information and FDA nutrition labeling compliance.

Indicates whether the item is certified organic. True if organic (typically PLU codes starting with 9), False otherwise. Used for pricing, labeling, and compliance with USDA organic certification requirements.

The 4-digit or 5-digit PLU code assigned to the item. 5-digit codes starting with 9 indicate organic products per IFPS convention. This is the externally-known unique identifier used at POS for manual entry.

Current lifecycle status of the PLU code. Active codes are available for sale; inactive codes are temporarily unavailable; discontinued codes are permanently removed; seasonal codes are available only during specific periods.
The abbreviated or formatted name displayed on POS terminals and customer receipts. May differ from the full item description for space constraints.

Free-text description of the seasonal availability window for this item (e.g., 'Summer only', 'Year-round', 'Fall harvest'). Used for assortment planning and customer communication.
