14-digit GTIN of the affected product. Global standard identifier for supply chain and distribution tracking.
Comma-separated list of affected lot or batch numbers. Used to identify specific production runs subject to recall.
Detailed description of the affected product including brand name, product name, package size, and any distinguishing characteristics to aid in identification.
Comma-separated list of US state codes where the recalled product was distributed. Used for targeted store notification and consumer alerts.

FDA recall classification indicating severity of health risk. Class I: life-threatening or serious injury risk. Class II: temporary or reversible adverse health consequences. Class III: unlikely to cause adverse health consequences.
Date when the regulatory agency officially closed the recall case. Indicates successful completion of all regulatory requirements.
Date when all recall activities (store removal, consumer notification, disposition) were completed. Precedes regulatory closure.

value_regex: Destroy|Return to Vendor|Hold for Inspection|Dispose per HACCP
description: Instructions for handling recalled product. Destroy: dispose of product. Return to Vendor: ship back to supplier. Hold for Inspection: quarantine pending agency review. Dispose per HACCP: follow food safety protocols.
Geographic scope of product distribution. Indicates whether the recall affects national, regional, state-level, or local distribution.
Estimated total financial impact of the recall including product cost, disposal costs, labor, and potential liability. Measured in USD. Business-confidential financial data.
URL link to the official FDA enforcement report for this recall. Provides access to the public regulatory record.
FDA health hazard evaluation summary describing the potential health risk to consumers, including severity, likelihood of occurrence, and affected populations.
Date the recall was officially initiated by the agency or manufacturer. Marks the start of the recall lifecycle.
Regulatory agency or entity that initiated the recall. FDA: food/drug/cosmetic. USDA: meat/poultry. CPSC: consumer product safety. DEA: controlled substances. Manufacturer: voluntary firm-initiated.
Internal case tracking number assigned by the retailer for recall management and cross-functional coordination across supply chain, store operations, and legal teams.
Timestamp when this recall record was last modified in the data platform. Audit trail field.
Indicates whether loyalty program purchase history was used to identify and directly notify customers who purchased the recalled product.

Phone number of the manufacturer contact person. Business-confidential organizational contact data.
Date when the recalled product was physically removed from store shelves and inventory. Marks completion of store-level recall execution.
End date of the production date range for affected items. Defines the latest production date of recalled products.
Start date of the production date range for affected items. Defines the earliest production date of recalled products.
Total quantity of product units subject to recall. Measured in units appropriate to the product (cases, pounds, bottles, etc.).
Unit of measure for the quantity recalled field. Defines how the recalled quantity is measured.
Detailed explanation of the reason for the recall, including the specific defect, contamination, mislabeling, or health hazard that triggered the action.
Official recall number assigned by the initiating agency (FDA, USDA, CPSC, DEA, or manufacturer). Serves as the external business identifier for regulatory reporting and cross-agency coordination.
Current lifecycle status of the recall. Tracks progression from initiation through store notification, product removal, completion, and regulatory closure.
Type of recall action. Voluntary: firm-initiated removal. Mandatory: agency-ordered removal. Market Withdrawal: removal of product not subject to FDA enforcement action.
Name of the source system that originated this recall record (e.g., Oracle Retail, SAP, Manual Entry).
Unique identifier of this recall record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Timestamp when store operations were notified of the recall. Critical for tracking compliance with pull-from-shelf execution timelines.

Unique identifier for the promotional calendar entry. Primary key for the promotional calendar planning entity.

Reference to the store cluster or zone that this promotional calendar entry applies to. Store clusters group stores by geography, demographics, or performance characteristics for targeted promotional planning. Nullable if promotion applies to all stores.
Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Calendar budgeting and supplier‑funded promotions need supplier attribution for financial reconciliation.

Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry will be featured in the printed weekly ad circular flyer. True means print ad placement is planned; False means digital-only or in-store signage only.
The week number within the fiscal year that this promotional calendar entry corresponds to, used for weekly ad circular planning and coordination. Aligns with retail fiscal calendar conventions.
The date when this promotional calendar entry received executive or merchandising leadership approval to proceed. Nullable if still in draft or planning status.
Name or identifier of the executive or manager who approved this promotional calendar entry. Used for governance and audit trail purposes. Nullable if not yet approved.

Indicates whether this calendar entry represents a blackout period during which no new promotions should be scheduled. True means this is a blackout period (e.g., inventory count week, system maintenance); False means normal promotional activity is allowed.
Short alphanumeric code used to reference the promotional calendar in operational systems and reports (e.g., 'Q4-2024-HOL', 'SP-FRESH-01').
Business-friendly name for the promotional calendar period (e.g., 'Q4 Holiday Campaign 2024', 'Spring Fresh Produce Event').

Current lifecycle state of the promotional calendar entry. Draft indicates initial planning; planned means dates are set but not approved; approved indicates executive sign-off; active means promotion is currently running; completed means promotion has ended; cancelled indicates promotion was terminated before completion.

Classification of the promotional calendar entry by its business purpose and funding model. Weekly ad circular represents recurring weekly promotional cycles; seasonal event covers spring, summer, fall, winter campaigns; holiday promotion targets specific holidays; category event focuses on specific product categories; vendor funded indicates supplier-sponsored promotions; markdown clearance represents end-of-season or overstock price reductions.

Free-text notes documenting any promotional conflicts detected with other calendar entries (overlapping dates, same category, same store cluster) and how they were resolved. Used for planning coordination and audit trail.
The date and time when this promotional calendar entry was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and planning timeline analysis.

Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry includes digital coupon offers that customers can clip via mobile app or website. True means digital coupons are part of the promotion; False means no digital coupon component.
The date when the promotional period ends. This is the last day that promotional pricing and offers are valid. Nullable for open-ended promotions.

The estimated total budget allocated for this promotional calendar entry, including markdown costs, advertising spend, and vendor co-op funding. Expressed in the retailer's reporting currency (typically USD).
The marketing theme or creative concept for the promotional period (e.g., 'Back to School', 'Summer BBQ', 'Holiday Feast', 'Fresh Start January'). Used for merchandising and marketing alignment.
The fiscal quarter (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) to which this promotional calendar entry belongs, used for quarterly promotional planning and performance analysis.
The fiscal year to which this promotional calendar entry belongs, following the retailer's fiscal calendar (e.g., 2024, 2025).

Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry is exclusive to loyalty program members. True means only loyalty cardholders can access the promotional pricing; False means promotion is available to all customers.
Free-text notes from merchandising team regarding assortment planning, display requirements, inventory positioning, or special handling instructions for this promotional calendar entry.
Name or identifier of the user who last modified this promotional calendar entry. Used for accountability and audit trail purposes.
The date and time when this promotional calendar entry was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.

Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry can overlap with other promotions in the same category or store cluster. True allows concurrent promotions; False enforces exclusivity to prevent promotional conflicts and margin erosion.

Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry is tied to a contractual slotting fee commitment with a vendor. True means vendor has paid for guaranteed promotional placement during this period; False means no slotting commitment exists.
The date when the promotional period begins. This is the first day that promotional pricing, offers, or ad circular content becomes effective in stores and digital channels.

The primary sales channel where this promotional calendar entry will be executed. In-store represents physical retail locations; online represents eCommerce website; mobile app represents mobile application; omnichannel indicates promotion spans all channels.

business_glossary_term: Target Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI)
description: The target Gross Margin Return on Investment ratio for this promotional calendar entry. GMROI measures the profitability of inventory investment and is a key performance metric for promotional effectiveness in retail. Calculated as gross margin dollars divided by average inventory cost.

Indicates whether this promotional calendar entry includes vendor-funded promotional support (slotting fees, co-op advertising, markdown allowances). True means vendor is contributing funding; False means retailer-funded only.

Identifier of the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) manufacturer or vendor funding the coupon offer. Null if retailer-funded.
GS1-compliant barcode number for Point of Sale (POS) scanning. Typically UPC (Universal Product Code) or EAN format for digital coupon redemption.
Current activation and usage status of the digital coupon: available for clipping, clipped by customer, redeemed at Point of Sale (POS), or expired without redemption.
Date and time when the customer activated or clipped the digital coupon to their loyalty account. Null if coupon has not been clipped.
Unique alphanumeric code identifying the digital coupon offer. Used for tracking and redemption validation.
Date and time when the digital coupon record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and coupon lifecycle tracking.
Current cumulative number of times this digital coupon has been redeemed across all customers. Used to enforce total redemption limits.
Detailed description of the digital coupon offer including terms, conditions, eligible products, and redemption instructions.
Type of discount mechanism applied by the coupon: percentage off, dollar amount off, Buy One Get One (BOGO), fixed price, or free item.
Date when the digital coupon becomes valid and available for customer clipping and redemption.
Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) identifiers eligible for this digital coupon offer. Null if coupon applies to entire category or store-wide.
Comma-separated list of store identifiers where this digital coupon can be redeemed. Null if coupon is valid at all locations or online only.
Comma-separated list of Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) identifiers explicitly excluded from this digital coupon offer despite category or store-wide eligibility.
Date when the digital coupon expires and can no longer be clipped or redeemed. After this date, the coupon is invalid.
Monetary value or percentage discount amount displayed on the coupon. For percentage discounts, represents the percentage; for dollar discounts, represents the dollar amount.
Uniform Resource Locator (URL) of the promotional image or graphic associated with the digital coupon for display in mobile app, website, and email.
Digital channel through which the coupon was originally issued or made available to customers: mobile app, website, email campaign, SMS, loyalty portal, or social media.

Indicates whether purchases made using this digital coupon are still eligible for loyalty program points accrual. True if points can be earned, false if coupon disqualifies points.

description: Indicates whether the coupon discount is funded by a Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) manufacturer or vendor rather than by the retailer. True if manufacturer-funded, false if retailer-funded.
Maximum dollar amount that can be discounted when redeeming this coupon. Applies primarily to percentage-based offers to cap total discount.
Maximum number of times a single customer can redeem this digital coupon during the offer period. Null indicates unlimited redemptions per customer.
Minimum transaction subtotal required to qualify for coupon redemption. Null if no minimum purchase requirement exists.

Method by which the coupon was personalized or targeted: mass distribution to all customers, targeted based on segment, behavioral analysis, demographic profile, or predictive Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) modeling.
Display priority ranking for the digital coupon when multiple offers are available. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for customer presentation.
Quick Response code data payload for mobile app or self-checkout scanning. Encodes coupon identifier and validation data.
Indicates whether this digital coupon can be combined or stacked with other coupons, promotions, or Temporary Price Reductions (TPR) in a single transaction. True if stackable, false if exclusive.
Defines which store locations or channels are eligible for coupon redemption: all stores, specific store list, store cluster, online orders only, or in-store purchases only.
Full legal terms and conditions governing the use, redemption, and restrictions of the digital coupon. Required for regulatory compliance and customer transparency.
Customer-facing title or headline of the digital coupon offer displayed in mobile app, website, and loyalty platform.
Total number of redemptions allowed across all customers before the coupon offer is exhausted. Null indicates no aggregate limit.
Unique identifier for the offer eligibility rule. Primary key.
Identifier of the user or system account that created the eligibility rule record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Reference to the customer segment that this rule targets. Used for household segmentation and personalized deal targeting.
Reference to the promotional offer that this eligibility rule governs.
Reference to the store cluster or group where the offer is valid. Used to target specific store formats, market types, or performance tiers.

Sales channel or fulfillment method where the offer is valid. Restricts eligibility to specific shopping channels such as in-store, online, mobile app, curbside pickup, or delivery.
Free-text field capturing regulatory compliance considerations, legal restrictions, or program-specific requirements (e.g., SNAP, WIC, age verification) associated with the rule.

Frequency at which the eligibility rule is evaluated or refreshed. Determines whether qualification is checked in real-time at transaction, on a batch schedule, or on-demand.
Business rationale or explanation for why certain customers or segments are excluded from the offer. Provides transparency for compliance and customer service purposes.
Code representing the geographic zone, region, or market where the offer is valid. Used to restrict offers to specific trading areas, ZIP codes, or store clusters.
Date and time when the eligibility rule record was most recently updated.
Maximum age in years for customer eligibility. Used for youth-targeted promotions or senior discount programs with upper age limits.

Indicates whether the offer is restricted to new customers only. Used for customer acquisition campaigns and first-time shopper incentives.
Numeric ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple eligibility rules apply to the same offer. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.
Minimum cumulative purchase amount required in the customer's transaction history to qualify for the offer. Used for spend-based eligibility rules.
Number of days in the lookback period for evaluating purchase history thresholds. Defines the time window over which customer spending or transaction counts are assessed.

Structured expression or formula defining the eligibility logic. May contain conditional operators, attribute references, and threshold values that are evaluated to determine customer qualification.
Human-readable name or label for the eligibility rule, used for identification and reporting purposes.
Current lifecycle status of the eligibility rule. Indicates whether the rule is actively enforced, temporarily suspended, in draft state, or expired.

SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for internal codes, UPC (Universal Product Code) for standard barcodes, GTIN (Global Trade Item Number) for GS1 standards, PLU (Price Look-Up Code) for produce, EAN for European Article Numbers, ISBN for books.

Mechanical role this item plays in the promotion structure. Buy item vs get item in BOGO (Buy One Get One) offers, trigger item vs reward item in mix-and-match promotions, qualifying item for threshold-based offers, or bundle component in multi-item packages.
Multiplier applied to loyalty reward points earned on this item during the promotion. For example, 2.0 means double points, 3.0 means triple points.

description: Timestamp when this offer item association record was last modified. Tracks changes to promotion eligibility, discount overrides, and item roles.

Code indicating the visual merchandise display plan location where this promotional item should be placed (endcap, gondola, front-of-store, checkout lane). Supports execution of promotional planograms.
Ranking used to determine which promotion applies when an item qualifies for multiple overlapping offers. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.

Unit of measure for required and maximum quantities. Each for discrete items, weight units for produce and bulk goods, volume units for liquids, case or pack for wholesale quantities. business_glossary_term: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this promotional item is eligible for purchase using SNAP benefits (EBT - Electronic Benefits Transfer). Required for compliance with USDA SNAP regulations.

Code identifying the operational system of record that originated this offer item association (Oracle Retail Price Management, SAP SD Sales and Distribution, proprietary promotion engine). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Indicates whether item substitution is permitted if the promoted item is out of stock (OOS). Relevant for online order fulfillment and click-and-collect services.

Indicates whether the promotional discount for this item is funded by the vendor (manufacturer) rather than the retailer. Used for slotting fee negotiations and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.

business_glossary_term: WIC (Women Infants and Children Program) Eligible Flag
description: Indicates whether this promotional item is eligible for purchase using WIC benefits. Required for compliance with USDA WIC program regulations.

Unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice record. Primary key for the AP invoice lifecycle from receipt through payment clearing.
Identifier of the associate (manager or finance analyst) who approved the invoice for payment. Supports audit trail and SOX compliance for segregation of duties.
Identifier of the company bank account from which payment was disbursed. Supports multi-bank operations and cash management reporting.

The cost center to which this invoice expense is allocated. Used for departmental budgeting, variance analysis, and management accounting. Typically applies to non-merchandise invoices (services, utilities, supplies).
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: ap_invoice needs a proper reference to the GL account master; gl_account_id replaces gl_account_code for normalization.
Reference to the warehouse or store goods receipt document used in three-way match. Confirms physical receipt of merchandise or materials before invoice approval.
Identifier of the SAP automatic payment program run (F110) that processed this invoice. Groups invoices paid together in a batch for reconciliation and audit purposes.
Reference to the originating purchase order for three-way match validation (PO, goods receipt, invoice). Null for non-PO invoices such as utility bills, rent, or service agreements.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.shipment. Business justification: Freight Invoice Reconciliation: AP Invoice for carrier freight must reference the Shipment it settles, enabling the Freight Cost Reconciliation report.

Identifier of the vendor or supplier who issued this invoice. Links to the vendor master record for merchandise suppliers, DSD (Direct Store Delivery) vendors, service providers, and operational expense vendors.
The date the invoice was approved for payment. Used to measure approval cycle time and identify bottlenecks in the AP workflow.
Current state of the invoice in the approval workflow. Tracks manager or finance approval for invoices requiring authorization before payment, supporting SOX internal control requirements.
The actual amount paid to the vendor after applying any last-minute adjustments, discounts taken, or partial payments. May differ from net_amount if early payment discount was exercised.
The date the invoice was cleared in the GR/IR clearing account, completing the procure-to-pay cycle. Indicates the invoice liability has been settled in the general ledger.
The legal entity or subsidiary code within the enterprise structure. Supports multi-entity financial consolidation and SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley) compliance reporting.
The timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the accounts payable system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN). Supports multi-currency operations and foreign exchange reporting.
The discount amount available if payment is made within the early payment discount period (e.g., 2% if paid within 10 days). Supports cash flow optimization and vendor relationship management.
The actual early payment discount amount deducted at payment time. Tracks discount capture performance and vendor payment optimization.
The date by which payment must be made to the vendor per the agreed payment terms. Calculated from invoice date plus payment term days (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).
reference: SAP FI Baseline Date + Payment Terms

description: Reference number for the EDI 810 electronic invoice transaction received from the vendor. Supports automated invoice processing and vendor integration via EDI (Electronic Data Interchange).
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the invoice was posted. Supports monthly close processes and period-based accrual accounting.
The fiscal year in which the invoice was posted, based on the posting date. Used for period-based financial reporting and year-over-year analysis.
Total invoice amount before any deductions, including merchandise cost, freight, slotting fees, or service charges. Represents the vendor's billed amount before tax.
The date the vendor issued the invoice. Used to calculate payment due dates based on payment terms and to determine early payment discount eligibility.
The external invoice number assigned by the vendor. This is the business identifier printed on the vendor's invoice document and referenced in payment correspondence.

Current state of the invoice in the accounts payable workflow. Tracks progression from receipt through three-way match, approval, payment scheduling, disbursement, and clearing in the GR/IR (Goods Receipt/Invoice Receipt) account. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|pending_match|matched|approved|payment_blocked|scheduled|paid|cleared|cancelled|disputed — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the invoice document. Standard invoices are typical vendor bills; credit memos reduce liability; debit memos increase liability; prepayments cover advance payments; recurring invoices are for subscription services.
Indicates whether this invoice payment must be reported to the IRS on Form 1099-MISC or 1099-NEC for vendor tax reporting. True for payments to non-corporate vendors exceeding the annual threshold.
Indicates whether the invoice has been successfully matched against the purchase order and goods receipt. Three-way match validates quantity, price, and terms alignment before payment approval.
The timestamp when this invoice record was last updated. Tracks changes to invoice status, amounts, or payment details for audit and reconciliation purposes.
The final payable amount after applying discounts and adding taxes. This is the amount that will be disbursed to the vendor (gross + tax - discount).

Code indicating a hold on payment processing. Common blocks include pricing disputes, quantity discrepancies, missing documentation, vendor compliance issues, or manual review requirements. Null when no block is active.
Detailed explanation of why the invoice payment is blocked. Provides context for AP analysts and vendor inquiry resolution.
The date the payment was executed and funds were disbursed to the vendor. Null for unpaid invoices. Used for cash flow reporting and vendor payment tracking.
The disbursement method used or planned for vendor payment. ACH (Automated Clearing House) and EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) are preferred for cost efficiency and speed.
The unique reference number for the payment transaction (check number, ACH trace number, wire confirmation). Used for bank reconciliation and vendor inquiry resolution.

The code representing the negotiated payment terms with the vendor (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Drives due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.
The date the invoice was posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period for financial reporting and GAAP-compliant accrual accounting.
Total tax charged on the invoice, including sales tax, use tax, VAT, or GST depending on jurisdiction. Used for tax reporting and reconciliation.
Code identifying the tax jurisdiction (state, province, county) where the tax was assessed. Supports multi-jurisdictional tax reporting and compliance with state and local tax regulations.
Unique identifier for the accounts receivable invoice record. Primary key for the AR invoice entity.

Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Order‑to‑Invoice Matching: AR Invoice is generated from a Fulfillment Order; linking them supports the Customer Billing & Revenue Recognition process.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR invoices record revenue against a GL account; linking provides proper financial posting.
Reference to the customer account (wholesale account, pharmacy insurance payer, fuel fleet account, or vendor for chargebacks) that is billed on this invoice.
Classification of invoice age based on days past due date. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) analysis and bad debt provisioning. Current = not yet due; other buckets represent days overdue.
Bank deposit batch or transaction reference number linking the payment to the bank statement. Used for bank reconciliation.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
End date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Used for revenue recognition period matching and accrual accounting.
Start date of the billing period covered by this invoice. Relevant for subscription-based or periodic billing (e.g., fuel fleet monthly billing, pharmacy monthly claims).
SAP FI clearing document number that records the payment application transaction. Links the invoice to the cash receipt document that closed or partially paid it.
Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the AR system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Number of days since the invoice date. Used to calculate Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) metrics and identify slow-paying accounts.
Number of days the invoice is overdue beyond the payment due date. Null or zero if invoice is not yet due. Used for dunning level determination.
Total discount applied to the invoice, including early payment discounts, volume discounts, promotional allowances, or negotiated rebates.
Date the customer formally disputed the invoice. Used to track dispute resolution cycle time.
Indicates whether the customer has disputed this invoice. True if customer has raised a billing dispute, pricing disagreement, or quality claim that blocks payment.
Free-text description of the customer dispute reason (e.g., pricing error, quantity discrepancy, damaged goods, service issue). Populated when dispute_flag is true.
Date by which payment is expected based on the payment terms. Used for aging bucket classification and dunning trigger.
Current escalation level in the collections dunning process (0=no dunning, 1=first reminder, 2=second reminder, 3=final notice, 4=legal action). Increments based on days past due and payment history.
Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice was posted. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods or 1-13 for 4-4-5 retail calendar.
Fiscal year in which the invoice was posted for financial reporting purposes. Used for period-based financial analysis and SOX compliance reporting.
Shipping and freight charges included in the invoice for delivery of goods to the customer location.
Date the invoice was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting. May differ from invoice date due to period-end cutoffs or batch processing.
Total invoice amount before any deductions, discounts, or adjustments. Represents the full billed value of goods or services.
Date the invoice was issued to the customer. This is the official billing date used for aging calculations and payment term determination.
Externally-visible unique invoice number assigned by the billing system. Used for customer communication and payment reconciliation.

Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts receivable process. Tracks progression from draft through payment or write-off. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|partially_paid|paid|overdue|disputed|written_off|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the invoice document type: wholesale invoice for B2B customers, pharmacy claim for PBM insurance billings, fuel fleet invoice for fleet card receivables, vendor chargeback for supplier deductions, debit memo for additional charges, or credit memo for adjustments.
Date the most recent dunning notice or collection reminder was sent to the customer for this invoice.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record. Tracks changes to status, amounts, or payment application.
Identifier of the bank lockbox service used for automated payment processing. Relevant for high-volume check and remittance processing.
Final invoice amount due after applying all discounts, taxes, and freight charges. This is the amount the customer is expected to pay (gross + tax + freight - discount).
Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice after applying all cash receipts, credit memos, and adjustments. Zero when invoice is fully paid.
Total amount received and applied against this invoice across all payment transactions. Sum of all cash receipts allocated to this invoice.
Date the payment was received and applied to this invoice. Null if invoice is unpaid. Used for cash flow analysis and DSO calculation.

Primary payment instrument or mechanism used (or expected) for this invoice: ACH (Automated Clearing House), wire transfer, check, credit card, EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer), lockbox processing, or cash. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ach|wire_transfer|check|credit_card|eft|lockbox|cash — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
External payment reference provided by the customer (check number, wire confirmation, ACH trace number, or remittance advice reference). Used for cash application matching.
Code representing the negotiated payment terms (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30 for 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Determines due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.
reference: SAP FI Payment Terms Master Data

description: Customer purchase order number provided for this invoice. Used for customer reconciliation and three-way matching in customer accounts payable systems.
Reference to the originating sales order or delivery document that generated this invoice. Links AR invoice back to the order fulfillment transaction.
Total sales tax, VAT, or other applicable taxes charged on the invoice. Calculated based on customer tax jurisdiction and product taxability.
Amount of the invoice written off as uncollectible bad debt. Recorded when collection efforts are exhausted and the receivable is deemed unrecoverable.
Date the invoice (or portion thereof) was written off as bad debt. Triggers bad debt expense recognition in the general ledger.
Reason code for the write-off: customer bankruptcy, deemed uncollectible after collection efforts, small balance not worth pursuing, dispute settlement, goodwill adjustment, or other.
Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key.
Foreign key to the master concentration account if this is a zero-balance account. Null for non-ZBA accounts.
Identifier of the Grocery legal entity that owns this bank account. Links to legal entity master for consolidation and intercompany reconciliation.
Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Must match legal entity name for corporate accounts.
Full bank account number. Stored in encrypted form for PCI compliance.
Business purpose of the account: operating (AP/AR), payroll (employee payments), tax (remittances), investment (short-term cash), debt_service (loan payments), escrow (restricted funds).

Current operational status of the bank account. Active: normal operations. Inactive: temporarily not in use. Closed: permanently closed. Frozen: blocked by bank or legal order. Restricted: limited transaction types allowed.

Classification of the bank account by operational purpose: checking (operating accounts), savings (reserve accounts), money_market (short-term investment), zero_balance (subsidiary accounts swept daily), concentration (master cash pooling), payroll (dedicated payroll disbursement), lockbox (AR collection). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: checking|savings|money_market|zero_balance|concentration|payroll|lockbox — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Current balance minus outstanding checks and holds. Represents funds available for immediate disbursement.
Country-specific bank identifier. In US: ABA routing number (9 digits). In EU: Bank Identifier Code (BIC/SWIFT). Maps to SAP bank key (BNKA-BANKL).
Legal name of the financial institution holding the account.
Full street address of the bank branch.
City where the bank branch is located.
Three-letter ISO country code for the bank branch location.
Name of the bank branch where the account is domiciled.
Postal or ZIP code for the bank branch.
Two-letter state or province code for the bank branch location.
Date the bank account was closed. Null for active accounts.
Four-character SAP FI company code to which this bank account is assigned. Used for GL posting and financial statement consolidation.
Timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD, MXN).
Most recent closing balance from the last processed bank statement. Used for daily cash position reporting.
GL account number in the chart of accounts to which bank transactions are posted. Typically a cash or cash-equivalent account.
SAP-assigned identifier for the house bank configuration. Links to SAP FI bank master (T012).
International Bank Account Number for SEPA and cross-border payments in Europe and other IBAN-participating countries.
Annual interest rate earned on positive balances or charged on overdrafts, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 for 2.50%).
Date of the most recent completed bank reconciliation. Used for SOX compliance monitoring.
Date of the most recent bank statement received and processed for this account.
True if this account is a lockbox account used for automated AR collection processing. False otherwise.
Masked account number showing only last 4 digits for display purposes (e.g., XXXXXX1234).
Date the bank account was opened with the financial institution.
Maximum negative balance allowed by the bank under overdraft protection agreement. Zero if no overdraft facility exists.
Nine-digit ABA routing transit number for US domestic wire and ACH transactions.
Name of the primary authorized signatory for the account. Required for SOX treasury controls and audit trail.
Name of the secondary authorized signatory. Used for dual-control requirements on high-value transactions.
Maximum transaction amount that can be authorized by a single signatory. Amounts above this require dual approval.
Electronic format in which bank statements are received: MT940 (SWIFT standard), BAI2 (US cash management), CAMT053 (ISO 20022 XML), PDF, CSV.
How often bank statements are issued: daily (for high-volume accounts), weekly, monthly.
Bank Identifier Code (BIC) for international wire transfers. 8 or 11 character ISO 9362 code.
Timestamp when this bank account record was last modified.
True if this is a zero-balance account that is automatically swept to/from a concentration account daily. False otherwise.

description: Total depreciation expense recognized to date since acquisition. This is the cumulative contra-asset amount reducing the net book value.

description: Original purchase price or capitalized cost of the asset in USD. This is the gross base amount for depreciation calculations.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Valuation

description: The date the asset was acquired or placed in service. This is the principal business event timestamp for the asset lifecycle and determines when depreciation begins.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Acquisition

description: Classification of the asset type (e.g., store equipment, refrigeration units, fuel pumps, POS terminals, MFC automation systems, delivery vehicles, leasehold improvements). Determines depreciation rules and account assignments.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Class Configuration

description: Detailed textual description of the fixed asset, including make, model, and specific characteristics.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Master Data

description: The main asset number assigned in SAP FI Asset Accounting. This is the externally-known unique identifier for the asset across the enterprise.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Master Data

description: Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset. Active assets are in service and depreciating; under construction assets are capitalized but not yet depreciating; retired/disposed assets are no longer in use.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Status Management

description: Sub-number for assets that are part of a larger asset group or complex asset. Used when a main asset has multiple components tracked separately for depreciation purposes.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Master Data

description: Physical tag or barcode identifier affixed to the asset for inventory tracking and physical verification during SOX audits.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: The date the asset was capitalized in the general ledger. For assets under construction, this may differ from the acquisition date.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Capitalization

description: The cost center to which the asset is assigned for expense allocation and budgeting. Typically represents a store, distribution center, or corporate department.
reference: SAP CO - Controlling Cost Center Accounting

description: The timestamp when this fixed asset record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost and all monetary amounts (typically USD for US operations).
Depreciation expense recognized in the current fiscal period (month or quarter). Used for EBITDA reconciliation and P&L reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 360 - Property, Plant, and Equipment

description: The valuation view for depreciation calculation: book (GAAP financial reporting), tax (IRS tax depreciation), group (consolidated reporting), or IFRS (international standards).
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Depreciation Areas; GAAP ASC 360; IRS Publication 946; IFRS IAS 16

description: SAP-specific depreciation key that defines the calculation rules, useful life, and period control for depreciation posting.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Depreciation Key Configuration

description: The method used to calculate periodic depreciation expense (e.g., straight-line, declining balance). Aligns with GAAP and tax regulations.
reference: GAAP ASC 360 - Property, Plant, and Equipment; IRS Publication 946

description: The date the asset was retired, sold, or otherwise disposed of, marking the end of its service life.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Retirement

description: Gain or loss recognized on disposal, calculated as disposal proceeds minus net book value at disposal date. Positive values indicate gain; negative values indicate loss.
reference: GAAP ASC 360 - Property, Plant, and Equipment

description: Cash or fair value received from the sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal.
reference: GAAP ASC 360 - Property, Plant, and Equipment

description: The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for the current depreciation posting.
The fiscal year for which the current period depreciation and accumulated depreciation are reported.
Total impairment loss recognized when the asset's recoverable amount falls below its net book value, per GAAP impairment testing requirements.
reference: GAAP ASC 360-10 - Impairment or Disposal of Long-Lived Assets

description: The date on which an impairment loss was recognized for the asset.
reference: GAAP ASC 360-10 - Impairment or Disposal of Long-Lived Assets

description: Flag indicating whether the asset is leased rather than owned. Leased assets follow ASC 842 lease accounting rules.
User ID or name of the person who last modified the asset record. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: The timestamp when this fixed asset record was last updated. Critical for change tracking and SOX audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: The date of the most recent physical inventory verification of the asset, required for SOX compliance and audit trails.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: Reference to the lease agreement contract number if the asset is leased. Links to lease liability and right-of-use asset records.
Physical location code where the asset is deployed (store number, DC identifier, or facility code).
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Location

description: Current carrying value of the asset, calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Represents the remaining capitalized value on the balance sheet.
reference: GAAP ASC 360 - Property, Plant, and Equipment

description: Reference to the purchase order used to acquire the asset, linking to procurement records.
reference: SAP MM - Materials Management Purchase Order

description: Manufacturer serial number or unique equipment identifier for physical tracking and warranty management.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Master Data

description: Total depreciation recognized for tax purposes to date. Used to track book-tax differences for deferred tax calculations.
reference: IRS Publication 946 - How to Depreciate Property; GAAP ASC 740 - Income Taxes

description: The IRS-approved depreciation method used for tax reporting, which may differ from book depreciation (e.g., MACRS, Section 179 expensing, bonus depreciation).
reference: IRS Publication 946 - How to Depreciate Property; IRC Section 168 (MACRS); IRC Section 179

description: Expected useful life of the asset in years, used to calculate depreciation rates. Determined by asset class and IRS guidelines.
reference: IRS Publication 946 - How to Depreciate Property

description: The date on which the manufacturer or vendor warranty expires, used for maintenance planning and cost forecasting.
reference: SAP FI Asset Accounting (AA) - Asset Master Data

description: Unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: intercompany_transaction should reference the cost center master; cost_center_id replaces the free‑text cost_center field.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: intercompany_transaction must reference GL account, cost center, and profit center masters for proper posting and allocation.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: intercompany_transaction should reference the profit center master; profit_center_id replaces the free‑text profit_center field.

The approval workflow status for the intercompany transaction: pending (awaiting finance approval), approved (authorized for posting), rejected (not authorized, requires correction). Critical for SOX segregation of duties controls.
The SAP user ID of the finance manager or controller who approved the intercompany transaction for posting. Null if approval is pending or not required. Part of SOX approval control audit trail.
The date and time when the intercompany transaction was approved. Null if approval is pending or not required. Supports SOX audit trail for authorization controls.
The SAP business area classification for segment reporting, such as Retail, Pharmacy, Fuel, or eCommerce. Enables intercompany analysis by line of business.
The date and time when the intercompany transaction record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for SOX compliance and financial controls.
The business date of the underlying transaction or invoice that triggered the intercompany posting. May differ from posting_date due to processing lag.
The date by which the intercompany transaction amount is due for settlement between entities. Calculated based on posting_date and payment_terms.

Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this intercompany transaction requires elimination during financial consolidation to prevent double-counting of revenue, expenses, or assets across legal entities.

Current status of the elimination process for this intercompany transaction: pending (awaiting consolidation run), eliminated (successfully removed in consolidated financials), not_required (no elimination needed), manual_review (requires finance team intervention).
The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction_amount to functional or group currency. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate table at posting_date.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the transaction was posted. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, used for period-end close and consolidation.
The fiscal year in which the intercompany transaction was posted, following Grocery's fiscal calendar. Used for year-end consolidation and elimination entries.
The transaction amount converted to the functional currency of the sending company code for local statutory reporting. Conversion uses the exchange rate at posting_date.
The transaction amount converted to the group reporting currency (typically USD) for consolidated financial statements. Critical for intercompany elimination at the corporate level.

Free-text description of the intercompany transaction providing business context, such as 'DC to Store 1234 inventory allocation for Q1' or 'Corporate IT shared service charge for January'. Supports audit trail and user understanding.
The SAP user ID or system process that last modified the intercompany transaction record. Tracks changes for audit and compliance purposes.
The date and time when the intercompany transaction record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under SOX.
The net amount after offsetting reciprocal intercompany balances between sending and receiving entities. Used for intercompany settlement and cash pooling arrangements.
The agreed payment terms for settlement of the intercompany transaction, such as Net 30, Net 60, or Due on Receipt. Governs intercompany cash flow and working capital management.
The date on which the intercompany transaction was posted to the general ledger in both sending and receiving entities. Critical for period-end close and financial consolidation timing.

SAP company code of the legal entity receiving the intercompany transaction (e.g., distribution center, shared service center, subsidiary). Four-character alphanumeric identifier per SAP FI configuration.
reference: SAP FI Company Code Master Data

description: The date on which the intercompany transaction was successfully reconciled and matched between sending and receiving entities. Null if reconciliation is pending or unmatched.

Status of the intercompany reconciliation process indicating whether the transaction has been confirmed and matched between sending and receiving entities: matched (both sides agree), unmatched (discrepancy exists), partially_matched (amount variance), disputed (under investigation), resolved (discrepancy corrected).

The source document number that triggered the intercompany transaction, such as an invoice number, purchase order, transfer order, or allocation run ID. Provides audit trail back to originating transaction.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this intercompany transaction is a reversal of a previously posted transaction. Used for error correction and period-end adjustments.
The transaction_number of the original intercompany transaction that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_flag is True, providing audit trail for corrections.
SAP company code of the legal entity originating the intercompany transaction (e.g., store, distribution center, corporate entity). Four-character alphanumeric identifier per SAP FI configuration.
reference: SAP FI Company Code Master Data

description: The actual date on which the intercompany transaction was settled through cash transfer, netting, or other payment mechanism. Null if settlement is pending.

The method used to settle the intercompany transaction: cash_transfer (actual funds moved), netting (offset against reciprocal balances), offset (applied against other intercompany transactions), accrual_only (no cash settlement, accounting entry only).
The tax amount calculated on the intercompany transaction based on applicable tax_code and jurisdiction rules. May be zero for tax-exempt intercompany transfers.
The tax code applied to the intercompany transaction for VAT, GST, or sales tax purposes. Some intercompany transactions may be tax-exempt depending on jurisdiction and transaction type.
The gross monetary value of the intercompany transaction in the transaction currency, before any adjustments or eliminations. Represents the amount posted to both sending and receiving entities.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany transaction was originally denominated (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN for North American operations).
Business-facing unique transaction number for intercompany postings, typically following format IC-XXXXXXXXXX for tracking and reconciliation purposes.

Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the cross-entity posting: cost_allocation (store-to-DC allocations), shared_service_charge (corporate overhead), intercompany_loan (financing arrangements), management_fee (corporate services), inventory_transfer (goods movement), royalty (brand/IP licensing).
The SAP user ID or system process that created the intercompany transaction record. Critical for SOX audit trail and accountability.
Unique identifier for the legal entity. Primary key for the legal entity master record.
Reference to the parent legal entity in the corporate hierarchy. Null for the ultimate parent holding company.
Reference to the top-level parent legal entity in the corporate structure. Used for ultimate consolidation and group-level reporting.
Name of the external audit firm responsible for auditing this legal entity's financial statements. Required for SOX-scoped and public entities.
Code identifying the consolidation group or reporting unit to which this legal entity belongs. Used for financial consolidation and intercompany elimination.
The timestamp when this legal entity record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The date on which the legal entity was officially dissolved or ceased operations. Null for active entities.
Trade name or DBA name under which the legal entity operates. May differ from the legal name for customer-facing operations.
Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet. Used for credit reporting, vendor registration, and business identity verification.
Federal tax identification number issued by the IRS. Used for all federal tax filings, payroll, and financial reporting.
Current operational status of the legal entity. Active entities are operational and filing; inactive or dissolved entities are no longer conducting business.
The legal structure of the entity (e.g., corporation, LLC, partnership). Determines tax treatment, liability, and governance requirements.
The month (1-12) in which the legal entity's fiscal year ends. Used for annual financial statement preparation and tax filing deadlines.
Code representing the fiscal year calendar used by this legal entity. Determines period structure for financial reporting and consolidation in SAP FI.
reference: SAP FI Fiscal Year Variant Configuration

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the primary currency in which the legal entity conducts business and maintains its books.

business_glossary_term: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (GAAP) Reporting Standard
description: The accounting standard framework used by this legal entity for financial reporting (US GAAP, IFRS, or local GAAP).
The date on which the legal entity was officially incorporated or registered with the state. Used to calculate entity age and anniversary dates.
The date on which the most recent external financial audit was completed for this legal entity.
The timestamp when this legal entity record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Short alphanumeric code used to identify the legal entity in financial systems and reporting. Typically used in SAP FI company code configuration.
The full legal name of the entity as registered with the state or jurisdiction of incorporation. Used for all legal documents, contracts, and regulatory filings.

business_glossary_term: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code
description: Six-digit code classifying the primary business activity of the legal entity. Used for industry benchmarking and regulatory reporting.
Email address of the primary business contact for the legal entity.
Name of the primary business contact or registered agent for the legal entity. Used for official communications and legal notices.
Phone number of the primary business contact for the legal entity.
reference: ITU-T E.164 International Phone Number Standard

description: Indicates whether this legal entity is publicly traded on a stock exchange. Determines SEC reporting requirements and audit obligations.
First line of the legal entity's registered address on file with the state. Used for official correspondence and legal service of process.
Second line of the registered address (suite, floor, building). Optional field for additional address details.
City of the legal entity's registered address.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the registered address.
ZIP code of the registered address. Supports 5-digit or 9-digit ZIP+4 format.
Two-letter US state code of the registered address.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for consolidated financial reporting. May differ from functional currency for foreign subsidiaries.
Four-digit code classifying the primary business activity. Legacy classification system still used by SEC and some regulatory bodies.
Indicates whether this legal entity is within the scope of SOX compliance and internal control testing. True for publicly traded entities and material subsidiaries.
Two-letter US state code where the legal entity is incorporated or registered. Determines governing law and annual filing requirements.
Code identifying the primary stock exchange where the entity's shares are traded (e.g., NYSE, NASDAQ). Null for private entities.
The ticker symbol under which the entity's shares are traded. Null for private entities.
Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction for the legal entity. Determines applicable tax rates and filing requirements.
VAT or sales tax registration number for entities operating in jurisdictions requiring VAT registration. Null for US-only entities without VAT obligations.
Primary key for payment_run
Self-referencing FK on payment_run (reversed_payment_run_id)
Identifier of the vendor or counterparty receiving the payments.
Actual completion time when the payment run finished processing.
Actual start time when the payment run began processing.
Timestamp when the payment run was approved for execution.
User ID of the person who approved the payment run.
JSON string capturing audit events for the payment run (e.g., status changes).
Business unit responsible for the payment run.
Cost center code associated with the payment run for internal accounting.
Timestamp when the payment run record was created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amounts.
Free-text description or notes about the payment run.
External system reference identifier for the payment run (e.g., SAP document number).
Indicates whether the payment run was generated automatically by the system.
Count of individual payment transactions included in this run.
Channel through which the payment run was initiated.
Primary method used to execute payments in this run.
External reference number for the payment run as assigned by the finance system.
Category of payments processed in this run.
Planned start time for the payment run execution.
Current lifecycle status of the payment run.
Total fees (e.g., processing fees) for the payment run.
Total gross amount of all payments before deductions.
Net amount after taxes and fees.
Total tax amount applied to the payments in the run.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment run record.
User ID of the person who created the payment run record.
Unique identifier for the budget record. Primary key.
Primary key for budget
Identifier of the cost center responsible for this budget. Cost centers represent organizational units that incur costs (e.g., store, department, distribution center).
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: budget should reference the GL account master; gl_account_id replaces gl_account_code.
Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Budget ownership is assigned to a specific associate for financial planning responsibility and audit traceability.
Identifier of the profit center responsible for this budget. Profit centers represent organizational units that generate revenue and incur costs (e.g., store, region, business unit).
Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_program. Business justification: Compliance Program budgeting requires linking each program to its financial budget for cost planning and reporting.
The actual amount spent or incurred to date for this budget line, as recorded in the general ledger. Used for variance analysis (actual vs plan).
The date on which this budget was formally approved by the authorized approver or budget committee.
Name or identifier of the individual who approved this budget (e.g., CFO, Budget Committee Chair, Regional Director).
Reference identifier linking this budget record to the audit trail or change log for compliance and SOX reporting. Tracks all modifications to budget amounts and approvals.
The remaining budget balance available for spending, calculated as revised_amount minus (committed_amount plus actual_amount). Used for budget consumption monitoring.
Hierarchical category or grouping for the budget line (e.g., Store Operations, Supply Chain, Marketing, IT Infrastructure). Used for rollup reporting and variance analysis.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the budget, typically formatted as BUD-YYYYNNNN where YYYY is fiscal year and NNNN is sequence number.

Current lifecycle status of the budget: draft (in preparation), submitted (awaiting approval), approved (authorized), active (in use), frozen (locked for changes), closed (period ended), or rejected (not approved). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|active|frozen|closed|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification of the budget by functional category: operating expenses, capital expenditures (CapEx), labor costs, marketing spend, administrative overhead, or Cost of Goods Sold (COGS).
The amount of budget that has been committed through purchase orders, contracts, or other obligations but not yet expensed. Used for encumbrance accounting.
The date and time when this budget record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget record (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR).
The date on which this budget expires or is no longer active. Typically the last day of the fiscal period or fiscal year.
The date from which this budget becomes effective and active for spending authorization and monitoring.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year, typically 1-12 for monthly budgets or 1-52 for weekly budgets.
The fiscal year for which this budget applies, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).
The date and time when this budget record was last updated or modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Free-text notes or comments about this budget, including justification for revisions, special instructions, or context for variance explanations.
Name or identifier of the individual or role responsible for managing and monitoring this budget (e.g., Store Manager, Regional VP, CFO).
The original planned budget amount for the fiscal period, as approved during the annual budgeting process. Represents the baseline target.
The revised budget amount after adjustments, reforecasts, or supplemental allocations. Reflects the current authorized budget target.
Boolean flag indicating whether this budget line is subject to SOX internal control requirements and audit trails (True = SOX-controlled, False = not SOX-controlled).
The percentage of budget consumption at which a critical alert is triggered and spending may be blocked (e.g., 95.00 means critical alert when 95% of budget is consumed).
The percentage of budget consumption at which a warning alert is triggered (e.g., 80.00 means alert when 80% of budget is consumed). Used for proactive budget monitoring.
The variance between planned and actual amounts (actual_amount minus planned_amount). Positive values indicate overspend; negative values indicate underspend.
The variance expressed as a percentage of the planned amount ((actual_amount - planned_amount) / planned_amount * 100). Used for threshold alerts and performance scorecards.
Version type of the budget indicating whether it is the original plan, a revised plan, a forecast, reforecast, supplemental allocation, or final approved version.
Reference to the original cost_allocation_id that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Enables tracing of reversal chains for audit purposes.
reference: SAP CO Reversal Traceability Standards; SOX Audit Trail Requirements

description: Unique identifier for the cost allocation record. Primary key for the cost allocation transaction.

Identifier of the cost element (expense category) being allocated. Cost elements represent the nature of the expense (utilities, rent, depreciation, IT services, HR services) and map to general ledger accounts.
reference: SAP CO Cost Element Master Data; Chart of Accounts Standards

description: Identifier of the cost center from which costs are being allocated. Sender cost centers typically represent shared service functions (IT, HR, facilities, corporate overhead, distribution center operations) whose costs are distributed to benefiting units.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master Data; Management Accounting Cost Center Standards

description: Identifier of the profit center receiving the allocated costs. Used when costs are allocated directly to profit centers for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Nullable if allocation targets cost centers only.
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Master Data; Segment Reporting Standards

description: Monetary amount allocated from the sender cost center to the receiver cost center or profit center. Represents the share of shared costs assigned to the receiver based on the allocation method and driver quantities. Positive values indicate cost transfers; negative values may represent reversals or corrections.
reference: GAAP Cost Allocation Standards; SAP CO Allocation Posting Rules

description: Name or identifier of the periodic cost allocation cycle run in SAP CO (Controlling). Examples include monthly overhead allocation, quarterly shared services distribution, or annual corporate cost assessment cycles.

Method used to allocate costs from sender to receiver objects. Percentage allocates based on proportional shares, quantity uses driver volumes (e.g., square footage, headcount), fixed uses predetermined amounts, assessment distributes full sender balance, distribution splits based on statistical key figures, direct assigns costs one-to-one, step_down cascades costs sequentially, reciprocal handles mutual service relationships. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage|quantity|fixed|assessment|distribution|direct|step_down|reciprocal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP CO Allocation Method Standards; Management Accounting Best Practices

description: Free-text notes or comments regarding the allocation. May include explanations for manual adjustments, special allocation rules, or business justifications for allocation method changes.

Percentage of sender cost center total costs allocated to this receiver. Used when allocation_method is percentage. Sum of all allocation_percentage values for a given sender and cycle should equal 100.00. Nullable if allocation method is not percentage-based.
reference: Management Accounting Allocation Standards; SAP CO Percentage Allocation Rules

description: Rate per unit of driver quantity used to calculate the allocated amount. Calculated as total sender costs divided by total driver quantity across all receivers. allocated_amount = driver_quantity × allocation_rate. Nullable if allocation method is fixed or percentage.
reference: Management Accounting Rate Calculation Standards; SAP CO Allocation Rate Logic

description: Current status of the cost allocation record. Posted indicates the allocation has been successfully posted to CO and FI documents. Reversed indicates the allocation was reversed due to error or period-end adjustment. Pending indicates the cycle has run but posting is not yet complete. Error indicates a posting failure. Cancelled indicates the allocation was voided before posting.
reference: SAP CO Document Status Standards; Financial Posting Workflow Standards

description: SAP CO document number generated when the allocation was posted. Links this allocation record to the controlling document for audit trail and drill-down analysis.
SAP company code representing the legal entity for which the allocation was posted. Used for multi-entity consolidation and legal entity reporting.
reference: SAP FI Company Code Standards; GAAP Legal Entity Reporting

description: SAP controlling area representing the organizational unit for cost accounting. Controlling areas may span multiple company codes for consolidated management reporting.
reference: SAP CO Controlling Area Configuration Standards

description: Timestamp when the allocation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking for SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Audit Trail Standards; GAAP Record Retention Requirements

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount. Typically USD for US-based grocery operations, but may vary for international subsidiaries.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; GAAP Multi-Currency Standards

description: Date when the allocation cycle was executed in SAP CO. May differ from posting_date if the cycle was run in advance and posted later.
reference: SAP CO Allocation Cycle Execution Standards

description: Quantity of the allocation driver (statistical key figure) used to calculate the allocated amount. Examples: square footage, headcount, transaction volume, sales revenue, labor hours. Used when allocation_method is quantity-based. Nullable if allocation method is fixed or percentage.
reference: SAP CO Statistical Key Figure Standards; Management Accounting Driver Standards

description: Unit of measure for the driver quantity. Examples: square_feet, headcount, transactions, dollars, hours. Provides context for interpreting driver_quantity values.
reference: SAP CO Unit of Measure Standards; ISO Unit Standards

description: SAP FI document number generated when the allocation was posted to the general ledger. Links this allocation record to the financial accounting document for SOX compliance and audit trail.
reference: SAP FI Document Numbering Standards; SOX Documentation Requirements

description: Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the allocation was posted. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, may include special periods 13-16 for year-end adjustments.
reference: GAAP Fiscal Period Standards; SAP FI Period Variant

description: Four-digit fiscal year in which the cost allocation was executed. Aligns with the company's financial reporting calendar.
reference: GAAP Fiscal Period Standards; SAP FI Fiscal Year Variant

description: User ID or name of the person or system process that last modified the allocation record. Supports SOX compliance and change tracking.
reference: SOX User Accountability Standards; SAP Audit Trail Standards

description: Timestamp when the allocation record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking for SOX compliance and financial reporting accuracy.
reference: SOX Audit Trail Standards; GAAP Record Retention Requirements

description: Date when the cost allocation transaction was posted to the general ledger and controlling documents. Determines the fiscal period assignment and financial statement impact.
reference: GAAP Posting Date Standards; SAP FI/CO Document Date Rules

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this allocation record is a reversal of a previous allocation. True if this is a reversal transaction, False if this is an original allocation posting.
User ID or name of the person or system process that created the allocation record. Supports SOX compliance and audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX User Accountability Standards; SAP Audit Trail Standards

description: Primary key for cost_center
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: cost_center should link to GL account master for proper account hierarchy; gl_account_id replaces gl_account_code.

SAP CO activity type code associated with this cost center, representing the primary type of work or service output produced (e.g., 'LABOR_HRS', 'MACHINE_HRS', 'DELIVERY_UNITS'). Used for activity-based costing, internal order settlements, and COGS allocation.
reference: SAP CO Activity Type Master (KL01); GAAP — Activity-Based Costing

description: Total approved annual budget allocated to this cost center for the current budget period, expressed in the cost center's operating currency. Used for budget vs. actual variance analysis, EBITDA reporting, and financial planning. Sourced from SAP CO cost center planning (KP06).
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Planning (KP06); GAAP — Budget Reporting; SOX Section 404 — Internal Controls

description: The fiscal year for which the current budget plan is active for this cost center (e.g., '2025'). Used to scope budget vs. actual variance analysis, EBITDA reporting, and departmental P&L reviews.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Planning (KP06); GAAP — Annual Budget Reporting; SOX Section 404

description: SAP FI business area assigned to this cost center, representing a distinct area of operations for internal balance sheet and P&L reporting (e.g., Grocery Retail, Pharmacy, Fuel). Supports segment-level financial disclosures.
reference: SAP FI Business Area (OX03); GAAP ASC 280 — Segment Reporting

description: SAP FI chart of accounts code applicable to this cost center's company code. Defines the set of GL accounts available for cost postings. Ensures consistency in financial reporting across Grocery's legal entities.
reference: SAP FI Chart of Accounts (OB13); GAAP — Uniform Chart of Accounts

description: SAP FI company code representing the legal entity to which this cost center belongs. Used for statutory financial reporting, GAAP-compliant disclosures, and SOX audit trails. Enables financial consolidation across Grocery's legal entities.
reference: SAP FI Company Code (OX02); GAAP — Legal Entity Reporting; SOX Section 302/404

description: SAP CO controlling area to which this cost center belongs. The controlling area defines the organizational unit within which cost accounting is performed. All cost centers within a controlling area share the same chart of accounts and fiscal year variant.
reference: SAP CO Controlling Area Configuration (OKKP); GAAP — Entity-Level Financial Reporting

description: SAP CO cost center category code (single or two-character) that controls which activity types and statistical key figures can be posted to this cost center. Common values include 'A' (General), 'B' (Administration), 'F' (Production), 'H' (Auxiliary). Governs posting rules and planning behavior in SAP CO.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Category (KS01 field: KOSAR); SAP CO Configuration Guide

description: The externally-known alphanumeric code assigned to the cost center in SAP CO, used across financial reporting, budgeting, and interoperability with ERP systems. Serves as the business-facing identifier (e.g., 'CC-STORE-0042', 'CC-DC-WEST-01').
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master (KS01/KS02); SOX Section 302/404 — Internal Controls over Financial Reporting

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the cost center as maintained in SAP CO (e.g., 'Store 042 - Produce Department', 'Distribution Center West - Receiving', 'Pharmacy - Store 101'). Used in financial reports, P&L statements, and budget dashboards.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master (KS01/KS02); GAAP — Segment Reporting (ASC 280)

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center. 'active' — open for cost postings and budget planning; 'inactive' — temporarily suspended; 'locked' — blocked for new postings (SAP CO lock indicator); 'pending_activation' — created but not yet effective; 'closed' — permanently decommissioned after validity end date.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master (Lock Indicator: SPERL); SOX Section 404 — Change Management Controls

description: Categorical classification of the cost center indicating the type of organizational unit it represents within Grocery's operations. Drives departmental P&L segmentation, shrink financial impact allocation, and labor cost attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store|distribution_center|pharmacy|fuel_center|corporate|administrative|manufacturing|logistics — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Category (Field: KOSAR); GAAP ASC 280 — Segment Reporting

description: Timestamp when this cost center master record was first created in the source system (SAP CO). Used for audit trail, SOX compliance documentation, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master Audit Log; SOX Section 404 — Audit Trail Requirements; GAAP — Record Retention

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost center's operating currency (e.g., 'USD'). Defines the currency in which budget, actual costs, and variances are recorded and reported for this cost center.
reference: ISO 4217 — Currency Codes; SAP CO Cost Center Master (Field: WAERS); GAAP — Foreign Currency Translation (ASC 830)

description: Internal department code associated with this cost center, used for departmental P&L reporting and labor cost attribution. Aligns with Grocery's organizational structure (e.g., Produce, Bakery, Deli, Pharmacy, Fuel, Receiving, Corporate Finance).
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master; Oracle Retail Merchandising System — Department Classification

description: Organizational division code to which this cost center belongs (e.g., 'GROCERY_RETAIL', 'PHARMACY', 'FUEL_OPERATIONS', 'ECOMMERCE'). Supports divisional P&L reporting, EBITDA segmentation, and same-store sales analysis by business division.
reference: SAP Retail Division Master; GAAP ASC 280 — Segment Reporting

description: SAP FI fiscal year variant code that defines the fiscal year structure (number of periods, start/end months) applicable to this cost center. Grocery typically uses a 52/53-week fiscal year. Drives period-based budget and actual cost comparisons.
reference: SAP FI Fiscal Year Variant (OB29); GAAP — Fiscal Year Reporting

description: SAP CO functional area classification used to differentiate cost by business function (e.g., Cost of Goods Sold, Selling, General & Administrative, Research & Development). Enables COGS vs. SG&A split in income statement reporting per GAAP.
