reference: SAP CO Functional Area (OKBD); GAAP — Income Statement Classification (ASC 225)

business_glossary_term: Cost Center Hierarchy Area (Standard Hierarchy Node)
description: The node within the SAP CO standard cost center hierarchy to which this cost center is assigned. Enables roll-up reporting across organizational levels (e.g., region, division, banner). Used for enterprise-wide P&L consolidation, EBITDA reporting, and comp sales analysis.
reference: SAP CO Standard Hierarchy (OKEON); GAAP ASC 280 — Segment Reporting; SOX Section 404

business_glossary_term: Is Locked for Actual Postings Flag
description: Indicates whether this cost center is locked for actual cost postings (True). When locked, no actual costs can be posted to this cost center in SAP CO. Used during period-end close, audit periods, or when a cost center is being decommissioned.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Lock Indicator (Field: SPERL); SOX Section 404 — Posting Controls

business_glossary_term: Is Locked for Plan Postings Flag
description: Indicates whether this cost center is locked for plan (budget) postings (True). When locked, no budget updates can be made to this cost center in SAP CO. Used to freeze budgets after approval for SOX compliance.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Lock Indicator (Field: SPERP); SOX Section 404 — Budget Freeze Controls

description: Indicates whether this cost center is a statistical cost center (True) used only for informational/reporting purposes without actual cost postings, or a real cost center (False) that accumulates actual costs. Statistical cost centers are used in SAP CO for parallel reporting structures.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master (Statistical Indicator); SAP CO Configuration Guide

description: Portion of the annual budget specifically allocated to labor costs (wages, salaries, benefits) for this cost center. Enables labor cost attribution analysis, workforce planning integration with Kronos/Workday HCM, and COGS labor component reporting.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Planning (KP06); Kronos Workforce Central — Labor Analytics; GAAP — Labor Cost Classification

description: SAP user ID of the person who last modified this cost center master record. Required for SOX compliance audit trails to establish accountability for master data changes. Corresponds to SAP CO change document user field.
reference: SAP CO Change Documents; SOX Section 302/404 — Management Accountability; GAAP — Audit Trail

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this cost center master record in SAP CO. Used for change tracking, SOX audit trails, and incremental data load processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master Change Log (KS03); SOX Section 404 — Change Management Controls

description: Physical location type associated with this cost center. Distinguishes between store-level, DC (Distribution Center), MFC (Micro-Fulfillment Center), corporate office, fuel center, and pharmacy cost centers for geographic and operational segmentation in financial reporting.
reference: SAP Retail Organizational Structure; GAAP ASC 280 — Segment Reporting

description: The overhead allocation rate applied to this cost center, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 0.1250 = 12.50%). Used in SAP CO overhead costing sheets to allocate indirect costs (e.g., corporate overhead, DC overhead) to store or department cost centers for full-absorption costing and COGS calculation.
reference: SAP CO Overhead Costing Sheet (KZS2); GAAP — Overhead Absorption (ASC 330 — Inventory Costing)

description: The SAP CO profit center to which this cost center is assigned. Profit centers represent autonomous business units for internal P&L reporting (e.g., individual stores, pharmacy division, fuel operations). Enables same-store sales and comp sales analysis at the profit center level.
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Accounting (EC-PCA); GAAP ASC 280 — Segment Reporting

description: Geographic region code to which this cost center belongs (e.g., 'NORTHEAST', 'SOUTHEAST', 'WEST'). Used for regional P&L roll-up, comp sales analysis by region, and regional budget variance reporting.
reference: SAP Retail Organizational Hierarchy; GAAP ASC 280 — Geographic Segment Reporting

description: SAP CO settlement rule code that defines how costs accumulated on this cost center are settled (allocated) to other cost objects (e.g., other cost centers, internal orders, profitability segments). Drives period-end cost allocation and COGS calculation.
reference: SAP CO Settlement Rule (KO02/KS02); GAAP — Cost Allocation Methodology

description: Abbreviated name of the cost center used in condensed financial reports, POS system displays, and space-constrained UI contexts. Corresponds to the SAP CO short text field (up to 20 characters).

Indicates whether this cost center participates in shrink (inventory loss from theft, spoilage, or damage) financial impact allocation (True). When enabled, shrink costs calculated from inventory management systems are allocated to this cost center for departmental P&L and COGS reporting.
reference: SAP CO Cost Allocation; GAAP ASC 330 — Inventory; USDA HACCP — Perishable Loss Tracking

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this cost center master record was sourced (e.g., 'SAP_CO' for SAP Controlling, 'SAP_FI' for SAP Financial Accounting). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver Layer provenance tracking.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards; SOX Section 404 — Data Integrity Controls

description: The date through which this cost center is active and valid for cost postings. Corresponds to the SAP CO validity period end date. A value of 9999-12-31 indicates an open-ended (indefinite) validity. Cost postings after this date are blocked.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master (Field: DATBI — Valid To); GAAP — Period-Based Accounting

description: The date from which this cost center is active and valid for cost postings and budget planning. Corresponds to the SAP CO validity period start date. Cost postings before this date are not permitted.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master (Field: DATAB — Valid From); GAAP — Period-Based Accounting

description: Primary key for cost_element
Self-referencing FK on cost_element (parent_cost_element_id)
Method used to allocate costs from this element to cost objects.
Higher‑level grouping of cost elements for reporting.
Unique alphanumeric code used to identify the cost element in financial systems.
Code of the cost center primarily associated with this cost element.
Timestamp when the cost element record was first created in the system.
ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for the default rate.
Default monetary rate (per unit) associated with the cost element.
Detailed description of the cost element purpose and usage.
Date when the cost element becomes effective.
Date when the cost element is no longer effective; null if indefinite.
GL account number linked to this cost element for posting.
Indicates whether the cost element is deprecated and should not be used for new transactions.
Human‑readable name of the cost element.
Current lifecycle status of the cost element.
Indicates if taxes are applicable to this cost element.
Default tax rate percentage applied to this cost element when tax_applicable is true.
Category indicating the nature of the cost element.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost element record.
Unique identifier for the SAP CO internal order. Primary key for the internal order master data.
Identifier of the cost center responsible for managing and approving costs on this internal order. The cost center owner is accountable for budget adherence and project completion.
Identifier of the profit center to which costs and revenues from this internal order are allocated. Used for segment reporting and profitability analysis.
Foreign key linking to workforce.associate. Business justification: Internal orders are initiated by an associate; linking enables approval workflow and cost accountability.

Identifier of the retail store location if this internal order is store-specific (e.g., store remodel, store opening, store equipment upgrade). Used for store-level CAPEX tracking and profitability analysis.
Total actual costs posted to this internal order to date, in the company code currency. Includes direct costs, allocations, and internal activity charges. Updated in real-time as costs are posted.
Actual date when the project or activity was completed and the order was set to technically complete status. Triggers settlement eligibility.
Actual date when work began on this internal order, typically when the first cost was posted or the order status changed to released.
Date when the internal order budget was approved by the authorized approver. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.

Current approval status of the internal order budget request. Orders must be approved before they can be released for cost posting. Workflow-driven based on budget thresholds and organizational hierarchy.
Full name of the individual who approved the internal order budget. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail documentation.
SAP FI asset class code if this internal order will be settled to a fixed asset. Determines depreciation rules, useful life, and GL account assignments for the resulting asset.
SAP FI fixed asset master number to which this internal order will be settled upon completion. Populated for CAPEX orders that create or enhance fixed assets.
Total approved budget for this internal order in the company code currency. Used for budget control and variance reporting. Budget may be original, supplemental, or revised.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the budget amount. Typically matches the company code currency but may differ for multi-currency projects.

Boolean indicator whether costs on this internal order are capitalized (True) or expensed (False). True for capital investments that will be settled to fixed assets; False for operating expenses settled to cost centers or GL accounts.
Total committed costs (purchase requisitions and purchase orders) against this internal order. Used for funds availability checking and budget consumption reporting.
Four-character SAP FI company code representing the legal entity to which this internal order belongs. Used for financial consolidation and legal reporting.
Four-character SAP CO controlling area code. Represents the organizational unit for cost accounting and internal management reporting. Multiple company codes may share a controlling area.
Date and time when this internal order master record was first created in SAP CO. Used for audit trail and lifecycle tracking.
Boolean indicator whether this internal order has been marked for deletion. True indicates the order is logically deleted but retained for audit purposes; False indicates an active or closed order.
reference: SAP CO Master Data Deletion Policy

description: Fiscal year in which this internal order was created or is active. Used for multi-year project tracking and budget period assignment.
SAP user ID of the individual who last modified this internal order master record. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Date and time when this internal order master record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.
Free-text description of the internal order purpose, scope, and business justification. Provides context for financial analysts and auditors reviewing project costs.
Business identifier for the internal order, externally visible and used in financial reporting and project tracking. Typically a 10-12 character alphanumeric code assigned by SAP CO.

Current lifecycle status of the internal order. Controls whether costs can be posted, whether the order can be settled, and whether it appears in active project reports. Status progression: created → released → technically_complete → settled → closed.

Classification of the internal order by business purpose. Determines cost collection rules, settlement targets, and reporting hierarchy. Common types include capital investments, store remodels, marketing campaigns, maintenance projects, and IT initiatives.
Planned completion date for the project or activity. Used to determine when the order should be technically complete and ready for settlement.
Planned start date for the project or activity tracked by this internal order. Used for project scheduling and resource planning.
Email address of the project manager for communication and escalation purposes. Used for automated budget alerts and approval workflows.
Full name of the individual responsible for managing this internal order project. Accountable for budget adherence, timeline, and deliverables.
Name or code of the business department that requested this internal order. Used for chargeback and accountability reporting.
Date when costs accumulated on this internal order were settled to the final receiver cost object. Marks the financial close of the order.
Identifier of the specific cost object (asset number, cost center code, GL account number) to which costs will be settled. Format varies by receiver type.
Type of cost object to which this internal order will be settled upon completion. Common receivers include fixed assets (for CAPEX), cost centers (for OPEX), or GL accounts (for period costs).

Configuration key defining how costs accumulated on this internal order are settled to final cost objects (e.g., asset under construction, cost center, GL account). Determines settlement percentage, receiver type, and settlement period rules.
Calculated variance between budget and actual plus committed costs. Positive values indicate under-budget, negative values indicate over-budget. Used for exception reporting and project controls.
Identifier of the WBS element if this internal order is part of a larger project structure. Links the order to a project hierarchy for integrated project and cost management.
reference: SAP PS Project System - WBS Master

description: SAP user ID of the individual who created this internal order master record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Primary key for profit_center
Unique identifier for the profit center. Primary key for this entity representing autonomous business segments for internal profitability reporting.
The retail banner or brand under which this profit center operates (e.g., 'KROGER', 'RALPHS', 'FREDS', 'HARRIS'). Used for banner-level comp sales and same-store sales reporting.
SAP business area for cross-company-code reporting and internal financial statements by line of business (e.g., grocery retail, pharmacy, fuel).
The date this profit center was permanently closed. Null for active profit centers. Used for historical analysis and asset impairment calculations.

Boolean flag indicating whether this profit center is eligible for comp sales (comparable store sales) reporting. True if the location has been open for at least 12 months and meets comp sales criteria.
The SAP FI company code representing the legal entity to which this profit center belongs. Required for legal consolidation and SOX compliance reporting.
reference: SAP FI Organizational Structure; SOX Section 404

description: The SAP Controlling Area to which this profit center is assigned. Controlling area represents the organizational unit for cost accounting and internal management reporting.
The default cost center associated with this profit center for overhead allocation and indirect cost assignment.
The timestamp when this profit center master record was first created in the SAP CO system. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for this profit center's local currency (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN). Used for multi-currency consolidation and EBITDA reporting.
The number of distinct departments or categories within this profit center (e.g., produce, meat, dairy, bakery, pharmacy, fuel). Used for assortment complexity analysis.
The district code within a region, representing a cluster of stores managed by a district manager. Used for district-level P&L roll-up.
The current number of full-time equivalent (FTE) employees assigned to this profit center. Used for labor productivity and EBITDA per employee calculations.
SAP fiscal year variant defining the fiscal calendar for this profit center (e.g., K4 for calendar year, V3 for 4-4-5 retail calendar).
The hierarchical level of this profit center in the organizational structure (1=top-level, 2=division, 3=region, 4=store, etc.). Used for roll-up reporting and consolidation.
The SAP user ID of the person who last modified this profit center master record. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.
The timestamp of the most recent change to this profit center master record. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Boolean flag indicating whether this profit center is locked for new postings. True when locked (no new transactions allowed), False when open for posting.
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Master Data

description: The market or metropolitan area code (e.g., 'NYC', 'LAX', 'CHI'). Used for market-level competitive analysis and same-store sales comparisons.
The date this profit center (store, pharmacy, fuel center) first opened for business. Used to calculate comp sales eligibility and age-based performance metrics.
The code of the parent profit center in the hierarchy. Null for top-level profit centers. Enables roll-up of comp sales, same-store sales, and EBITDA by region or division.
The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the profit center in SAP CO. Used in financial reports, general ledger postings, and management accounting documents.
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Master Data

description: Extended text description providing additional context about the profit center's purpose, scope, and business model (e.g., 'High-volume urban supermarket with full-service pharmacy and fuel center').
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Master Data

description: The full business name of the profit center (e.g., 'Store 1234 - Downtown Seattle', 'Pharmacy Division - West Region', 'Private Label Bakery').
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Master Data

description: Current lifecycle status of the profit center: active (operational and posting transactions), inactive (temporarily suspended), planned (future opening), closed (permanently shut down).
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Master Data

description: Classification of the profit center by business function: store-level P&L, banner-level aggregation, pharmacy operations, fuel center operations, distribution center, private-label brand division, or corporate overhead. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store|banner|pharmacy|fuel_center|distribution_center|private_label|corporate — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The geographic region code for this profit center (e.g., 'WEST', 'MIDWEST', 'SOUTH', 'NORTHEAST'). Used for regional performance analysis and comp sales benchmarking.
The date of the most recent major remodel or renovation. Significant remodels may reset comp sales eligibility timers.

Boolean flag indicating whether this profit center qualifies for same-store sales reporting (revenue comparison for existing locations year-over-year). Typically requires 13+ months of operation without major remodels.
The business segment classification for external financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP segment reporting requirements (e.g., 'RETAIL', 'PHARM', 'FUEL', 'PRIVLBL').
Abbreviated name for the profit center used in reports and dashboards where space is limited.
reference: SAP CO Profit Center Master Data

description: The total retail square footage of the store or facility. Used to calculate sales per square foot and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) metrics.
The date from which this profit center is valid and can accept financial postings. Used for time-dependent master data and historical reporting.
The date until which this profit center is valid. Null for open-ended validity. Used to track store closures, division consolidations, and organizational changes.
Unique identifier for the fiscal period. Primary key.
User identifier of the finance team member who executed the period close. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.
Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Fiscal periods belong to a legal entity; linking provides entity‑specific calendar context.
Reference to the corresponding fiscal period in the prior fiscal year. Enables year-over-year comp sales and same-store sales calculations.
Identifier for the budget version applicable to this fiscal period (e.g., 'Original', 'Revised_Q2', 'Forecast'). Supports budget vs actual variance analysis.
Number of business operating days (excluding weekends and holidays) in this fiscal period. Used for labor budgeting and productivity metrics.
The calendar month number (1-12) in which the fiscal period primarily falls. Supports calendar-based reporting and seasonality analysis.
The calendar year (Gregorian) in which the majority of this fiscal period falls. Used for calendar-year reporting and external benchmarking.
The actual calendar date when the fiscal period was closed and locked for posting. Null if period is still open. Critical for SOX compliance and audit trail.
Precise timestamp when the fiscal period close process was completed. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance documentation.
Timestamp when this fiscal period record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage.
Total number of calendar days in this fiscal period. Used for daily rate calculations and pro-rata adjustments.
The date used for currency exchange rate determination for this fiscal period. Typically period-end date or average rate date for multi-currency consolidation.
Boolean flag indicating whether this fiscal period is subject to external audit review. Typically true for year-end and quarterly periods for publicly traded companies.
Sequential period number within the fiscal year (1-13 for 13-period retail calendar or 1-12 for standard calendar). Used for period-over-period comparisons and comp sales calculations.
Fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this period belongs. Supports quarterly financial reporting and EBITDA analysis.
The fiscal year to which this period belongs (e.g., 2024, 2025). Aligns with Grocery's fiscal calendar and SAP FI fiscal year variant.

business_glossary_term: GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles) Compliant Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether financial reporting for this period follows GAAP standards. True for US-based public company reporting.
Boolean flag indicating whether this is the current active fiscal period for transaction posting. True if current, false otherwise.
Boolean flag indicating whether the fiscal year containing this period is a leap year. Affects day count calculations and year-over-year comparisons.
Timestamp when this fiscal period record was last updated. Tracks changes to period status, close dates, and other attributes.
Free-text notes or comments about this fiscal period. May include information about special events, adjustments, calendar anomalies, or close process issues.
The last calendar date of the fiscal period. Defines the end of the posting date range for transactions and triggers period-end close processes.
reference: SAP FI Posting Period Date Range

description: Human-readable name or label for the fiscal period (e.g., 'FY2024-P01', 'January 2024', 'Q1-2024'). Used in financial reports and dashboards.
The first calendar date of the fiscal period. Defines the beginning of the posting date range for transactions.
reference: SAP FI Posting Period Date Range

description: Current lifecycle status of the fiscal period. Controls whether transactions can be posted. Open allows posting, closed prevents new postings, locked is permanently closed, pending_close indicates close process is underway.

Classification of the fiscal period. Regular periods are standard operating periods, adjustment periods are for accruals and corrections, special periods are for extraordinary events, year_end is the final closing period.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for financial reporting in this period (e.g., 'USD'). Supports multi-currency consolidation and GAAP compliance.
Date when SOX certification and management attestation were completed for this fiscal period. Required for publicly traded companies.
Boolean flag indicating whether this fiscal period requires SOX certification and management attestation. True for publicly traded company reporting periods.
Number of weeks in this fiscal period. Typically 4 or 5 weeks for 4-4-5 retail calendar. Critical for normalizing comp sales and same-store sales metrics.
Primary key for gl_account

The externally-known alphanumeric GL account number as defined in the SAP FI Chart of Accounts (SKA1 table). This is the primary business identifier used across all financial postings, journal entries, and regulatory disclosures. Typically a 6–10 digit numeric code aligned with the company's chart of accounts structure.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – Chart of Accounts; GAAP ASC 210/220/230

description: SAP FI account group (KTOKS) that controls the number range and field status for the GL account. Groups accounts by functional category such as balance sheet accounts, P&L accounts, cost accounts, or reconciliation accounts. Used for mass maintenance and reporting segmentation.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – KTOKS; SAP FI Account Group Configuration

description: Numeric level within the GL account hierarchy structure (e.g., 1=top-level group, 5=detail posting account). Supports financial statement rollup, management reporting hierarchies, and drill-down analytics in EBITDA and comp sales dashboards.
reference: SAP FI Financial Statement Version Hierarchy; GAAP ASC 205

description: Extended descriptive name for the GL account providing additional context beyond the short name. Used in detailed financial disclosures, SOX audit documentation, and ERP configuration reports.
reference: SAP FI SKAT – Account Long Text; SOX Section 302/404

description: The human-readable short description or title of the GL account as maintained in SAP FI (SKAT table). Used in financial reports, trial balances, and audit documentation. Example: 'Cost of Goods Sold – Grocery', 'Accounts Payable – Trade'.
reference: SAP FI SKAT – Account Descriptions; GAAP ASC 210

description: Current lifecycle status of the GL account. 'active' accounts accept financial postings; 'blocked' accounts are temporarily restricted from new postings; 'marked_for_deletion' accounts are pending archival; 'inactive' accounts are retired but retained for historical reporting. Corresponds to SAP FI account blocking indicators.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – Account Blocking Indicators; SOX Section 404 – Internal Controls

description: High-level GAAP classification of the GL account indicating its role in financial statements. Maps to SAP FI KTOKS field. 'asset' and 'liability' accounts appear on the balance sheet; 'revenue' and 'expense' accounts appear on the P&L (income statement); 'equity' accounts appear in the statement of equity.
reference: GAAP ASC 210 (Balance Sheet); ASC 220 (Income Statement); SAP FI KTOKS

description: Country-specific or group-level alternative account number used for statutory reporting or financial consolidation mapping. Corresponds to SAP FI ALTKT field. Used when Grocery must report under multiple chart of accounts structures (e.g., local GAAP vs. group GAAP).
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – ALTKT; GAAP ASC 205; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Indicates whether this is a balance sheet account (True) or a profit and loss (P&L) income statement account (False). Balance sheet accounts carry forward balances at year-end; P&L accounts are closed to retained earnings. Corresponds to SAP FI XBILK field in SKA1.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – XBILK; GAAP ASC 210/220; SOX Section 302

description: The SAP chart of accounts key (e.g., 'GROC') that this GL account belongs to. Grocery may maintain multiple charts of accounts for different legal entities or country-specific GAAP requirements. Corresponds to the KTOPL field in SAP FI SKA1.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – KTOPL; GAAP ASC 205

business_glossary_term: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) Account Indicator
description: Indicates whether this GL account is classified as a Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) account. COGS accounts are used in gross margin and GMROI calculations, merchandise planning analysis, and EBITDA reporting. Supports Oracle Retail Merchandising System and SAP FI/CO integration for cost flow tracking.
reference: GAAP ASC 330 Inventory Costs; SAP CO-PA Profitability Analysis; Oracle Retail Merchandising

description: Indicates whether this GL account participates in SAP CO commitment management, tracking purchase order commitments and budget reservations before actual expenditure. Relevant for capital expenditure accounts, store renovation budgets, and DC equipment procurement.
reference: SAP CO Commitment Management; GAAP ASC 420 Exit or Disposal Costs

description: SAP Controlling (CO) cost element category indicating whether this GL account is a primary cost element (direct costs posted from FI, e.g., COGS, labor), a secondary cost element (internal allocations, overhead), or not applicable (balance sheet accounts). Drives cost center accounting, EBITDA reporting, and shrink financial impact analysis.
reference: SAP CO – Cost Element Category (KATYP); GAAP ASC 420

description: Granular classification of the cost element within SAP CO, such as 'material costs', 'personnel costs', 'depreciation', 'overhead allocation', 'revenue'. Supports detailed cost center reporting and profitability analysis (CO-PA) across store banners. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: material_costs|personnel_costs|depreciation|overhead_allocation|revenue|settlement|assessment|distribution — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP CO-OM Cost Element Accounting; GAAP ASC 330/420

description: The timestamp when this GL account record was first created in the source system (SAP FI). Provides the audit trail creation point required for SOX compliance and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Section 404 – Audit Trail; SAP FI Change Document; GAAP Internal Controls

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which this GL account is maintained (e.g., 'USD'). Grocery operates primarily in USD but may maintain foreign currency accounts for international vendor transactions or cross-border operations. Corresponds to SAP FI WAERS field.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP FI SKA1 – WAERS; GAAP ASC 830 Foreign Currency

description: The date from which this GL account became active and available for financial postings. Supports time-based account validity controls, ensuring accounts are only used within their authorized period. Corresponds to SAP FI account validity start date.
reference: SAP FI Account Validity; GAAP ASC 205; SOX Section 404 Change Management Controls

description: The date after which this GL account is no longer valid for new financial postings. Null indicates the account has no defined end date and remains open-ended. Supports account lifecycle management and year-end close procedures.
reference: SAP FI Account Validity; GAAP ASC 205; SOX Section 404 Change Management Controls

description: SAP FI field status group (FSTAG) assigned to this GL account, controlling which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during document entry. Ensures data completeness for cost center, profit center, and business area assignments on postings. Critical for CO integration and reporting accuracy.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – FSTAG; SAP FI Field Status Configuration

description: The primary financial statement section where this GL account is reported. Drives financial consolidation mapping and GAAP-compliant disclosure preparation. Used in EBITDA reporting, comp sales analysis, and SOX financial reporting controls.
reference: GAAP ASC 205; SOX Section 302/404; SAP FI Financial Statement Version

description: SAP FI/CO functional area classification used for cost-of-sales accounting and segment reporting. Segments expenses by business function such as 'manufacturing', 'sales', 'administration', 'research_development'. Supports GAAP income statement by function presentation and SEC segment disclosures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store_operations|supply_chain|merchandising|pharmacy|administration|marketing|finance|it|fuel_operations — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP FI/CO Functional Area; GAAP ASC 280 Segment Reporting; SEC Regulation S-X

description: Detailed GAAP line-item classification for the account, such as 'Current Assets', 'Long-Term Liabilities', 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)', 'Selling General and Administrative (SG&A)', 'Depreciation and Amortization'. Supports EBITDA calculation, COGS tracking, and SEC financial disclosure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current_assets|non_current_assets|current_liabilities|non_current_liabilities|equity|net_revenue|cogs|gross_profit|sga|depreciation_amortization|interest_expense|income_tax|other_income_expense — promote to reference product]
reference: GAAP ASC 210/220/230/360; SEC Regulation S-X; SOX Section 302

description: Indicates whether this GL account is a bank account (house bank) used for cash management and treasury operations. Bank accounts require special reconciliation procedures and are subject to PCI DSS controls for payment processing. Supports daily cash position reporting and bank reconciliation.
reference: SAP FI Bank Accounting; PCI DSS v4.0 – Payment Processing Security; GAAP ASC 230 Cash Flow

business_glossary_term: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) Mapping Code
description: IFRS account mapping code for international financial reporting or cross-border consolidation purposes. Used when Grocery operates entities in jurisdictions requiring IFRS reporting alongside US GAAP. Supports dual-ledger accounting in SAP FI parallel ledger configuration.
reference: IFRS Foundation – IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements; SAP FI Parallel Ledger

description: Indicates whether this GL account is used for intercompany transactions between Grocery's legal entities (e.g., store banners, distribution centers, pharmacy subsidiaries). Intercompany accounts require elimination during financial consolidation. Supports SOX-compliant consolidation and GAAP ASC 810 requirements.
reference: GAAP ASC 810 Consolidation; SOX Section 302/404; SAP FI Intercompany Configuration

description: Indicates whether direct financial postings are permitted to this GL account. False for summary/header accounts that only aggregate child accounts, or for accounts blocked for posting. Corresponds to SAP FI XLOEV (account blocked for posting) flag.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – XLOEV; GAAP Internal Controls

description: Indicates whether this GL account is a reconciliation account for a subledger (Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, or Asset Accounting). Reconciliation accounts cannot receive direct manual postings; they are updated automatically from subledger transactions. Critical for AP/AR balance integrity and SOX controls.
reference: SAP FI – Reconciliation Account (MITKZ); GAAP ASC 210; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether this GL account is subject to tax reporting obligations. True for accounts involving taxable sales, purchases, or excise duties (e.g., fuel center, alcohol, tobacco). Supports tax compliance reporting and FTC regulatory filings.
reference: SAP FI Tax Configuration; IRS Regulations; FTC Compliance

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this GL account record in the source system. Supports change tracking, SOX audit trail requirements, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline.
reference: SOX Section 404 – Change Management; SAP FI Change Document (CDHDR/CDPOS); GAAP Internal Controls

description: Indicates whether individual line items are stored and displayable for this GL account in SAP FI. When True, auditors and finance analysts can drill down to individual posting details. Required for SOX audit trails and GAAP disclosure support. Corresponds to SAP FI XKRES field.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – XKRES; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements

description: Indicates whether open item management is activated for this GL account. When True, individual line items remain open until cleared by a matching offsetting entry (e.g., GR/IR clearing accounts, bank clearing accounts). Essential for AP/AR reconciliation and month-end close processes.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – XOPVW; GAAP Internal Controls; SOX Section 404

description: The account code of the parent/summary account in the GL hierarchy. Enables hierarchical rollup of financial balances for management reporting, financial consolidation, and EBITDA calculation. Null for top-level accounts.
reference: SAP FI Financial Statement Version; GAAP ASC 205 Financial Statement Presentation

description: Indicates whether this GL account is used in SAP CO budgeting and planning processes. Planning accounts are included in annual budget cycles, labor budget planning (Kronos integration), and Blue Yonder demand planning financial inputs. Supports comp sales and same-store sales budget vs. actual analysis.
reference: SAP CO-OM Planning; Blue Yonder Demand Planning Integration; GAAP ASC 275

business_glossary_term: Profit and Loss (P&L) Statement Account Type
description: Classifies P&L accounts into their income statement reporting category for EBITDA calculation, comp sales analysis, and same-store sales reporting. 'cogs' accounts feed COGS tracking; 'ebitda_component' accounts are used in EBITDA reporting. Not applicable for balance sheet accounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_revenue|cogs|gross_profit|operating_expense|ebitda_component|interest|tax|not_applicable — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: GAAP ASC 220; SEC Regulation S-X; SOX Section 302; SAP FI Financial Statement Version

description: Specifies the subledger type this reconciliation account is linked to. Applicable only when is_reconciliation_account is True. Drives AP/AR aging reports, vendor payment reconciliation, and fixed asset depreciation schedules.
reference: SAP FI – MITKZ Reconciliation Account Type; GAAP ASC 210/360

description: Indicates whether this GL account is designated for recording shrink (inventory loss from theft, spoilage, or damage). Shrink accounts are critical for fresh produce and perishable inventory financial impact tracking, GMROI analysis, and loss prevention reporting across store banners.
reference: Grocery Internal Shrink Policy; GAAP ASC 330 Inventory; USDA Perishable Goods Standards

description: SAP FI sort key (ZUAWA) that determines the assignment field populated in line items posted to this account. Controls how line items are sorted and displayed in account statements. Common values: '001' (posting date), '003' (document number), '031' (vendor invoice number). Supports efficient account reconciliation.
reference: SAP FI SKA1 – ZUAWA; SAP FI Sort Key Configuration

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this GL account was sourced. Primarily 'SAP_FI' for the SAP Financial Accounting module. Supports data lineage, reconciliation between source systems, and Silver Layer provenance tracking.
reference: SAP FI – Source System Identification; Databricks Data Lineage Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this GL account is in scope for SOX Section 302/404 internal controls testing and audit. SOX-relevant accounts require documented controls, segregation of duties, and periodic reconciliation sign-off. Critical for Grocery's external audit and SEC financial reporting compliance.

description: Unique identifier for the journal entry document. Primary key for the journal entry product.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each journal entry posts to a GL account; linking enables traceability to the account hierarchy.
The user ID of the person who created or entered this journal entry into the system. Used for SOX compliance audit trails and segregation of duties monitoring.
The user ID of the person who posted this journal entry to the general ledger. Null if entry is parked but not yet posted. Used for SOX compliance segregation of duties monitoring.
The user ID of the person who parked this journal entry (saved without posting). Null if entry was never parked. Used for workflow tracking and segregation of duties monitoring.

The user ID of the person who approved this journal entry for posting. Null if approval workflow is not required or entry is not yet approved. Used for SOX compliance segregation of duties and approval workflow tracking.
The date and time when this journal entry was approved for posting. Null if not yet approved or approval not required. Used for approval workflow tracking and SOX compliance monitoring.
The baseline date used for payment term calculation in accounts payable processing. Typically the invoice date or goods receipt date. Used for payment due date calculation and cash flow forecasting.
The batch input session name if this journal entry was created via batch processing. Null for online entries. Used for batch processing tracking and error resolution.
The date on which this journal entry was cleared or settled. Null if not yet cleared. Used for aging analysis and cash flow forecasting.
The document number of the clearing entry that settled this journal entry. Null if not yet cleared. Used for accounts payable/receivable reconciliation and open item management.

The legal entity or company code to which this journal entry belongs. Represents the smallest organizational unit for which a complete self-contained set of accounts can be drawn up for external reporting. Used for financial consolidation and statutory reporting.
The date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Audit trail timestamp for SOX compliance and internal control monitoring.

The transaction currency in which the journal entry amounts are denominated. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency reporting and foreign exchange gain/loss calculation.
The date printed on the source document (invoice date, receipt date, contract date). May differ from posting date due to processing delays. Used for aging analysis and vendor/customer reconciliation.
The externally-known unique document number assigned by the financial system (SAP FI document number). This is the business identifier used in audit trails and financial reporting.

Classification of the journal entry document type. SA=General Ledger Document, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AB=Accounting Document, KG=Vendor Credit Memo, DZ=Payment Document. Determines posting rules and account determination logic.
The date when the journal entry was first created or entered into the system by the user. Audit trail timestamp for SOX compliance and internal control monitoring.

The foreign exchange rate used to convert transaction currency amounts to local currency amounts. Null if transaction currency equals local currency. Used for foreign exchange gain/loss calculation and multi-currency consolidation.

The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 if a 13th adjusting period is used. Critical for period-end close and monthly financial reporting.
The fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned. Used for year-end close, annual financial statement preparation, and multi-year comparative reporting.
Free-form text description at the document header level providing context or explanation for the journal entry. Used for audit trail documentation and user communication.

Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction between two company codes within the Grocery enterprise. True if intercompany, False otherwise. Used for intercompany reconciliation and elimination entries in consolidated financial statements.

The ledger or ledger group to which this journal entry is posted. 0L=Leading Ledger (GAAP), 2L=IFRS Ledger, 9Z=Management Reporting Ledger. Supports parallel accounting for multiple accounting standards.
reference: SAP FI New General Ledger Configuration

description: The local (company code) currency in which amounts are also stored for statutory reporting. Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code. Typically USD for US-based grocery operations.
The date and time when this journal entry was last modified. Null if never modified after creation. Audit trail timestamp for SOX compliance and change tracking.
The date and time when this journal entry was parked. Null if entry was never parked. Used for workflow tracking and aging analysis of parked documents.
Code indicating a payment block preventing automatic payment processing. Null if no block exists. Used for payment exception management and vendor dispute resolution.
Code indicating the payment method for this journal entry. C=Check, T=Wire Transfer, W=ACH, Z=Other. Used for payment processing and cash management.
The payment terms code defining the due date calculation and discount terms for this journal entry. Used for accounts payable aging, cash flow forecasting, and early payment discount analysis.
The date and time when this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Null if entry is parked but not yet posted. Used for posting workflow tracking and audit trail analysis.

The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the principal business event date that determines which fiscal period the transaction affects. Used for period-end close, trial balance, and financial statement generation.

Current lifecycle status of the journal entry. Posted=finalized and affecting GL balances, Parked=saved but not yet posted, Cleared=settled against another document, Reversed=negated by reversal entry, Cancelled=voided before posting.

External reference number from the source document (vendor invoice number, customer order number, check number, wire transfer reference). Used for reconciliation and audit trail linkage to source documents.

Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a prior entry. True if this document reverses another document, False otherwise. Used for accrual reversal processing and error correction tracking.

Code indicating the reason for reversal. 01=Accrual Reversal, 02=Error Correction, 03=Period-End Adjustment, 04=Reclassification, 05=Duplicate Entry, 06=Other. Used for internal control monitoring and audit trail analysis.
The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Null if this is not a reversal entry. Used to maintain audit trail linkage between original and reversing entries.

Code identifying the source system or module that originated this journal entry. SAP_FI=Financial Accounting, SAP_CO=Controlling, SAP_MM=Materials Management, SAP_SD=Sales and Distribution, POS=Point of Sale, WMS=Warehouse Management System, MANUAL=Manual Entry.
