Numeric day of the month or week that a pay period starts.
Scheduled date on which payroll is processed for the period.
Number of calendar days in each pay period.
Current lifecycle status of the calendar.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the pay calendar record.
Primary key for pay_group
Self-referencing FK on pay_group (parent_pay_group_id)
Short alphanumeric code used to reference the pay group in payroll systems.
Cost center identifier to which payroll costs for this group are charged.
Timestamp when the pay group record was first created in the system.
Detailed description of the pay group purpose and usage.
Date when the pay group becomes effective for payroll processing.
Date when the pay group ceases to be effective (null if open‑ended).
Business rules or conditions that determine employee eligibility for this pay group.
Indicates whether this pay group is the default for new hires (true) or not (false).
Descriptive name of the pay group (e.g., Hourly, Salary, Shift Differential).
Free‑form field for additional remarks or administrative comments.
True if employees in this pay group are eligible for overtime pay.
Standard payroll frequency associated with the pay group.
Financial account code used for posting payroll expenses for this group.
Current lifecycle status of the pay group.
Indicates whether payroll taxes are exempt for this pay group.
Classification of the pay group by compensation model.
True if the pay group is covered by a collective bargaining agreement.
Timestamp of the most recent modification to the pay group record.
Unique surrogate identifier for each payroll run record in the Workday HCM Payroll system. Serves as the primary key for the payroll_run data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Reference to the primary SAP CO cost center associated with this payroll run for labor cost allocation. Enables EBITDA analysis and store-level labor cost reporting. Individual associate-level cost center splits may vary.

Reference to the legal entity (company code in SAP FI) under which this payroll run is processed. Determines tax jurisdiction, employer identification number (EIN), and GL posting rules for SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP FI Financial Accounting; SOX Section 302/404; IRS EIN

description: Reference to the Workday HCM pay calendar that governs the schedule of this payroll run, including pay period dates, check dates, and processing deadlines. Determines the annual payroll schedule for the associated pay group.

Reference to the Workday HCM pay group that defines the population of associates included in this payroll run (e.g., store hourly, pharmacy salaried, DC associates, corporate staff). Determines pay frequency, pay calendar, and processing rules.
Reference to the Workday HCM user (payroll administrator) who initiated and submitted this payroll run for processing. Required for SOX segregation of duties controls and payroll audit trail.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Reference to the preceding payroll run that this run corrects or supersedes. Populated only for run_type = correction or off_cycle. Enables audit trail linking between original and corrected payroll runs for SOX compliance.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Date and time when the payroll run received final approval in Workday HCM before processing. Captures the approval event in the payroll lifecycle for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties documentation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Total number of associates included and processed in this payroll run. Used for payroll audit validation, headcount reconciliation, and detecting anomalies (e.g., unexpected count changes between runs).
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Scheduled date on which associates receive payment via direct deposit or physical check. This is the principal business event date for the payroll run and is used for GL posting dates in SAP FI and tax deposit timing per IRS schedules.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Publication 15; SAP FI

description: Date and time when this payroll run record was first created in Workday HCM. Serves as the audit record creation timestamp for SOX internal controls and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll run (e.g., USD). Grocery operates primarily in USD; this field supports multi-entity reporting and potential international expansion.

Identifier of the ACH (Automated Clearing House) NACHA-format direct deposit file generated for this payroll run and transmitted to the bank. Used for payment reconciliation and resolving associate pay inquiry cases.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; NACHA Operating Rules; Federal Reserve Regulation E

description: SAP FI document number generated when payroll journal entries are posted to the General Ledger. Provides the cross-reference between Workday HCM payroll records and SAP FI accounting documents for SOX audit and reconciliation.
reference: SAP FI Financial Accounting; SOX Section 302/404

description: Date on which payroll journal entries are posted to the SAP FI General Ledger. May differ from check_date due to accounting period cutoffs. Critical for period-end close, EBITDA reporting, and SOX financial controls.
reference: SAP FI Financial Accounting; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP ASC 420

description: Indicates whether this payroll run has been finalized and locked in Workday HCM, preventing further modifications. Final runs trigger GL posting to SAP FI and ACH direct deposit file generation. Non-final runs are still in preview or correction mode.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Indicates whether this payroll run is an off-cycle run executed outside the standard pay calendar schedule (e.g., termination pay, correction payments, bonus disbursements). Off-cycle runs require additional SOX approval controls.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Date and time when this payroll run record was most recently modified in Workday HCM or the Databricks Silver Layer. Tracks the latest state change for audit trail completeness and incremental data pipeline processing.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Frequency at which associates are paid for this payroll run. Determines the pay period cadence and aligns with Workday HCM pay group configurations. Common frequencies include weekly for hourly store associates and bi-weekly for salaried staff.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; FLSA (Fair Labor Standards Act)

description: Last calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll run. Defines the closing boundary of the earnings window. Must align with Kronos Workforce Central time and attendance cutoff for accurate hours capture.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Kronos Workforce Central; FLSA

description: First calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll run. Defines the beginning of the earnings window for all associates included in the run. Used for W-2 reconciliation and labor cost reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Publication 15; FLSA

description: Date and time when Workday HCM completed the payroll calculation and finalized the run. Marks the principal business event completion time. Used for payroll SLA compliance and downstream GL posting scheduling in SAP FI.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Date and time when Workday HCM began executing the payroll calculation engine for this run. Used for SLA monitoring of payroll processing windows and operational performance tracking.
Externally-known business identifier for the payroll run as assigned by Workday HCM (e.g., PR-2024-001). Used for audit trails, SOX financial controls, and payroll reconciliation references.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Current lifecycle state of the payroll run within the Workday HCM processing workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through completion or cancellation. Supports SOX audit controls and payroll operations monitoring.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404

description: Classification of the payroll run indicating whether it is a standard scheduled run, an off-cycle adjustment, a correction to a prior run, a supplemental payment, or a bonus payout. Drives processing rules and GL posting in SAP FI.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SAP FI Financial Accounting

description: Calendar tax year (e.g., 2024) to which this payroll run belongs. Determines applicable tax rates, wage bases, and W-2 reporting period. Essential for year-end W-2 reconciliation and IRS Form 941 annual filing.
reference: IRS Publication 15; IRS W-2; SOX Section 302/404

description: Total dollar amount disbursed via physical payroll checks for associates not enrolled in direct deposit. Together with total_direct_deposit_amount, reconciles to total_net_pay. Supports treasury and accounts payable reconciliation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SAP FI

description: Aggregate of all pre-tax and post-tax deductions across all associates in this payroll run, including 401(k), HSA, FSA, medical premiums, garnishments, union dues, and Roth contributions. Supports benefits reconciliation and garnishment compliance reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Section 125; ERISA; Consumer Credit Protection Act (CCPA) garnishment limits

description: Total dollar amount transmitted via ACH direct deposit for this payroll run. Should reconcile to total_net_pay minus any physical check amounts. Used for bank reconciliation and treasury cash management.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; NACHA Operating Rules

description: Aggregate employer-side tax obligations for this payroll run, including employer FICA (Social Security and Medicare match), federal unemployment tax (FUTA), and state unemployment insurance (SUI). Required for accurate labor cost accounting and IRS Form 941 filing.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Form 941; FUTA (26 U.S.C. § 3301); SUTA

description: Aggregate of all court-ordered wage garnishments, child support withholdings, and creditor garnishments deducted across all associates in this payroll run. Required for garnishment compliance reporting and remittance to issuing agencies per Consumer Credit Protection Act limits.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Consumer Credit Protection Act (CCPA) Title III; IRS Tax Levy

description: Sum of all gross earnings for all associates included in this payroll run before any tax withholdings or deductions. Includes regular pay, overtime, holiday pay, and premium pay. Key metric for SOX financial controls and payroll audit reconciliation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 302/404; GAAP

description: Total amount disbursed to all associates after all tax withholdings and deductions. Equals total_gross_pay minus total_tax_withholdings minus total_deductions. Primary cash outflow figure for treasury and GL posting in SAP FI.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SAP FI; SOX Section 302/404

description: Aggregate of all post-tax deductions across all associates in this payroll run, including Roth 401(k) contributions, union dues, and voluntary post-tax benefit elections. Does not reduce taxable wages but reduces net pay.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Section 402A (Roth); NLRA (union dues)

description: Aggregate of all pre-tax benefit deductions across all associates in this payroll run, including 401(k) contributions, HSA contributions, FSA contributions, and medical/dental/vision premiums under Section 125 cafeteria plans. Reduces taxable wages for W-2 reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Section 125; IRS Section 401(k); IRS Section 223 (HSA)

description: Aggregate of all federal, state, local, and FICA (Social Security and Medicare) tax withholdings across all associates in this payroll run. Used for IRS tax deposit scheduling and W-2 reconciliation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Publication 15; FICA (26 U.S.C. § 3101)

description: Native identifier of this payroll run in the Workday HCM source system. Enables cross-reference between the Databricks Silver Layer record and the operational system of record for audit, reconciliation, and support inquiry resolution.

Cumulative gross pay for all associates in this pay group from the start of the tax year through this payroll run. Used for W-2 reconciliation, Social Security wage base tracking, and annual compensation reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS W-2 Reporting; Social Security Administration wage base

description: Cumulative tax withholdings for all associates in this pay group from the start of the tax year through this payroll run. Critical for W-2 Box 2 (federal income tax withheld) reconciliation and IRS Form 941 quarterly reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS W-2; IRS Form 941

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each benefit enrollment record in the Workday HCM Benefits Administration module. Serves as the primary key for this data product.

Reference to the associate enrolled in the benefit plan. Links to the workforce employee master record in Workday HCM. Covers store associates, pharmacists, distribution center workers, and corporate staff.
Reference to the specific benefit plan in which the associate is enrolled. Links to the benefit plan master record maintained in Workday HCM Benefits Administration.

Reference to the open enrollment period during which this enrollment was submitted. Populated for enrollments sourced from open enrollment events; null for qualifying life event or new hire enrollments. Supports open enrollment administration and participation rate analytics.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; ERISA Section 702

business_glossary_term: Affordable Care Act (ACA) Affordability Safe Harbor Method
description: The IRS-approved safe harbor method used to determine whether the employee's required contribution for self-only coverage is affordable under ACA Section 4980H(b). Drives 1095-C Line 16 coding. W2 uses Box 1 wages; rate_of_pay uses hourly rate; federal_poverty_line uses FPL threshold.
reference: ACA Section 4980H(b); IRS Form 1095-C Line 16; 26 CFR 54.4980H-5; IRS Rev. Proc. 2014-37

description: Indicates whether this benefit enrollment constitutes minimum essential coverage (MEC) under the Affordable Care Act. True means the plan qualifies as ACA-compliant MEC. Drives 1095-C Line 14 offer-of-coverage code determination and employer shared responsibility payment (ESRP) calculations.
reference: ACA Section 4980H; IRS Form 1095-C; IRS Publication 974; 26 CFR 54.4980H-4

business_glossary_term: Affordable Care Act (ACA) Minimum Value Indicator
description: Indicates whether the enrolled health plan meets the ACA minimum value standard (covers at least 60% of total allowed costs). Required for 1095-C reporting and employer shared responsibility compliance under ACA Section 4980H(b).
reference: ACA Section 4980H(b); IRS Form 1095-C Line 14; 26 CFR 54.4980H-5; IRS Notice 2012-31

business_glossary_term: Affordable Care Act (ACA) Offer of Coverage Code
description: IRS-defined code for 1095-C Line 14 indicating the type of health coverage offered to the employee and their dependents/spouse. Codes 1A-1F correspond to specific offer scenarios defined by the IRS for employer shared responsibility reporting.
reference: IRS Form 1095-C Line 14; ACA Section 4980H; IRS Instructions for Forms 1094-C and 1095-C

business_glossary_term: Affordable Care Act (ACA) Safe Harbor Code
description: IRS-defined code for 1095-C Line 16 indicating the applicable safe harbor or relief provision for each month. Codes 2A-2F correspond to specific relief scenarios (e.g., 2C = enrolled in coverage, 2F = W-2 safe harbor). Required for ACA employer shared responsibility reporting.
reference: IRS Form 1095-C Line 16; ACA Section 4980H; IRS Instructions for Forms 1094-C and 1095-C

description: Total annualized employee contribution amount for the benefit plan, calculated as the per-pay-period contribution multiplied by the number of pay periods. Used directly in ACA affordability safe harbor calculations (W-2, Rate of Pay, Federal Poverty Line methods) for 1095-C Line 16 coding.
reference: IRS Form 1095-C; ACA Section 4980H(b); IRS Rev. Proc. 2014-37; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Total annualized employer contribution amount for the benefit plan. Used in total benefits cost analysis, EBITDA reporting, and financial planning. Expressed in USD.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; SOX Section 302; IRS Publication 15-B

description: Confirmation or member ID number issued by the insurance carrier or benefit provider upon enrollment confirmation. Used for carrier reconciliation, EDI 834 transaction matching, and associate ID card issuance tracking.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; HIPAA EDI 834 Transaction Set; ASC X12 Standard

description: Date on which the enrollment data was transmitted to the insurance carrier via EDI 834 or carrier feed. Used for carrier reconciliation, enrollment lag analysis, and compliance with carrier enrollment submission deadlines.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; HIPAA EDI 834 Transaction Set; ASC X12 Standard

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Election Date
description: Date on which the associate elected COBRA continuation coverage following termination of active enrollment. Nullable; populated only when COBRA is elected. Used for COBRA compliance tracking and carrier notification.
reference: COBRA (29 U.S.C. § 1161); DOL COBRA Regulations; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

business_glossary_term: Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act (COBRA) Eligible Indicator
description: Indicates whether the associate is eligible for COBRA continuation coverage upon termination of this enrollment. Drives COBRA election notice generation and compliance tracking under 29 U.S.C. § 1161. Applicable to medical, dental, and vision plan types.
reference: COBRA (29 U.S.C. § 1161); DOL COBRA Regulations; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Coverage level selected by the associate, determining the scope of dependents covered and the applicable premium contribution rates. Drives both employee and employer contribution calculations and ACA affordability analysis.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; ACA Section 4980H; IRS Form 1095-C

description: Timestamp when this benefit enrollment record was first created in Workday HCM Benefits Administration. Establishes the audit trail origin for the enrollment lifecycle and supports ERISA record retention compliance.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; ERISA Section 107; SOX Section 302

description: Number of dependents enrolled under this benefit plan enrollment. Used to validate coverage tier selection, support ACA dependent coverage reporting on 1095-C Part III, and drive carrier enrollment file generation.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; IRS Form 1095-C Part III; ACA Section 1001

description: Indicates whether the disability plan enrollment is for short-term disability (STD) or long-term disability (LTD) coverage. Applicable only when plan_type is 'disability'. Drives benefit duration, elimination period, and OSHA/FMLA integration for leave management.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; OSHA; FMLA (29 U.S.C. § 2601); ERISA

description: Date on which the benefit coverage becomes active for the associate. Determines the start of the coverage period for ACA 1095-C reporting, carrier billing, and payroll deduction initiation.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; IRS Form 1095-C; ACA Section 4980H

description: Per-pay-period pre-tax payroll deduction amount contributed by the associate toward the benefit plan premium. Used in benefits cost analysis, payroll reconciliation, and ACA affordability safe harbor calculations. Expressed in USD.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; IRS Publication 15-B; ACA Section 4980H(b); SOX Section 302

description: Per-pay-period amount contributed by Grocery toward the associate's benefit plan premium. Used in total compensation reporting, benefits cost analysis, EBITDA impact modeling, and ACA minimum value determination. Expressed in USD.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; IRS Publication 15-B; ACA Section 4980H; SOX Section 302

description: Percentage of the associate's eligible compensation that Grocery matches in the 401(k) plan, up to the match cap. Applicable only when plan_type is '401k'. Used in total compensation reporting and ERISA plan administration. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 3.00 = 3%).
reference: IRS Section 401(k); ERISA; IRS Publication 560; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Date on which the associate's benefit enrollment was confirmed and finalized in Workday HCM Benefits Administration. Distinct from the effective_date (when coverage starts). Used for enrollment audit trails and open enrollment completion tracking.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; ERISA Section 104

description: Externally-known business identifier for the benefit enrollment event, generated by Workday HCM Benefits Administration. Used for cross-system reconciliation with payroll, carrier feeds, and ACA reporting.
The event or process that triggered the benefit enrollment or change. Drives eligibility validation rules, enrollment window enforcement, and ACA special enrollment period tracking in Workday HCM.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; ACA Section 1311; ERISA Section 701; IRS Notice 2014-55

description: Current lifecycle state of the benefit enrollment record. Active indicates coverage is in force; terminated indicates coverage has ended; pending indicates awaiting carrier confirmation; waived indicates the associate declined coverage; suspended indicates coverage is temporarily paused.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; ACA Section 4980H

business_glossary_term: Flexible Spending Account (FSA) Annual Election Amount
description: Annual dollar amount elected by the associate for a Flexible Spending Account (FSA) plan. Applicable only when plan_type is 'FSA'. Subject to IRS annual contribution limits. Used in payroll deduction scheduling and benefits cost analysis. Expressed in USD.
reference: IRS Publication 969; IRS Section 125; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

business_glossary_term: Health Savings Account (HSA) Annual Election Amount
description: Annual dollar amount elected by the associate for a Health Savings Account (HSA). Applicable only when plan_type is 'HSA'. Subject to IRS annual contribution limits (self-only vs. family). Used in payroll deduction scheduling and ACA HDHP compliance tracking. Expressed in USD.
reference: IRS Publication 969; IRS Section 223; ACA; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this benefit enrollment record in Workday HCM. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail maintenance, and downstream data pipeline incremental load processing.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; SOX Section 302; ERISA Section 107

description: Face value of the life insurance coverage elected by the associate. Applicable only when plan_type is 'life_insurance'. May be a flat amount or a multiple of annual salary. Used in benefits cost analysis and imputed income calculations for IRS Section 79 compliance. Expressed in USD.
reference: IRS Section 79; IRS Publication 15-B; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Payroll system deduction code associated with this benefit enrollment, used to route the employee contribution to the correct benefit deduction bucket in SAP Payroll or Workday Payroll. Ensures accurate pre-tax and post-tax deduction classification.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll; SAP FI Payroll Integration; IRS Publication 15-B

description: Human-readable name of the specific benefit plan as defined in Workday HCM (e.g., 'Grocery PPO Gold 2024', 'Grocery HSA Bronze 2024'). Used in associate-facing communications, open enrollment portals, and benefits cost analysis reporting.

Category of benefit plan in which the associate is enrolled. Drives ACA reporting obligations, payroll deduction codes, and carrier EDI file routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical|dental|vision|401k|FSA|HSA|life_insurance|disability — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; IRS Publication 15-B; ACA Section 4980H

description: The calendar or fiscal plan year (e.g., 2024) to which this enrollment applies. Used to segment benefit enrollments by open enrollment cycle, support year-over-year benefits cost analysis, and align with ACA 1095-C reporting periods.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; IRS Form 1095-C; ACA Section 4980H

description: Indicates whether the employee contribution for this benefit plan is deducted on a pre-tax basis under IRS Section 125 (Cafeteria Plan). True for pre-tax deductions (medical, dental, vision, FSA, HSA); False for post-tax deductions (certain life insurance, disability). Drives payroll tax calculation.
reference: IRS Section 125; IRS Publication 15-B; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Specific type of qualifying life event (QLE) that triggered a mid-year enrollment change outside of open enrollment. Populated only when enrollment_source is 'qualifying_life_event'. Required for ERISA and ACA special enrollment period compliance documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: marriage|divorce|birth|adoption|death|loss_of_coverage|relocation — promote to reference product]
reference: ERISA Section 701; ACA Section 1311; IRS Notice 2014-55; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Percentage of the associate's eligible compensation contributed to the 401(k) or retirement plan per pay period. Applicable only when plan_type is '401k'. Used in payroll deduction calculations, employer match determination, and ERISA compliance reporting. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 6.00 = 6%).
reference: IRS Section 401(k); ERISA; IRS Publication 560; Workday HCM Benefits Administration

description: Date on which the benefit coverage ends for the associate. Nullable for active enrollments. Populated upon voluntary termination, qualifying life event, employment separation, or plan year end. Used in ACA 1095-C line-level reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; IRS Form 1095-C; ACA Section 4980H; COBRA (29 U.S.C. § 1161)

description: Reason provided by the associate for declining (waiving) benefit coverage. Populated only when enrollment_status is 'waived'. Used for ACA reporting, benefits participation analysis, and workforce analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: covered_by_spouse|covered_by_parent|covered_by_other_employer|medicare|medicaid|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; ACA Section 4980H; IRS Form 1095-C

description: Source system event identifier from Workday HCM Benefits Administration corresponding to the enrollment business process event. Used for source system traceability, audit reconciliation, and support case resolution.
reference: Workday HCM Benefits Administration; SOX Section 302

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each Grocery employee (associate). Serves as the primary key and enterprise-wide reference for all workforce-related records across Workday HCM, Kronos Workforce Central, and downstream analytics systems.

Associate ID of the direct manager (supervisor) of this associate, enabling the organizational hierarchy to be traversed. Used for approval workflows, performance reviews, and org chart reporting in Workday HCM.

Identifier for the primary work location (store, distribution center, pharmacy, fuel center, or corporate office) where the associate is assigned. Used for scheduling, OSHA incident reporting, and same-store sales labor analytics.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; OSHA 300 Log

description: The compensation grade or band assigned to the associate's job code (e.g., Band 1, Band 2, Grade A, Grade B). Defines the salary range minimum and maximum for the role. Used for pay equity analysis, merit increase eligibility, and internal mobility decisions.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; Equal Pay Act; EEOC

description: SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code to which the associate's labor costs are charged. Enables granular labor cost reporting by store, department, or function. Critical for EBITDA analysis and store-level P&L reporting.
reference: SAP CO (Controlling); SOX; Workday HCM Payroll

description: Date of birth of the associate. Required for age verification (e.g., alcohol/tobacco sales eligibility), benefits eligibility determination, and compliance with child labor laws under FLSA. Stored per CCPA and GDPR data minimization.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; FLSA; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Organizational department code to which the associate is assigned (e.g., Produce, Pharmacy, Distribution Center, Corporate Finance). Sourced from Workday HCM organizational hierarchy and used for labor cost allocation in SAP FI/CO.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; SAP FI/CO

description: Self-reported disability status of the associate per Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act and ADA requirements. Required for OFCCP Section 503 affirmative action reporting. Classified as confidential. Collected voluntarily.
reference: ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act); Section 503 Rehabilitation Act; OFCCP; Workday HCM Core HR

description: EEO-1 job category assigned to the associate per EEOC reporting requirements. Required for annual EEO-1 Component 1 filing submitted to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission. Classified as confidential to protect workforce demographic data. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exec_senior_mgr|first_mid_mgr|professionals|technicians|sales_workers|admin_support|craft_workers|operatives|laborers|service_workers — promote to reference product]
reference: EEOC EEO-1 Report; Title VII of the Civil Rights Act; Workday HCM Core HR

description: Full name of the associate's designated emergency contact person. Used by store management and HR in the event of a workplace injury, OSHA incident, or medical emergency. Sourced from Workday HCM Core HR.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; OSHA 300 Log

description: Phone number of the associate's designated emergency contact. Used by store management and HR in the event of a workplace injury, OSHA incident, or medical emergency.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; OSHA 300 Log

description: Human-readable, externally-known employee badge number used on store floors, scheduling systems (Kronos Workforce Central), and payroll. Printed on associate ID badges and referenced in HR correspondence.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; Kronos Workforce Central

description: Current lifecycle status of the associate's employment at Grocery. Drives access control, payroll processing eligibility, benefits enrollment windows, and workforce analytics. 'on_leave' covers FMLA, medical, and personal leave. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|on_leave|terminated|suspended|retired|pending_hire — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; FMLA; FLSA

description: Self-reported race/ethnicity of the associate per EEOC EEO-1 categories. Required for annual EEO-1 Component 1 filing. Classified as confidential. Collected voluntarily. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hispanic_latino|white|black_african_american|asian|native_hawaiian_pacific_islander|american_indian_alaska_native|two_or_more_races — promote to reference product]
reference: EEOC EEO-1 Report; Title VII; Workday HCM Core HR

description: Legal given name of the associate as recorded in Workday HCM. Used for payroll, benefits administration, associate self-service, and compliance reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Indicates whether the associate is classified as exempt or non-exempt under the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA). Non-exempt associates are entitled to overtime pay at 1.5x for hours worked beyond 40 per week. Critical for payroll compliance and wage-and-hour litigation defense.
reference: FLSA (Fair Labor Standards Act); DOL Wage and Hour Division; Workday HCM Payroll

description: Self-reported gender of the associate. Collected for EEO-1 reporting, pay equity analysis, and diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) analytics. Classified as confidential. Collected voluntarily per EEOC guidelines.
reference: EEOC EEO-1 Report; Title VII; Workday HCM Core HR

description: The official date the associate was hired by Grocery. Used to calculate tenure, benefits eligibility waiting periods, vesting schedules, and service anniversary milestones. Sourced from Workday HCM Core HR.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; ERISA; ACA Section 4980H

description: Indicates whether a terminated associate is eligible for rehire at Grocery. Set by HR during the offboarding process based on termination reason and disciplinary history. Used to screen applicants in the recruiting workflow.

Standardized job classification code from the Workday HCM job catalog. Used for compensation benchmarking, EEO-1 reporting, labor cost allocation to cost centers, and integration with Kronos Workforce Central for scheduling rule assignment.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; EEOC EEO-1 Report

description: Official job title of the associate as defined in Workday HCM job catalog (e.g., Store Associate, Pharmacist, Distribution Center Picker, Department Manager, Corporate Analyst). Used in org charts, compliance reporting, and associate self-service.

Date of the most recent disciplinary action taken against the associate (verbal warning, written warning, suspension, or termination). Used to track progressive discipline timelines and support wrongful termination defense. Null if no disciplinary history.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; NLRA; EEOC

description: Type of the most recent disciplinary action in the associate's progressive discipline record. Used alongside last_disciplinary_action_date to determine the next step in the progressive discipline process. Null if no disciplinary history.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; NLRA; EEOC

description: Legal surname of the associate as recorded in Workday HCM. Used for payroll, benefits administration, associate self-service, and compliance reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Primary mobile phone number for the associate. Used for shift notifications, emergency contact, two-factor authentication for Workday self-service, and Kronos scheduling alerts.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Last four digits of the associate's Social Security Number (SSN). Stored as a partial identifier for identity verification in associate self-service and payroll inquiries. Full SSN is stored only in the Workday HCM source system under PCI/PII controls and is NOT replicated to the lakehouse.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; IRS Publication 15; SOX; CCPA
